PROLOGUE.

★

Luminaria is a world beyond humans, a world for cruel people with magic. Heartless beings who live on power and feed insatiably on it. 

They stomp on the weak without mercy, shed their blood with celebratory grins.

Citizens of Luminaria are forbidden from having affairs with humans. 

If such happens and a pregnancy occurs between a Luminarian and a human, then the child is born powerless and weak and automatically becomes a slave for sale in The Dark Room while the parents gets executed.

Shayla grew up in one of the coldest slave cages of The Dark Room. Born of a human father and a Luminarian mother, her fate has been sealed since birth—destined to be a slave.

Now in Luminaria, there are two ruling families. 

The Millions—main owners of the city. They control the Grand Palace and are the most powerful beings existing. Pride is their armor, wickednes is their food, richness is their sign, and that even showed through their name. MILLIONS.

The Spectres—second owners of the city. They were the original owners, but they weren't as powerful. They completely lose their powers at midnight every night.

For this reason, The Millions family snatched the city from them, and they were made second-in-command.

The more slave a family has, the more their status elevate, and that's why both families are the major visitors of The Dark Room monthly to compete at the auctions.

Shayla's sale day finally came,, her cage was dragged out for auction in the Dark Room.

She watched the two families fight over her price, bidding hatefully with their bid-paddles, bloody voices and brutal utterances.

For the first time in years, The Spectres won over The Millions in the auction, but just as Shayla was about to get handed over to the Spectres, there was a turnaround.

A turnaround which reshaped her life—for worse, flinging her into the abyssal depth of darkness.

Are you curious? I know you are.

C'mon, let's dive in.

Don't dare miss out!

Chapter 1&2.

[Luminaria Grand Palace]

The palace is a towering paradise, dominating the city like a world apart. It's silhouette cut against the skyline of glass towers and neon veins. Though the city bursted with modern life, the palace seems untouched by time. It's like an empire within an empire.

It's in two parts, and this particular part is the East, meant for the High Royals—The Millions Family.

The main castle is high and dark with it's obsidian glass, blackened steel and skyscraper-like spires which are clawing to the clouds, their edges sharp like blades.

Besides that, there are several exclusive mansions for clansmen and extended family of the high royals.

There are quarters for maids, and small Wings for wretched slaves, so the palace is extremely full and is always alive every hour.

In the middle of the evasive palace, there's a luxuriously tall tower. "Heartless Tower" is written in big irregular letters in front of it.

Luxury won't be enough to describe the richness of this place.

Jude stood in the archery field at the back of the main castle, sipping from a cup of warm water which was brought for him by a maid.

The maid held a red tray, and her head is down as Jude sipped in large gulps, his throat bobbing. Thin lines of water rode down his flawless skin.

He's drinking with an hand, but he held a arrow and bow with the other.

After drinking, he dropped the cup back on the tray a little too rough, and the tray broke into two, together with the cup.

"Just dispose 'em off" He shrugged.

"Yes, Grand Prince." The woman took out a napkin, gently wiping the water on his throat with it before rushing out of the field.

Jude readjusted his stand in front of the dartboard, his long cross earrings dangling. The strands of hair falling over his eyes look overly attractive.

He's Jude Millions, first son of the Millions Royal Family.

"All that arrogance, and your powers are still low. Breaking a tray and cup means nothing, it just shows how useless you actually are." a cold, sad voice spoke behind, causing Jude to turn with a smirk, and it's exactly who he expected.

Sadie Millions, the second child of the Millions Royal Family.

She's a twin though, so it's safe to say she and her twin brother—Soren, are the second borns, and they have enough venom in them than snakes.

They're both crueller than the devil, and Jude might be their senior brother, but in this family, things just work differently.

Power buys respect, not age.

Sadie picked up a arrow and bow too,  her face holding nothing but cruelty as she stood beside Jude.

Her hair is dark in her neat bun, she's wearing sleek leather palazzo pants, black heels and a cropped blouse. She looks like she birthed evil herself as her lips are also black.

"You really wanna do this, little sister? We all you and Soren are useless without each other." Jude mocked, smiling.

Sadie shut her eyes, easily mind-linking with Soren...

"Get your rotten ass here, Soren!" She harshly ordered, but nothing came.

Just the sound of unstopping water splashes and the shower, meaning Soren is in the bathroom.

Sadie's eyes opened, a calm breath ran out of her nostrils.

"I'll go first," she muttered resolutely, setting her arrow into the bow.

"Without Soren? Are you sure?" Jude snickered hard.

Sadie went silent, focusing on her target — the dartboard.

With one unrelenting pull, she released the arrow. It whistled and flew to the dartboard violently, hitting the middle so hard.

She raised her chin proudly at Jude who was silent too as he reset his arrow in his bow.

Sadie stood behind him, watching darkly as Jude released his arrow.

It hit the middle of the dartboard, pushing away Sadie's arrow and splitting the board into two.

"Fûck" Sadie muttered shockingly.

Jude bursted into laughter.

"Told ya, little sister. You should have known your place. You're useless without Soren." 

"I hate it when you laugh, Jude." Soren's hard voice broke into the field as he strolled inside, twinning with his leather pants and jacket.

His face is just as round and cold as Sadie's. He's slightly taller than her though, but Sadie wouldn't have it, and that's why she's always on heels.

"Why the hell are you just coming!! You know how much I hate losing!!" Sadie yelled angrily at him.

"I was in the bathroom! You don't expect me to come running! You heard water splashes when you mind-linked! Didn't you?" He glared, but she was already at his face.

"You could have ran out nakedly and blind the maids who'd dare look at you! Jude won! He fuckin won!" 

Soren's face twisted into something gravely, and his stare shot to Jude who's still laughing at the two.

His laughter refused to stop—it's not stopping anytime soon, and that angered the twins so much.

They looked at each other, their eyes sharing faint glows before they faced Jude again, stretching their hands to him together.

The arrows in the weapons bag lifted on their own, and before Jude could retaliate or switch spots, the arrows already pierced his body.

There are 10 arrows, and they all went lodged inside his chest, coming out at his back.

His blood was raining in a matter of seconds, and he crumbled on his knees. He opened his mouth to curse, but it was blood instead.

"Fool" the twins smirked, leaving the field and returning to the castle.

The maids on their way had to run and hide, and some went gummed to the wall with their heads painfully bowed in fear as they walked across.

"I never expected this power competition to stretch to the point of piercing each other with arrows." a authoritative female voice stopped them when they got to the base of the gold stair railings.

In sync, they looked up, and there they are... their parents.

The rulers of this city.

Zeke Millions and Azura Millions—his wife.

Both are thousands of years old, but they still look as young as hell, their dark hairs is a testament to that.

Azura is in a long red silk dress, paired with black gloves while Zeke is in a equally red suit, both holding cups of red wine firmly.

They're looking down at the twins with unblinking eyes of disciplinarians.

"Jude started it, mother!" Sadie glared up venomously.

"I did nothing to warrant that treatment! All I did was defeat her!" Jude rushed in, body still bloody and arrows still stuck inside.

The pains are gone, he only has to get rid of the arrows and heal himself right now.

"You fuckin laughed!" Soren glared.

"So it's a sin to laugh after defeating you losers who can't do shit without each other?" Jude glared back.

"Yes! It's a grave sin! Better watch your laughter from now on!" Sadie fired.

"Now I see why you both hate Ryker to the core. He'd break you two to pieces, tendons by tendons without even lifting a finger!" Jude snapped.

"Don't you dare!!!" Soren yelled.

"Don't you dare compare us to that bastard!" Sadie completed the statement.

"I command this noise to go down, now!" Zeke ordered from the staircase.

"Shut up!!" The twins yelled at him together.

Dead silence fell in the castle immediately, and the twins calmed after realizing what they just did.

"That wasn't intentional," Soren muttered.

"Intentional or not, there's a punishment for every cross of boundary." Azura smiled, sipping from her wine cup.

Zeke called in the castle funding manager, and the man came running inside.

"Grand Supreme." He bowed.

"Lock the family joint account tonight, and it's not to be opened till daybreak." Zeke ordered.

"What!" The twins gasped as the manager ran out to carry out the order.

Jude smiled.

"Tonight is the Dark Rook auction, locking the joint account means we'll be losing the slave to the low Spectres Family!" Sadie said restlessly.

"Exactly my point. You both have to experience losing for once, so next time, you know it's not a good deal shouting at me." Zeke finalized.

"Father, you do realize it's been years since the Spectres last won against us, right? If it happens again tonight, I and Sadie would be mocked at the Ballet School!" Soren desperately hit the staircase.

"Son, you don't get it, do you?" Azura dropped her wine cup for a maid. "That's the point—the mockery."

She left with Zeke, but their commanding presence still remained in the hall as the twins faced Jude.

"Good riddance" Jude scoffed, removing one of the arrows from his stomach before going up.

The twins stood undecided for a minute at the hall, then they both broke into a run, up the staircase.

They went to their conjoined room.

"How much money do we have left in our personal safe?" Sadie asked.

"About five billion?" Soren's eyes threw the personal safe door open, and they entered the small room in the middle of their rooms.

There are wooden shelves inside, and the shelves contain bundles of cash in calculated sums, stacked up.

They got four bags, throwing the cash bundles inside and counting at the same time.

By the time they got done, the cash is exactly 4.9 billion.

"Not enough, we're surely losing at the auction tonight, I can't think! You mean to tell me those dogs from the lowly Spectres Family will mock tomorrow?" Sadie punched the wall, cracking that spot, but her knuckles stayed fine.

"There's a way, though." Soren talked, and Sadie flicked her eyes to him, getting it instantly.

"The bastard has to come to the auction, he's the only one in this family who manages his wealth without the joint account." Soren muttered.

"He left his tower with his lackeys since two days ago and hasn't returned. Where could he be?" Sadie held her forehead.

"We can always call him back with his weakness" Soren smiled, wickedly.

Sadie's face lit up with a deadlier smile, twinning with him.

They left the room with sharp steps, Sadie's heels clicking and Soren's boots thudding, crossing rooms till they arrived at the staircase.

They didn't bother descending, they just jumped, landing down easily. They entered the castle kitchen, meeting the old head of chefs with his daughter Xena, both eating breakfast on the kitchen island.

Sadie's nails dug into Xena's hair as she grabbed her, tightly.

The old chef stood, dropping his fork, but before he could defend his daughter, Soren picked the fork and stabbed his palm with it, literally nailing it to the table.

The old chef cried out, the custard and pie in his mouth pouring to his chest.

"Xena!" He shouted.

"Father!" Xena cried. That was the only sound she could make before the evil twins dragged her out of the wide wealthy kitchen.

"Xena!!" The old chef's crying voice still echoed behind them till they arrived at the pool room.

Xena was thrown to her knees, and she straightened up as the twins towered in front of her. Her knees sting so much.

"Please... please I did nothing wrong." She shook her head.

Sadie threw a immediate deafening slap at her face, and she fell, rolling fast into the pool.

The twins bursted into a fit of laughter, watching her struggle since she can't swim.

"Hel...p...ah...ugh...hel...p!!"

Their laughter increased, and Sadie rested her elbow on Soren's shoulder.

"Isn't she so fuckin pretty like this?"

"Tell me about it," the guy nodded.

"Let's get to business then," Sadie pulled out her phone, hissing out.

"The fact that we use human technology pisses me off."

She dialled 'bastard' on her phone, and it rang for two long times before the line connected.

"Hello, bastard. Your sister is chilling in the pool, and like you know...she can't swim." She said, blowing air proudly on her fingers.

"She won't die cos her lifeline is hidden, but if she drinks too much water from this poisoned pool, then she'd live with sickness for the rest of her life. You should come back home wherever you are, meet us at the Dark Rook auction and bring as much cash as you have along. Otherwise, sickness is knocking at your sister's door." She continued.

Nothing still came from the other end, so she hung up.

"You think he'd come?" Soren muttered.

Sadie looked at Xena who's still struggling in the pool, shrugging...

"Of course. That bastard might be bloody ice, but he loves his sister."

[West Palace]

This part of the palace belongs to the Low Royals—The Spectres.

And though it contains everything that the East palace has, but in smaller quantities.

Their castle is smaller, mansions for clansmen is smaller too. Their quarters for maids and Wings for slaves are even worse.

Their luxury is limited, and their slaves are 30+ while the Millions Family have 200+

Their gate is different from the East gate, and if anyone from this place makes the unforgivable mistake of using the East gate, then it's brutal punishment with acid rain.

The Spectres Family live in fear of The Millions, but at the same time, they're the only people who can contend with them as they're powerful too, just that they lose their powers at midnight every night.

It's some minutes past 7pm on the clock, and the family is sitting round the royal dinning, the soft clattering of spoons on plates and water journeying down throats filled the room as they ate in silence.

Kasia is the head of the family. She lost her husband some years ago, and that only contributed more to the weakness of the family.

She had two kids with the husband before his death though.

Winona Spectre is currently sitting beside Kasia 

She's the first child who's as brave and relentless as her mother. She's beautiful.

Blaise Spectre is sitting at the other side of Kasia.

He's the second child, a nonchalant handsome redhead.

At the opposite side of the table, there's Astor—Kasia's new husband, and Faye, his daughter.

Kasia is a powerful woman, so it was Astor who changed his surname to her own after they got married, and Faye hates that.

Astor is a soft, supportive man, but Faye is far from it.

She was rolling eyes with malice as she dropped her spoon, getting up from her chair.

"I'm full"

"Me too" Winona stood too.

Faye smiled at her. Winona is the only person she seems to like in this family.

"Dark Room will be open by 8, we've got one hour to drive there," Faye checked her wristwatch.

"We can just teleport, no one wants that driving hassle." Blaise spoke, pressing his lips with tissues.

"No one is teleporting! You know how exhausted your powers get if you try just one teleportation! We're not as powerful as the Millions, so careful. I'm not losing a child!" Kasia warned.

"You can stay home if you don't wanna go," Winona spoke.

"If I don't wanna go?" Blaise stood with a scowl.

He strolled out of the room with his hands tucked in his pockets.

"The money bags got taken to the car trunks already. Use the limo, and make sure you return before midnight." Astor said.

"Sure, new dad." Winona smiled.

"Be fine, Faye." Kasia nodded at her.

"Sure, new mum." Faye gave her a fake smile, then she left with Winona.

"Your daughter and her fake smiles, she doesn't have to pretend to like me." Kasia picked her glass of wine.

"Give her time, it's hard for Faye to like someone, and you talk as if your children are innocent too. Winona is the only one who seems to love me genuinely, Blaise is just hateful." Astor spoke across the table.

"Blaise is territorial, not hateful." Kasia raised her glass.

"Whatever you say" Astor clinked glasses with her.

"What's the use of going to the auction when The Millions will always win anyways." He relaxed.

"If we don't show up at all, The Millions will see us as cowards, and I'm not ready to add that to the list of their unending dirty names for us." Kasia sighed.

"Don't overthink, this torture from the tyrannical Millions family will soon be over." Astor assured.

"Hope so" Kasia smiled.

They stood at the same time and leaned across the table to kiss deeply.

[East Palace, Millions Castle~ Major Royal Bathroom]

A slow instrumental played in the large stereo disc in one side of the red bathroom, accompanied by the rippling of fresh bubbles from the excessively large tub.

The tub is so large that it almost took over the bathroom, and the white foams stayed fresh as Azura sat in it, her head slung back on the tub edge, and her eyes are closed. She's obviously enjoying the playing instrumental music, the violin.

Zeke entered the bathroom, and despite the silence in his steps, Azura's eyes opened, beholding him.

He stripped himself, entering the tub nakedly with her. He sat across her, his hard gaze falling on her breàsts.

"Many kids sucked those devilish tits, but they're still standing," He intentionally said.

"Still a slut for my body?" Azura smiled, caressing his balls with her toes underwater.

"Always" he groaned.

"And where the fûck did the bastard go to? Been a day since I last saw him. He's aware he can't be far from this castle, isn't he?" Azura smirked.

"If he's not back by tomorrow, I'm killing Xena." Zeke declared. 

Azura leaned closer, her suddenness not surprising.

"And are you really letting the lowly Spectres win the auction tonight?" She dropped her leg, her red fingernails seeming to stain the white porcelain tub body.

"Lose?" Zeke breathed hard. "Getting what they want is the twins speciality. Watch as they bring home the slave, even if it means killing everyone at the Dark Room. I trust my spawns." 

A glint of hostility flashed across his eyes as he vomited the words.

--

[Dark Room, 7:50Pm]

Crowd of royals filled the inside and outside of the room, caging cups of wine and bid-paddles in their hands as they flooded inside, ready for tonight's show.

The Dark Room could compete for one of the largest mansions in Luminaria, but calling it a Room is cos of the purpose it was built for.

For auctioning slaves—for selling forbidden breeds.

Winona led Faye and Blaise inside, and the other royal clansmen made way for them as they walked in synchrony to their spot in the midst of the toxic regality.

* The Spectres are here.

* Aww, look at Winona. I can't get over her beauty. Never.

* Isn't Faye prettier?

* Blaise's red hair and straight nose gets me always.

* Wait...the parents aren't here? It's just the children tonight? 

* Makes it more fun! Where are The Millions please?

"There they are!" A man pointed at the long staircase rail.

Sadie, Soren and Jude stood there, looking as imposing and majestic as ever with their wine cups and black suits. Jude has no plans to partake in the bidding, he's just here to laugh when the twins lose tonight.

No one is standing around them. Everyone stood at least five feet away, and that's how it's done.

Sadie and Soren have that degrading looks on their faces as they stared at the Spectres at the ground floor.

No one. Not even a single person would dare to comment about them. Everybody in this city knows about the evil twins hot temper, so it's better to not to mess with the spawns.

Everyone stood and bowed to them...

"Greetings, Grand Princes and Princess!!"

"Sadie became more arrogant," Winona muttered from the ground floor, rage crawling into her head.

"Suppress your rage, you can't start a fight right now. Do not stir trouble."  Blaise held her.

"That's right, Winona. We came to bid, not fight." Faye added.

Winona calmed, and the room git pregnant with surprised gasps when the auction committee came in with some guards.

The guards are the ones carrying the cage containing the slave that's for sale tonight, and she's the main reason for the gasps.

There's a round chain on her neck that looks damn heavy. The chains are cuffed on her wrists and ankles too. Her hair is tangled and twisted, and her skirt is brown—dirty. Her blouse is tattered, and she looks tiny and slim as a result of underfeeding.

But she's really beautiful with her full lips and soft brown eyes.

Winona's eyes are wide like the eyes of every other royal in the room.

Even Jude... He's staring too, his hand grabbing the rail edge tight.

"Love at first sight?" Soren spoke beside him, 

"Mind your business, Soren." Jude huffed, then he returned his interested eyes to the slave.

Sadie is busy looking at Winona, and there's a sly smile on her lips.

"That bîtch has probably fallen." 

"What do you mean?" Soren inched closer.

"Winona, look how she's staring at the slave like she wants to eat her. Have you ever seen her fancying any man?" Sadie drank all her wine.

"You're saying... Winona Spectre is into girls?" Soren snickered.

"That bîtch is les, and she probably wants the slave more than ever, judging by the way she's devouring her with her eyes." Sadie cleared.

"More reason to go hard on this auction. The bastard should better show up." Soren muttered.

The slave cage was set down in front of the room, and the poor slave knelt inside, holding the iron bars and staring hopelessly at the sea of wicked people, waiting to buy her.

The auction committee stood in front of the cage, covering her from the crowd at first.

"No time to waste, let's start the auction, and as usual, the highest bidder gets the slave. Her name's Shayla" Their chief declared.

"One billion!" Jude started, raising his bid-paddle.

The twins turned to him immediately, surprised that he's bidding.

Isn't he here to mock?

"1.5 billion!" Winona shouted from the ground floor.

"2.5 billion!" Sadie raised her paddle.

"3.5!" Winona shouted.

Silence.

There was silence in the room afterwards, and the committee are starting to wonder what's going on.

No one has started bidding with billions before, it always started from a million, but tonight is different.

The heat resumed when Soren raised his paddle too.

"4 billion!"

"6 billion!" Winona shouted from the ground floor.

The twins hands went down, they looked at each other.

Their limit is 4.9, so they can't go higher unless the bastard comes, but he's not even anywhere around.

Sadie bit deeply into her nails, but then Jude spoke...

"10 billion!"

The twins eyes snapped back to him in obvious shock.

10? 

Jude is willing to spend 10Billion on a slave?

"15 billion!" Winona raised from the ground floor, shocking the committee again.

Faye and Blaise look shocked behind her, too. 

"Don't tell me you actually like that malnourished slave." Blaise smirked.

"20 billion!" Jude went higher.

"This is crazier than crazy," Faye muttered.

"35!" Winona returned.

"40!" Jude raised.

"60!" Winona raised back.

Another round of silence fell in the room, the shock so intense and hard.

60? 

That's about the highest bidding price ever in this place.

What exactly is going on??

Even Jude has reached his limit. He can't raise more than 40 without the joint family account.

He grabbed the railings tighter, gritting his jaws.

"Going for 60... Anyone bidding higher?" The committee Chief announced.

Silence.

"Anyone going higher?" He repeated.

"Call the bastard again!" Soren faced Sadie.

"I've been calling him since we got here, it's off!" Sadie snapped.

"Stop calling him the bastard, his name is Ryker!" Jude corrected.

"He's not a royal, he's the son of the old chef!" Sadie argued.

"Son of the old chef, yet he's powerful than you both combined. He's more respected and much more powerful than anybody in this city!" Jude laughed.

"Seems the arrows you received earlier weren't enough, you want bonuses?" Soren glared.

"She's going for 60 billions!" The committee chief announced, pulling them out of their argument.

"No" the twins chorused, preparing to jump down and behead everybody.

Jude's jaws tightened again.

Winona smiled wide at the ground floor, ready to receive the slave.

The hotness in the room was scathing, but it got watered down suddenly, replaced with coldness.

Dry ice grew from the ground, so crusty and large, spreading to the walls and ceilings and breaking some parts, causing plasters and ceiling pieces to fall on the ground.

Two chandeliers plucked and broke to bits, scattering around.

The ice kept extending, growing larger and higher and colder, spreading on the grounds and running a path to the iron cage.

Shayla shifted back in fear, her eyes fearfully wide.

"He's here" Sadie and Soren said with matching smirks.

The ice and the coldness that hit the royals alerted them of his presence, too. Everyone fell on their knees, bowing their heads to the ground.

Everyone except the Spectres who're still standing.

"The bastard is here." Winona muttered, turning around with Faye and Blaise.

Truly, he stood there in the middle of the room. The bastard.

He's wearing a long black royal robe, messy dark hair with slight sprinkles of whiteness. He's barefoot, and water from the ice is cascading at his clean feet. A small ruby ring is on his middle toe. He smells like doom, and carries the aura of blood.

There's a smear of bloodstain on his face, but it's definitely not his own blood. His goodlooking face is intensified with his coloured lips.

Ryker Millions. The bastard. Demigod Of The Grand Palace.

His eyes rolled to the cage, and Shayla was fast to duck her head to the ground, bowing.

Jude was smiling on the top floor, seeing Ryker feels refreshing to him.

"You're finished, Winona! We're winning again!" Sadie grinned.

Soren laughed.

Winona's fists are balled, she stared hatefully at Ryker.

The royals who're bowing on their knees are still in that position.

Ryker powerfully waved his left hand, and the cage padlock broke away, shattering on the ground.

The cage door broke, too, falling away from the cage body and landing on the icy ground heavily.

Shayla shifted back on her butt inside, shivering as a result of cold and as a result of him.

Another wave of his hand, she was thrown out of the cage with a force she has no idea about.

She fell on her belly in front of him, getting wet by the melting ice. Her skirt lifted up her butt, revealing her thin underwear.

Her groan was low, painful and shaky as she continued laying there at his feet, using her elbows to tilt, not daring to look up.

With droopy frigid eyes, Ryker cast a look down at her, extreme tension dropping in the room once again.

He didn't raise any bid-paddle.

Didn't overreact.

He didn't raise his voice, but...

His voice is weighty—madly deep, sending extra chills down several spines as he said the first word.

"150 billion." 

"What!!!"

[East Palace, 10PM ~ Pool Room]

Xena is floating on the pool surface, her clothes ballooning around her and her lids tightly shut.

She had struggled for long before giving up a while ago. She looks totally dead.

The glass door opened with a forceful slam which hit the wall, breaking the glass away.

Ryker entered with his two knights—Cheyenne and Sage.

Ryker nearly jumped into the pool first, but Sage did it.

He jumped in roughly, his entrance sending water splashes across the room.

He pulled Xena out of the water, setting her beside the pool.

Ryker squatted neatly beside her, feeling her pulse with two fingers. 

It's still pulsing.

He placed his right hand on her chest, sending some of his energy into her. Frosty blue light surrounded his palm, and his gaze stayed so sure as he expected her to jerk up.

"Those inhumane twins," Sage muttered angrily behind, dripping with water.

"My Lord can't even kill them, and that's enough to make me mad." Cheyenne said in an equally angry voice, her face unfriendly.

"Quiet!" Ryker snapped, causing their lips to snap shut.

Xena coughed back to life, water splurging out of her mouth, ears and nostrils.

Ryker's hahd left her chest, but he didn't stand. His eyes stayed on her.

Xena sat up, her eyes falling on Ryker.

"Ryker! You came for me." She gasped, wanting to touch him, but Ryker stood. Her hand missed him.

"Sadie said the pool water was poisonous." She stood too, brushing her wet hair back.

"I fused you with my power, the poison is out of your system already." Ryker hardened his eyes, his robe nearly crawling on the ground as he walked out.

The blood on his face is still there, though it's dried now.

Sage and Cheyenne followed him closely, and Xena ran after them.

"What did the twins make you do before letting you come get me? Huh?" Ryker...talk to me." 

No answer, but Ryker's bare steps stayed perilous till he arrived at the kitchen where the old chef is.

His hand is still stuck with the fork inside.

The custard and pie on his chest already dried with his clothes, and his legs are shaking as he has been standing since then.

"Father!" Xena ran over.

Cheyenne removed the fork from his hand, and the old chef began checking Xena out quickly.

"Did they hurt you? Are you ok?"

As the questions fell out of his mouth, Xena began crying.

"Stop asking if I'm fine when you're the only one who's hurt here. Look at your palm." She sobbed, showing the wounded palm to Ryker who's still standing there with that gloomy gaze.

"Please...heal him." Xena begged.

"No." He firmly declared, going for the door.

"Ryker Morozov!" The old chef called after him, using his real surname.

Ryker stopped sharply on his tracks, anger settling deep in his marrow as he turned around with just one side of his face, staring over his shoulder.

The old chef received a chest lash, getting flung back. He fell on the kitchen floor, hitting his head.

"Father!" Xena knelt beside him, holding him by the shoulders. "Father stay awake! Don't pass out!"

But his eyes kept rolling in obvious pains, and he's grunting too.

"Stop it! He's our father!!" Xena yelled at Ryker, her tears continuing.

"Morozov?" Ryker sneered, his hate blooming out. "Don't make that mistake, ever again!" He warned, leaving the kitchen with his knights.

Xena gathered tissues, wiping the old chef's sweat with them.

"He'll come around one day, and you should stop calling him by our surname Morozov. It's death sentence for you if any of The Millions Family hear about it, and I don't want you to die yet." She said.

She also tied a bandage on his wounded palm.

"He has refused to heal you, meaning you'd have to wait for it to heal naturally. I'll get you capsules from the pharmacy later." She assured.

"You're too good to me, Xena." The old chef smiled through his tears.

"Of course... you're our father." Xena hugged him.

--

Ryker and his knights have barely left the kitchen staircase when Jude appeared, obstructing them.

Ryker's eyes rolled, obviously not ready to entertain anyone right now. Not even Jude.

"It's good to see you again after two good days. Who did you kill this time? I know you killed someone judging by the blood on your face. Or is it two people? Three people?" Jude smiled genuinely, wanting to touch Ryker, but Sage blocked him.

"My Lord doesn't appreciate touches from anyone, not even us, and you know it."

"Right, I forgot. But I wonder when I'll actually get a hug from you...Ryker." Jude sighed with a pout.

"Where were you when your twin siblings were drowning Xena in the pool?" Ryker questioned demandingly, his deep voice echoing with the need for an answer.

"What!" Jude blinked in disbelief. "Drowned Xena?"

"My Lord got a call, ordering him to come to the auction with enough cash if he wants to save her from everlasting sickness." Sage explained.

"Wait...the twins are the reason you came to the auction to bid a whooping 150 insane billions for the Shayla slave?" Jude's eyes widened.

Ryker whizzed past him, going down the staircase.

"I swear, Ryker. I didn't know!" Jude ran after him. "Believe me Ryker... I'll never lie to you. I like you more than anything!" 

"Stop using that word, it's upsetting. Don't like me. Just hate me like everyone else, it's easier." Ryker crankily replied with a glare that could cut through stones.

He used the exit door, but Jude still went after him.

"Ryk—"

Cheyenne blocked Jude this time, giving him a slight bow.

"You should stop here and let him be, Grand Prince. Don't provoke him further." She said and left.

Jude took three steps back, muttering...

"Those evil twin siblings of mineee"

He rushed upstairs, two steps at a time.

--

Ryker didn't leave for his tower immediately.

He went to the lone hall at the end of the castle rooms, standing behind the door with dull expression for a while.

"Here's it," Cheyenne handed him a sheathed dagger.

Then he opened the door and went inside alone. Cheyenne and Sage stayed by the door.

This room is blue, and it the blue light isn't from bulbs. 

There's a triangular black tray on top of a dark oakwood table, and on the tray, there's a shiny, glittering orb, distributing blue ray of light across the room, so bright that it might blind the weak.

It's the FrostOrb, the main reason why the Millions Family are more powerful than any family in this city.

It's the one thing that empowered them to be able to snatch the city away from the Spectres.

The orb must stay bright everytime to supply power to the family, and to stay bright, it drinks blood. Not just any blood... it's always Ryker's blood.

Ryker unsheathed the dagger, brandishing it with the blue light while staring at the blade through his lashes.

He cut the middle of his palm with the dagger, slashing through the flesh and peeling it apart.

Blood erupted, falling on the FrostOrb in large droplets. The more the blood dropped, the brighter the FrostOrb light turned, till the brightness reflected in his eyes.

"Finally decided to come back?" A thick voice spoke behind him.

He didn't have to turn before knowing who it is.

That's the Grand Supreme Zeke, and he's probably with his wife Azura.

Ryker dropped enough blood on the orb, making sure it reached the brightness limit before taking his bloody hand away from it.

He turned around, and it's really Zeke and Azura, both looking power-druk and evil as always.

"You know well that you can't be far from the orb, right? Your blood is the oil, meaning you can't go far from the castle! So tell me...where the hell have you been since two days ago?" Zeke demanded.

"Why bother ask when you know? Didn't you send spies after me? You know I've been looking for her, and I'm not sitting still in this rotten castle without making a move!" Ryker glared.

"How dare you glare like that!" Zeke spat out in anger, sending him bone-cracking strikes with two fingers.

Ryker vanished from that spot, appearing on top of the orb table with unshakeable balance.

The strike hit the wall instead, leaving two deep cracks.

Ryker raised his hand to strike back, but Azura stepped forward, laughing.

"You're gonna strike back? Do it if you have the balls. Your sister's lifeline is with Zeke, remember? One pull, and she'd die. So go ahead! Strike back and you'll receive Xena's dead body in return!" She threatened.

Ryker's hand went down, his fingers shaking so much but not with fear.

It's rage. 

Boiling rage which made deep frosty blue colour settle in his two eyes.

"You might be revered and feared outside this palace, but always remember you got that fame from this family! People fear you cos of The Millions surname, not because of your crazy powers! Everything you have belongs here!" Azura continued.

Ryker's fingers went closed, balling up. He's trying too too hard to contain his anger. Else, he might lose it, combust and ruin things.

"We know you've been searching for her, but the time you always spend looking for her, use it to look for the marked one instead, time is running." Zeke said authoritatively.

"Congratulations on buying your first slave though, but know that she doesn't belong to you. She belongs to the slaves camp and she'd work for the Family." Azura declared.

And with that, she left the room with Zeke, their steps fading off.

Ryker exited too, walking past Cheyenne and Sage by the door.

The two exchanged glances, knowing something happened inside with the Grand Supreme and his wife.

Each time those two meet Ryker, his anger grows. 

Ryker is always angry, but they have a way of making it swell, especially if they threaten him with Xena's lifeline.

--

[Starr Balcony]

Fluorescent lights hit the bodies of the thin glass cups on the table, sending glosses across the walls and table edges.

Sadie and Soren stood beside the table, and it was Soren who poured the wine into the cups.

They carried their cups at the same time.

"To more wins!" Sadie beamed.

"Endless feats!" Soren smirked, their glasses coming together in a soft clink before they had little sips.

"I bet Winona and her siblings are crying as we speak, I wish I could collect Winona's tears in a fuckin bowl and make a hot cup coffee with it." Sadie smiled, taking her seat and mounting her legs on each other.

"C'mon, don't be too cruel. We've done enough tonight." Soren chuckled.

"Stop talking like you're not thinking about the same thing, twinnie." Sadie shrugged, and Soren's chuckle darkened.

He picked his phone with his other hand, swiping up and down.

"As it should be, we're trending on the Dark Room web again. Ballet School is gonna turn hot tomorrow." He announced, showing Sadie his phone screen.

"Take it away from my face, you know how much I hate phones and cars! Human technology just pisses me off." She rolled eyes.

"What have you both done!" Jude bursted into the balcony.

"We made the bastard buy the slave for us," Sadie stood.

"And for the hundredth time, he's Ryker, not bastard!" Jude barked.

"He's a bastard! Everyone calls him that so why are you trying to be different! Why always defending him when he doesn't even like you! That bastard likes no one so why willing to die for him! We're your siblings, not him!" Sadie yelled.

"And that's why you drowned Xena his sister in the poisoned pool?" Jude muttered.

"Yes," Soren stood. "And we enjoyed it."

"You fuckin parasites!" Jude lunged towards them, but before he could even take three steps, their eyes shared the faint glow, and Jude automatically slipped.

He fell from the balcony railings with a loud scream.

The twins leaned on the railings, staring down at him from there.

Jude landed on the ground, breaking one of his right leg bones.

He sat there, groaning and unable to stand as he looked up at them.

Sadie pulled out some nyx notes from her bag, throwing them down at him.

Note; Nyx is the Luminaria currency.

"Your healing powers will take at least ten minutes for the bone to heal, but you might not wanna wait, so go treat yourself." Sadie laughed as the money landed on Jude.

"You're interested in the incoming slave Shayla. You and Winona like her? I mean...that dirty, smelly thing?" Soren laughed too.

"More reason to make her beg for death. We'll feed her pains like food, and we'll make you watch. That'd be your punishment for chosing the bastard over us." Sadie said.

Then the two of them left the balcony.

Jude grabbed his broken leg, groaning out.

They were right.

He's gonna have to sit here for at least ten minutes till the bone heals before he'd be able to stand.

He has to find a way to separate the twins and have his revenge.

--

[Heartless Tower]

The heavy scent of doom with a hint of frozen ice oozed from the walls, floors, pillars and everywhere noticeable in the tower.

The tower is almost as big as the main castle, but the luxury here is much more.

Everything is either in crystals, sapphire, gold, or jade.

It's like a land of honey, decked with enough richness to build several cities and capitals.

Ryker's demigod aura is in love with richness, so the tower shines with unbelievable wealth always. 

He's madly rich, and it's one of the qualities he intimidates the Millions Family with.

They might know everything about him, but they don't know the source of his wealth.

Maids don't work in this tower.

He hates maids. He hates servants or chefs. In fact, he hates everyone.

He does his things himself, and he's comfortable like that.

He's currently sitting on his throne-like chair in the center of the icy tower. Several blue flames burned brightly beside the dry ice walls.

He's relaxed on the chair, his back pressing on the soft structure. 

Again, the bloodstain is still on his face. He might be obsessed with blood on his face.

Cheyenne and Sage are in front of him, reporting...

"We gathered twenty females from the city again. They'll be in the palace tomorrow and will be directed to the Bamboo Groove so you can check for the mark." Cheyenne said.

"The new slave will be here tomorrow, but My Lord, are you really gonna let her work in the slaves camp for the Millions instead of working here? I thought you bought her for yourself." Sage asked.

"She's to stay at the Slaves Wing and work at the labor camp. I don't need a slave, or a servant. Keep that beggar as far away from me as possible." Ryker stated.

"Yes, my Lord." Sage stepped back.

He left the main tower with Cheyenne, both retiring to the tower quarters where they stay.

They're the closest to Ryker as his knights, and his richness even reflects in them.

They never wear cheap outfits. They're always in brand suits, and Cheyenne in particular is always wearing hats... expensive hats.

Ryker is more than enough to protect himself in this wicked palace, but they're his biggest extra protectors as they carry insane powers too.

--

[West Palace]

The castle is silent, except for the sound of Winona's harsh breathings.

She's in the lobby with the whole family, and they look bereaved like they've just lost someone.

Kasia and Astor are standing over the kids, both looking disappointed.

"Winona, I don't think you should be as angry as this. You're not even a student of the Ballet School so your mockery will be limited. I'm the only redhead in the school so let me feel all the anger!" Blaise stood.

"I'm Faye, of course everyone knows I'm Sadie's biggest enemy, so I'm the center of mockery tomorrow." Faye stood too.

"I didn't say that for you to follow up, stepsister." Blaise faced her, his hatred so obvious.

"I wasn't following up on you! I only said what's on my mind you pest!" Faye shouted.

Blaise nearly blinded her with his red hair flash, but Kasia stepped in.

"Blaise!!"

"Marrying Astor is like throwing me into a well. I hate him, and I hate his daughter more!" Blaise yelled, storming out of the lobby.

"Oh you think I don't hate you too? You think I like your mother too? Come back here cow!!" Faye went after him.

"Faye!" Astor yelled, but it's a lost cause. She's already out.

"I was wondering why they haven't fought since daybreak, turns out they were only saving it for tonight." Kasia muttered, sitting down.

"You know the most annoying part of the auction? We nearly won! I bet 60 billions, my life savings, but that bastard had to step in and ruin things with his 150 billions shit!" Winona held her head.

"Calm down, Winona." Astor patted her shoulder.

"I can't calm down, stepfather. I like that slave." Winona shook her head.

"Why the hell would the bastard suddenly come for the auction? He has never been a fan of the Dark Room. That demigod hates everyone so why did he appear in public tonight?" Kasia wondered aloud.

A servant entered the lobby, holding out a white card.

"From the East palace," he reported.

"Leave," Kasia told him after taking the card.

"Yes, Grandmistress." The servant took his leave, and Kasia tore the envelope open, reading it with Astor.

"We got invited to a meeting at the East palace tommorow night. It's to discuss the feast. Grand Supreme Zeke sent this invite himself." Astor announced.

"To the East palace? I'm definitely following you. Mother, stepfather, you don't object, right? I really need to see the slave again." Winona said desperately.

Kasia sighed, uncertain.

--

[Next Morning, East Palace ~ Bamboo Groove]

Apart from Heartless Tower, the Bamboo Groove is the second place of peace for Ryker.

Located at the back tracks of the palace property. The area is swamped by bamboo trees, and you can't even see the sky once you're inside cos it's shadowy and dark.

No one else is allowed inside except Ryker himself, then Cheyenne and Sage on official duties.

The two are ushering in twenty females right now, and the females are in a straight line as they head to the middle of the Groove.

Some of them are smiling with excitement, and some are shaking with fear cos everyone knows his history.

Each time he checks females for the mark, he always ends up killing one or two of them just to get blood on his face.

Ryker sat in the middle of the Groove, wearing another robe that's different from last night's, but still colour black with decorative jade treasures.

The sprinkles of white in his hair is now gone, but it's still messy, and he's wearing light shoes.

For most of the ladies, it's their first time seeing him in person, so their eyes came wide at the beauty. Even the ones who were scared forgot about their fear for the moment, lust taking over their souls.

They lined in front of him, and his eyes stayed unsettled.

Cheyenne and Sage turned their backs to the scene, and the females followed the usual protocol.

They began taking off their clothes, down to their panties and bras till they all became stark naked.

Ryker took out his dagger and cut his arm easily. He bled out.

The mark is supposed to shine on the marked one whenever he bleeds.

The females turned around one by one, presenting themselves for his eyes and waiting for when he's gonna announce that he found the mark on one of them.

But nothing came from him till he finished checking the twenty of them.

"Get out." He ordered, the deepness of his voice causing them to flinch.

Some of them couldn't even wait to wear their clothes. They gathered the fabrics and ran out.

Most of them forgot their panties and bras right there.

They ran out till it's just one of them left inside.

That one has refused to wear her clothes back, and she's smiling slyly walked to him.

"My Lord, maybe we only have to become partners first and the mark will appear on my body?" She said boldly, and that boldness was the end of her.

A raise of two fingers from Ryker, she froze in place.

The anger he couldn't vent to Grand Supreme Zeke and Azura last night, he released it all on her.

She froze from the insides.

Her lungs, blood, flesh and bones became ice, and her skin got covered completely with ice too.

She couldn't even make her last sound before she shattered to pieces. 

A portion of her non-frozen blood splashed on Ryker's face, and his smirk was soft as he felt totally refreshed.

"Clean up her shatters before they melt away, and take them to her parents. Tell them I killed her, and describe how I did it. Make them listen to the details till the end." He ordered, standing.

"Yes, My Lord." Cheyenne and Sage chorused.

"No marked one among them again?" Sage muttered.

"I'll be at the Spring Pool." Ryker further said, walking deeper into the Bamboo Groove, his robe swirling gently with the air.

--

[8AM]

Shayla's steps were shaky and unaligned as she stepped into the East palace grounds with the auctioning committee chief.

She's wearing tattered shoes like her clothes, and her arms are closed over her chest as she walked as fast as possible, following after the chief so she won't get shouted at.

She didn't even bother looking around at the luxury of the palace. Why would she look when she's here to suffer?

The luxury doesn't matter, she's doomed for life.

She got sold to the wickedest man in this place, and that's enough to shatter her more than she already is.

Her eyes stung with tears as she tried hard to keep up as the chief is walking too fast.

The heavy chains are still on her ankle, so it's hard to walk.

She managed to look up once, and she saw Sadie and Soren at the wide park. 

Both are about to enter their car, ready to leave for their Ballet School.

Their eyes met hers, and their smiles are crueller than poison.

"So...what are our plans for this slave dog?" Sadie asked.

"Mercury deserves to see the way we make her see hell." Soren said.

Mercury is their one and only friend.

"We'll bring her home from school to join us then, the fun starts tonight. But first, let's go experience the school fame." Sadie smiled.

They got in the car, driving out.

Shayla's toes hit a edge, and she fell, getting wounded.

"Get up, wench!" The chief yelled, and she quickly got up.

"I'm... sorry." She muttered, voice gentle but as beautiful as her face.

She ran after the chief, but she felt eyes on herself, so she looked up again.

It's Jude.

He's standing beside his car at the second park. He's about to leave the palace for official duties, and his eyes stayed on her wounded toes as she walked past.

With a bow, Shayla looked away from him, concentrating on her walk.

The chief took her to the Slaves Wing, then he left her to enter by herself.

She stepped into the hall, and at first, fear gripped her.

There are multitude of slaves in there, and there are beds, though they're flat, but they're still beds.

She stayed in the Dark Room cage all her life, so she has never slept on a bed. The need to fall on the mattress and sleep almost overwhelmed her.

She batted her lashes to stay awake, looking at the hall again. Her fear is gone already, so she's looking at everyone too, staring back.

They all have heavy chains to their ankles like her, and they look as shabby and underfed as her, too.

They're currently eating breakfast of pie and custard.

She has heard that's what slaves and maids eat here.

"It's the 150 billion slave!" A short lady appeared, smiling up at her.

She's almost a dwarf. 

No. She's actually a dwarf, but she looks cheerful, and judging by her looks, she's a slave too, just that she looks well-fed than the others. 

Also, there's no shackling chains on her ankles.

"I'm Lilith" she spoke again.

"Shayla" Shayla's small voice replied.

"I know, everyone knows the 150 billion slave. Even me the most popular slave here was bought for 10 million. No one was bought for a billion, but you raised the stakes." Lilith said loudly, rounding her.

"You're the most popular slave?" Shayla asked softly.

"Listen... When a Luminaria mates with a human, the human gene suppresses the Luminaria gene, and that's why their child is always powerless, but in rare cases, the Luminaria gene suppresses the human gene, and the child is born with certain powers. My father was a Luminarian, my mother was a human. My father's Luminaria gene suppressed my mother's human gene, so I was born with little powers, one of its kind." Lilith explained.

"Oh..." Shayla nodded in understanding.

"I'm treated better than the rest of the slaves cos of my little power, and I even got the post of a janitress." Lilith shrugged, walking out of the hall.

Shayla followed her, though her mouth is itching to eat pie and custard.

"I happen to like you, so I'll explain how things work here for you." Lilith faced her.

Shayla blinked her long lashes.

"In this palace, avoid the evil twins if you wanna live long. Avoid Grand Supreme Zeke and his wife. And also, don't call Lord Ryker a bastard whenever Prince Jude is around. He loves Lord Ryker like a brother, so he'd kill you if you try it." Lilith explained.

"Lord Ryker? My buyer?" Shayla asked.

"Lord Ryker might have bought you, but you're gonna be working at the labor camp for the family. You're not gonna be working for him. You might not know, but Lord Ryker hates slaves." Lilith cleared.

"Really?" Shayla felt peace instantly, though it won't last, but hearing that she won't be working for Ryker is a blessing.

"Also you'll need to know the reason why Lord Ryker is answering the Millions surname instead of his real surname, why his father and sis are maids in the royal kitchen, and why he stays in that rich Heartless Tower even though he's not a royal." Lilith continued.

"But not now, I'll tell you the story when we're back." 

"Back from where?" Shayla asked.

"Slaves Labor Camp opens by 9, but you have to go somewhere with me before then." Lilith smiled.

"Where?" Shayla asked again.

"You'll know when we get there. You're my favorite person now, so when we return, I'll get you the largest portion of food, then you can eat and we'll go join the others at the camp. Wait for me I'll be right back." Lilith told her, rushing inside.

Shayla stood there, thinking about what she just heard.

Lilith looks friendly, and she has never made a friend all her life, so she's kinda happy, slightly.

Her eyes caught her toe that was wounded earlier, and she was surprised to see the wound is gone like it was never there.

The hell? 

She's very sure she hit her toe earlier before she saw Prince Jude at the park, so where did the wound go?

"Careful, don't trust Lilith too much." A new voice said beside her, making her look up from her toe to see another female slave.

"She uses people and dumps them when she's done. She'd ruin you." The female said and left.

Shayla was still wondering what it meant when Lilith came back for her.

"Let's go!" She pulled her along.

Shayla looked back, but the slave is already gone from that corridor.

With a pure chaste mind, she followed Lilith.

Lilith took her to the Bamboo Groove, entering through the shadow of the leaves.

"Where are we?" Shayla asked as they tiptoed under the darkness.

"Shhh!" Lilith placed a hand on her plump lips. 

Shayla blinked once, then they continued their journey.

She could feel the iciness of the Groove. It wrapped around her like a nest of snakes, and for no reason, she's scared.

Something is telling her that this journey is dangerous, but Lilith seems too bold, so she followed till they got to the back of clustered bamboos where they both hid.

From there, they peeped out to see a Spring Pool in front of them, and in the middle of the pool, Ryker sat shirtlessly, steam rising calmly in the ambience surrounding him.

His back is on them.

"Lord Ryker!" Shayla gasped, turning away.

"Shh, just watch. That's my crush, don't ruin my picnic." Lilith whispered.

"But this is wrong. He's got sharp hearing like I heard, and I'm sure he knows we're here. I don't wanna die yet." Shayla shook her head.

"I told you I've got little powers, right?" Lilith smiled. "My little power can block noises from others, so he definitely can't hear us, trust me. Just look at that divine body. I want to touch him so bad." 

Shayla peeked out from behind the bamboo clusters again, unable to blink as she had another look at Ryker's back view.

He's still sitting still in the pool.

His body is clean—white as snow, but his back has a tattoo in the middle.

The tattoo of a frozen sword.

"See that frozen tattoo?" Lilith whispered. 

Shayla faced her.

"His partner has it on her body too, but her own would be a burning sword instead of ice. He has been looking for that partner for years with no success." Lilith explained.

"Partner? So... they'll get married?" Shayla asked.

"Darn, 150 billion. You're really naive." Lilith sighed.

"What do you mean naive? Isn't he meant to...get married to his partner?" Shayla softly said.

Her leg mistakenly hit a small stone beside her, and the stone fell, making a loud rolling noise.

Ryker's blue eyes snapped open in the spring pool, and the girls gasped.

"Darn!" Lilith pushed Shayla out.

Shayla landed on the dried bamboo leaves on the ground, then Lilith ran out of the Groove, escaping alone.

Shayla's chest thudded with irregular beats, bursting with fear and breaking at the taste of Lilith's betrayal.

She struggled to stand up, but the moment she stood, she was lifted off her feet by an arcane energy that definitely belongs to Ryker.

She levitated, and her body slammed up against the bamboo shadows

One of the stems pierced her tummy.

"Arghh!!! Wait please it wasn't...I didn't...come here on my own!!" She cried out, legs struggling frantically in the air.

Her rag shoes fell off her feet, and her blood wet the bamboo.

"Pleasss...I..I'm..."

Ryker stepped out of the Spring Pool, his feet bare. 

Long white transparent pants hung low on his waist, and his well-built body flexed with the water which rode down his showing v-line.

He paused after few steps,, his head tilted threateningly.

"It's the beggar," He muttered, looking utterly unimpressed. 

"Since you love death so much and even came here boldly for it, I'll gladly give it to you." 

He raised that hand to strike, and Shayla's eyes popped in terror, widening beyond their sockets.

"No no no.... Please don't kill me! Please don't... please!!!"
