CHAPTER 5&6.

ARE Y'ALL FOR REAL WITH THE MADLY LOW ENGAGEMENT ON LAST NIGHT'S CHAPTER? 

DON'T MAKE ME START GHOSTING YOU GUYS TOO BEFORE SOME WILL START SAYING SHE'S DOING TOO MUCH.

COMPLETE REQUIREMENTS IN TIME TOMORROW. I'M NOT GIVING COMPLETION DEADLINE YET COS WE'VE NOT REACHED THE TENTH CHAPTER, SO ENJOY THIS GRACE TILL THEN.

Reply My Comments, Drop Urs, Like and Share Massively.

★

Shayla's yellings.

The tears welling in her eyes.

Her kicks against the air.

Her pouring blood.

None was enough to stop Ryker.

His strike met her like a raging stormcloud, and though he's not exactly holding her, he has a magical hold on her.

He's choking her wretched life out of her wretched lungs.

Her nose is bleeding too, her lower lip changed colour. Death knocked at her door, and it opened wide.

Ryker stood there, beside the pool, just doing his thing—taking her life.

Shayla's life only has a thin thread left when Cheyenne entered the Groove, her eyes stretching for a sec when she saw what's going on.

"What are you doing here?" Ryker questioned, still not lowering his hand from the crying slave in the air.

"I and Sage found the boss of the hidden warehouse where she was last seen." Cheyenne reported.

Ryker's hand went down, and Shayla went down too, her body hitting the ground with a deadly thud.

She held her bleeding tummy with a palm, shifting away from him on her butt. Her glassy eyes stared up at him with fear, and her body is trembling.

Her whole skin is red—eyes inclusive. And she's panting so bad.

Ryker didn't give her another gaze.

He just grabbed his white linen jacket  from where it was hung, put it on and started walking out with his effortless steps.

"What should I do to her, My Lord?" Cheyenne asked.

"Drag her out, make sure she gets the heaviest workload at the labor camp everyday. And clean this Groove thoroughly. Clean out her smell." He replied without turning, then his steps faded out.

Cheyenne's fingers grabbed Shayla's hair, tugging her up.

"I...I didn't come here on my own!" She spoke up. "I was brought by—"

"Shut up already, I do not want to hear what your voice has to say." Cheyenne cut her off harshly.

Shayla's mouth went clamped.

She's struggling to hold her bleeding tummy, struggling to walk as Cheyenne dragged her, and at the same time she's struggling with the sting of her hold on her hair.

It was hard till they got out of the Groove, and Cheyenne threw her down. Her bleeding tummy hit the ground, and the stem inside it fully entered her skin, making her cry out.

"I ... Was brought here by Lilith! I'm new here and—"

That was interrupted by a boiling slap from Cheyenne.

Shayla fell back to the ground again, her blood dripping a lot.

"Believe me, I was—"

Another slap.

Shayla's tears sprang out, and she held her cheek with her second hand before looking up at Cheyenne's ruthless face.

"Do you know what Lord Ryker hates most?" Cheyenne spoke with a practiced accent. 

"It is when you break into his territory or breach his rules." she continued, raising her heels to place the sharp edge on Shayla's forehead.

Shayla gulped.

"That Heatless Tower and this Bamboo Groove are sacred places you should never make the mistake of entering. They're not for roaches like you, they're exclusively for him. And if you're one of the slut slaves seeking for attention, give up already. He'd kill you before you get even a little bit of warmth from him." She said heartlessly, each word stabbing Shayla's chest like a knife.

"I'm...I'm not a slut." She managed to talk through her pouring tears.

Cheyenne dropped her heel from her forehead, smiling hatefully.

"I see you're the type who talks back. Well, I'll have fun messing with you in here. Entering that Groove would be your biggest mistake ever, and you'll regret it over and over again."

With that, she walked off, leaving Shayla there... bleeding and weeping.

Shayla stood, her palm still holding her bleeding tummy tightly.

She looked at the Bamboo Groove entrance, wiping her tears with her second hand, then she sniffled, remembering his words...

"...AND CLEAN THIS GROOVE THOROUGHLY. CLEAN OUT HER SMELL."

She brought her arm close to her nose, sniffling her own skin.

She doesn't smell.

Even when she still lived like a dog in her cage at the Dark Room, she was allowed to bath and brush always. The only things she lacked were food and decent change of clothes.

That statement from him hurt more than it should.

But then...his personality has always been like this like she heard.

He has his own beliefs, and no one could change them, so if he believes she smells, then good for him.

She returned to the Slaves Wing, and as she walked past the corridor with bleeding tummy, the eyes of her fellow slaves stared at her, some with pity and some with worries.

Some stared with curiosity about where she got the wound from.

"She must have encountered the evil twins." One of them mumbled.

"Oh my goodness! 150 billion!" Lilith ran out of the hall to meet her.

She gasped again when she saw her bleeding tummy.

"Did Lord Ryker do this to you? Your nose is bleeding too! This is serious... We slaves don't get treatment. Once you get wounded, you rot and die...no one cares. Geez, are you gonna die now? You're so unlucky!" She pitifully said.

Suddenly, Shayla pushed her away, and everyone received shocks, including Lilith herself.

"You tricked me into the Groove because you wanted to use me as cover, isn't it? Because I'm new, you took advantage of me. I might have lived my whole life in a cage, but I'm not stupid, Lilith." Shayla said hurtfully, and Lilith laughed.

"I was only trying to make friends with you and make you the best slave after me, and you're taking it for granted? So what if you took the fall for me? You're basically my servant!" Lilith snapped with hostility.

The other slaves couldn't say anything. It's not the first time Lilith treats new slaves badly.

Shayla stayed speechless too.

She's getting weak cos of her bleeding tummy. She's losing too much blood.

"One meal portion left, who hasn't eaten breakfast?" Xena's voice broke into the corridor, and the slaves dispersed instantly.

Xena appeared from the end of the corridor, holding a big basket from the slaves kitchen. She's actually here to pack the plates they used for breakfast.

"Watch out from now on, you're on my blacklist!" Lilith threatened Shayla before leaving too.

"How did you get hurt?" Xena asked Shayla, looking genuinely worried as she stared at her tummy.

"I...the Groove." Shayla tried to explain.

Xena dropped her basket, gasping. "You entered my brother's Groove? And you're still alive? You're lucky! He's insane about that place." 

"I was tricked inside by Lilith." Shayla replied, sitting on the pavement and supporting herself with the pillar.

"You should stay away from Lilith, she's evil. You're new, so she'd wanna take advantage of you." Xena said.

"I wish I knew that earlier. How did you know I'm new?" Shayla asked, her lips pale with both hunger and blood loss.

"Everyone knows you're the 150 billion slave, Shayla. Try to stay as far away from my brother as possible. He kills for fun, and it's not funny if you trespass." Xena offered.

"Thanks for the additional advice..." Shayla drawled.

"Xena, my name is Xena, Ryker's sis. I and my old chef father are in charge of the two kitchens" Xena introduced.

"Oh..." Shayla nodded, groaning in pains from her tummy.

"Darn it...your tummy." Xena muttered, gently taking Shayla's hand away from it.

She raised her blouse from the wound, frowning when she saw how deep it is. The bamboo stem is even still inside it, that's why it's bleeding so much.

"This is bad, there's no treatment for slaves here at all. What are we gonna do? My powers can't heal." Xena muttered.

Shayla's face twisted in pains again.

Xena quickly removed the scarf she's wearing on her head. She wrapped it round on Shayla's tummy.

Blood dampened it immediately, but she kept wrapping till it went three rounds, then she knotted it.

"It's 8:50, and the Labor Camp opens at 9, so you should eat and join the others to work. I'll secretly search for some capsules from the pharmacy and keep them for you, so when you return from camp in the evening, we'll apply it." Xena said.

She took the plate of custard and the only one wrap of pie remaining. She handed them to Shayla who took them with a smile.

"You're so kind, Xena."

Xena smiled back, "And you're beautiful"

Shayla smiled again, taking a bite from the pie then topping with the milkless sugarless custard.

"Why do you and your dad work in the kitchen though?" She asked suddenly.

A sigh left Xena's mouth, and she backed the pillar.

"It's a long story..."

Shayla kept staring at her, so she sat up, facing her.

"Several years ago, I and my father and my mother and my twin sister lived together in peace." She started.

"You're a twin?" Shayla blinked, licking custard away from her full lips.

"Yeah..." Xena said, sadness riddling her voice.

"The four of us lived together as one happy family, and back then... mother was pregnant with Ryker, so we were expecting him as out fifth family member. We weren't rich and we weren't very powerful, but at least we lived fine. Then one particular night, mother began feeling labour pains...but father wasn't at home. It was just me and my twin sis with her. We were just crying cos we were three years olds and knew nothing." 

"Where did your father go?" Shayla asked, getting more curious.

"He was at a bar, drinking..." Xena smiled sadly.

"What! But you said y'all were one happy family... Why would he leave his pregnant wife alone at home with three year olds twins?" Shayla asked.

"I dunno, that night is still a mystery. Father never behaved like that before. He got drunk at the bar and spanked Azura's ass" Xena continued.

"Azu...Wait, the Grand Supreme's wife? What was she doing at the bar too?" Shayla blinked.

"Back then, The Spectres were still the ruler of this city, the Millions Family were just one regular rich family. So like I said... father spanked Azura's arse, and she wanted to behead him, but father was like; please spare me...I have a pregnant wife and two young twins and I can't die yet." 

"Azura was even more enraged to hear that. She dragged father to our house with the intention of killing him and his pregnant wife and his twin kids. By the time they arrived at our house, mother already gave birth to Ryker all by herself, but Ryker wasn't a random child. He was born powerful, I mean... he's a Demigod who carries the power of millennial ice in that body, and when he was born, he was holding the FrostOrb in his right hand. That was when Azura knew he wasn't just a normal child." 

Xena was already crying by now.

Shayla held her hands, urging her to continue.

"Azura told her husband Zeke about it, and they decided to ruin our family just to take Ryker. Zeke killed mother. He killed her..." Xena sniffled.

Shayla began rubbing her hands.

"After killing mother, they decided to capture I and my father and my twin as slaves, but my twin disappeared magically... no one knows her whereabouts till now. They took me and father and Ryker. Using the FrostOrb, they also took the palace from the Spectres, and they became rulers. They threw us in the kitchen and made us cook for them, and they made Ryker a member of the Millions Family forcefully. He mustn't show affection to me or father, else we'd be killed. Also, if they hear anyone calling him our Morozov surname, it's death sentence." Xena sobbed.

"But...he's so powerful...he should fight for you both..." Shayla muttered.

"Azura and Zeke knew that when he grows, he'd wanna save us, so to prevent that, Zeke extracted my lifeline and hid it where no one would find it. That's what they always threaten him with." Xena said.

"So...your life-line is with Grand Supreme Zeke? The red thread?" Shayla's eyes widened.

"Yeah, he only has to cut that red thread, and I'll die. But there's a solution to that though... If I find my missing twin sister, another lifeline can be created for me from her own, and that's why Ryker has been looking for her so much. I bet all the struggle will be over when he finds my twin." Xena exhaled.

"That's...that's heartbreaking." Shayla pitifully said.

"I know, right?" Xena looked around, seeing that the other slaves are already leaving for the labor camp.

"You should join them. Like I said, I'll sneak you some capsules from the pharmacy, and I'll come treat your wound myself after I finish cooking dinner with father." She said.

"Thank you, Xena. I'm glad I met you." Shayla smiled, standing after dropping her custard plate.

She finished it already, but there's still a bit of pie left.

"See you later, friend!" Xena waved her, leaving with the basket.

"Friend?" Shayla muttered. "She's more like my big sister. If she's older than Lord Ryker, then she's far older than me." 

She was chewing the rest of her pie as she head to the camp, following the lead of the others.

Her barefoot doesn't matter.

Her cold feet doesn't matter too.

She's used to hardships, and she only wishes to stay alive till the end of her slavery here.

Also, the thought of the story Xena just told her is making her wonder.

Grand Supreme Zeke and his wife Azura are the most villainous beings existing.

Erasing a whole family just to get their powerful son? All because the old chef spanked Azura's arse?

Crazy.

And now the son is a bloodthirsty demigod who nearly killed her.

She sighed, entering the crowd of slaves after eating the rest of her pie.

--

[Black Swan Ballet School]

* It's the Millions royal twins!!

* Yo! Grand Princess Sadie and Grand Prince Soren!

* I'd do anything to get invited to the East Palace!

* Their bastard demigod bought the slave and I couldn't stop howling!

* Me too! I love seeing the Spectres getting humbled! What was Winona feeling like?

There was no stop to the praises and screams as Sadie and Soren got down from their car at the large park.

They're not smiling, but their arrogance is high. They obviously love the praises.

"And here comes my friends!!" Mercury ran out of the school building, jumping down the staircase.

She's a lousy arrogant one just like them. Also, her mother is the owner of this school.

"I watched the auction last night on the web and I've never been as proud! You guys were so cool! Winona was humbled! I bet Faye and Blaise would walk with their faces on the ground today." She gushed.

"You're too loud sometimes," Sadie chuckled.

"Of course I'm loud! My friends are big shots!" Mercury jumped.

Soren shook his head, and then another car halted in the park. Faye and Blaise came out of it.

Heavy laughter filled the park from everyone.

"Why did Winona try to get the slave last night? Tell me." A student said.

"How much is in your family account? Is it even up to the 60 billion that she bid?" Another one said, laughing out.

"Now I get why you guys are stuck in the West Palace! If your parents are the main rulers of this city, we'd have all died of hunger! Broke people!" Another student laughed.

Faye and Blaise stood in the middle, trying to keep their anger under their clothes cos causing trouble will bring problem for their family for sure.

They received all the insults silently, and while all that is happening, The evil twins and Mercury were smiling at the other side of the park.

They're enjoying this.

"So what if my sister lost to the bastard! The twins hate the bastard so I see no reason why his win should be their win!" Blaise said when he couldn't take it anymore.

"Yeah...that's true. I hear his source of income is different from the family's own so he definitely got the slave with his own money!" Faye added.

Sadie pushed some students out of her way, coming to them.

She faced Blaise in particular.

"The bastard belongs to The Millions Family, so everything he has belongs to the family too! Don't give me shit about how he got the slave with his own money!" She shot.

Blaise wanted to talk back, but Faye held him.

"Your father died not long ago, his grave is still wet, but your mother Kasia already married Faye's father Astor? Really impressive. I bet they married for the fûck." Sadie continued, and that was when things snapped.

Some of Blaise's red hair strands got carried by the air, and that strand went to Sadie.

She saw it coming, but she allowed it. She didn't move till the strand entered her eye.

That eye turned red.

The eyeball disappeared. She went blind in that eye alone.

"Sadie!" Soren came forward with Mercury.

"Sadie your eye!" Mercury gasped.

"What did you do to my twin!" Soren yelled at Blaise.

He'd have killed him on the spot, but Sadie held him back.

"I'll manage with one eye for the rest of today, leave him alone." 

"What do you mean leave him alone? He ruined your eye!" Soren glared.

"I said leave him!" Sadie glared back.

She went inside. Soren and Mercury went after her.

Faye quickly pulled Blaise aside. "Are you insane? You hurt Sadie with your hair? You'll get us all killed! Blaise!!"

Blaise yanked her away. "I didn't say you could advise me, go tell your father to break up with my mother instead!" He glared and went inside.

Faye scoffed, flipping her long hair.

"His character is getting more and more unbearable."

--

[Inside, Dressing Room]

The males dressing room is different from females, but for the sake of the twins who have to be in the same place all the time, a different dressing room was built for them, and Mercury uses that same dressing room with them.

Soren and Mercury have finished dressing, but Sadie is wearing only her leotard, and she's currently trying to get her tutu to her waist.

"Are you really gonna leave Blaise like that? Burn the West Palace with his family!" Mercury said.

"Leave him alone, you don't trust me?" Sadie smiled, finally getting her tutu to her waist. Her one eye is still wholly red.

She signalled at Soren with her thumb, and Soren immediately got it.

"You should come to the East Palace palace with us later. We have a welcoming ritual to do for the slave." He told Mercury.

"Really?" Mercury clasped her palms together in eagarness.

"I can't wait!!"

--

[12Pm, Warehouse]

Cheyenne had to inform the Slave Labor Camp about Shayla's punishment. She also had to clean up the Groove to get rid of her smell, and it took a lot of time.

Ryker had waited for her with Sage, so they left the palace late, and that's why they're just arriving right now.

Sage quickly jumped down from the big black car and rounded it. He almost opened the door for Ryker, but Ryker opened it himself.

He came out, looking like a symbol of richness in his pressed white shirt and loose black pants.

He loves free wears.

He enjoys robes, but it's not meant for this particular occasion. His hair is a little put together right now, but still healthy as always.

"I warned you severally...Do not attempt opening car doors for me. I'm not some king." He told Sage savagely.

"I always seem to forget. Pardon me, My Lord." Sage apologized.

Cheyenne came out of the car too, settling her figure beside the two.

Ryker's eyes shifted to the warehouse, and darkness loomed in there.

He didn't raise his hand as usual, his mind did the work this time.

The warehouse froze, getting covered by ice within a minute. It started cracking.

The workers inside ran out for their lives, and they were all lucky to get out before the building collapsed.

The moment the workers saw him, they voluntarily fell on their knees.

The collapsed building melted away behind them.

"Did your boss run off?" Ryker asked straight to the point, his stance so fuckin majestic.

"Our boss came earlier, but he didn't spend much time before leaving." One of the workers said.

Ryker slowly raised his hand with destructive intents, and that worker went from his knees to prostration.

"I swear! My Lord! It's not a lie! He left in about an hour before you arrived!! I'll never lie to you! I'm not crazy why would I lie!!" 

Ryker dropped his hand.

"Where did he say he was going?" Sage asked.

"America or something..." The worker quickly said.

"America?" Cheyenne frowned.

"That's in the human world. No one can travel to the human world without the crossover ladder, and to get access to the ladder, the managers collect more than ten bags of money. Your boss is a rich one." Ryker sighed, looking at the time.

"The ladder opens only at night, so your boss will surely climb it tonight at 7. By 7, I'm freezing this city." 

"Freeze this city? Everyone will freeze." Sage said.

"Isn't that the point? The boss will freeze on the ladder too, and we'll get him." Cheyenne said.

"We? We won't be with Our Lord! Did with forget that no one can escape his freezing ice? If he's freezing the city just to catch the boss, then we're freezing too. Meaning he'll be catching the boss alone." Sage said.

"So be it," Cheyenne shrugged.

"After catching the boss, you'll have to make the ice melt, but you can't melt that much ice from the city without your partner fire, and you haven't found your partner yet." Sage argued worriedly.

"Of course he can melt it, it'll only consume a lot of energy. Don't you trust him?" Cheyenne argued.

"But—"

"No pointless arguments, Sage. This city is freezing by 7" Ryker interrupted flatly.

Sage went quiet.

Cheyenne looked at Ryker and smiled proudly.

--

[East Palace, Slaves Labor Camp]

When Ryker told Cheyenne to make sure Shayla gets the highest workload, Shayla didn't think it'd be a work of death, but now she's experiencing it.

The others only have to carry shattered rocks on a bowl to the other side of the palace, but Shayla?

She's the one shattering the rocks. 

She was given a sledgehammer to do the work, and right from the first hit she gave the rock, she knew she was in for a lifetime of sufferings here.

Her tummy wound contributed a lot to it, and right now Xena's scarf that's around it is already soaked with her blood as the wound is bleeding again.

She's trying her hardest best to split the rocks, and she's really doing it 

She has split more than hundred bowls for the other slaves to carry, but she's still not fast enough for the slave supervisors.

They're holding sticks behind her, and each time she slacks, they beat her on the back.

Right now, the bowls of every slave is on the ground, begging to be filled, but Shayla can't even lift the sledgehammer anymore.

Her palm is full of big blisters, and they're bleeding too. Her sweat could compete with a stream.

The blood from her tummy is even soaking through her blouse.

She lifted her back, and five strokes of cane landed on her back from the supervisors immediately.

"Agh!!! I...I can't...I can't do this anymore!!" She cried, staggering.

She received more  strokes from the wicked canes, and her back peeled, bleeding out.

"Ahhh!! I really can't! I... I can't..." She sniffled, trying to reach her stinging back with her fingers.

"Go easy on her, she's a female, not a male." Lilith spoke from the midst of slaves.

"You're the slaves janitress doesn't give you the right to dictate to us." the supervisors replied.

Lilith smirked.

"Shayla the expensive slave?" Sadie's sad voice echoed from the base of the rock.

Everyone whipped around to see her there with Soren and Mercury.

"Crap?...I hate that she's beautiful. Let's start the torture from her face." Mercury said.

"Get down here, slave!" Sadie ordered.

--

[Slaves Wing]

The slaves are starting to return from the labor camp, and Xena is expecting Shayla to be among the first batch to arrive, but Shayla is nowhere.

Xena stood in front of the Wing Hall, looking so anxious.

She finished cooking dinner at 6 cos dinner here always come early. She had gone to the pharmacy to sneak a capsule for Shayla, and she needs to apply it before Shayla bleeds to death.

She's sure the scarf would be soaked with her blood right now since the bamboo stem is still inside her.

As the slaves entered the hall, Xena's anxiousness grew, till Lilith appeared.

"I see...150 billion got herself a new friend...that's cute." She said.

Xena stayed quiet.

"She got dragged away by the evil twins." Lilith said again.

"What!" Xena faced her.

"I bet she won't last a day after all. Pity." Lilith made fake pity faces before leaving.

Xena rushed out of the Wing Hall and was clutching at the capsule as she rushed towards the kitchen.

"No...not the twins. She's greatly wounded, if they touch her with their fire, she'd die..." She muttered.

She was so confused and worried that she didn't even look ahead, till she slammed into someone.

She came back to her senses with a gasp, looking up to see Jude.

"Grand Prince Jude? I'm so sorry! Please forgive me! Please!" She went on her knees, slamming her forehead to the ground repeatedly.

"Stop that, and stand." He spoke.

Xena quickly spranged up, her forehead slightly wounded.

"Why are you so out of it?" Jude asked, lids shadowed.

"The twins...they took Shayla." Xena reported.

Jude stayed indifferent for a minute, remembering their promise that they'd make Shayla eat pains 

Are they really manifesting that?

"What did you say?" Winona joined them, looking anxious too.

She's only just arriving with Kasia and Astor for the royal meeting, and she was about to leave the park when she heard what Xena said.

"Why are you here, Winona?" Jude asked. He didn't even pretend. It's obvious he doesn't like her.

"I didn't jump the fence. The West Palace got invited to a meeting here, so I came with my parents." Winona rolled eyes.

Jude threw his face away.

"Where did they take my girl to?" Winona asked.

"Your girl?" Jude scoffed.

"Stop this unnecessary drama and tell me where your evil twin siblings might take her to!" Winona glared.

Jude scoffed again.

There are many torture spots in this palace for slaves, but the twins would surely choose the least suspicious one, and he knows that.

"Follow me" he told Winona, then he led the way.

--

Shayla was blindfolded till she arrived at an unknown spot in the palace where the twins dropped her on the cold ground.

Her blindfold was removed, and she nearly screamed when she saw herself beside a deep old well.

The evil twins stood tall in front of her, together with Mercury who's grinning creepily.

This place seems like it's not part of the palace as there are straw grasses everywhere, but it's part of the palace cos she could still smell doom.

Shayla's pale lips parted to talk, but her throat hurts, so she couldn't even say a thing.

She looked at the well, fighting back her tears.

She arrived at the palace today, and today is already the worse day of her existence.

Maybe it's just better to die?

"Do you like it? The well?" Sadie squatted beside her, her perfume so intentionally choky.

Shayla didn't try to move back, she just sniffled, her eyes stinging.

"Don't worry, you won't die. But there are some hungry fishes inside the well, and they only eat faces. They'll eat that face of yours first, then we'll pull you out and start the real torture." Sadie smiled, stroking her hair like a dog's.

"Time is one minute to 7pm, and the torture of Shayla the slave starts!!" Mercury announced happily.

The twins eyes glowed.

They didn't have to use their hands, Shayla was magically lifted from the ground.

She shut her eyes, expecting to fall in the well anytime soon, but...

She has only lived as a slave all her life. She hasn't enjoyed anything.

Her plan is to escape this world one day, go to the human world to settle down peacefully, live well and eat good food for once in her life.

So she can't die yet.

She opened her eyes, and she screamed when she saw she's already on top of the well, ready to fall inside.

"No...no... please... Don't ruin my face!!! It's the only thing I have left!! Please don't ruin me!!! Please!!!"

That begging only increased the twins arrogance.

"Stop!!!!" Winona and Jude yelled from afar.

They're both running towards the scene already.

The well cover went off, and Shayla's cries turned sore as she began falling in, but then...

Like a dream...

Everything stopped at exactly 7pm.

And it's not the natural kind of stop.

Ice suddenly covered the place. The grounds, the atmosphere..

Even the evil twins and Mercury.

Jude and Winona too.

The well water also became ice.

Shayla fell from the well top to the ground, and she groaned painfully, looking so pitiful with the blood all-over.

Her blood stained the ice on the ground, colouring the whiteness.

She stood to her feet, her eyes so damn wide as she looked around.

What's going on?

She ran away from that part of the palace, holding her tummy.

She was matching ice cubes, slipping on ice carpets and going through ice doors like she's the only one left here.

When she got to the inner palace, she came to a fast stop, her breaths congesting hotly in her throat.

Ice has covered everyone here too.

The maids, the slaves, the cars in the park, the palace building itself.

Ice covered everything and everyone, EXCEPT HER.

CHAPTER 7&8.

COMPLETE REQUIREMENTS IN TIME TOMORROW. I'M NOT GIVING COMPLETION DEADLINE YET COS WE'VE NOT REACHED THE TENTH CHAPTER, SO ENJOY THIS GRACE TILL THEN.

Reply My Comments, Drop Urs, Like and Share Massively.

Complete Requirements In Time.

★

Turning around in circles for over a minute didn't help Shayla's situation.

The confusion only got worse.

She asked 'what's going on' in her mind for uncountable times already.

"Ok, Shayla, calm down first." She told herself, placing an hand on her chest. She began taking slow steps away from her spot, moving forward.

"This palace is frozen, and it's possible that the whole city is even frozen, judging from the intensity of the blocks. Only one person here possess the ice element... Lord Ryker?" She muttered, stopping.

"Is it really him? But why would he freeze the whole city?" She blinked.

When she remembered her bleeding palms and stomach, she looked at the two, and she smiled when she saw the bleeding has stopped.. For Now.

The freezing cold temperature made the bleeding pause, and it'd resume whenever this ice melts.

It should better last for long, but come to think of it. Why did she not freeze like the others?

"Is it because I'm wounded?" She nodded, stopping in front of the Millions castle.

"Would have loved to escape this cruel city right now, but I don't even know where the crossover ladder is. Tough life, Shayla...tough life." She shook her head.

"I'll just have a tour then," she sighed, entering the castle.

She met Grand Supreme Zeke and Azura in the lobby.

They're with Kasia and Astor, about to move to the meeting room before this freezing happened, so they're all in walking positions as the ice covered them.

Shayla smirked lightly at Zeke's frozen statue. 

"You took him because his father slapped your wife's butt? Unbelievable...the more I get to know about Luminarians, the more thankful I am that I'm not one."

She went into the castle kitchen which looks like her dream bedroom. The kitchen is larger than the Slaves Wing Hall where 200+ slaves sleep every night.

And the food here? They're too good-looking, they made her mouth water.

She glanced at the kitchen wall clock, and she was surprised that it didn't freeze at all. Time is still running.

Only the people froze, time is unaffected. 

Cool.

She opened the deep freezer and got herself a turkey sandwich and a bottle of sparkling water.

She settled down in the silent kitchen and ate as much as she could, making sure she finished it all.

She washed it down with the water, disposed the bottle and sandwich bowl before leaving the castle.

When she got back outside, it's raining, surprisingly.

Ice rain, but there is a little bit of melting water in it.

She took off her skirt and blouse, now left in her old singlet and panty.

Though her hands hurts, but she still managed to wash her clothe, getting rid of the sweat and soil.

She returned to the Slaves Wing and hung the clothes on one of the hanging ropes inside.

Getting back outside, she stood under the pouring ice rain, feeling at peace with herself for the first time ever.

Even if it won't last, she'd treasure this moment forever.

She closed her eyes, hugging her almost naked body under the rain.

It's too good.

--

[Crossover Boundary]

Just like every other spot in the city, this place is frozen too.

The ice rain is still falling slightly, but it didn't affect the freezing.

The barricade steel lifted, and Ryker strolled inside, hair wet from the rain and body full of goosebumps from his own ice.

His clothes are slightly wet, especially the shirt which is literally his second skin right now. It's gumming tight to his wife chest, and he hates it.

His discomfort is obvious on his face, carving his brows as he made his move to the Managers desk.

Everyone there are frozen on their seats, and he was about to melt down one of them to ask questions when he sighted the person he came here for.

The boss.

He's holding a briefcase, going for the ladder room. His hand was already on the silver door knob before he froze there.

Ryker moved closer, touching his head slightly to give him a meltdown.

Immediately the ice melted from his body, he fell on the wall weakly, but when he saw Ryker, his back left the wall, and his knees hit the ground with trepidation.

"Lord Ryker!" 

Ryker stared down at him, his dark lashes lowering.

"One question. One answer. Where is Xena's twin presently?" 

The boss's eyes blinked twice, then he swallowed emptiness before mustering a fake smile.

"Xena? Your sister? She's a twin? I didn't even know.." he chuckled awkwardly. "I have no reason to know where she is."

Ryker's lip lifted—not a smirk, but a eerie smile which made the boss shift back. However, he wasn't lucky.

Ryker's palm conjured one sharp ice spike, and he sent it to him, the merciless edge driving into his chest.

"Ahhh!!" The boss fell on his butt, grabbing the chest tightly. His eyes rotated, and his heart felt like it was squeezing out hot lava instead of blood. Another groan left his throat.

Despite the freezing world, sweat formed in his armpits.

Ryker didn't move, didn't talk. His oceanic gaze didn't leave him either.

The second ice spike formed in his palm, and the boss went back to his knees, spitting out a little amount of blood as his chest is still squeezing.

"No... My Lord! I lied. I know her. I know Xena's twin. She came to my warehouse just last week, and I even thought she was Xena cos they looked exactly like each other, but then she told me she's the twin, and that she needed me to make her a perfume to mask her body scent." He confessed.

"And?" Ryker's palm folded. He took a step closer.

"And I made her the perfume, combining garlic and ginger and lemon...I made her the rare perfume, and she didn't even pay for it. She gave me a head strike, and I passed out. By the time I woke up, she was gone." He further talked, blood drooling from his lips.

"The exact time she came? What she wore? Her accent? Body tone?" Ryker asked calmly.

"I don't know the exact time, but the sun was already dipping below the horizon. She wore a short leather gown which looked like a knock-off, and her shoes were peeling. She had the same accent as you, and her body tone? Just like Xena's." The boss explained, hoping to get out of here alive, but that's just wishful thinking.

A second later, Ryker's palm went up again, conjuring three ice spikes.

"B..ut I told you everything you asked!" The boss stood, voice shaking.

"You lied at first. That's unforgivable. Lies are ugly, and I detest it." Ryker muttered.

The boss took two steps backwards, then he turned around.

He was already running out of the boundary, but the ice spikes drove into his back.

He stopped abruptly. His legs twisted, and his arms went limp by his sides. His mouth bursted out blood, splashing Ryker's face.

That eerie smile appeared on his face again as the boss's body hit the ground, completely dead.

He left the boundary, and as he walked away from the area, he couldn't help but feel more discomfort in his chest.

This isn't about his wet clothes. It's about something else.

He's supposed to be the only one unfrozen in this city right now.

This discomfort means there's someone else, and that someone has the ability to make him feel uncomfortable?

Him?

"Great talent, whoever you are..." He muttered, stopping in the middle of the road. Water rode down his white shirt softly. 

Is it the partner?

He might have been constantly pressuring Sage and Cheyenne to bring girls from the city to check for the mark, but he's actually not interested in finding the partner.

If he finds her, it's a bonus for The Millions Family. A bonus he's never gonna give to them.

So wherever that partner is, she should either die on her own, or never cross paths with him.

He didn't leave that road, and the blood smear on his face made him look murderous as he levitated, lifting himself from the ground.

He stood in the air with his two hands stretched out, connecting back with the ice.

He started the melting process, defrosting the city all alone.

And Cheyenne was right. It's consuming a lot of his energy.

He heightened the melting rate, and the meltdown process started.

--

[East Palace, Thirty Minutes Later]

The ice melted down completely, and the frozen people got their lives back at the same time.

Everywhere dried up like ice never happened.

Sadie, Soren and Mercury staggered, but they were quick to hold each other down.

Their eyes met, and their heads shot up, then their teeth clattered with cold.

"What just happened? Someone explain!" Sadie yelled.

"We were all here together before things went blank." Soren muttered.

"Are we still in Luminaria? Are you sure?" Mercury shook her head.

Jude and Winona faced each other too, their eyes wide.

"What was that?" Winona muttered, blowing steam out of her mouth.

"The hell is this cold?" Jude said, looking around to see the panicky state of everyone else.

It's like they were in a trance and just waking up. Nothing feels real.

"Shayla" Winona murmured, taking fast steps to the twins who're still looking confused.

"Where is Shayla?" She queried.

"How were you able to enter this palace with that stinky feet of yours! And you dare ask me about that slave? What disrespect is this!" Sadie charged forward.

Her one eye is still blind, and seeing the redness, Winona just knows it's her brother Blaise. Only his red hair has this blinding ability.

"I see you received a big share from Blaise. I wish he blinded your two eyes." Winona mocked.

"What did you just say?" Soren came closer, glaring.

"Is she mad?" Mercury scoffed.

"Where is Shayla?" Winona questioned again.

"Since you miss her so much, go inside the well instead of her, you powerless idiot!" Sadie pushed Winona's chest.

Winona didn't even stagger. She fell straight into the well with a piercing scream which shook the grounds.

Sadie leaned on the well edges, smiling... "If you can hear me Winona!! Your brother Blaise is dying soon! You don't really think I'd let him go freely after he messed with my eyes, right?"

She dusted her palms and left.

Soren and Mercury tagged along, all leaving Winona to her own fate.

Jude arrived beside the well some minutes later, holding a long thick rope which he suspended into the wall.

"Hold on to it, Winona!" He shouted, waiting for few seconds before starting to pull the rope up.

He felt heaviness, meaning Winona is already holding it.

He kept pulling relentlessly till he finally got her out, but surprisingly, her face is fine, and she's not soaked.

"The fishes didn't eat your face?" Jude scoffed, throwing down the rope.

"I can hide inside walls, so I hid inside the well wall. I didn't fall in the water." she sighed.

"What a useless power to have in this cruel city. Hide in walls? You're useless." Jude frowned, walking off.

"I used to be the most powerful in the Spectre family before your family stole the city from us." Winona replied.

Jude didn't stop or reply.

"Take me to the Slaves Wing to see Shayla. She wasn't in the well, so she's definitely fine." Winona went after him.

Jude quicked his steps, but Winona walked faster too.

--

[Millions Castle, Meeting Court]

The shock from the occurrence from some minutes ago is still in the bones of everyone, so even as they settled for the meeting, no one's talking.

Zeke is just sipping from his flute of wine, occasionally grunting as the liquor travelled down his throat.

Azura sat beside him, looking curious and confused at the same time.

Kasia and Astor are at the other side of the table, then a lot of clansmen from both families.

The atmosphere is always like cold this in their meetings, but it's colder tonight.

"Ryker actually froze the time?" Azura spoke up first.

"If he really froze time, then the time shouldn't be 7:36 right now. We got here at 7, so time has been running without stop." Zeke replied.

"How are you so sure?" Azura frowned.

"Whatever happened has nothing to do with Ryker. Also, he has been feeding the FrostOrb with his blood, and it consumes power, so how would he be able to freeze this whole city without his partner to meltdown for him? Do you know how big Luminaria is? You're telling me he did the freezing and did the meltdown all alone?" Zeke said.

"You're underestimating him, and I hate it! We both know the power he carries in his body, we saw it on the day he was born! He almost died cos he couldn't contain that power alone!" Azura stood angrily.

"And I'm saying it's not him! If he used that much power, the FrostOrb light would have turned off by now, but it's still bright. Calm down, Zura. I know what I'm talking about." Zeke huffed, trying to be calm.

Azura sat back, balling her ring-filled fingers. The ring in her nose glittered like her anger.

Kasia, Astor and the clansmen have been watching in silence, and they dare not talk till the meeting starts officially.

Astor is calm, but Kasia is glaring at Azura. She's not even hiding it, but Azura wouldn't even care if her eyes fell off.

Azura loves miserable people just like her evil twins.

Zeke cleared his throat before starting...

"The Grand Feast has been scheduled and marked in the city calendar. I called you here from the West Palace to discuss the date, but it seems that's gonna be for another day cos right now, there's a more pressing issue." 

"What could that be?" Astor asked politely.

To answer his question, Blaise was pushed into the Court. His hands are tied to his back, legs tied together too. Even his mouth is gagged, and he's bleeding from the face. He was slashed there with a cutlass obviously.

"Blaise!" Kasia spranged up.

"Blaise?" Astor stood too.

Blaise wanted to talk, but his gag stopped him, so he shook his head frantically, his slashed face looking terrible as his flesh is out.

"What have we done to deserve this scorching hostility? What!!! He's my only son, Azura!!" Kasia shouted.

"Shut up, Kasia!" Azura stood.

"This is beyond keeping quiet about. He's our son!" Astor raised his voice.

"He's not your son, Astor. Faye is the only child you seem to have" Zeke scoffed, grabbing his smoking pipe.

"But i married Kasia, and that means Winona and Blaise her children are mine too!! You've done your worse by snatching the city from us and dumping us in the West Palace! We didn't complain, and now you're gonna start killing our kids too? Grand Supreme!!" Astor yelled.

"One more word, and I'll force Ryker to freeze your tongue." Zeke warned.

Astor swallowed, biting the inside of his mouth.

"You should have brought him up well. How dare he blind Sadie's eye with his hair!" Azura howled.

"What! Blaise did what?" Kasia gasped.

Blaise wanted to talk again, but his gagged mouth wouldn't let him, so he just made unintelligible noises.

"I'm sure Sadie did something to deserve it. We all know how evil the twins are. Blaise wouldn't get angry just like that." Astor defended.

"I do not care, Astor. Blaise's hair is to be shaved completely, and he's getting fifty thunder strikes. That'd be his punishment tomorrow, and I don't care if he dies." Zeke pronounced.

"What! Fifty? He won't survive it!" Kasia's eyes widened.

"You should be glad I didn't order acid rain. Prepare to come watch his punishment tomorrow." Zeke glared.

Kasia and Astor went on their knees, begging...

"I'll take the thunder strikes instead." Kasia said.

"And you can shave my head instead." Astor said.

Azura chuckled cruelly.

"It's Blaise himself or no one." Zeke said with no room for negotiation.

Kasia began crying, and Astor had to hold her as Blaise was taken out of the court.

A messenger guard entered, going to Zeke and Azura's seats.

"Granny Jojo is awake." he reported.

"Granny?" Azura stood, smiling delightedly.

"Finally some good news" Zeke sighed.

--

[Sadie And Soren's Room]

The twins are in their pyjamas, both dark black like burial outfits.

They're laying in their different beds at each side of the room, pillows on their backs to tilt them up.

Mercury is laying in Sadie's bed with her, both sipping from the same cup of pineapple drink with straws.

Soren is on his phone, looking bored.

"I should have known you'd take it this far earlier when you were telling us to leave Blaise alone. He's gonna have his hair shaved and receive 50 thunderbolts? I can't just wait! He won't survive it, let's make a coffin gift for his parents." Mercury said.

"He should have known his place." Sadie shrugged.

Mercury looked over at Soren who has been quiet.

"Why is Ren always on his phone? Is he dating?" She asked.

Sadie's angry eyes met her immediately.

"Ren? It's Soren! Call it in full, you're not entitled to shorten it. And dating? No one can date him without my permission, just the way no one can date me without his permission. Besides, love? We'd rather eat poison." She said.

"I love the sound of that... poison." Soren chuckled from his bed.

"By the way...what about that slave girl... please tell me she's still gonna get fucked up." Mercury said.

"Don't you trust me?" Sadie smiled.

"Grand Prince and Princess! Granny Jojo is awake!" A guard announced by the door.

"What!!" The twins jumped down from the bed.

--

[Slaves Wing]

Xena is currently stepping out of the hall with Shayla, and Shayla looks better than before.

The ice had melted right in front of her, and though her clothes didn't dry up yet, she wore them like that.

At least they're clean now.

Xena came immediately the ice melted earlier, and she helped her remove the stem from her tummy wound. She sprinkled the capsule on it before wrapping another scarf on it since there's no bandage.

She also capsuled her palm.

If she's lucky, the wound will improve before tomorrow, but if she's not, then she's just unlucky.

"It was like time stopped one hour ago, I still don't understand." Xena said, looking confused.

"I'm still confused about it, too." Shayla lied, though she feels bad for not telling her the truth, yet.

She's just not ready to say it yet. She's very sure Ryker did the freezing, so if anyone especially him hears that she didn't freeze, that's additional trouble.

"You better come back earlier. We're going to the camp very early tomorrow, and also... I'll be locking the Slaves Hall door by 9" Lilith said vengefully behind them.

Both turned around, and Xena scoffed with disgust.

"The Slaves Hall closes by 10, stop this bullying." She told her, then pulled Shayla away.

They came across Jude and Winona right in front of the building.

The two of them are smiling gently, staring at Shayla like she's some jackpot to be won.

Shayla went behind Xena quickly.

"You got it wrong, my girl. I'm not gonna hurt you." Winona said.

"Your...girl?" Shayla frowned behind Xena.

"I'll call you that, hope you won't mind." Winona said warmly, her smile widening.

"Don't listen to this wall gecko, Shayla. How're you doing?" Jude asked kindly.

Ok...this is weird.

'Why are they behaving like rivals?' Shayla thought.

"You can talk to them, Grand Prince Jude is nice, and Winona is not bad too...she's from the West Palace." Xena said.

"I know them, they were bidding aggressively during the auction." Shayla said, coming out.

Winona nearly touched her hair, but the arrival of a guard stopped her.

"Your brother Blaise got 50 thunder punishment for messing with Sadie." He reported.

"What!" Winona screamed.

Another guard came by, bowing to Jude.

"Granny Jojo is awake" he said.

"Seriously?" Jude gasped surprisingly.

A minute later, Winona and Jude were already gone to tend to the news they received.

"Now some breath of non-toxic air" Xena inhaled.

"They don't look bad" Shayla said.

"I know, but listen... Shay. In this our world, trust no one. Not even me." Xena replied.

"I trust you," Shayla held her hand.

"Don't" Xena chuckled.

She led her to the Slaves kitchen.

--

[City, 8pm]

Ryker waited inside his car, sitting in the silence and breathing through the unrivaled serenity.

The car windows are down, and he's staring out of it, expression so ruffled and lacking warmth like his ice.

After the meltdown earlier, he was exhausted like he suspected, so he had returned to the car to rest.

He already recharged, though he'd still have to go soak himself in the hot Spring Pool later.

He has always felt Xena's twin in this city, but the problem is he couldn't trace her location with her scent.

Now he gets it.

Sage and Cheyenne entered the car from the two different sides, both panting like they ran here.

"My Lord, I forgot to ask about the FrostOrb. The light probably went off already, and if the Grand Supreme and his wife finds out that you're behind the freezing..." Sage said.

"The last time I wet the orb with my blood, I over-wet it, so no matter how much I exhaust myself, it'd stay aglow." Ryker said simply, winding up with his fingertips.

"I forgot you always have a backup. I was worried for nothing." Sage smiled, feeling relieved.

"Are you ok though? That freezing went too hard" Cheyenne asked.

"I'm fine. Ryker assured, and she smiled too.

"Did you find anything from the warehouse boss? Judging by the blood on your face, you definitely killed him. Is it because he didn't give you any info?" Sage asked.

"Xena's twin visited him last week for a masking perfume. She doesn't wanna be found, she has been masking her traces." Ryker said softly.

"But why?" Cheyenne asked.

"Obviously scared of the Grand Supreme. But now I know the perfume she got, and I know the scent to trace." Ryker stated.

"Why did you kill the warehouse boss though?" Sage asked.

"He lied," Ryker replied, getting down from the car.

"I'll go first" he coldly said, then he vanished as if he was never there.

"The way he hates lies needs to be studied..." Sage sighed.

"Add slaves to that list. That Shayla slave is about to cry from my tortures. How dare she enter the Groove. She was like...I'm...I'm not a slut. And it's very obvious she's lusting over My Lord." Cheyenne started the car.

"Just leave her alone," Sage said.

"I won't listen" Cheyenne shrugged, riding down the tar.

--

[East Palace, Granny Jojo's Chambers]

Granny JoJo is Azura's mother who fell into coma since two months ago as a result of her cold feet illness which seemed to be getting worse.

Luckily, she's up now, and the first thing she was given is the medicinal herb from the palace physician.

As she drank it, her grandkids stayed around her, waiting for her first speech.

The bowl of medicine went down from her lips, uncovering her face. 

She didn't age like normal grannies. Her grey hair is the only old thing about her. She looks well and agile.

"Granny!" Sadie sat by her right side.

"Granny, how're you feeling now?" Soren sat by her left.

"Welcome back, granny." Jude smiled in front of her.

"Mother, it's so good to have you back..." Azura gushed.

"Are you feeling any uneasiness, mother?" Zeke asked gently.

Granny Jojo looked around, waiting for another person to come inside. It's like their attention means nothing.

"My demigod, where's he? Where my Ryker?" She asked impatiently.

The smiles on their faces fell off, one by one.

--

[Slaves Kitchen]

Shayla has been chatting with Xena for more than an hour now.

They're doing the chats over cups of water since it's the only thing Xena could afford.

The rations of food in the kitchen always got measured and weighted, so no one can cook unless it's time to serve. The rules are tight.

The blowing fresh air made Shayla open her arms wide to embrace the freshness, but Xena hugged herself.

"Wait... Shay. With your thin clothes, you don't ever feel cold?" She asked.

"I've never felt cold all my life... even when I was in the Dark Room cage." Shayla smiled.

"I'm jealous" Xena muttered, frowning..."Your clothes look cleaner than it was before." 

"That's untrue, you just be seeing wrong. Where would I possibly get detergent and water to wash?" Shayla said, trying not to sound awkward.

She feels guilty for lying since Xena is the same person who told her her family story, but then... it's not time to burst the bubbles yet.

"Vashi plany na budushcheye" Xena suddenly asked.

"What language is that?" Shayla blinked fast.

Xena laughed, "That's our Morozov family language."

"Your family has its own language? Strange. So what did you just say?" Shayla asked interestedly.

"I asked what your future plans are. What do you plan to do if you luckily escape this city?" Xena translated.

"I want to go to the human world, eat good food and sleep and rest. That's all." Shayla said.

"What a simple-minded plan. I plan to become a ballerina." Xena said.

"Ballerina?" Shayla blinked.

"Yeah, a type of classical dance like the one the evil twins do in their school. You need to see how beautiful it is whenever they're practicing their moves at the Starr balcony. I'm sure you'd love it if I make you peek at them one day." Xena said.

"Maybe," Shayla shrugged." 

"I'll write you some of my family language and their meanings on paper for you to memorize. We can talk in secret when you get it to some point. And try to keep it from Lilith please." Xena said.

She went inside and came back shortly with a paper containing ten new words which she handed to her.

"Hide it from Lilith the short devil." Xena warned again.

"I will, thank you Xena..." Shayla took it happily.

"Time to go to sleep, Xena." The old chef entered the kitchen.

"Goodnight, Shay." Xena waved before joining the old man.

"Goodnight, and you too...sir." Shayla bowed at the old chef.

"Return to the Slaves Hall so you won't get locked out." He told her.

"Sure." Shayla nodded.

She folded the words paper and returned to the Slaves Wing, but the hall door is already locked.

Lilith actually did what she said. She locked her out.

She could already hear different snores from inside, meaning they slaves are asleep already, but she knocked anyways.

One, twice, thrice.

No one replied.

She stepped away from the door, resting her back on the pillar. The bleak lights shadowed her figure.

She looked at the corridor ground, thinking of sleeping there tonight, but she stopped herself before she could land on the ground.

She left the corridor, stepping out of the Slaves building completely.

The lights in this area is as bleak as the lights at the corridor, so she made sure her steps are cautious.

She's gonna find a bright light in another corridor and read the words Xena gave her before going to sleep.

She stopped walking suddenly, her breaths catching in her lungs when she sighted him.

Lord Ryker.

He's on his way to the Bamboo Groove, looking as foreboding as the darkness. His dark robe is back, and there's blood on his face again!

Now that she thinks about it, she hasn't seen him without blood on his face before.

She took a look at the Bamboo Groove, it's so bright with insanely white lights. She could count the bamboo trees if she wants right now cos of the intense brightness.

"Must be extra beautiful in there right now. That Spring Pool, I wonder what it'd be like to bath inside." She muttered, taking her eyes away from the Groove direction.

It didn't take long before she found another corridor. The lights aren't bright, but better than the Slaves Wing Corridor.

She got under the light and started reading the words and their meanings out, strolling round and round under the lights and trying to memorize them.

--

[Bamboo Groove, Spring Pool]

Ryker retired to this place immediately he got back to the palace. There's no place of peace for him cos his inner ice is always acting up with the intense coldness, but sitting in this hot pool reduces the coldness a bit.

His eyes are closed with the lashes entangled, body shirtless and lower body covered by his transparent long pants as usual.

He let the silence calm him, the water steam added to the slight warmness.

His stillness was uninterrupted till sounds began entering his ears.

Normally, he has sharp hearing and an extra sharp sense of smell, though he rarely uses them unless on special occasions...for his peace of mind.

But this time, his ears didn't even ask for his permission before the sound of the voice penetrated.

And that voice is reading nonsense in his family language.

He recognizes Xena's voice, so it's definitely not Xena.

So...who?

--

[Corridor]

"Krasarvistta?" Shayla muttered, staring at the last word in the paper she's holding.

She has been reading the words with no hindrance since the first one, but this last one is posing as a problem.

Xena didn't even write the meaning in front of it. She must have forgotten.

"Krasarvi...ugh! I give up. I already have a miserable life as a slave and I'm even gonna be sleeping on the corridor tonight. I can't complicate my life further with Xena's family language." She groaned, resting her forehead on the wall.

"But then... Does Lord Ryker speak this language too?" She mumbled, taking her head away from the wall.

"He was taken away from his family immediately he was born, so he probably doesn't know the language...I get it." She muttered.

She didn't even realize she's starting to talk to herself.

"It's cool to have such unstoppable powers, but is it a must to be wicked? I can't believe he actually raised me up the air without touching me...that wicked hand of his." She scoffed, raising her hand up.

"Was it like this?" She muttered, trying to stand like him.

She cleared her throat thoroughly, deepening her voice...

"Since you love death so much and even came here boldly for it, I'll gladly give it to you." She mimicked his voice, chuckling.

"The arrogance..." She chuckled again before turning to the other side.

Then she stopped.

Her heart skipped three...

No—five beats.

Her hand dropped, and her eyes went wider than she could explain cos...

He's right there, in front of her.

Ryker.

Open chest, frosted face, tilted head, scent of doom.

Shayla stepped back—three steps away from the bloodthirsty demigod.

"Continue," his voice dropped deep, hitting her chest like a mallet.

"Sir? I mean... My Lord?" She moved back again, eyes still expanded. Her back kissed the pillar.

"Raise your hand, and continue your show. I'll be your viewer. How's that?" He ordered calmly, but it echoed.
