CHAPTER 9&10

This is the tenth chapter, and it's the chapter where I start taking my requirements serious like y'all know. Check the new requirements and complete it before awaiting the next chapter tomorrow.

I'll never post with incomplete requirements.

Sit tight and take your time, cos if it takes days, then I'll fuckin wait. I have a feeling we'll be getting a lotta breaks in this book and I'm fine with it. I'm due for rest actually.

And to y'all commenting "interesting"  we don't do that here. Say something about what you read, or I'll ban u.

Reply All My Comments, Drop Yours, Like, Share Massively.

★

Shayla didn't have the balls to stare up at him, but his gaze stayed on her, demanding the right reaction to what he had ordered her to do.

Her head only went up once, and the moment she did, their eyes met for the first time. She fell on her knees.

"I... Was. I was wrong. It was wrong to play with your words. Please find somewhere in your benevolent heart to pardon me." She rushed the words then bowed again, her forehead meeting the ground.

"Benevolent?" Ryker's voice picked up. Her head raised.

"You said I was arrogant and wicked. Benevolence and those two can never go hand-in-hand. I ordered for your continuation, not your knees on the ground." He said without mincing words, taking his seat on the windowsill that's on the corridor.

He looks damn serious, and it's not like she was expecting a joker, but still... telling her to continue mimicking him is a little—

"Keep me waiting for one more second, and your blood will be on my face." He interrupted her thoughts.

Shayla swallowed, bringing her legs together before standing. 

The restraining chains rang as they dragged against the ground, but that wasn't enough to soak the silence that followed.

She cleared her throat again, tilting her head before continuing her mimick.

"Drag her out, make sure she gets the heaviest workload at the labor camp everyday. And clean this Groove thoroughly. Clean out her smell." She said in his voice, and she surprisingly didn't sound awkward.

Ryker sat there, watching silently without a word, so she released a long needed breath...

"I do not smell though. If I do, you wouldn't be able to sit in this corridor for two straight minutes when I'm also here." She said boldly.

"You've been counting the minutes? Bold." He stood back.

"No no I dare noy. My mind just keeps records very accurately, and it tells me the time even if I don't see any clock. I memorize things pretty fast." She said gently.

"Is that why you still remember all the words I said this morning at the Groove?" He replied, voice still powerful but less intimidating now.

"I don't have to memorize it, things simply stay in my memories."

Silence followed that statement, and Shayla's eyes blinked again as the weight of his gaze settled on her.

It felt like forever before he talked again...

"And stop reading nonsense in that language. It's disturbing to the ears."

"Disturbing? But it's your... family... language." Shayla muttered.

"It's disturbing coming from YOU. You're reading nonsense." He replied blankly, and she went quiet.

He started walking out of the corridor, taking his looming presence with him, but then he suddenly turned back...

"And the correct pronunciation is krasavitsa, meaning beauty in the Morozov family language." He corrected.

Shayla smiled lightly, taking a bow. "Thank you, My Lord."

"Durak..." Ryker muttered gloomily before taking his leave completely, leaving the corridor the way it was before he came.

"Durak? What's that?" Shayla muttered, checking the paper with her. It's not one of the words Xena wrote, and she's dying to know what it means cos she's sure it's not something good. 

He probably insulted her.

"Well i'm luckily alive. Maybe he's in a good mood today, and that's why he didn't hurt me after catching me mimicking him." 

She folded the paper into her skirt and left for the Slaves Hall.

She knocked again, but she still got no answer from inside, so she lay on the corridor ground.

The stones and sand prickled her back since the ground is not concreted, but it meant nothing.

She was staring up at the almost broken planks on top of the roof, muttering durak repeatedly before she fell asleep right there.

It didn't take long before Jude entered the corridor.

He left Granny's chamber long ago, and he has been hanging around the Slaves Hall since then.

He saw when she went to the other corridor and when Ryker came to meet her. 

He watched everything from afar then he followed her here and waited till she fell asleep before coming out.

He did a squat beside her, bringing out his phone and shinning the flashlight to see her better.

Seeing the ground she's sleeping on made him nearly shed tears.

'C'mon, Jude. She's not the first slave to get sold here, so why feeling this bad for her?'

He stopped the tears before they could fall, his lids lifting when he saw her knotted stomach wound and the wounds on her palms.

Xena might have poured capsules on the wounds, but with this extreme cold weather, capsules won't do it. The wounds will probably open up tomorrow again.

He placed his hand close to her tummy, transmitting white fumes to her body to heal her.

Healing his own wounds takes ten minutes at least, but to heal others, it won't only take more minutes but also consume almost all his energy.

He's good at fights, but his healing ability is still very weak.

But he did it anyways. He stayed there for more than thirty minutes, healing her tummy.

By the time he was done, he was already bleeding from the throat.

He stood and rushed to the corridor edge to spit blood, then he weakly came back to squat beside her again.

Sensing she must be feeling cold, he took off his jacket and covered her with it, gently.

But Shayla changed positions, and the jacket fell off her body.

Jude covered her with it again. Still same, she shook it off sleepily as if she likes the cold.

He took the jacket and spread it on the ground instead, so when she changed positions again, she slept on the jacket.

He stood and wiped the bloodstain in his lips with his palm before leaving the corridor, returning to the main castle.

Lilith opened the hall door after he left. She came out with her contemptuous look, sucking her teeth as her short shadow appeared on the wall.

"Don't tell me he's in lov—no. I must be crazy to even imagine that." She muttered, looking down at Shayla.

"She's not all that, so why would he heal her wound despite the fact that it made him weak? What's so special about her?" She grimaced.

"He even spread his jacket on the ground for her to sleep on? This is the height of it..." She mumbled, dragging the jacket away from Shayla's beneath.

"The hell! It's the popular studded jacket he bought for millions from the family store last month!" She gasped shockingly.

"I can sell this and actually make some money. I guess it's my lucky night." She smiled, folding it had returning inside.

She locked the door again.

--

[Main Castle, Twins Room]

There hasn't been a moment of peace since the twins returned from Granny Jojo's chamber

Even Mercury couldn't calm them.

She just sat in bed, eating fries and ignoring the fit they're throwing.

"Why would Granny wake up and ask for him first? Is he the grandchild or us? I just don't understand." Sadie said by the door.

"You should have gotten used to it by now. No one likes us in this family, but they worship him like some god." Soren replied by the wall.

"He's a Demigod, so it's only normal if they worship him." Mercury said.

"Shut up!" They yelled at her, and she held her lips with her fingers.

"I'mma get some fresh air outside." Soren buttoned up his pyjamas shirt and left the room.

"You know we can't be apart, Soren Millions!" Sadie shouted.

"Nothing is gonna happen, I'm going to the lobby, not exactly outside." Soren replied, never looking back.

Sadie fell in bed, sighing heavily.

"By the way what's your plan for the slave girl?" Mercury inched closer.

"You even seem more eagar than me" Sadie said, closing her eyes.

"I just can't stand servants with pretty faces..." Mercury shrugged.

"After Blaise's punishment tomorrow, we start her own plan." Sadie said.

Mercury stood from the bed, pacing the room. "At the upcoming Feast, the school is gonna perform, right?"

"Why asking me? Your mother is the owner of the school so you should be well-informed than me. But I really wish we'd perform. It's hightime I show the whole of this city how good my ballet skills are." Sadie proudly said, opening her eyes.

"I'll ask mum when I get home tomorrow, but if we're really gonna perform, it'd be you, me, and Soren in the center." Mercury sat back.

"As usual," Sadie smiled.

--

[Lobby]

Soren arrived and grabbed the remote to watch ballet practices on TV, but he was surprised to see Jude sitting on one of the sofas.

Jude is glaring vengefully at him, and for the first minute, he got so scared that Jude might try something, now that Sadie isn't here.

He easily mind-linked with her...

'Come to the lobby' he communicated through his mind, and Jude stood, coming for him.

Soren shifted back, putting space between them immediately.

Before Jude could get to him, he felt blood clog in his throat, and he stopped to spit out the blood.

He's still suffering backlash from healing Shayla with his powers, and he's still very weak.

A glad smile covered Soren's lips, he boldly went closer to Jude.

"What happened to you, big brother? You can't even face me when I'm alone? I bet you're even weaker than our baby of the house at this rate." He said with jest.

Jude couldn't even move. He dropped on the nearest couch, wiping his bloody lips again.

Soren left the lobby, meeting Mercury and Sadie who're just coming downstairs.

"Did Jude try anything funny? Tell me so we can kill him right here. Our parents won't even ask for his corpse." Sadie said.

"He wouldn't dare. Dude is so weak, he couldn't even face me when I was alone." Soren arrogantly said.

"I knew he was hopeless" Sadie grinned.

Mercury went to Jude, waving her hand tauntingly at him.

"So not sorry, hopeless Jude..." She mocked before joining the twins.

With continuous laughter, they went back upstairs.

Jude lay on the couch, shutting his eyes as blood drooled out of his mouth.

--

[Granny Jojo's Chamber]

Azura and Zeke are still here with the old woman, trying to coax her to sleep, but she's not listening.

Her back is in bed, but her eyes are widely open as she kept staring at the door, expecting Ryker.

"You should take your sleeping pill and go to sleep, mother." Azura's tried persuasion again.

"I just woke up from a two months coma, and you're talking about sleeping pills again? Haven't I slept enough?" Granny Jojo said wearily.

"But you still have to rest to be able to function well tomorrow morning." Zeke insisted.

"I'm not closing my eyes if I don't see him, so quit trying." Jojo replied, and then as if planned...

Ryker entered the chamber, his presence filling everywhere.

"Ryker" Granny Jojo sat up in bed, joy appearing in her features.

She stood and met him halfway when she couldn't wait anymore. She held his shoulder blades with her two hands while staring up at his face with a round smile like he's his real grandchild.

"It's so good to see you're doing well, Ryker." 

"How're you feeling?" Ryker asked, and her smile brightened.

"I'm well, even more well now that I've seen you." 

"Sit, don't exhaust yourself." Ryker said, and she nodded, following his lead back to the bed.

Azura and Zeke left the chamber angrily, unable to take the shower of affection to Ryker, a mere bastard.

Jojo sat in bed, and Ryker sat on a stool in front of her.

"Did you find Xena's sister yet?" She asked worriedly.

"No." Ryker replied straight.

"I wish I knew where Zeke hid Xena's lifeline, but one of Zeke's crazy talents is hiding things where no one would suspect, and I feel sorry that I can't even help." Jojo said guiltily.

"Quit this talk, Grann. You're not at fault. It's my fault for getting born with the millennial ice power." Ryker cautioned her.

"So what about your partner? Did you find her yet?" Jojo asked.

Ryker stood from the stool, taking few steps away from her.

"I don't wanna find her. I don't wanna see her." 

"But you need her. If you find her, then she can help you with your everyday cold. With her, you won't be needing the spring pool again. She'd shower you with her warmness and you both can become unstoppable together." Jojo stood.

"And did you forget the second option? If she's found, you know what Azura and Zeke would do to her." Ryker faced her.

"So you're protecting her indirectly?" Jojo smiled.

"Protect her? I don't care about anyone that's not me or Xena. Those are the only two lives I have to protect." He replied sharply.

"So you're still mad at your father?" Jojo sighed.

"He deserves to die the moment he decided to leave my mother in labor at home and visit that bar that night, getting drunk to the point of hitting Azura's back and causing the death of my mother, throwing I and Xena in this hellhole. I'm only waiting for the right moment to have his blood on my face." Ryker spoke without attachments at all.

His hate and resentment is too much, and it left the old woman speechless.

"Go to bed, Grann. The night is getting too old." He said, guiding her back to bed.

He lay her down and covered her with her thick pullover blanket.

There are slim slices of ice on his skin. Jojo took his hand when he saw it. She caught the flu immediately.

"You can't keep suffering in cold like this, Ryker. Find your partner. Azura and Zeke truly have plans for her when you find her, but you can still protect her in your own way. You can do it, and you know it. You're not scared of the Family, I know you're not that frail. You're only scared that if you find that partner, she'd become a even bigger weakness." She said.

"Go to sleep" Ryker replied, slipping his hand away from hers and tucking it under the blankets too.

He left the room after putting the lights out, but the moment he got out, he received a text on his phone.

He rarely touches his phone, so it's still as sleek as the day he started using it.

He slid it open, seeing a pleading message from the West Palace.

It's definitely from Kasia and Astor.

He heard Blaise got 50 thunders as punishment, and he's not even surprised.

Azura and Zeke are very overprotective of the twins, so touching them means death.

They must be wanting him to intervene tomorrow during the punishment.

Why would he even intervene when it'd be fun to watch another death?

--

[Heartless Tower, Knights Quarters]

Thanks to Ryker, there's a really big playhouse in the quarters, majorly for Cheyenne and Sage's power practices.

They're both inside, testing each other's abilities and energies.

Cheyenne's nails are out, and Sage is holding a small black rope.

Their practice uniform should be soaked with their sweats right now if this place is not a home of ice. They've been practicing for more than an hour.

Cheyenne ran forward, leaping in the air with too much ease and coming down for Sage with her nails, but he was smart to back the wall intentionally.

When she landed at his face, he curved his rope on her wrists.

That small rope turned long, and he wrapped it on her arms too, up to her elbows.

"I only allowed you to catch me, don't be arrogant later." Cheyenne laughed.

"You're the arrogant one here, so you're saying I can't beat you? Your nails are useless against my rope." Sage smiled with a lip bite, unwrapping the rope from her arms.

The rope went back to the small form, and he wore it on his wrist like a bracelet. Cheyenne's nails retracted too, and they both lay on the floor together, catching their breaths.

"You think Blaise is really gonna die tomorrow? Honestly I want him to die. It's been long since I watched thunder punishment here." Cheyenne said.

"You and Lord Ryker are so the same. Why obsessed with blood?" Sage faced her.

"When you kill someone suddenly, their blood is always warm, and he loves that warmness on his face, so he loves killing to spill blood. And besides, he's always more goodlooking with his bloody face." Cheyenne shrugged.

"I just hope we find his warm partner soon. Him soaking in that Spring Pool for half of his days isn't making me happy." Sage said.

"He doesn't look like someone who wants to find a partner...I don't see him in any romance boat soon... it's unimaginable." Cheyenne lay back.

"What do you mean? If he's not interested, then why always telling us to gather females to check for the mark?" Sage said.

"You don't understand him, but I do." Cheyenne sighed.

Sage just went quiet.

--

[West Palace]

It's another sad night, and no one is sleeping. Blaise is still in captivity at the East Palace.

It was Kasia who messaged Ryker earlier, and they're still awaiting his response.

"He never replies texts, I'm telling you. Ryker never types." Winona said.

"I don't want him to reply, I want him to come!" Kasia snapped.

"So what if he comes? You think he'd agree to help? He loves seeing people die and I'm sure he'd take the front seat at Blaise's punishment arena tomorrow." Winona argued.

"Stop talking badly, Winona. There's still time before daybreak, meaning we can still save Blaise. Don't jinx it." Astor said.

"And Faye, why have you been silent?" Winona faced her.

"My words wouldn't make Blaise come back, just leave me." Faye replied.

"Faye!" Astor cautioned.

"I get that you hate me and Blaise, but showing a little concern won't kill you." Kasia said.

Before Faye could reply, Ryker entered the lobby, steps deliberately slow but precise.

Kasia met him halfway, blocking him completely with teary eyes.

"You have to help us tomorrow. Blaise can't die.  You're the only person I could think of. I can't lose my only son." She sobbed.

"And if I say no?" Ryker muttered.

"I know you don't care about lives, but please... please save Blaise." Astor came closer too.

"What if I said no?" Ryker repeated.

"Are you trying to rile us up and get on our last nerves?" Winona stepped in, crying so much.

"If Blaise dies in there, then we won't sit still!"

"You can do nothing. If you're really gonna do anything, you'd have fought and brought Blaise back home. But you know if you did that, Azura and Zeke would kill y'all in a blink, so you layed low and decided to call me... it's predictable." Ryker said curtly.

"But you have no reason to turn us down!" Faye stood finally.

"If you weren't born, this family would still be ruling the city right now. Your birth brought the FrostOrb which we were dethroned with, so basically you're the reason we lost this city! You have no reason to not help!" She said angrily.

Next minute, she tumbled on her own, landing on the ground and bleeding water from the mouth so much that it soaked her dress in the front.

"Faye!" Astor rushed to hold her up. She's panting for breaths, eyes opening and closing abnormally.

"Faye!"

Winona and Kasia exchanged glances, they stepped away from Ryker.

"Bold of y'all to think you can invite me here and threaten me with my existence. I didn't kill anyone here tonight doesn't mean I won't do it later, so mark your limits, I'm not a cliche demigod from the bright side." He warned and left as suddenly as he came.

Faye is still coughing on the ground, but her watering mouth has stopped. She still can't talk though.

"I think he did something to her voice..." Astor said.

Winona opened Faye's mouth, and just like she suspected, her tongue is frozen.

"He froze her tongue..." She muttered.

Faye was already crying before she could finish the statement.

"That bastard..." Kasia sniffled.

--

[Next Morning, East Palace~ Slaves Hall]

The chatters of the other slaves as they came out of the hall was what woke Shayla up.

She sat up on the corridor with a gentle yawn, and she expected her tummy to react to it, but it didn't.

She quickly rolled up her blouse and unwrapped the scarf.

She was shocked to see her wound has closed up like it was never there.

"What happened? I was still wounded here before I went to sleep.." She muttered, pressing the tummy.

"Grand Prince Jude probably likes you...I saw him healing you last night." Lilith said beside her.

Shayla stood. "Why would you lock me out last night?"

"Are you insane? You're questioning me? You were out late and I locked the door as the janitress!" Lilith glared.

"This is why you'll never grow taller!" Shayla said before she knew it.

Her breaths refused to calm as she stepped away from the corridor.

Lilith sighed, smirking...

"That wench, i should probably find a way to tell Grand Supreme Zeke about his son's feelings for a slave. Maybe she'd get killed?" She said, returning inside.

--

[Slaves Kitchen]

Xena was counting pies inside the serving basket, and the old chef was pouring the custard in the big bowls when Shayla arrived.

She waited patiently till they were done, and Xena came out to meet her.

"Did you sleep on the ground? Your back is dusty." She said immediately, rubbing her back with her palm.

"I was locked out, so I slept on the corridor ground." Shayla said.

"Oh dear...I'm sorry about that. Lilith is terrible" Xena sighed.

"It's fine, but my wound healed before daybreak, and Lilith was telling me it's Grand Prince Jude...I don't understand." Shayla said.

"Seriously?" Xena quickly pulled up the blouse from Shayla's tummy.

"It's really gone, oh my gawd, I knew it! I knew he had a soft spot for you!"

"For me? Why?" Shayla blinked.

"Why why? You're beautiful. Look at your luscious lips and lofty eyes." Xena said, and Shayla quickly took her lips into her mouth.

"I don't think so... maybe he just pities me...yunno?" She said.

"No he definitely lik—"

"Shh" Shayla cut her off, and Xena giggled.

"I forgot to ask who Granny Jojo is" Shayla frowned.

"She's the Granny of this place, Azura's mother." Xena explained.

"Oh... Shayla nodded."

"And the news has been confirmed. Winona's brother is really gonna get punished with 50 thunders." Xena broke the news.

"Seriously?" Shayla's eyes widened a bit. "Why? They already took the city from the parents, why trying to kill the children?"

"Cos he blinded Sadie's eye with his hair, and I heard they're gonna shave his head too." Xena fully explained.

"What! That's...too much." Shayla muttered.

"Nothing is too much in this place, Shay. This place is cruel, and that's why I told you to never trust anyone. Just hope for liberation." Xena said.

"So they're really gonna kill him?" Shayla said worriedly.

"By 10am, the thunder arena would be opened, and he'd receive the strikes." Xena said, sighing out again.

A instrumental song suddenly whirred into the air, causing Xena's eyes to widen.

"That sound! It belongs to the twins, meaning they're practicing at the Starr balcony. Let's go watch!" She pulled Shayla's wrist.

"But you're gonna serve breakfast to the hall!" Shayla pulled back.

"Not until 8, and time is still past 7. There's still time." Xena cleared.

"What about the main castle?" Shayla asked, trying to be free of trouble.

"I and father wake up at 4 everyday, and by 7, we finish cooking both in the slaves kitchen and the castle kitchen. Breakfast is completely done so stop worrying and come!" Xena pulled again.

Shayla didn't resist. She followed her, and though the chains on her ankles slowed down their rub, they shortly arrived at the back of a pillar beside the old park.

From there, they peeked out to the Starr balcony where the twins are practicing their ballet with Mercury. 

The moment Shayla set eyes on them, she couldn't blink nor look away from their moves.

Their dress up, their moods, the way they became one with the music.

It's so beautiful to watch.

"See? I knew you'd like it. I love the dance so much." Xena whispered beside her.

"Wow," Shayla muttered, looking stunned. Stars sparkled in her eyes, and she smiled as she watched.

She wants to do that.

She wants to be a ballerina too.

"My future plans restructured. I'll escape this city and go to the human world, eat good food and sleep then become a ballerina." She said.

"Future deal" Xena raised her palm.

"What?" Shayla muttered, not understanding.

Xena took her palm, slapping it with her own.

"Deal" Shayla smiled.

They parted ways at the park.

Xena returned to the kitchen while Shayla made her way back to the Slaves Hall.

She was still on her way when she saw an oldie not far from herself.

Dark hair, but her face says she's old, and as she walked, she held her sides, looking tired.

"The Granny?" Shayla muttered, then she continued her walk.

She barely went two steps before looking back again, seeing that the woman is swaying as she walked.

It's like she wants to fall and pass out.

Shayla changed lane, rushing to her. She held the woman before she could fall, and the woman also held on to her tightly.

"Ma? Ma'am!" Shayla blew air on her face, trying to keep her alive. She's also caressing her wrist, putting pressure on her pulse.

"Ma!"

Granny Jojo's eyes opened slightly, she slid her arm on Shayla's shoulder, using her as support.

"I heard you just woke up from coma, you shouldn't be walking yet, ma'am." Shayla said.

"There," Jojo pointed with frailty at her chamber route.

Shayla guided her there, making sure their bodies stayed together as anchor till they arrived at the building behind the castle.

Jojo opened the door, and Shayla further helped her in, setting her in bed. She supported her back with a pillow.

"You should rest a bit more, and drink water." Shayla poured her some water from the jug on the room table, bringing her the cup.

Jojo took the cup and drank without arguing, the young lady took the cup from her and set it down.

"Rest well, ma'am. I should take my leave. If any of the family finds me here, I'll get killed with thunder too." She bowed.

"Stop!" Jojo said before she could even take a step.

Shayla paused, their eyes meeting.

"Who are you?" Jojo asked.

"Shayla, a new slave. Lord Ryker bought me." She replied.

"Oh...it's you." Jojo smiled.

"Yes, ma'am." Shayla politely said.

"How did you know to rub my wrists and blow air on my face earlier? I could have passed out and slipped back into coma if you didn't do it." Jojo asked.

"It's a secret," Shayla said boldly, and Jojo smiled again.

"Secret?"

"Yes. Slaves can keep secrets too. And I'm not trying to disrespect, but I'm just...not very ... open." Shayla slowly replied, and Jojo chuckled this time, sounding amused.

"You're bold" 

"I'm not trying to be bold, though..." Shayla blinked, and the woman smiled again.

"You're oddly interesting too, and adorable. I'll reward you for bringing me back here. What do you want?" 

"Me?" Shayla touched her own chest.

"Don't ask me to free you cos I don't have that power, but ask anything else." Jojo said.

"Then can I ask... two?" Shayla said slowly.

Jojo chuckled again. "Two? Again, you're bold."

"I'm sorry, ma'am." Shayla apologized with a quick bow.

"Go on, let's hear what you have to say." Jojo got serious.

"The boy from the West Palace, can you...help him... revoke his thunder punishment?" Shayla asked.

"Someone from the West Palace is getting a thunder punishment?" Jojo frowned.

"Yes. The male child. I just feel like... it's... unfair." Shayla said firmly.

Granny Jojo stared into space for a minute, then she looked at her...

"Fine, done. What's the second one?" She asked.

Shayla's stomach rumbled in response to that, and her eyes met with Jojo again.

"I'm guessing the second one is good food?" Jojo asked.

"Can I... really?" Shayla asked slowly.

Jojo got her telephone from beside the bed, calling the castle kitchen.

"Bring my breakfast in two portions." She ordered, dropping the phone.

"It'll be here shortly, have your sit." She gestured at a stool—same one Ryker sat on yesterday.

Shayla bowed again and had her sit. If she's gonna go do the work of death at the camp today again, she needs good food, not the custard and pie.

"Who were your parents?" Granny Jojo's question made her look up.

"Huh?"

Jojo remained silent, and Shayla remained silent too, just staring into space. That's a subject she doesn't wanna say. Not now.

"It's ok, maybe it's one of your secrets.  You don't have to tell me if you don't want to." Jojo said.

"Thank you, Grand Granny."

"Grand Granny?" Jojo laughed. "Just call me Granny."

Shayla suddenly shot up from the stool, remembering something.

The word Ryker said last night.

"Durak"

"Durak?" Jojo squinted.

"Don't mind me." Shayla sat back.

"That's a word from the Morozov family language" Jojo said.

"You know it?" Shayla smiled up.

"Of course. And durak means FOOL." Jojo translated.

Shayla's face fell immediately, her arms dropped.

"He... called me a fool?"

"Who?" Jojo asked, just not getting enough of her voice.

"Lord Ryker." Shayla muttered.

"Ryker? Ryker talked to you?" Jojo said surprisingly.

"Last night, I was senselessly mimicking him in a corridor. I called him arrogant, and he caught me. He ordered me to continue." Shayla mumbled, and Jojo chuckled.

"And? So what happened after that?"

"Of course I continued, and he called me durak. I'm not a durak." Shayla muttered, and Jojo laughed out louder this time.

Shayla blinked, wondering why she has been laughing and chuckling.

Is she that funny?

Meanwhile, by the front door of the chamber, Ryker stood with Cheyenne and Sage by his sides.

He came here with the purpose of discussing Blaise's punishment revoke with Jojo, but he stood by the door when he heard that disturbing voice from last night.

Shayla's.

He has been listening to her conversation with Jojo.

Cheyenne and Sage just stood by his sides, wondering why he's not going inside.

"Fine...fine... you're not a durak, ok?" Granny Jojo's voice said from inside.

"Yes I'm not," Shayla mumbled a reply, and Jojo laughed again.

Ryker's lips pressed a little bit. A soft but genuine smile hung on his lips corner, revealing his deep dimples before he knew it.

CHAPTER 11&12.

Reply My Comments(everything), Drop Urs Personally, Like and Share Massively.

Complete Requirements In Time Tomorrow or nothing is coming on Monday.

★

The arrival of the old chef was what made Ryker step away from the door.

He had brought Granny Jojo's breakfast in two portions like she had ordered, but the moment he set eyes on Ryker, he smiled.

Ryker's eyes rolled away from him, but the old chef kept standing there, staring at him.

Ryker stayed stiff.

"Aren't you gonna go in?" Sage told the Old Chef, and that seemed to wake him up.

He placed a gentle knock on the door to announce his arrival before wheeling the food trolley into the chamber.

Granny Jojo's laughter stopped, but Shayla's eyes widened with satisfaction.

She didn't expect the meals to be this much, but here it is, looking like it was brought for ten people.

She touched her stomach, stylishly asking if it's ready for the upcoming devour journey.

"A good morning to you, Grandmother." Old Chef bowed, but the old woman stayed the way she was—unimpressed.

He began settling the food on the wide table, making sure to set it on two sides.

Turkey sandwich, fried turkey, congee as the main dish, and stewed fish. The meals all look too good, and Shayla's mouth was already watering.

"Dig in," Jojo told her.

"Thank you" she politely smiled and took a spoon...

"You should use a fork, it's easier to eat the turkey with it." Jojo said.

"I've never used a fork," Shayla looked up. The old woman blinked.

"When I was in my dark room cage, we weren't served with spoons, so I ate with my fingers, and when I came here at the slaves hall, I ate custard with a spoon." Shayla explained.

"I was thoughtless to say that, go on...eat." Jojo gestured at the food, her pity obvious in the way she's looking at her.

Shayla digged into the food, starting with the congee before the turkey, but despite her roots, she's gentle with it. She eats really gently.

Jojo was smiling as she watched her eat. She was so invested in staring that she didn't realize Old Chef is still in the chamber with them.

Ryker finally made his entry with his knights, and when Cheyenne set eyes on Shayla eating in the chamber, her nails grew angrily. 

She was already rushing to her with intention of tearing her to pieces when Granny Jojo stood.

"Stay back!" She ordered.

"Grandmother, she's dirty! You've been in coma for two months! Her presence is enough to send you back to coma! Entertaining her here might endanger you!" Cheyenne raised her voice.

"I wouldn't raise that voice higher if I were you." Ryker warned with a growl.

She looked at him, finding his glaring eyes on herself.

"I'm sorry, my Lord, but—"

"It's her chamber, and I won't stop her from taking your head if you say anything else. Raising your voice at her? It's dirtier!" Ryker snarled.

Sage touched Cheyenne's arm, urging her to do the right thing.

She went on her knees, interlinking her fingers in front of herself.

"Forgive me" she apologized, but the unfriendly look is still on her face as she sideyed Shayla.

And Shayla herself?

She knows what's going on, but she chose not to look up from her food. Labor Camp opens at 9, and it's some minutes to 9 already, so she can't afford to waste any time.

She's gotta eat every bite she can take before she'd leave.

"You will behave yourself the next time you enter here. I'm a Granny, not a play toy to be messes with." Granny Jojo said.

"I'm sorry, forgive me once again." Cheyenne replied.

Jojo sat with a heave.

"Stay outside, you and Sage." Ryker ordered.

Cheyenne got up, leaving the chamber with Sage.

"You should take your leave, Old Chef. I'll call you if I need additional dishes." Jojo told the man who has been standing speechlessly.

"I understand," he bowed, looking at Ryker deeply before leaving, his reluctance obvious.

Ryker took his seat, but Jojo didn't even look at him. Her eyes already returned to Shayla.

Seeing that, Ryker turned his neck a bit too, giving the foodie a gaze across the room.

"Isn't she so adorable?" Jojo said.

"You hated slaves and didn't want to have anything to do with them. You even refused to visit the Slaves Hall because of it, so what's so special about her?" He replied, bringing his eyes back to Jojo.

"I wanted to take a stroll earlier, but I didn't know it was too early to walk. I nearly passed out, but she guided me back here." Jojo explained.

"Take maids with you whenever you're going out, what part of that isn't understandable? There are cleaning maids in the main castle and just a telephone call would bring them running, why going out by yourself?" Ryker scolded.

"Maybe I love it when you scold me?" Jojo said intentionally.

"You're silly" Ryker scoffed, making her chuckle.

"I'm..I'm full." Shayla's voice interrupted them, but this time, only Jojo looked at her.

Ryker's face remained unturned.

"Thank you, and I won't take it for granted, Granny." She said, not bothering to use tissue on her lips before rushing out of the chamber.

"Just look at her, she's cute. I can only imagine how much cuter she'd be if she's not a slave." Jojo said fondly.

"Stop imagining the unimaginable, and talk about the West Palace boy." Ryker said.

"Shayla told me to help free him, and I won't talk to Azura or Zeke about it. Talking to them or begging them just boosts their ego. I'll visit the thunder arena myself by 10." Jojo said craftily.

"You have a plan?" Ryker asked.

"No I don't, but I trust you have one already, so I'm just gonna follow your lead and do whatever you say. What should I do?" Jojo asked.

--

[Outside]

Cheyenne is still sulking, and Sage is still trying to calm her down when Shayla came out of the chamber.

Cheyenne's nails grew. She walked few steps to Shayla, raising her nails and bringing it down to her arm, tearing her skin with four long lines.

Before Shayla could scream, Cheyenne covered her mouth with her palm, thudding her back on the wall. Tears welled in Shayla's eyes.

"You will not scream cos you're not entitled to scream. You're a slave, so behave like one! First was entering Lord Ryker's Groove, now you're entering Grandmother's chamber? Are you crazy? Who do you think you really are? Huh? I'm asking you!" She demanded, preparing to tear her second arm too.

"Cheyenne!" Sage tried pulling her away from the poor girl, but Cheyenne refused.

Shayla's torn arm has started bleeding now.

"And I knelt in that room, but you sat there and was eating like a slut! A slut that you are!" Cheyenne continued.

"I'm...I'm not a slut." Shayla sniffled.

Cheyenne would have torn her face apart immediately she said that, but Sage quickly pulled her away, putting space between them.

"Go! Run!" He faced Shayla.

Shayla ran out of the building, and Cheyenne pushed Sage away from herself.

"What do you think you're doing? You've always been on my side so what's going on!" 

"Stop it already! Why acting overprotective of Lord Ryker? You think he'd say thank you if he hears about this? You know how much he hates when we act without telling him first, so I'm warning you... don't get him angry, I don't want him to hurt you!" Sage slammed, and Cheyenne nearly laughed.

"Hurt me cos of a dirty slave? You deserve a knock on the forehead right now." She hissed, readjusting her hat on her head.

With a broom, she cleaned up Shayla's blood from the ground, doing it as smartly as possible.

Ryker came out of the chamber with Jojo, the old woman is holding his arm for balance.

They waited for Ryker to move forward before they followed behind, but Ryker suddenly looked back.

His eyes landed on Cheyenne's nails. Though they're back to short form right now.

"Is there a problem, My Lord?" Cheyenne asked innocently.

Without a reply, Ryker continued his walk with the granny.

Cheyenne looked at Sage, smiling wickedly as they followed.

--

[Slaves Kitchen]

Xena nearly screamed out her lungs when Shayla appeared with torn, bloody arm.

Even Old Chef looks shocked.

"What animal did this to you!" Xena gasped.

"It's not an animal. You don't know the only person with long nails weapon in this place?" Old Chef said.

"Wait... Cheyenne?" Xena blinked.

"I dunno," Shayla wiped her tears before continuing. "She's the beautiful female who always follows Lord Ryker around."

"That's Cheyenne" Xena muttered.

"She must have gotten angry cos she knelt, and she took it out on you." Old Chef said, and Shayla nodded.

"Father arrived from the chamber and told me he met you there. I was already so happy that you're clicking with Grandmother, but Cheyenne had to do this? I've never liked her. I bet she likes Lord Ryker, and why did you call her beautiful?" Xena scoffed.

"Cos she's beautiful?" Shayla said.

"Beautiful my foot! Who's interested in her beauty? Geez, what do we do about your arm? You have to go to the camp to work. You can't go like this" Xena muttered.

"I have leftover capsules though," she said further.

"And I have a short bandage, though it's rough but it's still a bandage." Old Chef said, digging out of one of the kitchen old pots.

Xena got the capsule remnants, cleaning blood from the wound before capsuling it.

She wrapped it with the bandage, making sure the knot is secure.

"You should be fine till the end of the camp today, that's if they don't overwork you." She said.

"I'll manage, thank you. Thank you, Old Chef." Shayla said.

"It's fine, you should go now." He nodded.

Shayla stood, pulling two turkey portions out of her skirt pockets.

"Oh my Shaylaaa" Xena gushed, taking them from her.

"You can share it with him, I'll see you later" Shayla waved, rushing off.

"When last did I eat meat! Finally something to eat instead of custard!" Xena brought the wrapped turkeys to her nose, sniffling the aroma.

"Father...great breakfast" she winked, and the man smiled as they entered the kitchen together.

--

[Main Castle, Lobby]

Jude had slept here till daybreak. 

He didn't go to his room. He wasn't even aware of when he slept off last night cos of the pains, but now that he woke up to a fresh morning, he feels better.

He sat up on the couch, wiping the lingering sleep away from his eyes and then flashing back to when he healed Shayla last night.

He smiled coolly, standing to his feet. His eyes caught the lobby wall clock.

9:50, meaning Blaise's punishment is in ten minutes.

He's gonna have to go shower before then, so he began strolling up.

The twins and Mercury met him by the wide staircase. They're dressed for school already, but not in uniforms.

The Ballet School don't do uniforms, they just wear any black outfit and it's okay.

"You look more handsome this morning, Jude. I believe you should spend more time at the lobby, you might become more powerful too." Sadie talked first as usual.

"No. A loser is forever a loser, Sadie. Don't give him hope." Soren said, and Mercury grinned out.

"I'll have fun mixing your faces with sand anytime I get the smallest hold on you two. Just wait." Jude replied.

Soren came closer, leaning into his ear to whisper...

"You'd break your own neck before you'd be able to reach our faces. Stop dreaming, you might get crowned as the king of delusions at this rate." 

"Let's go, Soren. We can't miss the thunder punishment" Sadie said behind, smiling mockingly at Jude.

The twins left with their friend, and Jude stood there helplessly too long before resuming his journey to his room.

--

[Thunder Arena]

As a spot designed for punishing offenders with thunder, it's situated not far from the bamboo groove, and there's no single chair for the audience to sit.

Everyone watches punishments on their feet, including Azura and Zeke who're currently standing close to the middle where there's a pinnacle.

Kasia, Astor, Winona and Faye are also here from the West Palace. Here to watch Blaise's doom.

Kasia looks weak, same as Winona. They had cried till daybreak.

Astor is the one giving them useless consolations, and as for Faye, her tongue is still frozen, so she could do nothing but watch.

Cheyenne and Sage are present, but Ryker and Granny aren't with them.

The twins arrived, and Mercury's laughter started when she saw the ready pinnacle.

"This is my first time watching a thunder punishment, thanks for inviting me here peeps!"

"You haven't even started witnessing the punishment yet, so keep calm and await the climax." Sadie said.

Xena and the Old Chef came too, but they had to hide beside a pillar to watch from there.

Thanks to the numerous pillars in this place, watching things from hiding is easy.

"Seems like he's really gonna die" Xena muttered.

"No one can tell..." Old Chef replied.

Blaise was dragged in by the servants from the quarters, and the moment Kasia saw him, she broke down in tears again.

Astor placed an arm on her shoulder,, but it's never working.

Winona nearly entered the middle, but Faye pulled her back, shaking her head and telling her no.

Blaise was dropped in the middle pinnacle, then his wrists were raised up above his head, chained up to the crossed-metals on top of the pinnacle.

The gag is still on his mouth, now damp with his saliva. His hopeless eyes settled on his father and sister.

Jude strolled nonchalantly into the arena, hands in his pockets and gum in his mouth which he's chewing softly.

He looks even more hopeless than Blaise as he awaited the punishment with the others.

Mercury already set her phone camera, ready to capture the moment and broadcast it.

"There are rules that binds citizens in this city, and at the same time there are rules that binds the palace!" Zeke started his speech.

Silence took over the arena.

"Low royals from the West Palace will eat dirt if anyone from this East Palace tells them to. They dare not object, so why do you think you have the right to fight back if Sadie touches you! You should kneel and even thank her!" He continued.

"And that's right" Azura took over, standing beside him. Her red robe moved with the breeze, and her nose ring dangled from her nose.

"Now take a look at what happens when you decide to go against the rules. Watch closely, and carefully" she finished up, connecting her mind to the sky.

She's the thunder controller.

The clouds blackened in a whim, no silhouette in sight. It threw the arena into darkness, void of all light as the skies rumbled with anger, ready to start releasing the strikes.

Blaise was shaking on the chains, and Kasia dropped on her knees, shaking too as the light returned, then the thunders started coming from a hollow space in the clouds, hitting the arena and targeting the pinnacle.

The first one hit Blaise, and his eyes rolled back, arms straining against the chains and bones stiffening.

He should start bleeding right from the first strike, but nothing happened.

The second strike followed, he didn't bleed.

The third strike shook the arena turbulently with the energy of a starving tsunami. It cracked the ground in several places.

Blaise still stood strong on the pinnacle, and Azura's eyes widened.

She doubled the striking power of the thunders, her face shooting up to the sky, eyes glaring to the clouds.

The thunders turned harsher, harder.

Nothing still happened to Blaise.

Zeke looks confused too, and Kasia is up from her knees right now, wondering like everyone else.

"What's going on?" Mercury lowered her phone camera.

The twins couldn't talk, but Jude is looking around, knowing this can only be the work of someone.

Someone is repelling the strikes of the thunder.

"Lord Ryker?" Cheyenne looked around too, even Sage.

"I don't understand, at all." Xena said behind the pillar.

The fiftieth thunder sounded, giving the metal pinnacle several cracks too, but Blaise didn't get affected.

His chains were released from his wrists, and he fell on the ground.

Kasia and Astor rushed to the pinnacle, both kneeling with him.

Kasia hugged him, and Astor covered them.

Zeke tried walking into the pinnacle, but Winona blocked him.

"Grand Supreme, I think it's over now. The 50 is over and he's still alive. You must stand on your words and leave him alone." She said.

Zeke stopped, eyes rotating tentatively between Blaise and Winona who's in front of him.

"He didn't die, but his hair is still to be shaved" Azura spoke up.

"This red hair is the only weapon he has to defend himself with, you can't take it!" Kasia stood.

"Says who?" Azura pulled out a long scissors, but before she could climb the pinnacle, Granny Jojo entered the arena, and the silence returned.

Most of the clansmen weren't aware that she woke up, so shock mixed with their reactions.

"What are you doing here, Granny?" Zeke went for her.

She brushed him aside and took the scissors from Azura.

"Mother?" Azura snapped.

"Let him go...he's just a kid. Whatever happened, Sadie is at fault. Sadie is probably the most hateful girl in this city. You know that, so why so bent on destroying him?" Jojo said sternly.

Sadie walked fast into the center, getting in front of Jojo.

"You can never support me, can you? You've proved that again and again! You hate I and Soren, but you hate me more! You treat the bastard like your son, but now you're even saving the boy from West Palace who blinded my one eye? Look at my eye!" 

"Go to school, Sadie. I'm not gonna talk to you." Jojo said.

"I hate you!!" Sadie yelled at her face petulantly, storming off afterwards.

"You love disappointing us, it has become an habit. Go back to coma!" Soren added before following her with Mercury.

Zeke and Azura left the arena after shooting laser looks at Jojo too, then the arena began emptying as people began leaving one by one.

Faye suddenly spit out water, and she was surprised that the ice on her tongue is gone.

Ryker probably decided to finally let her go.

She rushed to Jojo, giving her a kowtow. "Thanks for your help." 

"You should thank Ryker instead. If he hadn't repelled the thunders, it'd have been ugly." Jojo said.

"Ryker did?" Winona said surprisingly.

"For his own good though. He might wanna use Blaise in the future. Ryker wouldn't do anything that's not gainful for him." Jojo smiled.

She faced Kasia and Astor who're still with Blaise on the pinnacle.

"Keep him safe, it's not over yet. He might have escaped this, but you know how the twins are... especially Sadie. She won't let him go." She advised.

"Thank you, Grandmother." Kasia knelt halfway.

Jojo left the arena in the company of some maids.

--

[Main Castle, Orb Hall]

Ryker's eyes are stilled as he placed his bleeding palm on top of the orb, wetting it silently.

His knife is in his second hand, wet with his blood too.

He had stood here during the thunder punishment, and he repelled the thunders from here, so he's expecting the tyrant couple to burst inside very soon.

The orb light reflected in his eyes, indicating that it's enough.

He immediately stopped wetting it, then he closed his eyes and connected with the city.

He was roaming in his imaginations, trying to pick the scent of ginger and garlic and lemon according to what the warehouse boss said.

That scent should be on Xena's twin body right now, and if she hasn't masked it again, he should be able to find her in his thoughts.

For minutes, he roamed the city in his imaginations, but nothing happened.

No ginger, garlic or lemon.

No one smells like that in the city.

He opened his eyes, slamming his fist on the orb table.

Did she switch scents again?

Azura and Zeke broke into the hall like he expected, and before he could even turn to them, they both attacked him, striking him at the same time.

The flashes of their strikes missed his stomach cos he changed positions, then he gave them his strike back without holding back.

They didn't escape it. 

Both Azura and Zeke got hit severely on the chest. Their hearts ruptured internally, and they had to hold the walls as blood soaked their mouths, pouring out to the ground.

They both groaned out at the same time, their chests feeling like it's being slashed with an axe repeatedly.

The hit was cruel, and even their eardrums felt it. They're bleeding water from their ears too.

"You're playing with Xena's life, and you know it! She's dying! I'm killing her!!" Zeke roared, his bloody teeth making him look cannibalistic.

"No you won't." Ryker said flatly, walking slowly from his spot, steps so clean. "You won't kill Xena yet. You need me to find my partner so you can use her, and till I find my partner, you'll continue threatening me with Xena. So you can't kill Xena yet." 

"Now don't act too arrogant" Azura faced him.

Ryker frowned. "Arrogance is like my trademark, aren't you used to it now? Don't get smart with me, and yes...I repelled the thunders. You should look forward to more, maybe I'll take the slave I bought next...who knows?"

"Ryker!!" Zeke yelled.

"It is 150 billions, Zeke! One fuckin fifty! You really think I was gonna let her be for the family? Especially now that Grann took a liking to her?" Ryker smirked, his eyes shadowed by his lashes.

"Watch out, and double the spies you always send after me. Be more hardworking in watching me." He said, then left the hall smoothly.

"Cut Xena's lifeline, now!!" Azura yelled at Zeke's face.

"It's the only thing keeping him down in this palace and you know it! Cutting it means you want him to destroy this city completely! We need his partner first. By the time we get the partner, then Xena's lifeline, Ryker himself, and the Old Chef would be gone. We just need to find his partner first." Zeke said.

"You know how much I hate when he acts arrogant like this... He's intentionally riling us up, and Granny is even on his side! My twins left angrily earlier, and I hate it! Zeke..." Azura complained.

"Trust me, Zura. I know what I'm doing. Just trust me..." Zeke assured.

"He said something about taking back the 150 billion slave. Are you really gonna allow that?" Azura said.

Zeke sighed, smiling.

"Of course not, her death means he'd be losing 150 billions, so I'll just send a word to the Slaves Labor Camp, and she'd be killed with a push. I'm itching so see how long his wealth is, maybe he'd still stay sane after losing that much money." 

"Good, but now we need to hasten up the search for his partner. Now that things have become like this, we can't let him find her first." Azura said.

"I know, I'll work on that before dusk." Zeke replied, and they both bled from the mouth again, the effects of his strike still in their bodies.

"That bloody bastard" Azura muttered.

--

[Heartless Tower]

Cheyenne and Sage were outside the tower when Ryker arrived.

He went in without giving them a word, and they followed him.

"What happened in the orb hall?" Sage asked.

"Nothing much" Ryker replied, never stopping his steps.

Cheyenne quickly got in front of him, and he responded with a squint.

"My Lord, you know you can't provoke them, right? Xena's lifeline can't be messed with. We can't lose your only available sibling." She said.

"I only told them what they needed to hear, no one is proving anyone. Also, Xena's twin might have changed her scent. I scouted the city in my imaginations earlier, I didn't smell her." Ryker replied, walking past her.

"Seriously? But...why? Why does she keep changing scents? This isn't about fearing the Grand Supreme anymore, maybe she's running from someone else?" Sage said.

Ryker stood below a blue light, the blue seeming to match with his face more than required. He looks damn divine under it.

"I'm pausing the search for her for now, I'm focusing on the partner. I felt her when I froze the city yesterday, so she's close." 

"Seriously?" Sage's eyes lit.

"I wonder who she's gonna be though, the daughter of a billionaire?" Cheyenne smiled.

"Gather more females tomorrow, over hundred this time. And don't just gather rich brats, add maids and servants to the gathering." Ryker ordered.

"Sure!" The two chorused.

"Cheyenne." Ryker faced her.

"My Lord."

"Go to Granny's chamber, and arrange a small room over there, for the...beggar." he said.

"Beggar? You mean slave? Why would she stay with Grandmother?" Cheyenne gasped.

"Do what I ordered." Ryker said.

"But—"

Ryker's hand closed around her neck suddenly, making the words die in her throat.

She wasn't expecting it cos her eyes peered so wide.

"My Lord!" Sage's eyes widened too.

"When did you learn talking back at me? You did it earlier at the chamber, now resuming it here?" Ryker scoffed.

"I'm not gonna say sorry... That slave isn't worthy of being with Granny." Cheyenne said stubbornly, though her neck is still between his palm.

Ryker hardened his deadly chokehold, and water ran out of her eyes forcefully.

"My Lord, please pardon her. She's just being overprotective of you and Granny." Sage said.

Ryker's palm tightened on her throat again. She closed her eyes, ready to die as the coldness of his body wrapped around her neck.

"My Lord!" Sage bellowed.

With a push, Ryker released Cheyenne. She fell back, holding her cold blue neck.

His fingerprints left blue marks instead of red, actually.

"Go to the chamber, prepare that room. You have thirty minutes." Ryker commanded.

"Yes" she bowed reluctantly, leaving the tower with a gulp.

"But... didn't you say the slave belonged to the family?" Sage asked.

"Anything that makes Jojo smile belongs to her." Ryker replied, and Sage smiled a bit.

"I understand. I'll go help Cheyenne prepare the room." Sage rushed out.

Ryker lifted his shirt from his arm, seeing slim ice carpets are starting fo form on his skin again.

He took off the shirt completely, and it's not surprising that the ice is forming on the whole of his skin.

He'd have gone straight to the spring pool to soak again, but he decided not to. He stayed under the blue lights, and the longer he stayed, the more ice formed on his skin till they started falling in small cubes to the ground.

He's freezing madly, but relying too much on the spring pool isn't what he plans to do all his life, so he stubbornly stayed there, allowing the freeze. It's not the first time his ice would be acting up.

The ice formed on his lashes too, and it was hard to open his eyes, but when he did...

His eyes caught the tiny ruby ring in his middle toe.

His late mother had left it on him when he was born. It's basically the only thing he has from her.

His most priceless treasure.

He was still staring when the disturbing voice penetrated into his ears again...

"I'm so tired...if there's another word bigger than tired then I want it."

Ryker's lids went low, his ears going still as her voice resounded.

She's definitely at the labor camp, but why has her voice been sounding in his ears lately?

"Annoying" he muttered deeply, shutting his eyes back.

--

[Labor Camp]

"So tired...too too much." Shayla grunted tiredly.

As expected, she was made to split rocks today again.

She got used to it very fast since she did it yesterday anyways. As a fast learner, it was easier today.

The supervisors just stood behind her with their long canes which they haven't touched her with since she's doing it well without rest.

She's tired, but she's not planning to stop. Cheyenne already tore her arm with her nails, so if the supervisors beat her with canes again, she'd get weaker which she can't afford.

Even the supervisors are looking at her surprisingly, wondering why she's not tired cos Cheyenne specifically ordered them to make her cry if she rests.

Lilith stood beside the supervisors, folding her arms on her chest and watching her with contempt.

"Who knows where I can sell a jacket? I can't go out of the palace as I'm still binded by rules, so someone has to go sell it for me since you supervisors have permits to leave the palace." She said.

"What jacket are you talking about?" One of them asked.

"The studded one from the family store. Grand Prince Jude's own." She replied, and they all gasped out.

"How did you get that?"

A castle maid interrupted their chats by entering the camp.

She handed them a note and left without a word.

"What's that?" Lilith took the note, opening it for them to read.

It's an order to kill Shayla, and it came from Grand Supreme Zeke.

"Ou, I never knew this day would come this soon." Lilith smiled, tearing the note and discarding the pieces before looking at Shayla who's still splitting rocks not far from them.

"Leave this to me, no one will do this when I'm here." She said, cracking her neck and fingers.

"There's a pit down the valley. Just take her there and push her inside. It leads to the woods where there are wild boars. She'd get eaten within a minute, trust me. Make it fast." A supervisor advised.

Lilith picked a thick stick from the ground and moved, going to Shayla who's about to raise the sledgehammer again.

"Stop, and follow me." She ordered.

Shayla's arms went down, she faced the dwarf.

"Where to?" 

"No questions, just tag along." Lilith rolled eyes, leading the way down the valley and dragging her stick along.

Shayla dropped the hammer, her palms full of blisters and bruises again, but she just folded them and followed her.

The other slaves dropped their bowls, wondering what's going to happen.

"Where are we going?" Shayla asked again, unable to shake off her curiousness.

"Just follow me, no questions!" Lilith insisted, her steps quickening when she got closer to the pit.

There's a rock on top of it, so the rock covered the obvious part of the pit.

"Climb up." She faced Shayla.

"Why?" Shayla asked in confusion.

"Climb, are you deaf?" Lilith repeated glaringly.

Shayla climbed the rock. Her eyes popped wide the moment she saw the deep dark pit behind it.

"You little—"

"Happy deathday!" Lilith smiled, pushing her with the stick.

Shayla's barefoot slipped on the rock, but she was damn fast to grab the stick tightly, pulling Lilith with it.

"I'm not dying alone, never." She muttered, dragging Lilith up the rock with herself using the stick.

She slipped back, falling straight into the pit with Lilith.

"Witch! Save me!!!!" Lilith's earthshaking screams pierced the air.

CHAPTER 13&14

It's another week, and if y'all are sure you want all-round post this week, start proving it with his chapter.

We've gone past chapter ten, so this week will be different from last week. I'll be stricter.

Reply All Comments, Drop Urs, Like and Massively Share.

Complete Requirements Early Or Don't Expect Anything Tomorrow.

★

The fall was fast. Dark. Perilous. 

Despite dragging the short devil along with her, Shayla's heart was thudding faster than winter rain thrums as she fell in the darkness.

She didn't scream. Her eyes just stayed terrifically wide, but Lilith was wailing till they both landed at the bottom of the pit.

Shayla's body took the largest hit though. She was at the bottom, Lilith's body landed on her.

They found themselves on top of dried rotting leaves and fallen stems. The place looks beyond time with the strange trees with insane heights.

It's suspiciously calm, but the sounds of their fearful breaths kept interrupting the calmness.

Lilith stood from Shayla's body, her stunted figure looking evil and scared altogether.

"Did I really...fall into the beast forest? Oh no, am I gonna get eaten? Am I? No no...this can't be reallll!"

She remembered she's not here alone, so she turned face to Shayla who's still laying flat on the ground.

Her eyes are open, but she can't even stand. It's like her backbone got fractured since she received the weight of Lilith's body during the fall.

"You actually pulled me with you?" Lilith grinned. "You're much more bolder than I think, your tiny appearance is only a facade. You're a bîtch!"

"It feels bad taking a dose from your own medicine, right?" Shayla muttered tiredly from the ground.

"And you think you can escape death here? Even if the wild boars don't kill you, I'll do it!" Lilith picked a large stone from the ground to throw.

Shayla struggled to stand, leaving that spot. The stone missed her and fell against the leaves.

"I can't die yet. I've been a slave all my life, and I have to at least live a good enjoyable life for a bit before accepting death, so I won't let you or the wild boars kill me." Shayla shook her head, holding her waist with two hands as she talked.

Lilith laughed out loud, pointing an hand forward.

"We were all born slaves, and our parents were executed immediately we were born. Afterwards, we were sent to the census office as babies. We grew up in the census office till we were 5yrs old before we were sent to the dark room cages. That's the normal protocol for all slaves, but you... you're a different one, aren't you?"

Shayla gulped, knowing where she's going.

"I heard your mother is a popular fashion diva in this city. She fell in love with a human and had you, but—"

"Stop, Lilith! Stop talking about me!" Shayla warned sensitively, dropping her arms from her waist.

"What?" Lilith laughed again, her expression so unyielding. "Are you scared to hear your own history?"

"You should probably tell me why you are a dwarf instead." Shayla replied, knowing that's gonna trigger her, and it did.

Lilith's neck nearly bursted, resembling a cobra's as she stalked towards her.

"How about I kill you instead?"

Shayla moved back, continuously walking backwards without taking her eyes away from the short lady.

"Remember I told you the other time that I have the ability to block noises? Well I also have the ability to create them. Now take this." Lilith smiled evilly, opening her mouth wide.

The scream she let out shook the whole forest, and shockingly, it uprooted several trees, making them fall all-over each other.

The trees fell exactly towards Shayla's spot, and she didn't wait for calamity before running away from there.

As she ran, Lilith chased, screaming and falling more tree logs which rolled after her, going after her life.

Shayla was relentlessly running for her life too, not bothering to even look back for once.

Looking back in danger will only make the danger come closer, so she ran faster, her calves nearly hitting her occipital lobe.

She didn't even notice when the bandage fell from her arm.

Lilith's screams, the falling trees, and the shaking forest pumped her adrenaline more, but she came to a standstill when she sighted a pack of wild boars coming in front of her.

They're mighty, tall and huge.

So it's true. This forest is really a beastly place.

With trembling sweaty body, she turned back to see Lilith is still coming with her screams.

Lilith at the back, and boars in front of her. There's really no hope anymore, is there?

She just stood there, awaiting the upcoming doom.

Her new dream to become a ballerina?

Shattered.

Her lifelong dream of going to the human world?

Ruined.

Her wish to see her mother again?

Broken.

She was already crying even before the boars got to her.

She fell on the ground, eyes slamming short as she pointed her arms forward.

The boars should at least eat her arms first and let her stay alive for more minutes.

But instead of feeling their sharp teeth on her arms, she felt cold water on her skin.

Extra cold water.

Her eyes opened back, then they went up fast to see the figure in black. The only one who wears robes in the palace.

Of course it's the demigod.

Shayla stood to her feet, and she was more shocked to see the wild boars are already in ice form.

He froze them.

His eyes are the usual melancholic nature, but with a dash of anger this time. He looks angry, and his hand is in a dull stretch in front of him.

Right in front of her, the frozen boars shattered to pieces.

More cold water splashed, and her skin received another wetness.

With one smooth turn, Ryker faced the back where Lilith is standing shockingly too. She has stopped screaming, and her falling trees already stopped too.

"You were also in the Groove the last time, weren't you?" Ryker questioned, his anger still prevalent.

Like a naive innocent saint, Lilith fell on her knees.

"No. I've never entered the Groove!" She lied through her teeth.

"And today?" Ryker intentionally asked, voice hardening.

"Shayla and i...we were thrown here by the supervisors! I was trying to scare away the wild boars with my screams, believe me!" 

"Enough!" Ryker snapped, glaring with simmering intensity.

That one glare did it.

Lilith's head immediately felt like a thousand nails and rocks are slamming rhythmically in there.

She grabbed her head, her eyes popping out as the headache scratched her skull, tickled her brain.

"Arghhhgg!!!!! Please please please!!!!! It's... it's too pain...arghhhhggg!!!!!"

One more second, she was already rolling on the ground, her watering eyes a confirmation of her hellish pains. 

Blood was rushing in another second, and it's coming out of every hole in her body including her private parts.

Ryker's glare didn't drop, his torture didn't stop. He even heightened it, and it got worse cos Lilith began sweating blood too.

"Please please please!!!!!....it hurts!!!!!!!!! Arghhhgg!!!!!!!"

Shayla stayed speechless beside him, watching the torture with twisted feelings.

Lilith is a mean girl who almost killed her, but still...

"Please, please...let her go." She found herself begging on Lilith's behalf.

"You think I'm doing this for you?" Ryker's eyes hit her hard, and she shifted back.

"Huh?"

His force went back to Lilith's body again, and Lilith's eyes began shedding blood too.

Shayla swallowed.

Looks like she's really gonna die cos Ryker doesn't look like he wants to let her go at all.

When the torture got unbearable, Lilith passed out, her body going completely limp on the leaves. Her blood pooled around her.

"A banshee?" Ryker muttered with crumpled brows.

His glare stopped, and he went closer to Lilith's body, standing beside it.

Shayla just stood behind, watching like a spectator.

He called Lilith a banshee, no wonder her screams were that powerful.

Ryker stood beside the dwarf's body for a little while before coming back.

Shayla's face went down, but he didn't look bothered by it. His eyes went to her wounded arm.

It was definitely done by nails. No wonder he smelled blood from Cheyenne's nails earlier.

His gaze moved to Shayla's face, but she's still looking down.

"What happened to the bold beggar? You were mimicking me fine that night, now you can't even look up?" He said, but that only made her shove her face to the ground again.

"Look up!" He ordered.

Slowly, her head came up, and he saw the wetness on her face. This isn't from his ice water. They're her tears.

He took a black embroidered handkerchief out of his pocket, throwing it at her.

She caught it to her chest, and he didn't say another word before starting to walk away.

Shayla looked at the handkerchief, then at his backside as he walked.

She smiled before looking at the handkerchief again, bringing it to her face.

She wiped the tears then rushed after him to catch up, but she made sure to keep space between them so their bodies won't touch.

Getting him angry would be dangerous, and she definitely doesn't want that after he showed mercy by giving her his handkerchief.

The walked in silence for a while, just the sound of their footsteps, singing birds and her own breaths.

"Wow, your powers are...cool." She boldly started a conversation.

"Your boldness came back?" He replied, the deepness of his voice rattling the forest.

"I was just scared earlier, you looked like a demon when you were making her bleed all-over." She said.

"And now?" He looked at her.

"Now...you still look intimidating, but less." She replied, fondling with the handkerchief she's holding.

Ryker rode his frowny eyes away from her, other features unaffected.

"Pardon me with my running mouth, I'm still a kid." She talked again.

"How old are you?" He looked at her again.

"26" she sharply replied.

"Kid at 26?" He smirked. "Durak."

"Fool?" She secretly made faces.

"Aren't you?" He's still looking at her as he said it, and she blinked away.

"How old are you then?" She asked, but then she covered her mouth with her palm, realizing she has no right to question him.

"350" He replied surprisingly.

She fell on her knees in front of him, making him stop.

She bowed her head too.

"You really deserve the worshiping and kneeling and bowing. You're like my ancestor..." She said, not realizing how funny she sounds saying that.

Ryker stayed there, staring at her funny self on the ground.

"350 with that goodlooking young youth face and model-like body? So you're an Eternal Luminarian, you'll live forever? You'll never grow old? I heard everyone in the East Palace are Eternals too. Grand Prince Jude is five years older than you, so he's...355? The twins are ten years younger than you so they're...340? Meaning Grand Supreme and his wife are thousands of years old? And Granny is probably uncountable. Omg!" She gasped.

"You want part of my long life?" Ryker rolled eyes to the side, and she quickly got up.

"No. I only want to live fine in the future and die when i grow old." She muttered, then their walk resumed.

"Though the living fine part is looking impossible, I dunno if I'll ever stop slaving here." She said to herself.

"You'll be staying with Grann from now on." He suddenly said.

It sounded like her ears were hearing trash at first, but when it resounded, she got in front of him again.

"Me? With Granny?"

"You have nothing to pack, so you're going there straight from here. Tend to her needs." Ryker stated.

"Meaning I won't be coming to the labor camp anymore?" She blinked.

"What? It's too much?" He blankly replied, and she jumped.

"No... It's not too much! Thank you! Thank you!!"

"Wake up at 5, sweep and clean her chamber. She'll stop eating from the castle kitchen, you'll cook for her in her chambers. Run her bathwater, pick her clothes out, wash the unclean ones."

"I'll do everything! I can do anything... except cooking." She muttered the last part like a secret, biting her lip inwardly.

"Then return to the labor camp." He said curtly, bypassing her and moving forward.

"I spent almost all my life in a cage so it'd be weird if I can cook! I need someone to teach me and I'll grasp it and even hone my skills myself! I promise I'm a fast learner!" She said loudly, her voice following him.

He didn't stop walking.

"My Lord!" She went after him again, her voice echoing.

They went out of sight, and the castle guards appeared from the pit entrance.

They expected to see Shayla's body torn to bits by the boars, but they met Lilith's unconscious body instead.

She's almost dead.

"Pick her up" their leader instructed, and the others obeyed.

--

[Black Swan Ballet School, 2PM]

There are no silly classes distribution here in the school.

Upgrade happens by tiers. There are Tier 1, 2 and 3.

Tier 2&3 are for the other billionaire dancers from powerful families, but 1 is obviously for the twins and Mercury.

They all practice in the same hall and receive ballet classes in the same room, but whenever it's time to perform publicly, the twins and Mercury are always at the center.

They always take the shine and glory. No one else can enter Tier One except them. They're like Diamond 3.

Presently, it's the exercising period in the gymnasium, and it's full of dancers who're stretching their bodies and sweating out their calories to prepare for the next class.

Soren sat in a corner at the gym, his hair slickly wet cos he had poured bottled water on it earlier. His armless shirt looks sweaty cos he had exercised a little too much, but he's not preparing to take it off at all.

He loves when girls drool over his sweaty body.

Mercury sat beside him. Her face is into her phone, but she's secretly peeking at Soren's sweaty body.

Sadie is standing on pointe not far from them, looking as devious and devilish as ever with that black lipstick and her short skirt that barely covered her arse.

She still can't get her mind off what happened at the thunder arena. It gets more and more upsetting when she thinks about it.

The other ballet dancers around her kept quiet, exercising in silence cos whenever Sadie is like this, then it's gets messy with noise.

"Sadie, enough with the venting, don't break your toes. You've been standing on pointe for an hour now." Mercury said.

With a loud heave, Sadie dropped on her full foot, but just as she was about to go grab a bottle of water, she sighted a girl who's photographing Soren with her phone.

With three long steps, she was with the girl. She snatched the phone from her, scrolling through her gallery to see she snapped over thirty pictures of Soren already.

She looked up glaringly, and the girl gulped emptiness.

"I was just...I was..."

"What's going on?" Soren and Mercury joined them. 

"The bîtch was snapping your pictures!" Sadie barked, showing them the phone screen.

"I bet I'm the only one on her gallery. Gawd, It's always the ugly ones." Soren said rudely.

"Don't tell me you have a crush on him? You're not aware that you can't like Soren without Sadie's permission?" Mercury pushed the girl, and she fell back.

Sadie shattered the phone on the ground, and the girl started running out of the gym, but Sadie attacked her halfway.

Her eyes did the glow, and Soren joined her, both striking her down at the same time.

She didn't bleed. She didn't scream, but it was instantaneous.

She fell down and died on the spot, and the worse part?

Her body started smelling immediately.

Sadie picked a bottled water, pouring the water on her dead body.

"That should be enough to row you to hell where you'll burn to ashes" she laughed, dumping the bottle on her body too.

The other dancers converged around, covering their noses to block the smell.

"Well-deserved." Mercury scoffed.

"Let this be a deterrent to the rest of you! Don't like my sister, and do not fall for me!" Soren announced.

It didn't take long before morgue attendants rushed into the gymnasium, and as usual, the body was carried away.

No one questioned the twins.

No one dares.

"Let's go, Soren." Sadie raised her shoulders, going for the door.

Soren followed her, and the other dancers released the breaths they've been holding.

Mercury didn't follow the twins. Instead, she went to the Head Teacher's office.

She slid the silver door open, going inside with a large smile.

Her mother—Esme, sat on the swivel chair behind the large desk, her beauty so feral.

She has been crowned the most beautiful woman in Luminaria twice, and no doubt she's gonna take the award this year again at the Feast.

She's really beautiful, and that makes her pride skyrocket all the time.

Her bright yellow nails dropped from the laptop in front of her. She relaxed on her chair till Mercury got to her and sat on her desk with one part of her butt.

"Your evil twin friends killed another dancer again? They really won't stop till they kill every ballet dancer in this school, right?" Esme said, removing her eyeglasses.

"But she started it by snapping Soren's pictures. You know how much Sadie hates phones right? So they killed her, and it's even better. People fear Ryker cos he kills everyday, they should make people fear them by killing everyday too." Mercury shrugged.

"People fear Ryker cos he's a demigod who carries the millennial ice inside him, and his aura is ultimate. His bloodthirst is just a bonus. They wanna be like him?" Esme scoffed.

"Whose side are you on? Our side? Or Ryker's?" Mercury said.

"Why are you here?" Esme went back to working on her laptop.

"The Feast...the upcoming Lantern Night Feast, we're performing, right? We didn't perform last year and you promised to make it happen this year! Motherrr" Mercury held her hands.

"It's been confirmed, my dancers are performing, happy now?" Esme sighed, taking her hands from Mercury.

She picked her iced tea cup and had a short sip while brushing her hair with her fingers.

"You're the absolute best! And make sure I and my twin friends are in the center!" Mercury kissed her cheek before rushing out of the office.

"Brat" Esme smiled, dropping her tea cup. She received a call from a known number, and the smile that perched on her face showed that she has been expecting the call for long.

She picked up, turning her swivel chair to back her desk.
