[West Palace, Blaise's Room]

Going to the ballet school after escaping death would be stupid, so Blaise has been resting at home.

He's laying in bed, facing the ceiling while caressing the fur of his fluffy pet dog that's beside him.

When he was chained to the thunder arena chains on the pinnacle, he was ready to hug death but like a dream, he's suddenly saved?

Whatever Ryker is planning to use him for is definitely not anything nice. Ryker isn't nice.

And as for Sadie... Maybe he's just gonna fight her from the shadows from now on. Physical fight isn't advisable, he'd only get hurt.

His door opened, and he sat up in bed  as Faye came in with a tray of syrup in a small cup.

"Your mother said you'd feel more at ease if you drink it." She said, dropping the tray beside him.

"I'm not drinking" Blaise pushed the tray away from the bed, wasting the syrup on the ground.

"She made so much effort before she was able to squeeze this syrup out of the honeycomb herself!" Faye snarled.

"Then maybe she shouldn't have let you bring it." Blaise got down from the bed.

"Oh...so this is about me again? This is about my father marrying your mother? I'm not comfortable with it too! I don't like you or your mother, but I still tried to speak up for you when the bastard was invited here, and he froze my tongue because of you!" She shouted.

"He should have left it frozen forever, he shouldn't have melted it." Blaise muttered, throwing on long pants.

"What!" Faye's arms dropped.

"Let's go, Faye!" Blaise gestured at his pet dog. 

The dog jumped down from the bed too, following him towards the door.

"You gave your dog my name?" Faye said unbelievably.

"Cos that's what you are? A dog?" Blaise replied, leaving with the dog.

"Damn you! Blaise Spectre!" Faye yelled, but it wasn't enough to bring Blaise back.

She was panting as she flung his antiques bowl yo the wall.

In the lobby downstairs, Kasia and Astor are seated, drinking happily from small cups of liquor while staring at Winona who's currently packaging a gift.

"Who's the gift for?" Kasia asked.

"Shayla. I heard she was the one who asked Grandmother Jojo to intervene." Winona smiled.

"You like the slave girl that much?" Astor smiled back.

"Gawd forbid a lady wants to say thank you." Winona said.

Astor grinned. The weight of Blaise's misfortune that's off his shoulder made his maid lighter.

"But I like her. Even if I can't have her for now, I believe I'll have her later. We'll still get this city back, and by then, she'll be mine." Winona said.

"I see you have a plan" Kasia chuckled.

"As always" Winona shrugged.

"Ryker helped save Blaise, and Jojo. Those two seem like the people who'd bend to our side..." Kasia said.

"Jude too. I might know how to persuade him." Winona sat, placing the gift box on her laps.

"Making three of them. We can actually use the three of them to take the East back. If we use them well, it'd be productive." Kasia said.

"I'm not bothered about Jojo or Jude, but Ryker? Are you serious? You think he's easy to get to? He saved Blaise cos he wanted to, not because we begged him, so don't think he's on anyone's side." Astor said.

"But we're in this situation cos of him. The FrostOrb was born with him, and the Millions Family snatched the city from us with it." Winona said, supporting Kasia.

Astor went silent.

"I don't care whichever tactic or gimmick I'm gonna apply. I'll rein him in here." Kasia said, downing another small liquor cup.

[East Palace, Soul Hall]

Soul Hall was made for preserving bodies of dead palace maids or slaves or guards.

Not because they're important, but because after their deaths, Azura or Zeke takes out their souls and merges with them.

Their powers don't only come from the FrostOrb, but also from the souls they eat here.

Lilith's body was brought from the forest. A little life is still hanging on her throat as she lay on a bed.

Azura is the only one inside with her, and she's busy checking out the body.

She'd have killed her completely to eat her soul, but there's just something about her.

"What's going on?" Zeke asked as he entered the hall.

"What took you so long to come?" Azura asked.

"There was a report that the twins killed a fellow ballet dancer in school, so I was sending condolence money to the deceased parents." Zeke replied like it meant nothing.

When he arrived at the bed, he frowned cos he was expecting Shayla's body instead.

"Why her?" 

"According to the supervisors, she took it upon herself to do the killing, but the slave pulled her down the pit with herself. Ryker went in there to save the slave, and he wounded this one fatally. Her heart bursted, and her lungs are intertwined already. Also, most of her body bones are broken, and her brain melted too. He must have tortured her too much." Azura explained.

"Ryker saved the slave?" Zeke frowned. "Strange, that bastard never saves, he only kills. His 150 billion cash is important to him after all."

He returned his eyes to Lilith in bed.

"Why is she still alive?" 

"The guards reported that there were abnormally fallen trees in the forest, and there were sound waves echoing. Also, look at the splits on her lips." Azura analyzed.

Zeke looked at it, his eyes shadowing.

"She's a banshee?" 

"Exactly, we have to bring her back to life fully. Ryker destroyed her body parts, it's impossible to repair them, but we can change them. Order the parents of the girl that was killed by the twins and tell them to bring her body here. We're gonna give this banshee her organs." Azura said.

"Smart enough, it's been centuries since I came across a banshee. And she's a slave?" Zeke said.

"She's a rare breed, her Luminarian parent gene must have suppressed the human one. We'll be needing her, so get the body." Azura sighed.

"Sure," Zeke pulled out his phone to make the call.

[Granny's Chamber]

Shayla's new room is small, but she can't even ask for more. It's more than enough.

Though, there's only a bed inside since she has no clothes except the one on her body.

Still, she's happy. Maybe her life is gonna change after all. Maybe she won't die being a slave.

She's currently in the room, checking everything out and smiling loudly, her cute set of teeth flashing.

"I have a room?" She sat on the bed, pressing the mattress with her fingers. "And a bed too!"

"Shay!" Xena's voice made her stand from bed. Xena rushed inside.

"Xena!" She rushed to meet her.

"Goodness! You're really gonna start living here from now? I'm so happy for you!" Xena hugged her tightly, swaying side to side as Shayla's small arms wrapped around her too.

"I heard Lilith tried killing you and you pulled her, I love you!" Xena broke the hug, and Shayla smiled.

"I did well?"

"Of course! And your buyer came to save you? The news is all-over the palace." Xena gushed again.

"He was cool with the powers, and I got terrified when he was torturing Lilith. I had to start begging, but he was like; You think I'm doing this for you?" Shayla mimicked, and Xena laughed, pushing her forehead with a finger.

"How dare you even beg for her?" 

"Where's she by the way?" Shayla asked.

"She's in the Soul hall, and her soul is probably getting sucked out now." Xena shrugged.

Before Shayla could question further, Jude strolled inside.

"Grand Prince Jude?" Xena shifted back and went out to give them privacy.

Jude came straight to Shayla, and she tried to bow, but he placed a finger below her chin, raising her face up.

"Don't" he muttered.

Shayla carefully stepped away from his touch,. His hand dropped.

"Thank you for healing me, I've been wanting to thank you but no chance." She said, but his eyes stayed on the tear wound on her arm.

"How did this happen again?" He asked, wanting to touch it.

Shayla slowly shifted back again before he could.

"It's nothing, it's not painful." She said. His hand dropped again.

"Are you allergic to touches?" He asked, sounding offended.

"Sir?" She blinked up.

"Shay! I think you should dress up!" Xena came back inside, holding a dress. It's not new, not colourful, but it's ok. Must be one of Granny's clothes when she was still a youth.

"Lord Ryker is taking you somewhere I guess." Xena said as Shayla took the dress from her.

"Somewhere?" Shayla blinked.

Xena just left wordlessly.

Shayla looked at Jude, he stared at her with folded palms, then he took out a candy from his pocket and emptied it in his mouth before leaving.

Shayla finally had the opportunity to dress up in the dress which balanced below her knees cos it's so long.

She was adjusting the dress when the black handkerchief fell from her.

She quickly took it and dusted it, folding it neatly and placing it under her small pillow.

[Outer Chamber]

Granny Jojo sat in front of a mirror, posture so energetic and fingers stretched.

She's trying to speak to her own shadow through the mirror, but it seems the coma aftereffects is still in her body. Her powers are still kinda weak.

The mirror only became blurry, her shadow refused to appear no matter how much she tried.

"Guess I still need more sleep. Urg! I hate weakness." She complained.

"Go to the FrostOrb room and absorb some energy." Ryker came in.

"The FrostOrb contains power from your own blood, Ryker. I'd be greedy to actually absorb energy from it. I might be part of the Millions Family, but I'll never share in your power, keep that in mind." Jojo stood.

"Inspirational much." Ryker sideyed, and she smiled.

"Thank you for sending her here." 

"Where is she?" Ryker squinted.

Jude came out of the inner chamber, smiling when he saw Ryker.

"You're too scarce these days. You're hard to see." He complained.

"What are you doing here?" Ryker asked, his squint still there.

"He's the only sane one among my grandchildren, of course he's welcome here anytime." Granny said.

"Of course" Jude hugged her lightly by the side.

Ryker's squint faded, but he stayed as stiff as a stone.

Shayla came out of the inner chamber, looking much better in the dress.

"Nah...she looks like a granny in that old-fashioned dress. There should be better ones." Jude said in disapproval.

"Leave her—for now. I know what I'm doing." Granny said immediately.

Jude blinked in confusion, and Shayla faced Ryker, awkwardly adjusting her dress on her shoulder.

Jojo took off her flat shoes, giving it to her to wear.

"Thank you," Shayla smiled appreciatively and slipped them on.

Luckily, her feet is just as small as the Granny's, so the shoes fit.

When she looked up, Ryker's palm is already spread.

She looked at the palm, then at Ryker's stoic face, wondering if she's supposed to take it.

"Come here" Ryker ordered, and that was when she moved to him. She gently placed her palm on his cold palm, and he made them teleport.

"Did Ryker just touch someone? For real? Where did they go by the way?" Jude asked.

"Secret" Jojo winked at him.

Jude rolled eyes.

Behind the door, Cheyenne stood with Sage, and her nails are digging into her palms as she folded them.

Her blood dropped on the ground from her bleeding palm.

"We're gathering the hundred maidens before tomorrow, it's hest if we go for the selection now." Sage said, stepping away.

Cheyenne followed with madly hardened jaws.

Shayla and Ryker reappeared in front of a Cooking School.

She took a long look at the signboard in front of it...

EASTSIDE CULINARY SCHOOL. She's gonna be learning cooking here, and she should be able to grasp the concept within 24hrs.

She faced Ryker.

"Did we just teleport?" She smiled, but her smile faded when she saw his eyes, staring down at her differently.

They look hard, thorny. Something between disbelief and rummage.

Then she noticed he's still holding her hand too. 

He's holding it tighter than before.

She softly tried removing it, but he held tighter, and she looked up at him again,...with a gasp this time.

"My...Lord?" She drawled, voice slightly shaky.

Ryker's eyes dropped lower. He's still not letting her go.

Not when her warmness is much. Her hand is warm—very warm.

It's the warmest he has ever felt.

"My Lord? You're scaring me again." Shayla repeated, gulping again.

Ryker let go of her, and his dagger appeared on his palm.

He unsheathed speedily it and cut his palm with it. He bled out.

"My—"

Shayla couldn't say 'Lord' before he grabbed her, turned her around and pulled down the zip of her dress.

Cold breeze hit her bare back, and her mouth widened with her eyes.

CHAPTER 15&16

Like I said, I'll never post with incomplete requirements. if y'all are sure you want all-round post this week, complete requirements in time.

The zipper stopped on Shayla's waist at the back, and she quickly took a step forward, trying to get out of his grip, but it was futile.

His free hand held her chin from behind. He easily pulled her to himself with that.

"You...what are you... doing?" She stuttered, her heartbeats quickening up with fear.

"If you do not stay still, the consequences will be dire." He replied stiffly behind, causing her breaths to seize.

His eyes rummaged through her back, but he found nothing except the wounds from canes.

She definitely sustained them from when the supervisors whipped her at the labor camp.

The wounds are the only thing visible on her back skin. 

No mark. No shine.

It's supposed to be a fire sword, so if it's on any other part of her body, it's gonna shine beyond her clothes.

But nothing at all.

"I think I have a spare dress in my car, I'll get you that. They ruined your dress completely." She said gently, but Shayla is still hiccuping, she couldn't reply.

She left and came back shortly with the dress for Shayla.

She gave her privacy, and Shayla changed into the bright pink dress. Its got thin sleeves, the tail spread from her waist to her knees.

Luckily it has inbuilt bra like the torn dress, so her bóobs are well covered.

Shayla wore the shoe back too, then she sniffled and faced the door.

"I'm done."

The teacher came back in with a smile. "Whoa, you look good in it"

"Thank you." Shayla said, still trying to stop her sniffles.

"I'm sorry for coming late to get you. Those girls are ruthless, I should have known this would happen. Tell me what you'd like to eat. I have some cake in my freezer and some juice, would you have them?" The teacher offered.

Shayla almost said yes, but she controlled herself.

She might love food, but there's a limit to her craves.

"No. I'm fine." She said, and she saw the teacher's disappointed look immediately.

"Can you not tell Lord Ryker what happened here?" She asked.

Shayla looked up.

"Please I'm begging you earnestly. He wouldn't move with just anyone, and the fact that he brought you here himself means I'm in trouble if this gets out. I'm talking about big trouble. If he freezes this school, then I'm done for. My career would be over and I can't afford that. So please...can you do that for me?" The teacher continued, holding her hands.

Shayla blinked slowly, eyes still red from the tears she shed.

The teacher quickly got wet tissues, pressing them on her eyes to quell the redness.

"Please don't tell." She begged again.

"It's ok..." Shayla took the tissues from her, resuming the quelling herself. "I won't tell."

"Seriously?" The teacher smiled in relief.

Shayla nodded simply.

"Thank you! And are you sure you're not interested in the cake? I promise it's not bribe." The teacher touched her hair.

"I'm seriously fine." Shayla nodded.

They left the office at 12, returning to their kitchen where they met the other girls chatting and laughing beside their cookers.

They stopped chatting when Shayla entered, and her eyes stayed up, never going down as she stared back at them.

Twelve girls bullied her, and she knows their faces, so she was returning their looks till she got to her cooker at the back of the class, but even the cooker has been messed with.

The wires got cut already, obviously the work of the mean girls.

The teacher didn't talk about it. She replaced it with another cooker, and it was Shayla herself who put fire on it from the lighter.

The second half of the class started, and everyone cooked their delicacies with the remaining hours of the night.

By 6am, they were done. 

Shayla cooked all ten dishes she was taught in the manual, and they were all ready for tasting at 6pm too.

She set her meals on the table like the other girls, and the teacher called in tasters from other kitchens.

The tasting started.

Shayla stayed anxious till they got to her side, tasting her dishes one after the other.

"How many hours did you prepare these ten dishes for?" One of them asked her with mouthful of meat.

"6 hours." Shayla replied, her anxiousness growing.

"You're not only excellent, but time-efficient and know how to effortlessly merge the taste of food with the aromas. Just by smelling the scent, I know what to expect, and your beef and chicken? They're tender and I feel like I'm eating jelly. It melts." The taster said.

Shayla smiled widely.

"Thank you, and it's all thanks to my teacher."

"Leave teacher out of this, young girl. When did you start cooking?" The taster asked again, eating from the salad.

"This is my first cooking" Shayla replied.

"That can't be true..." The taster said with disbelief.

"It's her first, she's a fast learner." The teacher intervened.

By now, the other girls are glaring at Shayla, wishing they had torn her apart earlier. How dare she take all the praises here?

"Then would you like to work in my kitchen? I operate the second largest kitchen in this city, and I serve exclusively." The taster offered.

"Huh?" Shayla said unbelievably.

The girls at the back gasped, jealousy mixing with their hatred.

"I'm afraid she can't." The teacher said.

"Why can't she? She's good, and you know I pay my workers well." The taster insisted.

"I'm...a slave" Shayla muttered, and that was enough to kill any interest the taster had in her skills.

"Oh...I... didn't know. Your pink dress didn't give you away." He said awkwardly.

"Lord Ryker brought her here by himself, and she was trained to cook for the Granny only." The teacher explained.

The kitchen emptied out as the tasters took their leave, and the teacher handed a packaged buffet to Shayla, containing the several dishes she made.

With a thankful smile, Shayla took it and left the kitchen, going back to the front of the school to wait for Ryker.

It's 7am, so the day already broke. She could feel her own hunger, rumbling through her empty stomach.

She had cooked all night, perceiving different aromas without tasting, so it's normal to feel the pangs.

She set the food package down and squatted beside it before opening it, picking from the sliced cake.

She covered the package and took a large bite from the cake while still squatting there.

She moved some of her greasy hair away from her face as the strands are trying to enter her mouth.

She had another bite, and just as she was about to start chewing that, Ryker's voice sliced through the morning air.

"Is it that delicious?"

She spranged up, her cheeks still swollen with the cake.

Ryker stood there, in his robe, right arm slightly raised and the left one staying fluidly beside him, covered almost completely by the large robe sleeve.

"My Lord" she smiled her usual cheerful one.

He faced her, and she quickly swallowed the cake in her mouth, wiping her lips to get rid of the crumbs.

"Why are you in pink?"

"Oh..." She said slowly, looking at her new dress then back at him...

"I spilled stew on my dress during the cooking, so the teacher gave me this to change into." She lied.

His eyes gave her a gaze she doesn't quite understand, and she started wondering if he caught on.

Did he somehow figure out that it's a lie?

"And the class?" He asked. 

That somehow lifted her worries, and her smile returned to her face.

"It was enjoyable. The tasters commended me, and I really cooked the best dishes. They gave me a full buffet package." She said, lifting the package from the ground.

His expression turned so sour as if the sight of the food package irritates him.

"Do you not eat?" She asked.

"I don't have to eat to live, I never get hungry." He replied, spreading his palm for her.

"There are many beneficial peaks for being a demigod." She said, placing her palm on his own.

They teleported back to the palace—granny's chamber.

"Finally back?" Jojo stood from her bed, smiling as she welcomed Shayla in her arms.

"How was it?" She asked.

"Nice" Shayla nodded, showing her the food package.

"You really can't do without food, can you?" Granny laughed.

Ryker's nose picked up the smell of urine, and he went inside, tracing it to Shayla's room.

Soren's pee got cleaned by the maids, but it's still smelling a lot.

"The twins...they barged in and did this." Jojo came in, and Shayla followed behind her.

Ryker scanned the room silently without allowing an hint in his eyes—as if what Jojo just said meant nothing.

Shayla couldn't even talk. What would she say? She has no choice, maybe she's gonna return to the slaves hall.

"But it's all good. There are more rooms, and she can stay in the one at the back. I built a barrier around it with my blood, so the twins won't be able to enter." Jojo said again.

"Good." Ryker faced Shayla.

"You know the timetable."

"Yes. I'll clean the chamber first, then cook breakfast." She replied.

Ryker left the room, and she stared at his back view till he was outta the door.

"Come with me, I'll show you to your new room." Jojo said, leaving the room too.

Shayla went to the pee-soaked bed and lifted the pillow.

Luckily, the handkerchief is still there...dry.

It's unaffected by the pee.

She took it, tucked it inside her gown pocket before following Jojo.

Her new room isn't far from the ruined one, and this one is more spacious. The bed is larger too.

By the window, there's a wind chime with a fire-shaped suspension.

Shayla sat on the bed after dropping her package on the ground.

"You like it?" Jojo asked.

"Thank you for everything. This is the largest kindness I've ever received all my life. It almost feels unreal." Shayla said, looking emotional.

Jojo sat beside her in bed, stroking her shoulder for a second.

"I just like you, and though I dunno why. But I know my soul likes you too. And I hope we stay good forever. Don't disappoint."

"I won't. I promise." Shayla smiled.

"Yunno what? You don't have to clean. Just cook and go to sleep. You didn't sleep last night so you need it." Jojo said.

"No. I must clean. I really don't want anything to anger Lord Ryker." Shayla stood.

"Well then, as you wish." Jojo gestured, and Shayla left the room with a chuckle.

--

[Soul Hall]

Azura dropped her arms after about an hour of raising them above Lilith's body in bed.

She finished replacing her broken ruptured organs already, so she fused them with her powers to make them merge with her body.

It's now done, and all that's left is for Lilith to wake up.

She picked a cup of wine from the table behind her, drinking it all at a go. Zeke entered immediately she lowered the cup from her lips.

"How's it going?" He asked.

"You're always absent, and I'm tired of asking you where you went." Azura glared at him.

"This morning is the maidens check, and I had to select the guards who'd stay around the Groove to see how it goes. I wasn't slacking, so chill." Zeke replied at once, staring at Lilith.

"She's ready. The organs already merged with her body, she only has to wake up." Azura said.

"How many days do we have to wait for?" Zeke asked.

"She should be up in some hours." Azura sat in front of him.

"After she wakes, what next?" She asked.

"She becomes our unmatched messenger of death, what else?" Zeke replied, and Azura filled her glass.

--

[Heartless Tower]

Ryker stood between the two treasure shelves of artifacts and antiques in the middle of the tower.

His eyes are roaming around the shelves, from one to another. And his demeanor is madly calm.

He reached for the left shelf and picked a wrapped paper from it. He unwrapped it and threw something like candy in his mouth.

"That's the blood poison." Cheyenne said, entering the room.

"Are the maidens here?" Ryker asked, ignoring her first words.

"You just swallowed the blood poison." Cheyenne said again.

"Are the maidens here? You either answer this time, or I force you to swallow the blood poison too." Ryker replied, and though his calm demeanor is still intact, it's eerie.

"Yes. The two hundred maidens are here. Sage is currently leading them to the Groove." Cheyenne replied with reluctance.

"Go join him, I'll be there in ten." Ryker instructed.

With a bow, Cheyenne left the room, and Ryker left too.

He didn't go straight to the Groove. He went to the main castle, sliding into the FrostOrb Hall.

He gave himself a cut and wet the orb with his poisoned blood, very well.

He was about to exit the room when the twins came in, their eyes still heavy with sleep.

It's obvious they came here immediately they woke up.

But their sleep flew off immediately they saw Ryker, and their expressions bittered up.

"It's the infamous bastard, been awhile since I ran into you like this. What have you been up to? Have you been sleeping around with the slave? Or plotting treason with granny?" Sadie said, her arms going crossed across her chest.

"I go for the first one. I mean...she might be a slave, but I must admit she's a little fine. I understand if you're laying with her, but getting her involved with our granny? I won't take that! I will never!" Soren stepped forward, getting in Ryker's face like they're mates.

"Step back." Ryker ordered with absoluteness, and Soren frowned, his bravado falling flat at his feet.

"Stay back?" He repeated.

Sadie pulled Soren away from Ryker's path, and Ryker left the room.

"Why did you pull me that way?" Soren asked.

"I mean... he's a bastard who's held down here cos of his sister's lifeline, but still... going against him in this FrostOrb Hall would be senseless. He owns all the aura in here, and we only share by absorbing." Sadie replied.

"That's new, Sadie is actually scared of someone?" Soren scoffed.

"It's not fear. It's called giving what belongs to Cesar to Cesar." Sadie rolled eyes.

They entered the room fully, both shutting their eyes and embracing the aura of the orb. They absorbed the power they'd be needing today.

--

[Bamboo Groove]

Nothing is more enjoyable to Cheyenne right now than the bored look on Ryker's face.

He's already checking out the maidens, and as he checked them out nakedly, they file out of the Groove through the side exit.

Cheyenne is the one controlling the exit, and Sage is controlling the entrance.

Ryker's knuckles are rested on his temple as he watched them with disinterested eyes.

He has checked 50+ maidens already, but none bears that stupid fire mark.

He's aware that Grand Supreme's men are surrounding this Groove, but when if the partner would be among these maidens, the probability that they'd get her before him is zero. He's not that dense.

Grand Supreme's men would only see what he wants them to see, and that's why they can't even see the fact that he checked the slave for the mark.

When the 60th maiden entered for her check, Ryker closed his eyes, getting extra bored.

"I cooked pasta for breakfast. I hope you enjoy it, Granny." Shayla's voice entered his ears, and his eyes opened slowly.

"My Lord, you still have 140 maidens to check. Time is running." Sage urged.

The maidens resumed their entries, and Ryker resumed his check.

For the next one hour, he didn't stop, making sure he got over the boring check once and for all.

"Again, we got nothing. Where the hell is the partner hiding?" Sage groaned tiredly.

"Can't believe we gathered maidens all night only to end up with nothing."

"And the most painful part? They're all so hot. I was wishing one of them would be." Cheyenne said, both standing in front of Ryker.

"But My Lord, why did you swallow the blood poison earlier?" Cheyenne asked.

"He swallowed what?" Sage gasped.

"You know what it's for, stop with the annoying news fishing." Ryker snapped.

"You wet the orb with your poisoned blood so first two people to absorb energy from it today would get wounded internally? But why?" Sage said.

"Wait... first two people would be the twins. They're always the first so they'll be the victims. Is it because of what they did to the slave's room? Did you do it cos of the slave?" Cheyenne said, her eyes darkening with rage again.

"What slave?" Ryker glared up, and she batted her lids.

"The room they ruined is in Granny's chamber, they deserve more than internal wounds." Ryker continued.

Cheyenne and Sage went silent.

"Find me the whereabouts of twelve particular girls." Ryker suddenly said, and their eyes went back to him.

"Twelve girls?" They chorused.

"And their families." Ryker added.

--

[Granny's Chamber]

Jojo is done eating her breakfast of pasta, and she fell into a peaceful nap afterwards, so Shayla is currently cleaning the places she left out.

She's eating from the snacks in her buffet package as she wiped dust from Jojo's treasury with a napkin.

Xena sat on a stool not far from her. She's eating the snacks with her, and they're chatting at the same time.

"What's your age?" Shayla suddenly asked.

"353. Three years older than my brother." Xena replied, and Shayla sighed.

"You guys that are gifted with long life and everlasting youngness are the luckiest.

"Luckiest? You really dunno how many times I've wished for death just to get out of this hellhole, but I can't even die cos my lifeline is with that wicked Grand Supreme." Xena replied, munching her cake.

"Do you think it's right to keep things from Lord Ryker?" Shayla asked the question that has been bothering her.

"Don't try it. He dislikes lies. Just tell him what's up. By the way, what are you hiding?" Xena asked.

"Nothing. Just to be safe." Shayla smiled awkwardly, returning to her cleaning.

She dropped her cleaning napkin when she came across a treasure tv, and Xena stood too.

"Does that still work?" She asked, pressing the button in front of the TV.

Surprisingly, it came on. There's only one TV station in the whole of Luminaria, and it either shows news about fashion, beauty, ballet, richness, royalty or power..

That's how crazily vain they are.

And right now...a fashion show is ongoing, with the most fashionable diva in this city on the red carpet.

"That's Liza! Liza King! The fashion diva. I swear I've been hoping to meet her one day. Apart from ballerina, I'm hoping to really meet this woman. His son Kade is so hot too and I have a crush on him! I heard she's attending the Upcoming Feast so I can't wait... maybe I'll be able to see her!" Xena gushed like an obsessed fan girl.

Shayla sat there, watching the TV with folded emotions that are trying to ripple out.

Liza King is her mother.

And every Luminarian who gets pregnant for a human is supposed to get executed, but how Liza is still alive today is a story Shayla isn't ready to think about.

She turned off the tv, and Xena faced her.

"I was watching my idol!" 

"Can we practice ballet instead?" Shayla went to the middle of the room where she began making her moves, standing on her toes to do the dance just the way she saw the twins doing it.

"You're a witch, aren't you?" Xena gasped, coming closer.

"What?" Shayla laughed.

"I only made you watch the twins with me once, and you already know this much moves? Give me your brain!" Xena held her head, and Shayla laughed again.

They both eventually turned the treasury to a practice room, secretly doing their dance without grabbing attention.

--

[Lumi Fashion Show]

The show is majorly for showing off the most fashionable women in the city, and there are many wannabes but none can ever stand beside Liza.

Liza doesn't have to try. She tops the fashion chain here, owning the major styling websites in the city where citizens pay millions to get her to style them. She's crazy about it.

The show ended as early as it started, and the fashion divas began leaving the red carpet, returning to their cars.

Liza was getting greeted by the others as she neared her car, but as usual, she didn't reply. She never replies.

She's beautiful too, and that gets into her head too much.

She competes yearly with Esme for the crown of beauty.

She entered her car where her son—Kade sat, eating apples.

"You weren't with apples when I was leaving this car." Liza said.

"Don't act like I'm a powerless fool like your daughter." Kade replied, and another apple appeared on his palm. He took a fresh bite from that one and grinned.

"Stop mentioning the daughter, you never listen." Liza sighed.

"I just can't help it. I mean... she's a slave and it's interesting to tell. I'm just glad you didn't birth us for the same father, I'd have hung myself." Kade sighed.

"Kade..." Liza warned again.

"I'm curious, what spell did you put on her to cover the fire mark? How powerful is the spell that even the insane demigod hasn't known who she is?" Kade leaned forward.

"Must you do this?" Liza faced him.

"Tell me...or I'll announce to everyone that she's your daughter." Kade said seriously.

"I'll tell you when we get home, now shut that smart mouth." Liza rolled eyes, purring the car engines to life.

Kade was smirking as she drove them home. The fact that Shayla is even still alive is bad.

Meaning he's not the only child of Liza? Bullshit.

May Shayla die in that place palace soon, and may her stupid powers remain buried.

--

[West Palace]

Faye looked mad as she came out of the castle. She just got in her car and rode out of the gate.

Same with Blaise. He came out and screeched off, nearly bursting his car tyres.

At the same time, Jude drove in, his car nearly colliding with Blaise's.

He halted at the small park and came out, scoffing as he watched the back view of Blaise's car.

"Is he always angry? That red hair must be driving him nuts." He shook his head, throwing a glance at the castle.

He was about to proceed inside when Winona came out, holding the gift package she had gotten for Shayla.

She handed it to Jude who took it with a sneer.

"Try to put some respect on the texts you always send. I risked my life to come here, my parents mustn't find out. You should thank me." 

"Thank you, and make sure it gets to my girl. She'd like what I prepared." Winona smiled.

"Sure... helping the helpless is fruitful." Jude smiled back, and Winona sent him middle fingers.

He returned raised his middle fingers too before getting back in his car, driving out of the castle.

Kasia joined Winona outside the castle, and she let out a heave beside her.

"You were right, Jude might be helpful too." 

"I told you, so when are you gonna start making your move? I seriously can't wait to get my girl." Winona faced her.

"Calm down, we can't do this in a hurry. Relax for me." Kasia said.

Meanwhile, Jude hasn't driven far from the castle when he tossed Winona's gift package out of his car window.

"Give it to her? If only you know how crazy I am about the slave too." He smirked, driving his way back to the East Palace.

--

[Black Swan Ballet School]

The weather became too cold, and the air turned even colder before Blaise and Faye got to school.

They both came out of their respective cars and pulled their pullovers over their heads.

Blaise covered his own head completely with the cap, and the coverage even spread to his face cos he doesn't want attention from the students.

Despite the fact that he escaped the thunder punishment, they're still gonna mock him.

He could feel Faye's steps faltering behind him as they walked into the building together, and he doesn't even care.

It's good that she's keeping her distance, or he might just tell her something to make her hang.

They curved the corridor that led to their practice hall, and they had to move out of the way cos things are happening.

The school is in chaos.

The twins are crying in pains. They're both holding their bellies, rolling on the hallway ground.

They're bleeding too, from their bellybuttons. And it's the strangest bleeding spots ever.

Mercury is crying as she knelt in-between the two of them.

"What's really happening? Someone call the palace! Sadie! Soren!!!"

Blaise left the corridor, going outside to see the cold weather is getting worse. Now the clouds are even turning black like it's gonna rain.

And just as he thought about that, the rain began falling as if it's monitoring his mind and thoughts.

--

[East Palace, Royal Office]

Azura and Zeke are reviewing the tax documents that Jude worked on, and they both look hella bus.

They're not even looking at each other.

They don't even care about the black atmosphere or the pouring rain.

A guard ran into the office without knocking, and Zeke was ready to strike him down, but the news he brought was more important.

"The twins, they're in danger in school." 

"My babies?" Azura stood, throwing away the pen she's using.

Another guard came inside, and that one looks terrified than the first one.

"The bastard...he entered the city, and we followed him like you instructed. We thought he wanted to go in search of Xena's twin again, but then he started gathering girls. Twelve girls from the culinary school, but not only the girls, he also gathered their families—parents, cousins, nephews, nieces, friends and grandparents. He slaughtered them all at Mount Zhitomir. They're over 300." He reported.

"He what???" Zeke and Azura chorused, their eyes widening.

--

[Granny's Chamber]

Xena was panting as she ran inside, and she's very wet from the rain cos she held no umbrella here.

The weather is still dark, and the rain is still very heavy.

She met Granny at the outer chamber, and she's communicating with her mirror.

"Xena?" Jojo stood.

"Grandma, where's Shay?" She asked breathlessly, and Shayla came out of the inner chamber, her hair twisted and dress crumpled cos she was sleeping before Xena came.

"What happened at the culinary school yesterday? Be honest with me. You said something about lying to my brother." Xena rushed to her.

"Nothing happened" Shayla said, determined to uphold her promise to the teacher.

"Stop lying! If nothing happened, then why did he gather twelve girls from the school and their family generations to slaughter ?" Xena said.

"What!" Shayla's eyes widened.

"Slaughtered?" Jojo stood.

"I started suspecting the moment it started raining suddenly. But now it has been confirmed. The news is everywhere. He killed them all." Xena said breathlessly.

Shayla swallowed twice and thrice, trying to digest everything.

Twelve girls bullied her last night, and he killed twelve girls and their families today.

He knew? 

He fuckin knew?

She must have been so silly to think he wouldn't find out.

He's the feared demigod, so what was she thinking?

She rushed out of the chamber, refusing to listen to the callings of Xena and Jojo.

She doesn't even know the location of Heartless Tower in this palace, but she heart it's in the middle, so she found her way to the middle after ten minutes of rallying round in the rain.

She was soaked by the time she arrived there, but she vet Cheyenne and Sage in front of the tower, and she just knows it'd be hard especially with Cheyenne.

"Is Lord Ryker back?" She asked.

"Now that you got a little room at the chamber, you're suddenly asking of him? Damn, I hate your guts." Cheyenne went forward, but Sage pulled her back.

"Let me go I should put this slût in her place!" Cheyenne pulled back.

"I'm not a slût, stop calling me that. Call me a slave instead." Shayla replied.

"Did she just talk back? Seriously? Sage I need to end her life, now!" Cheyenne spat, wanting to move again, but the rain suddenly stopped, though the dark weather still stayed.

"My Lord?" Sage blinked as Ryker appeared behind them.

Cheyenne relaxed too, inhaling deep.

With a soft turn, Shayla faced him too, and she wasn't surprised with his appearance.

Bloody face, again. More bloody than she has ever seen on him.

Even his robe is dripping with blood as if he bathed with it.

His hands are bloody, too. Down to his feet.

He signalled to his knights, and they both went inside. Cheyenne was glaring obviously at Shayla till she was out of sight.

Ryker stepped Shayla aside, walking past her on the paved ground without sparing her a glance.

"She begged me! The teacher begged me not to tell, so I had pity and didn't tell!" She quickly said.

With an unexpected twist, Ryker whipped around, facing her. There's no warmth in his eyes again. There has never been.

"Pity? I'll kill you if you mention that one more time in front of me. I took you there, and they did that, meaning they lost regards and fear. How dare anyone touch what I planted? And you thought it was natural to let it go and have pity on the teacher by not telling me? I badly want to kill you right now. So shut up, and don't make it come to pass." He said, easily as if death is his favorite chant.

The words shoved a jagged ice to Shayla's mind, and despite the induced fear, she looked him straight in the eye, her palms getting sweaty even with the rainwater on her skin.

Ryker resumed his walk, but just as he was about to get to the main door, she went after him again, boldly grabbing his bloody hand.

Ryker stopped, his face turning back and his eyes landing on the hand she's holding.

Her warmness wrapped around him again like a second skin, spreading from that arm to his chest, his back...

"I'm sorry, Moy Vladika. I won't lie to you anymore." She said softly, meaning every word.

Moy Vladika means My Lord in his family language.

He looked up, his eyes catching hers.

"Let go of that hand, now!!" Cheyenne bursted out, coming for them with her long red nails.

A wave of Ryker's free hand landed Cheyenne on the floor. She passed out like a lump.

Shayla held Ryker's hand with her second hand too, feeling his intense coldness deep under her skin.

CHAPTER 19&20

[Mount Zhitomir]

Bodies lay scattered everywhere, all bloody and out of life. Many heads are separated from the bodies, and several legs are far from the bowels.

The few bodies that are still intact are either dented in the tummy or pounded with a ghastly hole in the head. Blood covered the rocks till there's no bloodless spot left.

The smell of gore and doom lingered thickly in the air.

The scene isn't for the weak-hearted, there's no single living soul in sight.

Azura appeared on top of the mountain, and Zeke landed right beside her, both unable to conceal the shocks on their faces at the realization of the scene.

They're stepping corpses like rugs as they walked forward, blood painted the base of their shoes.

"It's a total massacre here" Azura muttered, the smell of blood driving her wild already.

"They're 301 in number" Zeke rounded the bodies with his large eyes. "Twelve full generations. He did it as if he has been waiting for this day for years."

"He didn't use the ice power. If he did, then these people would have shattered and melted, we wouldn't even meet their corpses." Azura said.

"Of course it's the ice. The ice can't only freeze and shatter, it can also slaughter so far he fuses it with the core of his own soul. It works in stages, and you just witnessed the second stage. I told you this before, did you forget?" Zeke explained.

"You never said anything to me about stages!" Azura argued, stalking to him like a child. "And did you just say stages? So there's still another stage beyond this one? He killed this much at once at stage 2, so when he gets to the last stage, he'll be able to wipe out the whole of this city at once?"

Panic surged in her eyes as the words slipped out of her mouth.

"He won't be able to get to the last stage without his partner, and did you forget we'll never let him use her? Even if he gets to that stage, I have his sister's lifeline. He'll forever remain our captive, our tool! The more ice stages he unlocks, the more power we get to absorb from the orb. It's all working for our own good after all." Zeke held her shoulders, levelling his eyes with hers.

"I'm still restless. You mean to tell me we're just gonna let him be after he killed this much?" Azura scoffed.

"There are over five million citizens in this city, so him killing 301 means nothing. We have to keep quiet and let him think he has absolute power for now." Zeke smiled.

"But why? Why would he do this? Agreed, he's always killing one or two, but what drove him to kill this much?" Azura wondered, still very restless.

"We'll have to find that out when we get back. Now, shall we feed our hunger?" Zeke faced the bodies.

Azura did the same, their eyes went shut, and they connected with the aura of death.

They began sucking out the souls of the dead bodies. It merged with their own bodies in form of a dark fog.

The fog surrounded the mountain.

--

[East Palace, Heartless Tower]

Ryker is still in front and tower, so is Shayla.

Her two hands are still holding his one hand, and their eyes are still staring deeply at each other, as if they're communicating with the stare.

Cheyenne's unconscious body is gone from the ground. Sage came for it already, so it's just the two of them.

The dark weather is gone, the day is as bright as it should be, though the weather is insanely cold.

Shayla's eyes went down to the contact, and they stared at it for long.

He's got beautiful slender fingers, but she was quick to push that thought away before looking up again.

He's still looking at her.

"Um...will you...kill me if I let go of your hand?" She talked.

"Depends" he replied, a little low.

Slowly, she slipped her hand away from his own, and Ryker folded his hand, wanting to keep the lingering warmness there.

Normally, during extremely cold weather like this, his ice acts up more than usual. 

Also, he just unlocked the second stage of the ice power, so he's supposed to feel cold like never before and get weak for hours, but none of it is happening.

He's fine, and he's stronger than ever... just because she held his hand? Just because he felt her warmness?

If she's not the partner, then who is this innocently bold slave?

His brows went arched before he knew it, and the blood on his robe made DRIP sounds as they dropped on the rocky ground.

"Then...am I... forgiven?" She spoke again, raising those lashes at him.

"You think forgiveness is that easy to buy?" He replied sharply.

"Then what do I have to do?" She asked, her stutters disappearing.

"Should I bring you some food? I can make you fruits salad." 

"I told you, I do not eat." He declared, point-blank before proceeding further.

"Moy Vladika!" Shayla went after him again, blocking his way.

"Do you want to die?" He glared.

"I know you don't have to eat to satisfy your hunger, but it's my way of saying sorry. I promise I'll make it moderate, you'll like it." She smiled, moving away from his path.

Ryker finally went into the tower, and the door closed on him.

Shayla left in a rush.

[Knights Quarters]

Cheyenne lay flat on the small treatment bed, and Sage stood beside her with a blue pill between his fingers.

He opened her mouth slightly, pushing the blue pill inside. 

Despite her unconscious state, Cheyenne swallowed.

"C'mon, c'mon." Sage muttered, watching her eyes and waiting for them to peer open.

In exactly one minute, her eyes opened, widely.

"Cheyenne!" Sage sat beside her quickly, smiling in relief.

Cheyenne sat up, recollecting what had happened before she passed out.

A smirk took over her face.

"Lord Ryker stroke me down cos of her? There was unity between the three of us, and now that she's suddenly here, she's trying to tear us apart? She's clearly seducing him!"

"Seducing who? Are you serious? He won't get drawn to anyone who's not his partner, you know it!" Sage cautioned.

"The why is he doing all these for her? Even hurting me for her?" Cheyenne flared up, showing him her wounded elbow.

She sustained the injury when Ryker made her land on the ground earlier.

"Look at it! Has it ever happened?"

"He's not doing anything for her! Calm down and think!" Sage said.

"He killed 12 people and their generations just because they bullied her! And you're telling me it's not for her? Make it make sense, Sage!" 

"He needed to kill as much as he could to unlock his second stage! Don't you get it? He needed to shed as much blood as possible! He might have killed them cos they bullied her, but trust me it's just an excuse. He has been waiting years for this, he just needed an opportunity, and it appeared the moment they bullied her! Calm down for once in your life!" Sage shouted.

Cheyenne released heavy breaths, looking him dead in the eyes.

"He's in the second stage already?"

"Damn it! Yes!" Sage said, looking totally frustrated.

"Fine, he killed them cos he needed to unlock second stage, but we both know he hates physical touch like mad! The only person he allows touches from is Granny, that's because her aura is pure, and he only even allows her touch once or twice a year. When you mistakenly touched him during the feast last year, tell me what happened?" Cheyenne demanded.

"He paralyzed my hands for two full days." Sage replied calmly.

"Correct! But earlier in front of the tower, he allowed that girl to touch him and...geez! I'm going crazy. I just don't feel good about her." Cheyenne held her head with an hand, pacing around the room.

"He's our Lord, you can't go against him. I'm warning you again Cheyenne. He knows what he's doing and you know his temper is fiery. I don't want him to kill you so lay low very well. As for why he allowed the slave girl to touch him, I'll investigate." Sage said.

"I don't care, all I know is that any girl that's not the partner is not allowed beside him. I won't allow him to fall into that slût's seduction!" Cheyenne barked, exiting the room.

Sage took a long deep breath, resuscitating his tight lungs.

"I can't with her anger issues."

[Ryker's Resting Domain]

Ryker changed into another outfit when he came back from the spring pool. Usually, he spends more time soaking inside the water, but today he only washed away the blood on his body and clothes.

No matter how much he loves blood on himself, he can't afford to get it on his treasure tower, so he got clean before coming here.

His robe material is open on his porcelain chest, and he just sat on his large throne-like chair, relaxing his mind and trying to  measure the extra abilities that comes with this second stage.

He tried mind travelling on his seat, and his mind's eye travelled beyond this city, extending it's sight to the human world.

Perfect.

His sense of smell is sharper, his teleporting ability got stronger too. He could feel his inner energy bubbling, and his eyesight grew clearer than before. Even his hearing ability broadened...

"Fruit Salad? Hmm! Looks delicious, who's it for?" 

"I'm making it for Lord Ryker. Not sure if he's going to eat it, but I'll make efforts at least...show him how sorry I am."

Those voices belongs to Granny and Shayla, and he's not even surprised.

But why always Shayla's voice? And she's really making fruits salad? 

It's been years since he ate, so he has even forgotten what food tastes like. It's as mundane as everything else about lowly humans.

Cheyenne and Sage entered the slumber, his eyes stayed on Sage only as he relaxed on his chair.

Cheyenne went on her knees quickly...

"It's always easy for you to get on your knees after going against me, and now seeing you on your knees angers me." Ryker spat.

"That would be the last time I'd be unreasonable. Also, I was only trying to shoo her away cos she looked like trouble. I'll stay off the matter from now on." Cheyenne apologized.

Ryker said nothing more, but he's surely counting her sins in his mind, awaiting the next time.

"Now that the second stage is attained, what's going to happen?" Sage asked.

"The news will spread to the West Palace, and Kasia's desire to get the FrostOrb will heighten." Ryker replied with high sureness.

"Then?" Cheyenne pressed forward.

Ryker smirked, "The rest will play out according to their poor tactics, we only have to wait. Also, they're not the only ones after the FrostOrb. There are several bloodlines who want the orb in this city and even in the human world. Get ready for more enemy attacks."

Sage and Cheyenne exchanged looks.

[Kings Residence, City]

Her back is on him, and her palms are folded at her sides. She has finished talking, and Kade is only trying to process what she just said.

"You covered her mark with a restriction spell, and you won't even tell me how the spell can be removed from her body?" He stood.

"I know you, Kade. If I tell you, you'll go ahead and do something stupid!" Liza faced him.

"The moment the mark appears on her body, Ryker would want to kill her, and you're the one person who wants her dead the most. Telling you how to remove the spell is like giving you the key to her death cos you'll find a way to tell Ryker!" Liza said.

"Oh...so it's about protecting her after all. How dare you even protect a girl you abandoned! The whole Luminaria only know me as your child cos you kept her birth a secret and I've been keeping it with you cos you're my mother anyways, but if you do not tell me how to remove the spell, I'll do something crazy." Kade threatened.

"You know better than to threaten me, Kade. You want me to lock you in the coffin again? You must have missed the smell of the sandalwood." Liza smiled, dusting imaginary dirt from his shirt.

"This time, I'll make sure you spend a year in the coffin before I wake you." She got serious, and Kade slapped her hand away from himself.

"You're the worse" he muttered, walking down the corridor.

"Where are you going!" Liza shouted.

"Ballet School! The dancers will be performing at the Feast, and I can't miss it. Gotta show everyone this handsome face!" He shouted back.

"You said you grew tired of dancing! You can't continue going to a school where Esme is the head teacher!" Liza ranted.

Kade turned back to face her, smiling with cruel intentions.

"Esme is your rival and the only woman you badly wanna win against, but I'll support her in this upcoming Feast. I'll make sure she wins the beauty crown again! That'll be your punishment for trying to protect your slave daughter!" 

He stepped away, and Liza bit the inside of her mouth.

"That scum of the earth." She mumbled, then a letter came flying with the air.

She took it and opened it. It's informing her about Ryker's 301 killings at Mount Zhitomir.

"301 people? Meaning he has attained the second stage. Now the struggle for his FrostOrb gets more intense. This year's Feast will be a whole level of different." She muttered.

[Black Swan Ballet School, Resting Haven]

The twins are sitting on the same bed, and they both look much better than they were before.

Their bleeding stopped immediately the rain stopped earlier, but they're still under the watchful eyes of professional alchemists.

Alchemists dominate this city instead of doctors. They're the drug manufactures, and they establish the magical pharmacies.

They were contacted to make elixirs and syrups for the twins.

"You should drink more of the medicine from that cup. Take the complete dose to avoid side effects." An alchemist said.

"You expect me to drink that bitter thing again? Are you trying to ruin my buds?" Sadie glared up.

"I was just being considerate of your health, Grand Princess." The alchemist apologized.

"Considerate of my health with a cup of medicine? Are you trying to say that's how weak we are?" Sadie stood.

"No! I didn't mean it that wa—"

Sadie already poured the cup of black medicine on his face before he could finish.

"I'm so sorry! I didn't mean to anger you!" The alchemist shoved his head down.

"Get out! All of you!" Soren ordered.

The alchemists exited the room, now it's just the twins inside.

Soren returned to his phone.

"Why have you been on your phone!" Sadie demanded.

"I'm texting the Old Chef. He needs to tell his daughter to come to this school. I need someone so kick." Soren replied.

Sadie sat beside him, placing an arm on his shoulder.

"Yunno the more I think about it, the more I get to know you're more wicked than me." 

"Thank you." Soren replied, and Sadie laughed.

"Peeps!!" Mercury screamed, rushing inside with unbalanced breaths.

"What the hell! Stop screaming like that!" Sadie spranged up.

"He came back to school! He's back!" Mercury announced.

"Who?" Soren stood too.

"Liza King's son!" Mercury said.

"Kade?" The twins chorused.

A minute later, they're already leaving the Haven.

When they arrived at the hallway, it's total chaos again.

Kade enjoys attention just like Soren, and the females spoil him with attention anytime he comes to school, just like now.

"Kade is back!!"

"OMG! I miss that face!"

"Kade King!!"

They're going bunkers for him, and he's grinning arrogantly as he entered, feeling like the owner of the world in his brown shades and studded outfit which costs a fortune.

Kade has a heavy crush on Sadie Millions, though Sadie is a cold-hearted devil who hates him more than anybody else. Sadie probably doesn't even have a heart.

"Words can't express how much I hate the guts of this animal. His crush on me is as disgusting as his face." She muttered.

"The fact that his mother is my mother's beauty rival, I boil ragingly whenever I see him." Mercury said.

"He's back to share the spotlight again, and he'll probably be the next one to die by my hands." Soren said hatefully.

"Share the spotlight? He has never had a chance. You remain the s3xiest guy in this school." Mercury replied, secretly checking Soren's face out.

Sadie saw that, but she kept quiet.

"Yo! I'm back." Kade removed his shades when he arrived at their faces.

"Back to die? I hear your mother locks you in a coffin anytime you make her angry. You might be entering the coffin for life if you don't learn how to stay away from me and my twin." Soren warned.

"You? I'm not interested in you. And yes, I was interested in Sadness, but not anymore. Her time is past." Kade laughed.

"What did you just call her? Sadness?" Mercury flew to him.

He almost attacked him, but Blaise joined the gathering.

"My redhead bud!" Kade grinned.

"You didn't tell me you'd be coming back!" Blaise smiled.

"It was meant to be a surprise, and it worked! I missed you!" Kade hund his arm on Blaise's shoulder, going away with him.

"Both losers, of course they'd be friends." Mercury scoffed.

"Good thing Kade is back. I'll just kill him and Blaise at once. Mercury, get me some of your dead father's ashes." Sadie said.

"What for?" Mercury blinked.

"Poisoning, what else?" Soren rolled eyes.

"Oh...of course." Mercury shrugged.

They're standing in front of the bulletin board, so they stepped aside when Esme arrived with some other ballet teachers.

Notice about the upcoming Feast was pasted, and everyone began checking it out. Names of the dancers that are gonna dance got picked out, and as expected, Mercury and the twins top the list.

"Wait...mother! Only one person will take the center this time? What kind of weird formation is that!" Mercury questioned.

Esme faced her.

"You and the twins have been occupying the center of the dance floor for years. We need a change this year. Only one person will occupy the center and stand under the spotlight. That one person will be chosen after several practices." Esme sternly said.

"And what's this second poster about? You're gonna be taking in new dancers through auditions starting from next week?" Sadie came forward with a smirk.

"Yeah. People already know the talents of everyone in this school. We need new talents and new faces. This year's Feast won't be the usuals, and Grand Supreme has ordered the best ballet dance performance so I can't disappoint." Esme replied flatly and left with the other teachers.

"Your mother is growing annoying...these days." Soren muttered.

"Yunno what? Sadie... I'll slow down my dance during the practice so you can be picked to be at the center. Trust me." Mercury said.

"Don't follow me, I'm mad at you and your mother right now." Sadie rolled eyes and left.

"Soren!" Mercury held his shirt.

"You can't be mad at me too! My mother made the announcement, not me!" She said.

But the disregard in Soren's eyes made her quickly let go of his shirt.

Soren left, and she stumped her foot on the ground.

"I hate when they're both angry!" 

"Hey, is Grand Prince Soren with you?" A calm voice made her turn.

She was creeped out to see it's Xena, looking as homeless as ever in her faded clothes.

"Eew! How did you even get into this school environment?" She poured saliva on Xena's face.

"Grand Prince Soren ordered me to come. Said he wants to kick me." Xena said, not bothering to wipe the saliva.

"Soren texted you to come? Or you came just to see his face? Wait...do you like him by chance?" Mercury drew closer.

"That's impossible, I'm here following his orders." Xena replied.

"Then go back! Leave this school right now! Soren wouldn't wanna see dirt like you!" Mercury pointed at the door.

"But he's going to punish me if I don't show myself to him." Xena said.

"I said go!" Mercury pushed her.

Xena staggered to the wall, and her eyes caught the posters briefly before she left the school.

"Ugly thing. Wanna see Soren? Sounds like a curse." Mercury rumbled as she left too.

[School Coffee Bay]

Blaise and Kane dropped the cups of warm coffee from their lips at the same time, their faces clashing.

"So...how has school been without me?" Kade sighed.

"Boring. But the girls missed you. You're their food." Blaise winked, sweeping his red hair back with his palm.

"I heard the gist about how you blinded Sadie's one eye and was almost killed by thunder but Ryker repelled the thunders for you? Do you now see why I say I love that man? I wanna be like him. He even killed 301 today, I'm in love with his brutality!" Kade laughed.

"301? Where?" Blaise gasped.

Immediately she sat, Blaise stood.

[West Palace]

She's in the lobby with Winona and Astor.

[East Palace, Slaves Kitchen]

Xena had to borrow her father's phone, and though the phone is already malfunctioning cos it's slightly broken on the screen, she still managed to use it.

She can't miss this opportunity.

She saw the audition website name on the school poster, and she has to make a move before the spots gets filled.

She typed the ballet website name on the phone, waiting for it to load for minutes.

When she was finally able to enter the website, she was overjoyed.

She applied for auditions for her and Shayla, but she used different secret names though. If she uses their names, they'd be caught.

When she got to the pictures column, she just downloaded random pictures from the human web and inserted them on the columns.

She clicked submit on the two documents, and she folded her palm on the phone.

"Yes!"

She's gonna tell Shayla about it tonight.

"Xena, you didn't take dinner to the basement yet." Old Chef said from outside.

"I'm coming!" She rushed out of the kitchen.

[Soul Hall, 7PM]

Azura and Zeke are walking the corridor behind the hall, and their mouths are running as they discussed the same thing...

Ryker.

"Did he actually got seduced by a female who's not his partner?"

Lilith is already standing beside her bed. She's fully awake.

"Welcome back, messenger of death." Zeke smiled.

[Granny's Chamber]

Jojo sat in front of the mirror again, trying to speak to her shadow.

And it's working. Her eyes are widely open, and she's smiling as her shadow self stated back at her as a reflection.

Finally after several trials, she got this ability back.

"Been a while, shadow self." She waved shortly.

"You didn't die?" The shadow asked from the mirror.

"If I died, you'd be dead too... dunce." Jojo laughed. "I need new techniques, and you better teach me everything you know."

Shayla came out of the inner chamber, wearing another one of Jojo's youth skirts.

This one is long free yard which covered all her legs except her flat shoes. It's color black but with white stripes at the base.

Her button blouse is slightly cropped, so her extra slim waist is showing.

She's carrying the small cooler of fruits salad which she had made in the morning. 

She went to the the tower to deliver it, but she knocked several times and it wasn't open, so she brought it back here and kept it in the freezer to maintain the coldness.

Now she's just gonna give it to Xena to eat so it won't turn sour before tomorrow morning.

Since Jojo is busy in front of the mirror, she left the chamber without saying goodbye.

She's gonna be meeting Xena at the bleakly-lit corridor. Same corridor where she mimicked Ryker.

Xena has promised to teach her more of the language.

On getting there, Xena isn't there yet, so she set the cooler of fruits salad on the open windowsill, sighing.

"I knew it, she's always the late one." 

She sat on the windowsill too, holding the cooler to her side.

"Shayla?" Jude appeared in the corridor, and she quickly stood.

"Grand Prince...

She tried to bow, but just like the other time, he stopped her.

"Don't bow for me, it's irksome." 

"But I can be punished if the Grand Supreme or the twins sees me talking casually to you." She replied carefully.

"None of them are here, so talk as casually as you want." Jude insisted.

"I can't do that, ...sir." Her face went down.

"Why? Do I look untouchable?" Jude came closer, and she shifted back.

"I want to live long, and I don't want complications." She said.

"You're doing that running away thing again. I was starting to think you're you allergic touches, but I saw you placing your hand on Ryker's palm when you teleported with him, so the problem must be me. Do you hate me?" Jude questioned, eyes looking hurtful and needy again.

"No... Why would I hate you? You healed me, and I'm still grateful for that." Shayla muttered.

"Then why can't I touch you?" Jude went closer again.

Again, Shayla shifted back, but he grabbed her two arms, shoving her closer to himself.

The fruits salad cooler nearly fell from her, but she held tight, trying to break free from his hold.

"Can you... please let...go?"

"I don't want to." He replied, and she hastily looked up at him. 

The moment their eyes met, his eyes squinted suddenly like he's in pains, and he let go of her arms with difficulty.

"Grand Prince?" She blinked.

He squinted his eyes deeper, grabbing his head tight.

"Grand Prince?" She repeated, and he stepped back, groaning.

"It's headache, I think." He sniffled, water forcefully crawling out of his eye corners.

"Headache? Out of the blues?" Shayla said, not understanding.

"Fûck it!" Jude groaned again, his head banging in a loop.

He staggered out of that corridor, leaving Shayla all alone.

"That was... strange." She muttered, adjusting the cooler stes holding.

She made to place it back on the windowsill, but then she saw him, standing there on the same spot he stood that night...

Ryker.

The bleak lights didn't do justice to his face, but it's unmistakable.

"Moy Vladika?" She blinked.

"Does that sound better in your ears?" He deeply talked, coming closer with cold steps.

"If you don't like it, I'll go back to calling you My Lord." She replied when he got to her.

His eyes dropped on the cooler she's holding, and she noticed it.

"Oh...um...I came to your tower with it, but no one opened the door. It's still fresh though." She said, opening the cooler to reveal the fruity meal. 

It smells temptingly good too, and there's a really small fork with just two tines.

"Is that your idea of an adult fork?" He said, glancing boringly at the fork.

"I can get you a bigger fork." she quickly said, but he already took the fork before she could move.

He stabbed the fruit salad with it, taking from it, and without waiting, he brought it to his mouth.

Shayla gasped inwardly in shock. 'He's eating it!'
