★

Ryker chewed slowly as if savoring the taste, trying to let his gums feel every succulence and trying to let the buds feel every taste.

Shayla couldn't blink as she awaited what he has to say, but he didn't say anything before taking another forkful to his mouth, chewing just as slowly as he chewed the first one.

"How's it, Moy Vladika?" She asked when she couldn't wait for the review any longer.

He ate the third forkful without replying her, and she smiled. Maybe that's enough to answer her.

He ate three full forks from her meal? She'd relive this moment over and over in her head.

But it didn't end in three forkfuls. He ate more, and she lost count of it. All she could do is hold the cooler and watch him eat as perfectly as every of his moves.

Is he supposed to eat this perfectly too? And oh...

It's the first time she'd actually be seeing him up close without blood on his face, so it's different in a weird kind of way.

He dropped the fork when he ate half of the meal, and she felt satisfied. At least he ate.

The teacher at the culinary school said if half of your food gets eaten, then it's a proof that you're more than a average chef.

She covered the remaining food, then dropped the cooler on the windowsill.

"I'll get you some water" she suggested, but by the time she looked back at him, he was already gone.

"That fast? I mean...he needs water to wash it down," She mumbled. "Maybe he's gonna drink when he gets to his tower."

She shrugged and opened the cooler again, then she digged into the fruits, eating with the same fork he used.

"Of course he'd eat half, It's so delicious." She smiled, licking salad cream from the fork.

"What are you eating without me!" Xena finally came.

"After three long years?" Shayla exaggerated.

"Oh please...i was only late for ren minutes." Xena rolled eyes, snatching the cooler from her.

She began eating fast, filling her mouth with the fruits.

"Hmm! This is sweet. You made it yourself?" She said as she filled her mouth for the second time.

"Of course. Should you even be surprised? I learnt cooking." Shayla shrugged.

"It wasn't even up to 24 hours learning. You went at night and came back the next morning, and you learnt these dishes? I'm seriously scared of your brain." Xena sighed.

"I'm just gifted" Shayla shrugged again.

"But what if your Luminarian parent ability suppressed your human parent? What if your retentive memory is your own power?" Xena suddenly said.

"Impossible." Shayla said immediately.

"Why? If Lilith can be a rare breed, then you can be one too." Xena insisted.

"I'm not that lucky, Xena. I'm always the unlucky one." Shayla said.

"Fine, we won't talk about your family anymore. Do you even know how far a banshee's power can go?" Xena asked.

By now, she has finished the salad, so she dropped the cooler.

"I only know their screams are dangerous and can kill. Isn't that why she was able to fall trees with them? Also, they can foresee deaths and they wail whenever someone's death is drawing near." Shayla explained.

"Good. But there's only such power limits in the human world. Here in Luminaria, banshees can't only foresee deaths and scream dangerously, they can also mark people for death, and that's why they're treasured here. Once she marks you, you die mysteriously." Xena further explained.

"Does that mean Lilith is like a walking death waiting to kill right now?" Shayla said, sudden fear gripping her.

"But we'll just be careful, let's stay away from her when she wakes." Xena said.

Shayla nodded, wrapping her arms around Xena. She still can't shake away her fear.

"You're so warm" Xena smiled.

"What about you? What's your power? Back then when I was hurt, you said your powers can't heal, so you have powers definitely." Shayla asked.

Xena looked at her, smiled before looking around. She nodded when she saw a small lizard on the wall close to the ceiling.

"Come here, friend." She said gently.

The lizard crawled down the wall, then it crawled up Xena's clothes till it came to stay at the back of her palm.

"You can speak to animals?" Shayla gasped, and Xena nodded.

"That's so cool!" Shayla clapped twice, and Xena chuckled.

"Not a big deal, i bet there's gonna be a family meeting tonight, so we better stay here till 10. Their family meetings are always chaotic. Last meeting, Sadie threw a cleaning maid outta the castle window." Xena said.

"Is she always angry?" Shayla blinked.

"She's even angry when she's asleep. Somehow, I feel like Soren is still better. At least he cools down once a year." Xena said, and Shayla laughed.

"Once a year?"

"Well I have a news, and don't scream. Don't scold me. Don't do anything. Just listen." Xena cleared her throat.

"Yes?" Shayla said expectantly.

"I applied for the two of us. The ballet school will be taking in new dancers and I happened to see it. So I applied. Final audition happens next week, but first and second audition happens this week. In fact, it's starting tomorrow."

"What!! Xena! You're lying." Shayla held the pillar, stepping away.

"I'm real. I've been watching them practice for years and there's no move I dunno. Also, you only watched once and you already know that much, so we're qualified. We only have to wear masks and look untouchable whenever we're going. I have permit to leave the palace, and you can just ask Granny to help you with a permit too. C'mon, you said you wanted to be a ballerina too. Are you giving up already?" Xena persuaded.

"You could have told me before applying for me. What if Lord Ryker gets angry? What if Granny doesn't like it?" Shayla said worriedly.

"I'll take all the blames if any of them have a problem with it. Trust me and just prepare." Xena assured.

--

[Heartless Tower]

Ryker stood beside a tall treasure chest in his Domain, eyes staring into space but lips still fresh with the taste and sensations of the fruits.

His arms are folded behind him, and he had listened to her conversation with Xena.

Not like he wanted to listen, but his ears just wouldn't stop bringing her voice. And he actually ate the fruits salad? Surprisingly, he only got back on his usual high horse after he ate half of the fruits.

And that's because even as he stood beside her without touches, he was still feeling heat.

It might not be as much as when touches are involved, but at least it was still warm, and it was soothing.

Whoever she is is not an issue to be left flat anymore. It's hightime he digs, and that's gonna start tomorrow.

He needs to travel.

"Sage" he coolly called, and sage rushed into the Domain.

"My Lord!"

"Contact Eternus in the clouds castle, and tell him I'll be coming for a visit tomorrow. Not a quick one, I might take days before leaving his residence." 

"Sudden travel? Why?" Sage asked respectfully.

"I have questions, and I need full pages of answers." Ryker replied stiffly.

"Understood. I'll make the call." Sage said and left,but Cheyenne entered almost immediately.

"Family meeting, you're required to be at the main castle." She reported.

"Lead the way" Ryker said as if he has been expecting this.

Cheyenne obeyed, and he followed.

--

[Soul Hall]

"I was seriously ready for death, didn't expect to be alive at all." Lilith said, sounding stronger than before.

She's sitting back in bed, and there's a small bowl of medicine which she's drinking from.

"And I was saved by Grand Supreme and Madam Azura?" She looked up at the two.

"I still don't understand."

"You know what you are, Lilith. And you know how much power you carry. We need you, and you can be useful in many ways." Azura smiled.

"But I'm a halfbreed, aren't I supposed to be a slave?" Lilith dropped the medicine plate.

"You'll stop being a slave from now on. Your sufferings is ending as we speak. We can adopt you to be a part of the Millions Family if you want, and you'll become a proper member of this East Palace." Zeke said.

"A member of the family?" Lilith got up with a gasp and dreamy eyes.

"Yes. You work for us, we cater for you. Quid pro quo." Azura assured.

"Agreed, I'll because a part of the family and work for you, but I also have a request." Lilith said.

"And it's?" Zeke asked.

"I'm aware that Lord Ryker can't get attached to anyone that's not his partner, and I know I can't ever and will never be the partner, but can I at least be his second wife? I'll occupy the space till his partner arrives. He doesn't have to like me, I just want the title of his wife." Lilith said.

The couple exchanged glances, and Lilith's head went angled.

"What? You both promised you'd do anything."

"It's done. We'll plan the wedding as soon as possible, but he assured that you're the only one who's gonna attend the wedding." Zeke said.

"I know that, I just want his real last name Morozov." Lilith smiled.

"And you'll get that." Azura smiled.

Zeke received a text, and he pulled his phone out of his pocket, then he went outside to read.

--

[Main Castle]

Family meetings happen at the royal dinning table, and every member is on seat already, including Ryker who looks like he's the maker of everyone of them with his poker egocentric face.

Jude is present too, and his headache is much better now, though it's still lingering.

Granny Jojo is sitting not far from Ryker, and she looks calm but stern, her hat sitting in the middle of her grey hair.

The twins and the parents are at the left side of the dinning table, and they're all glaring at the right side.

Everyone is eating except granny and Ryker who're only here to listen to the next gibberish.

"301 citizens are gone, and it's only because twelve of them bullied your 150 billion slaves?" Zeke said, cutting his steak while raising an eyebrow at Ryker.

"I killed them cos I wanted to, bit it's true that their bullying was the needed excuse. And I'm not done killing. There are still two members of their generations in the human world, and I plan to hunt them down and kill them too to complete the number...303." Ryker replied.

"Gutsy, and it's annoying." Soren muttered.

"You shouldn't be talking here right now, considering the fact that you and your twin only joined the ballet school because you both are too dull to learn how to read and write. Family meetings shouldn't include dullards." Ryker flatly replied.

Sadie stood, picked a glass of water and poured it at his face, but the water froze before it could even get to his face. The ice cubes landed on the table.

"Shut up!! You aren't even a part of this family you bastard! If there's anyone who shouldn't even be here in this meeting at all, it's you!" Sadie pounded her palms on the table angrily.

"Seems like I hit home." Ryker muttered. "Sit down, Sadie."

"You fuckin—"

"Down." Ryker interrupted her, and her butt landed on the chair.

Soren and Jude gasped.

Even Azura and Zeke.

"Sadie actually...sat?" Granny Jojo said shockingly.

"Different new abilities comes with this stage 2. So far you absorb from my FrostOrb power, you're bound to obey whatever I say." Ryker said.

Sadie stood, staring at Ryker with wide eyes before running upstairs.

Soren was looking at Ryker in awe as he went after Soren, leaving give people at the table.

"Seems this meeting has been pointless from the beginning, I'll take my leave now." Ryker said and stood, walking out with Cheyenne who's by the door.

"I hope things like this will keep happening. Someone really needs to put everyone in their place, don't you think?" Granny Jojo stood, smiling as she took her leave too.

Jude remained there, thinking...

Does this mean his headache earlier was because of Ryker?

Did Ryker give him that?

Why??

He has always treated Ryker like a true brother, so why??

He stood and left the dinning too.

"I was wondering why he came to the meeting without rejecting, so this is his plan?" Azura stood.

"You said this stage would mean nothing to us, but he's already commanding my babies?"

Zeke just sat there, looking too shocked.

"Zeke!!" Azura yelled.

--

"This stage 2 comes with command-obey ability? Then you could have used it on Zeke earlier. He'd have told you where he kept Xena's lifeline." Cheyenne said as she made her way back to the Heartless Tower with Ryker.

"It doesn't work that way, Cheyenne." He faced her.

"Why?" She asked.

"Azura and Zeke don't only draw powers from my orb, but they also suck souls, and that's enough to strengthen their minds." Ryker explained.

"Oh...I get it." Cheyenne nodded. "Of course the ability won't work on them. They're smart after all."

"Ryker" Granny Jojo joined them, and Cheyenne stepped back.

"You look like you want to tell me something." Jojo said suspiciously.

"I'll be on a trip to the Cloud Castle tomorrow. Not sure when I'll be coming back." Ryker replied.

"Then I'll hope for your quick comeback." Jojo smiled.

Behind them, Cheyenne smiled too, knowing an opportunity just presented itself.

--

[Twins Room]

Sadie has thrown everything she could see across the room, and they're all broken.

Soren isn't even ready to start calming her down cos he's still in shock too.

"That bastard has control over us now? Why!!" Sadie yelled, looking madly deranged.

"Don't overstress, it's a condition that can be mended with just one talk with mother and father." Soren said from the door where he leaned casually. Even the dinner aftertaste is sour in his mouth.

"I don't care if there's a solution to it, but the fact that he made me sit in front of Granny and Jude? I don't wanna think about it!" Sadie threw another antique across the room, nearly breaking Soren's head with it.

"I'll go have a talk with mother and father. Try to calm down before I return." He said, going out of the room and descending the staircase.

Thinking the parents would still be at the dinning room, he went there, but they're not there anymore.

Instead, he met Xena who's packing the dinner plates for washing.

Her head quickly went down, but he just ignored her since kicking her is not even appealing to him right now.

He was already leaving when he suddenly stopped, turning around.

Xena froze, the tray she's holding shook in her hands, and the used plates on it clattered.

"I ordered you to come to the school earlier." He questioned.

"I came, I swear on my life. I did! But Mercury told me to go back. She said you wouldn't want to see me!" Xena quickly said.

"Mercury?" Soren frowned.

"Yes! I met her at the hallway!" Xena swallowed.

Soren rolled eyes, the intensity showing how annoyed he is, then he went back upstairs.

Xena dropped the tray, sitting on the ground and holding her chest.

"Damn! I wasn't breathing!"

--

[Next Morning, West Palace]

Everyone is preparing to go to one place or the other, so everyone is dressing up for their appointments.

Astor and Kasia are preparing to go meet Granny Jojo at the conference.

Winona is preparing to meet Jude and try to sweettalk him.

Faye and Blaise are preparing for school, and Kade is preparing with them cos he came home with them last night. He spent the night here.

He came out of Blaise's room with him, and at the same time Faye came out of her own room.

The rooms are right beside each other actually.

They all look gothic in their black outfits which attached to even their earrings and necklaces.

Blaise gave Faye the usual look of disapproval, then he made to leave, but Kade pulled him back.

"Come back here, bud. The practice for the Feast starts today, and auditions starts today too. You both should at least mark today as the first day you'll get along. I can't stand opposing siblings." He said.

"We're not siblings." Faye and Blaise chorused, their hate shimmering.

"Faye! Let's go!" Blaise hollered, and his dog sauntered out of the room, following him downstairs.

"Scum..." Faye muttered, masking her hurtfulness.

"We can be besties instead. Let's leave Blaise the redhead to his antics." Kade said, placing an arm over her shoulder.

"Aren't you just the same as him?" Faye rolled eyes, pushing his arm away.

"At least I don't hate you," he chuckled, and she scoffed, going downstairs too.

Kade followed them with a run.

"Faye, Blaise...is it possible for you both to try to get along with the twins?" Kasia asked when they got downstairs.

"Don't even think about it" Blaise glared, drinking from a cup of milk and taking from the toasted bread before leaving.

Kade did the same, then he took a bite from the bread...

"Getting closer to a cluster of nails us better than the twins. Sadie on her own deserves to be in what humans call psychiatric hospital." He said, then waved before going after Blaise.

"He's interesting" Astor grinned on his seat.

"You guys still aren't over your obsession about taking the city from the Millions?" Faye shook her head.

"Just know that I don't wanna die. Whatever you're gonna do, have me excluded." She added.

She didn't bother to take from the breakfast, but Winona took some and dropped it in her backpack.

"Eat them in school." She smiled.

"Thank you. You're the only reason I'm still in this house." Faye smiled back, giving her a peck before leaving.

"Isn't it weird that your daughter loves my daughter but not me?" Kasia faces Astor.

"Double standards?" Astor smiled.

"Are y'all done dressing up? The conference is starting soon." Winona alerted.

"More than ready." Astor stood, smoothening his suit.

Kasia readjusted her shoes by slamming them on the polished ground, then they left the house together.

Winona was the last to leave.

--

[East Palace, Park]

The twins threw their backpacks into the car, both looking sullen.

They didn't eat breakfast, and their parents already assured that there'll be solution to the problem on ground before they return from school, but they're both still unable to think.

Sadie hopped in the car, looking more hateful than usual.

Soren nearly followed her, but his phone buzzed. 

It's a text from Mercury.

Mercury: I'm already in school, and I brought my father's ashes like Sadie requested. Kade is surely dying.

Soren showed the text to Sadie before getting in the car with her.

"Why would she text you and not me? I'm the one who told her to bring it. That snake." Sadie muttered with irritation.

"Ask her when you get to school." Soren said as the car rolled out of the palace.

Some minutes after they left, Granny Jojo arrived at the park with some maids, and she's dressed for the conference.

She left in her car too, easily slipping out of the palace with her driver.

Shayla emerged from the chamber, dressed in a different outfit.

This one is a black gown which covered her whole body cos it's long sleeved and free, and her hair is thoroughly washed. She used some of Jojo's shine cream on it, so it looks better than the normal greasy state.

She asked Jojo for a permit last night, and Jojo gave it to her without even arguing or asking what she needs it for, and she's still thinking why.

The permit only allows her to leave the palace for two hours though, so she has just two hours to spend at the ballet school.

According to granny, Ryker travelled, and he left with only Sage, so she has to be careful to not cross paths with hot tempered Cheyenne or Lilith the banshee.

Immediately she thought that, she sighted Lilith not far from the main castle, and she quickly hid behind a pillar.

Thanks to the pillar again.

Her heart was skipping beats as she stayed there, counting the minutes to calculate just how much longer she has to hide.

She came out at two minutes, and she ran the remaining meter to the slaves kitchen where she met Xena looking like she's in a dilemma.

She's dressed too, but there's a tray of food in front of her. Also, there's a tray of capsules.

"Aren't we going anymore?" Shayla asked.

"We're going, but my father is slightly sick, and I need to give him his medications before leaving. At the same time, I need to take food to the basement." Xena explained.

"Basement?" Shayla said in confusion.

"Yunno what? Take the food. Take the side door beside the castle second staircase. Go up the staircase or use the lift. Open the door you see and go down the basement. Just drop this tray of food there, don't open the basement door. I'd have finished administering the medications when you come back, then we can go." Xena explained in a hurry, then she took the capsule tray and left the kitchen in a hurry.

Shayla took the food tray too. If that's all she's able to help with, then why not, but who the hell lives in a basement?

She went into the castle using the second staircase like Xena described. She didn't use the lift, she climbed by herself till she arrived and opened the door that leads to the basement.

She went down the short flight of stairs and blinked when she saw the door she met.

The place is silent except for the Granny sounds of breathings that's coming from inside that room.

Shayla squatted, dropping the tray below the door as instructed.

A rough blistered hand stretched out from under the door, dragging the tray inside the room.

"Ahh!!" Shayla shuddered, falling back on her butt and shifting away from the door with wide eyes.

"What...what was...that?"

She stood as fast as she fell, and she was already going upstairs when she suddenly stopped, looking back again.

Who was that?

The question hung in her chest as she left the staircase and went back to the door, placing her ear on the wood to listen to the sound of breathings.

Her weight was resting too much on the door, and she nearly screamed when the door opened and she fell inside the darkness.

She threw her palm against her mouth, muffling her own sounds.

The lights in the room came on, bright and white.

A young girl sat in the corner of the room, looking like the most miserable person existing.

A white cloth is tied over her eyes, and despite that fact, her misery and sufferings weren't covered.

She's definitely blind, but why is she here? In this dark cold room that smells like hell?

The food tray is with her, and she's currently drinking from the water.

Shayla stood quickly, going for the door and deciding to leave her mind questions unanswered.

"Thanks for making me feel warmth for the first time in years. Your presence is soothingly warm." The voice of the girl came, and that was enough to stop Shayla.

She turned back, not hiding her fear since she still doesn't understand.

"Who're...you?" She asked breathlessly, sweating.

"Pearl." The girl said.

"Pearl?" Shayla whispered.

"Pearl Millions, I'm the last child of the Millions family, but I was born blind, so I'm a sign of weakness. I've been locked up since birth."

CHAPTER 23&24

THE REQUIREMENTS WENT HIGHER. WE'RE IN CHAPTER 24, SO WHY NOT?

COMPLETE IT BEFORE 7PM TOMORROW IF YOU REALLY WANT THIS BOOK TO CONTINUE.

CHECK IT SO YOU'LL KNOW HOW TO REPLY ALL MY COMMENTS, DROP YOUR PERSONAL COMMENTS, LIKE AND SHARE.

I MADE THIS CHAPTER LONG, SO THERE'S A LOT TO TALK ABOUT, THE BOOK IS JUST STARTING FROM HERE.

COMMENT SOMETHING LONG AND SWEET TO GEAR MY SPIRIT, OR YOU'LL SERIOUSLY GET BANNED.

★

The Millions Family are heartless folks. Even if some are better than the rest, their heartless side is never completely buried.

Shayla has spent her few weeks here learning that, but she never expected it to stretch to this level.

As she stood glued by the door of the basement, staring at the blind helpless girl in the corner of the room, her heart broke for her. 

If she's an Eternal like the rest of the family, she must have been trapped in this dusty, rusty, cold room for hundreds of years.

The hell?

"But... there's the FrostOrb. They could have used it to open your eyes. I heard the orb can do anything." She spoke up softly, making sure to keep it in a low hush.

Pearl dropped the bottle of water she's holding, then she opened the plate of food and took from the pie.

It's pie and custard? That's what they serve maids, servants and slaves here.

So they consider Pearl a servant?

Shayla couldn't even talk, she just watched with pity as the pretty girl took a large bite from her pie.

With the way she's smoothly taking the pie and the water from the tray, it's hard to detect her blindness.

"Yeah...the legendary FrostOrb can do anything, but not my eyes. My blindness is a curse I inherited from my mother's womb, so it'll only leave when the curse lifts on it's own." She explained.

"I don't understand." Shayla's face lined in a hard squeeze.

"I was supposed to be triplets with the twins, but they both came out before me. They escaped mother's womb on the same day, and I stayed in the womb for one extra year before mother was able to birth me. That extra year I spent is my curse." Pearl explained.

"I'm so sorry..." Shayla crouched, her pity for this female increasing.

"It's nothing." Pearl took another bite from her pie, smiling. "At least you came, and I got to feel warmth for the first time in years."

"People often say I'm warm." Shayla smiled a little.

"Of course you are...I guess you're special. What's your name?" Pearl asked, smiling too.

"Shayla, a slave." Shayla introduced.

"Oh... Who was my food deliver before? She has never entered." Pearl asked, drinking water again.

"Xena. She's my friend, the Old Chef's daughter, and Lord Ryker's sister." Shayla cleared.

"Lord Ryker...How handsome is he?" Pearl asked, and Shayla blinked. 

She didn't expect that one.

"I might have been trapped here all my life, but the cleaner maids pass by the top of this basement, and I hear their whisperings. He was forced to be a part of this family with his sister's lifeline, but despite that...he's the most feared demigod here, also the most goodlooking." Pearl smiled, her admiration so obvious.

"Yes... right." Shayla stood, sounding awkward.

"Have you seen him up close before? What's the most beautiful part of his body?" Pearl asked another weird question.

Shayla frowned, snapping out of it and remembering she's not even supposed to be inside this basement in the first place.

Xena specifically told her not to enter. What was she thinking?

"I need to go now." She stepped back, her shoes marching the broken concretes on the floor.

"You came here and gave me warmth, but now that I ask a simple question, you're running away? You really don't know what your answer would do for me..." Pearl maintained the smile.

'Isn't she smiling too much?' Shayla thought. It's odd for someone who has been suffering since birth.

But then, that soft side of her refused to heed to that suspicion, so she said what's on her mind.

"His hands... Fingers." 

"Hands?" Pearl repeated.

"Lord Ryker's fingers are the most beautiful part of his body. His hands as a whole, they're attractively perfect, and his skin..." Shayla rushed, opening the door to get out...

"An advise for you, Shayla." Pearl said suddenly, and Shayla turned. 

Pearl is still smiling.

"Never trust any member of the Millions Family. Never." She said. 

Shayla didn't miss the foreboding undertone of that statement.

"Shay!" Xena's voice rang from the basement top, and with thrumming chest, she left the room and slammed the door shut.

A minute after she was gone, Pearl dropped the bottled water back in the tray, her hand folding for a moment before she slid it behind her head to untangle the rope of her eye cover.

She took the cover away from her eyes, and an obsessive smile appeared on her lips.

Shayla might have closed the door when she was leaving, but right now, the door is open again.

--

"What took you so long? I was getting worried!" Xena held Shayla when she climbed up the staircase.

"The place looked stuffy and strange, so I was awed." Shayla lied.

She regretted it, but what would Xena think if she tells her she actually entered the room?

"Tell me, you didn't enter the room, right?" Xena asked, and Shayla gave her lip a bite on the inside.

"Why did I even ask that?" Xena grinned suddenly. "Of course you can't enter. That door has been sealed by Azura for years, and no one can remove the seal except her. I dunno who's inside, but it's a big secret they're keeping with their lives." 

"You don't know who's inside?" Shayla gulped.

They're already walking down the side stairs which leads outside.

"How would i know when I've never entered the room? But I know someone is inside." Xena shrugged.

And that only heightened Shayla's fear. Does that mean she opened the door? How?

If Azura sealed it for years, then how was she able to open it?

She was still on the wheel of thoughts when she was hauled to a solid pillar by strong, unyielding hands.

A gasp tore out of her throat, and that was enough to snap her out of her thoughts as her eyes clashed with Cheyenne's.

"Cheyenne?" She muttered.

"Don't call my name with your unworthy mouth, you slût!" Cheyenne spat, raising her hand. 

Her eyes are glaring evidences that she has not even the slightest likeness for Shayla. The hate is bloody.

"Cheyenne!" Xena shouted behind, but it's like a dog's bark to her. She wouldn't care.

She brought her hand down to Shayla's face, and Shayla closed her eyes quickly, expecting the nails.

But they never came. 

Cheyenne's nails stopped a little before it'd get to her face, and her eyes are slightly wide.

She has touched Shayla once when she tore her with the nails at Granny's corridor, but maybe her anger back then didn't make her feel what she's feeling right now—her warmness.

She could feel it as she held her arm, and that made her stop. 

Slowly, she let go.

Xena quickly pulled Shayla to herself.

"If you had dared to hurt her again, I'd have begged my brother to bash you to the dust even if it means kneeling in front of his tower for months. He might be a Millions by force, but he's a Morozov by birth, and you're nothing but his knight who has no last name." She said angrily.

Cheyenne stayed still, her awed eyes still on Shayla.

"You can hurt me all you want, but stop calling me a slût at any chance you get. I'm not lusting after Lord Ryker, I wouldn't dare. I'm just a lowly slave who was given a chance." Shayla said, then she left with Xena.

Cheyenne stood there, confused and shocked as her eyes drooped.

"She's definitely not the partner. If it's her, Lord Ryker would have told I and Sage, so who's she? Why this rare warmness?" She muttered.

Answers didn't appear, so she sighed, calming down.

--

[Clouds Castle]

It's a golden building which sat somewhere in the deepest clouds of Luminaria, and there's no rival to it's beauty. It stood there alone, and it shone alone, day and night.

The gold is rimmed with white at some few points, but not enough to dim the golden rays which reached the roof and floors and even the olive branches surrounding it.

There are fog fountains around, and they're pouring pureness into the heavenly harem

Eternus is a seer, the oldest and wisest in Luminaria. He sees into the future and peers into the past with one quick glance. 

He's got long white hair and calm, pure presence. Always in a flowing white robe and a peaceful smile.

He stays alone in the castle, and that's why he came to open the entrance door by himself when Ryker arrived with Sage.

"Look who finally paid a visit after about 100 years." He said, his smile not breaking. His white hair fell in two long gentle curls on his shoulders.

"105 years." Ryker corrected, stepping inside the castle. His black robe is a contract to Eternus' white one.

The door seemed to embrace him as it closed behind him and Sage.

"Saying the wrong number was on purpose. I know you have an obsession with exact numbers. You didn't change." Eternus replied, walking beside him.

Sage is behind, looking around the unending richness. 

Eternus isn't on Ryker's level of rich, but he likes showcasing his riches just like Ryker, so his harem is vainglorious.

"If Ryker isn't Ryker anymore, then who'd blow Luminaria out of existence when it's time?" Ryker replied, giving him a glance.

"Of course, that's what you'd say." Eternus tilted his head.

They entered a room where a small plum garden is, and they sat in the center of the trees, on thick glass chairs with the desk between them.

Sage left them to go arrange a room for their night rest.

"Why the sudden visit? You didn't give me a heads-up this time." Eternus asked.

"There's a female," Ryker started. Eternus leaned in, eyes wide.

"Your partner finally?"

"She has the warmness, but not the mark." Ryker explained.

Eternus pulled back on the table. "Not the mark? Then she's not. Maybe she was just gifted with the warmness." 

"I ate her meal, I held her, I can hear her voice even when I don't want to. I have never saved anyone...my nature only kills but I saved her from the banshee!" Ryker's voice went up.

"Oh..." Eternus leaned in again. "I heard of how you killed generations too, cos they bullied her?"

"Cos I needed to attain the second stage. Don't twist this." Ryker rolled eyes shortly.

Eternus sighed.

"Her mother Liza is still alive. She wasn't killed like other rule-breakers cos the human who impregnated her did it with force."

"You made your findings? Good." Ryker pressed his back softly to the backrest.

"She's not your partner..." Eternus repeated, getting serious.

"And it's not like anyone hid her mark from me on purpose?" Ryker raised his lashes.

"Liza's powers were stripped from her the moment she got raped by a human, so there's no way she hid the mark. Even if she did, do you really think there's anyone in this world who can hide anything from you?" Eternus gave him a respectful look.

"Zeke is hiding Xena's lifeline from me, and it's still hidden in the shadows till now. You might wanna write that down." Ryker deadpanned.

"You can fool everyone else with your plans, Ryker, but not me. You really want me to believe you don't know where Zeke hid her lifeline? You?" Eternus laughed shortly.

"You got more annoying." Ryker muttered with another eye roll.

"Can we not ruin the night with your partner talks? You're spending more than one day here, so there's still time to talk. You said you ate her meal, why not eat mine too?" Eternus waved his hand.

The plum trees in the room vanished, getting replaced by several additional tables of lavish extravagant dishes. 

Colourful chandeliers hung from the ceiling, and beautiful decorative flowers bloomed on the walls, completing the dinning mood.

"I'm not eating your food." Ryker stood from the glass chair.

"Sit back." Eternus said from his seat.

Ryker went for the door, but the glass slammed close before he could touch it, so he turned back to face the sneaky old man.

Eternus is already holding his sword, ready for a duel.

"You should have asked for it nicely. Don't blame me if I strip you out of your skin." Ryker muttered with deep resonance, summoning dry ice on his right palm.

The rare black jewels on his robe were moving with his steps.

"I missed this" Eternus smiled.

They went for each other at the same sharp time, and their big duel started.

In one short minute, the room is in fatal irreparable ruins.

Shattered chandeliers, tables, mashed dishes, smashed chairs, broken grounds and walls. Falling ceilings.

Sage opened the door slightly, peeping inside to see the chaos ongoing. 

"I guess they're going to do this till tomorrow morning." He smiled.

His Channeler Shell brightened in his pocket, and he pulled it out quickly.

"Cheyenne?" He spoke into it.

"She's warm, the slave." Cheyenne said straight to the point.

Sage paused.

"Warm?"

"I think she's a fill-in till the real partner would be found, fair enough. That warmness saved her, I was gonna tear her face earlier." Cheyenne continued.

"Try to be nice sometimes, Cheyenne." Sage sighed as he walked away from that door.

--

[Loco Conference Rendezvous]

The ambassadorial conference didn't take as long as Granny Jojo predicted.

At 10am, they were already done.

She wasn't able to leave early as the other ambassadors were overfilling her with greetings like they do each time they attend the conference like this. They accord her great respect as the East Palace Granny which she hates, but she can't avoid it.

Now there are just two people left in the room with her, and as they came forward to give their greetings, she raised her brows at them.

Kasia and Astor.

"You West Palace folks just don't know when to stop." She said.

"We're not here to tackle you, Grandmother. We're here to seek cooperation." Kasia assured.

"On what?" Jojo scoffed, grabbing her bag already.

"You know exactly what, Granny." Astor said.

Jojo's eyes slipped to him for a second.

"I know you're not happy with the power abuse of Azura and Zeke, I know you're not fine with the unstoppable attitude of the twins. We just need you and Lord Ryker on our side, and on the night of the Feast, we'll do the needful. We only have to get the FrostOrb." Kasia explained.

"Nice explanation," Jojo stood. "I assume you spent days practicing that line before coming here, but sorry... You're not worthy of my cooperation, talk more of Ryker's."

She was glaring as she pushed Kasia's chair out of her way, and she nearly walked past her when Kasia stood suddenly.

She pulled Jojo back, holding eyes with her without blinking.

"You'll connive with us in the West Palace, you will convince Ryker to join us too, and you both will help us steal the FrostOrb on the night if the Feast." She compelled.

Everything went still for a second, but then like an unexpected wind, Jojo's palm smacked Kasia's face.

The slap was brutal, and it sent Kasia to the ground at Jojo's feet.

She tried standing, but she can't. Jojo held her there magically.

Astor attacked Jojo with a strike, trying to save his wife, but his strike was only able to shake Jojo subtly before she pulled a relic mirror out of her bag, throwing it at him.

His ear got a big cut, and the poisonous mirror reflection made his eyes black. He fell on the ground too.

The mirror came back to Jojo, she caught it smoothly.

"Granny! Granny stop!!" Kasia yelled fearfully on her knees.

"How dare you try to compel me! Is this pride? If you have this much pride, you should have fought Azura and Zeke when they were trying to kill your son Blaise!" Jojo yelled.

"We're no match for those two, we know that very well!" Kasia defended.

"But you think you're a match for me?" Jojo laughed. "I might not be sucking powers from the FrostOrb, but I'm not weak. You can't even face me, but you're dreaming of going against the whole of the East Palace? You both are fools!"

"Before you think of going against The Millions, first find out why you lose your powers at midnight. Solve that mystery before planing, cos if you make any move without preparations, then even Jude is enough to tear you both and your kids apart with your mediocre powers that can't lift a stone." Jojo continued, then she left the room.

Kasia was finally able to stand up. She rushed over to Astor and helped him up. His black eyes are back to normal, but his ear is bleeding very much. The cut part is on the ground.

"Her mirror technique got crazier." He muttered.

"She was right, we made a senseless move. Now I lost her trust." Kasia said regretfully.

"I told you. Didn't I?" Astor sighed, holding his bleeding ear.

--

[Black Swan Ballet School]

It's a full house.

Shayla and Xena didn't expect the crowd, they kept multiplying.

It's like everyone in Luminaria came for this audition, so it's definitely gonna be a tough one.

Luckily, Shayla and Xena are among the ones who came early, so they're number 20&21 respectively.

They're sitting outside with the others, and they're the only ones wearing black nose masks to conceal their identities.

Also, they're doing all they can to keep their heads low since the evil twins are inside the building.

If Sadie gets them, she's going to kill the both flatly.

They're both holding hands, avoiding talks too so their voices won't leak.

Luckily, there are people from poor families among the others, so they're not the only ones wearing cheap poor clothes, and that got them covered.

"20 and 21!" The call-in teacher yelled from the door.

They both raised their number tags, and they were called in.

Getting to the dressing room, fear gripped Shayla again.

The ballet outfit is a no for her.

The leggings and tutu is enough to cover her feet completely, but the leotard is tight and short on the arms, so she won't be able to wear that.

If she does, it'd expose the wounds on her back and arms, and that'd give her away.

She just wore only the provided ballet shoes, then she entered the main audition hall with Xena.

Inside the hall, Esme sat proudly with ten other teachers who're the Judges. She's in their middle with her usually flashy extravagant clothes and yellow over-painted nails.

The moment she saw Shayla, her countenance froze.

"Do you think this is a joke site? You're wearing a mask, and the only ballet stuff on your body is the shoes!" She snapped angrily, then she faced Xena too.

"And you, thankfully you're wearing the complete ballet wears, but why the mask?" She questioned.

"I have wounds on my body, and it might stain the white leotard if I put it on." Shayla spoke.

"And about the masks, we have our personal reasons. But we promise we won't disappoint you." Xena added.

"You both!!" Esme slammed her palms on the desk.

She was about to stand when one of the teachers spoke.

"Director, why not give them a chance? If they soil it, then we'll hand them over to the punishment camp. They'll clean sewages for a year, and they'll starve like street dogs."

"Fair enough..." Esme sat back, smiling interestedly. "Play Cruel Symphony Classical. Let them dance to it for me."

"Cruel Symphony? Mercury your daughter and the twins who're the baddest dancer here haven't even mastered that." A judge said.

"Do what I asked." Esme insisted.

The song was slid into the background speakers, and the moment the tunes started flooding into the room, the girls started their moves, rawly.

Fortunately, it's the same dance they saw the twins practicing at Starr balcony, and Shayla fell into the strings, becoming one with the song.

Xena isn't as perfect, but she followed Shayla's fast lead, and she didn't lag. She matched her pace.

Their arms flew in symphony, legs stretched in sync.

Their laps spread equally. They went back to back at the same time, slid on pointe multiple times then raised their weights to land on different spots before coming together again, then they went down, rolling back to each other and smiling.

By the time they stood, the entire judge are on their feet plus Esme herself, and they all have wide mouths and popped eyes.

Esme has mad shock in her eyes.

Xena caged Shayla from behind, and Shayla's slim body went slanted, then they went apart again...

"This is...." Esme gasped, dropping her pen.

She was clapping before she knew it.

"Awesome!!" The teachers joined her clappings.

"I've never seen this before!"

"Who are they?"

"Mama Mia!"

"Whoa!!"

By the time the girls made the last move and went on pointe again, the clappings were thundering around the room so hard.

"Can you both at least show your faces?" Esme went closer to them.

"Not yet, ma'am." Shayla said under her mask, sweating a lot.

"Fine...fine. Next week is the final audition like you both know, and you both have to come." Esme took tag 1 and 2, giving it to them.

"You both have to definitely show up, please." 

"Sure, we won't disappoint." Xena smiled under her mask, leaving the room with Shayla.

The moment they got back to the dressing room, they hugged tight.

"We're so lucky! I love you so much Shay!" Xena gushed.

"I love you too" Shayla smiled.

--

[Gymnasium]

Since the teachers are busy with auditions right now, dancers are here, just chilling out.

Each to their own side of the place.

Sadie and Mercury are sitting at a side, their legs are mounted arrogantly, and their soy milk bottles are held to their lips. 

They're drinking with straws, and they're glaring at two people in particular...

Kade and Blaise.

Soren is currently at the washroom, so he's not glaring with them.

"After their deaths, let's go break-in tonight. It's been long, and I have a mansion in mind." Mercury said.

"Sure, but I'm going to ask you one question." Sadie faced her.

"I'll never lie to you." Mercury shrugged.

"Are you in love with my brother?" Sadie asked flatly.

For the next minute, Mercury was blank, then she laughed, unable to keep her awkwardness low.

"In love with Soren? That'd be betrayal and I'll never do that to you. I know what's going to happen if one of you should fall in love, so that's never happening."

"You won't mind if I tell Soren to rape Xena and touch her right in front of you?" Sadie asked craftily, taking her milk straw away from her dark lips.

"Why would I care? In fact, it'd be sweet to watch." Mercury replied, wiping her sweaty hand secretly on her leggings.

"Great, then give me the ashes I told you to bring." Sadie stood.

"Right!" Mercury brought her dead father's ashes pack out of her bag. She was about to hand it over to Sadie when Faye came in.

She's holding her phone, smiling as she read out a news from lumi web.

"The Millions Family have to be the craziest family existing. Locking in their last daughter cos she was born blind? Precarious!" She laughed.

Sadie and Mercury gasped, shooting up from their seats at the same time. 

Soren came in, staring at his phone too. He has seen the news.

Sadie snatched the phone, and when she saw it, she gasped.

Kade, Blaise and the other dancers drew closer, converging around the twins who look obviously shaken.

"Oh...so there's a fourth child to the Millions family? And she's blind? I get the shame, her lock up is normal." Blaise chuckled.

"It's the news of the damn century. The oh-so-mighty Millions Family from the East Palace have a blind chic as the baby of the house. She's completely powerless? What a jinx!" Kade grinned.

"I bet she's ugly too." Faye added, popping her gum, and that made many dancers laugh.

Kade pushed everyone out of his way till he got to the middle to face the twins who're still dumbfounded.

"Your insane family isn't only power-drunk, but y'all are also lying crooks? That's unacceptable." He slammed his rigid chest into Soren's.

--

[Millions Family Exclusive Store]

The first thing Lilith came to check out is new clothes since she's a new member of the family now, and she'd be becoming Ryker's secret wife too.

She was humming excitedly as she shopped alone with enough assistants behind her who're pushing the carts of her plenty picked items.

Her makeup could paint two gigantic mansions right now.

"I feel so empty, I guess I'm eating next." She nodded with a pout, facing an assistant.

"Call the upper floor, I need carbonara in like twenty minutes." She ordered.

"Yes ma'am." The assistant bowed, leaving to do what Lilith asked.

"Lilith the dwarf?" Jude entered the store, looking utterly shocked at what he's seeing.

This place is exclusive, so how?

"Hey, upcoming big brother! I should have known we'd be becoming family the moment I got your jacket." Lilith waved.

Unexpectedly, Lilith gripped her neck, throwing her over to the other side with planned harshness.

"Why the hell are you in the family store you short irritating witch!" He demanded furiously.

"You damn thing!" Lilith stood, opening her mouth to scream, but Winona's entrance stopped her.

"Jude!! " She entered with a laugh, flashing her phone screen at his face.

"You haven't heard about the ongoing chaos? Luminaria is burning with the news of BBOTEP." She laughed again, enjoying the jest.

"BBOTEP?" Jude blinked.

"Blind Baby Of The East Palace! Your last sibling Pearl Millions is out in the open! Y'all are psychos in that family and I get the chills just thinking about it! Fûck!" Winona laughed louder, flipping her sleek healthy hair.

"What!" Jude gasped.

--

[East Palace, 6PM]

Azura and Zeke look so out of it as they ran their restless races into the main castle, their breaths chasing each other till they were inside.

They heard clattering sounds from the dinning, and they followed, meeting Pearl there.

She sat majestically, eating like a royal that she is. She's wearing one of Sadie's black dresses, and though the eye cover is away from her eyes, her blindness is obvious with the way her hands missed the plate few times.

"Mother? Father? I can smell your presence." She said, pushing a small cut tiramisu into her mouth with her plastic fork.

One dangerous hit from Zeke made the dinning table break down, sending glass splinters everywhere.

"How where you able to come out? How!!!" He roared.

"That's not a good way to welcome the baby of the house." Pearl smiled innocently, sitting still.

Azura dragged her up with her dress, and Pearl grunted.

"How were you able to get out? It's a question!"

The twins barged in at this point, followed by Jude and Granny Jojo.

"It's true." Soren muttered, his shock settling in better.

"She's even wearing my goth dress? That blind sorrow is wearing my dress!!" Sadie shouted.

"How are you out?" Jude spoke, bewildered.

"Pearl..." Granny Jojo muttered, her eyes wide too.

"A slave named Shayla saved me from the basement. She came and opened the door for me, and she gave me directions to come out. She did it, said she was doing me a favor which I didn't reject." Pearl said.

"Shayla?" Azura and Zeke chorused.

"Shayla?" Jude and Granny Jojo chorused too.

"That damn animal!" Sadie growled.

Soren's fists clenched.

Meanwhile, Old Chef is listening by the opposite wall.

He covered his mouth to block his gasp as he rushed off.

--

[Granny's Chamber]

Shayla leaned gently on the window of her room, rubbing her fingers on the wind chime and humming the Cruel Symphony song she danced to earlier at the audition.

She didn't meet Jojo at home when she arrived earlier, so she's alone here, accompanied by nothing but the scent coming from Ryker's embroidered handkerchief.

With a slim slice of smile on her face, she brought out the handkerchief from her gown pocket, pushing it to her nose to sniff.

"Wonder where he travelled to." She whispered, sniffing again.

"Shay! Shayla!! Shay!!" Xena's trembling voice rang from outside. 

"Xena?" Shayla left the window, and Xena came in tearfully.

She's with Old Chef.

"Shay, don't lie to me. Did you really open the basement door like Pearl said? She just confessed that you did! Please say you didn't! They're going to kill you!!" She cried shakily.

Shayla's eyes widened too much.

"Pearl? Is...out?"

"Talk to me!" Xena snapped.

"The door opened when my body touched it, and I went in. We talked for a moment and I left after shutting the door back." Shayla confessed fearfully.

"This won't work, they'll be here to take you any moment from now." Old Chef said with urgency.

Another person came in, making them turn. It's Cheyenne.

"Get here" she strictly ordered, but Shayla didn't understand, so she didn't move.

With powerful force, Cheyenne pulled her, and Shayla found herself in front of the wicked lady.

Cheyenne took her hand surprisingly, and she started taking her out of the room, but the royal guards were already at the door.

At once, they all attacked.

Cheyenne pushed Shayla behind herself, brandishing her nails at them. She tore two at a time, their flayed skins gumming to her bloody nails before they fell off to the ground.

Red stained the walls, and Shayla's heart thundered faster than it has ever done all her life.

The cries of the two torn guards wasn't enough to scare off the rest. They charged forward.

Cheyenne pulled off her hat and flung it forward, cutting off one head with it.

Blood sprayed the ceiling, then her legs left the ground. She leapt up fluidly and kicked the door metal away from the hinges.

The metal slammed into the faces of two others, and she landed back on her feet, her hair covering her eyes.

She took Shayla's hand again, attempting to escape, but this time, Azura and Zeke entered.

Cheyenne was pushed back with the brute force of their boiling anger, and her body easily rolled out of the room through the window.

"I didn't release her, I didn't even know how the door opened!" Shayla shook her head, shifting back.

Her organs shivered inside her as if detached from her hot insides.

"Keep quiet, slave." Zeke muttered, his rage affecting her from his voice.

Shayla staggered.

Azura immediately raised her hand up, waving it from from the dark skies to the ground.

A single thunder cracked in obeisance to her powers.

The thunder slipped into the house through the open window and hit Shayla's legs cruelly.

Every bone her legs crushed within that fleeting second, and her screams were louder than a banshee's as she went down, falling on her face flatly.

The twins came in with a black coffin which they dropped beside her, the weight so impactful on the ground.

Together, they threw Shayla's slumped body inside the coffin.

"The thunder arena is ready, the pinnacle is cold, waiting for her blood." Sadie said mercilessly.

"Great news, let's get it started." Azura's voice echoed darkly.

The coffin was carried out, Shayla's blood dripping out of it like water.
