[King Mansion]

With restlessness and uneasiness, Liza sat at the edge of the dinning table, eating dinner with a flat face.

Though, the dinner is barely digesting. She's even having problem swallowing it. Her face might say she's nonchalant, but her innermost self knows her better.

Two hours ago, the news of the additional member of the Millions Family broke out. Pearl Millions got revealed to the eyes of everyone, and every single person in this city made fun of the East Palace.

But then an hour ago, another news broke out about the person who released Pearl from where she was locked, and it turned out to be the 150 billion slave... Shayla.

She's gonna get executed at the pinnacle on the arena. In fact, she's already getting tortured by now as far as she knows.

For someone who opened the family secret to the public, the Millions won't let her go easily.

Liza spooned her soup and brought it to her mouth to swallow again, but Kade barged into the house, interrupting her with his loudness.

"I was thinking of tricking you to tell me how to uncover her mark so I'd tell Ryker and he'd kill her for me, but seems the gods are on my side and they don't want me stressing!" He laughed, shifting her food away.

He sat on that table instead.

"I'm sure you heard it, mum. Your slave daughter is getting executed! I heard there's gonna be a live streaming of the execution and I'm gonna go watch with Blaise...care to join us?" He winked.

Liza stood and left the dinning silently, but Kade wouldn't have it, so he followed her, his steps pounding with her own.

"Tell me, do you love her more than me? I'm your only son remember? The one who'd carry the King surname till death!" 

"You're my son, and she is my daughter!" Liza faced her.

"Her human father raped you when you were sick and powerless in the human world! She wasn't supposed to happen! You should have aborted her! She doesn't deserve your damn love!" Kade grabbed her shoulders.

"Yeah, her human father raped me, but it was never her fault! She's just unlucky to be born in this place where rules are toxic! And abort her? I might have been raped, but she is still my child! Execution?" Liza laughed, pushing Kade's grip away from her shoulders.

"No matter how hard the torture is, she won't die! The mark might be gone from her body, but she still carries that warm aura and that's enough to keep her alive in any space, so stop your obsession with getting rid of her. You'll never be able to do that." 

And Liza didn't expect what happened next. Kade's hand flew to her face, giving her a loud hit which left imprints on her face.

Liza fell to the ground, holding her face and looking up at Kade shockingly.

Kade squatted in front of her, lifting his hand to her red cheek to touch, his smile resembling a maniac's.

"You lost all powers just to give birth to her, remember? So watch how you talk to me about her. You might be my mother, but respect boundaries. And now I get it, you lost your powers so it's impossible for you to do the job yourself. You probably hired someone to cover her mark, didn't you?" He muttered, tongue curling in his mouth.

Liza swallowed, and he smiled again, slowly taking his fingers away from her red face.

"There are many people who're good at spells in this city, I just have to gather them and search." He nodded, standing up.

"Blaise is waiting, I should go meet him before the live stream ends. Bye mother!" He blew her a kiss with two fingers then left the house.

Liza stood to her feet, caressing her red face as she walked back to the dinning to sit.

"Endure, Liza. You have to endure if you really want the results you're looking for." She muttered to herself.

"And you, daughter. You must endure too. Only then we can rule Luminaria together. You must live, please..." She clenched her jaws and fists.

--

[East Palace, Arena]

Just like the other time when Blaise's execution was happening, the arena is crowdy again with the clansmen and the main royals.

Shayla has been chained hands up to the pinnacle metals. Her body isn't even on the ground, it's floating in the air, and the chains are suspending her from their claws.

The current state of her legs is the most pitiful. They look obviously crushed as they're dangling with the bones gone, and her blood is trickling down, dropping on the pinnacle and flooding down the arena.

More than a litre left that body already, but her eyes are still slightly opened under her wet lids.

Even her face is bloody with the cuts she got from the chains, and her fingers are shaking above her head.

Granny Jojo isn't in the arena, but Jude is there, and he looks calm as he stood behind his parents.

Lilith is present too, and her smile is the brightest here, no arguments.

The twins had invited Mercury again, and she's scoffing as she awaited the start of the torture.

Xena is present too, behind the pillar. She's crying, and Old Chef had to cover her mouth so she won't give them away.

"How were you able to open the basement door?" Azura asked the question that has been bothering her, but Shayla stayed quiet, just crying and bleeding there.

"How were you able to break the seal and open the basement door!!" Zeke asked the question louder, but Shayla has no energy in her mouth to reply them. They're still going to kill her if she talks or not, so why not just keep quiet and conserve her energy for the punishment.

"The gun" Zeke ordered, and it was Sadie who threw him a wooden gun which he caught.

"That's the rotten barrel." Old Chef said behind the pillar, and Xena looked at him with her teary eyes.

"Rotten what?"

"It's one of their antiques in the family, and a bullet from it will make your bones rot inside your body till you start smelling. You'll rot from the inside and die. It's wicked than the thunder punishment." Old Chef explained.

Xena gasped in shock and nearly came out of her hiding, but Old Chef dragged her back.

"Are you gonna get us killed too?"

"Leave me alone! She's just too softhearted it's not her fault! She didn't open that door, I'm sure it was the blind Pearl. She already looks like trouble! She's behind everything!" Xena struggled.

Old Chef pulled her away from behind the pillar, then he started dragging her back to the kitchen, leaving the arena with her.

Zeke didn't hold back before pulling the gun trigger three straight times, and the bullets hit Shayla's upper body, all on the chest.

Her face shot up, and blood splurged out of her mouth and nostrils, wetting her dress and face too.

Jude's eyes turned away from the pinnacle. He closed his eyes and was biting himself repeatedly, unable to watch the cruelty.

"Oh...pity. Her legs got crushed already, now her upper body has those bullets inside? Sweet." Mercury laughed, facing Soren.

"Right, Soren?"

Soren didn't reply.

Sadie smirked, shooting Mercury a short glance.

"How were you able to open the door?" Azura asked calmly, taking the gun from Zeke and preparing to release another three bullets into Shayla's body.

The arena atmosphere froze, and the wickedness in the air could kill the weak as it moved with the breeze.

The clansmen and the rest of the east royals are beginning to murmur.

"She has no regards for her life!"

"She's a slave, what were you expecting?"

"Slave? I doubt it. How was she able to open the seal then?"

"That's the question she's refusing to answer, and it's annoying."

"She has to die!"

"Letting someone like her stay at the chamber was senseless from the start, but letting her stay alive? That'd be more stupid."

As Lilith listened to the different takes, she folded her short arms on her chest, her smile broadening.

She was planning to mark her for death before, but now she doesn't even have to stress.

Things are working in her favor.

"How were you able to open the door?" Azura asked again, cocking the gun and preparing to pull.

"I...I..." Shayla started, her mouth severely wet with the blood, so much that it affected her already powerless voice, draining life from it.

"I... dunno" she managed to push out the few words, and Azura pulled the trigger, releasing one more bullet to her belly.

Shayla went limp, arms dragging tight against the chains as her whole body lost essence.

Azura shot the chains next, and as they broke, Shayla fell from the air and landed on the pinnacle.

Her head hit the metal, and she sustained injury over there too, her body so slumped as it lay lifeless in the pool of blood.

"Get her back into the coffin." Zeke ordered. The twins came to do that.

Shayla's body was thrown back into the black doom space, and Lilith was called forward to mark her.

She placed a hand on Shayla's bloody face, and a tiny red mark appeared on her forehead.

"Marked for death, in three days. No matter how much the pains from her rotting flesh is, she won't die till the three days arrive. And not just her body, her soul is dying too. If her soul dies, then there's no hope of reborn for her. Just the right thing." She said.

"Even better" Azura commented.

The coffin was closed, and Zeke was the last to touch it before it vanished.

"Father, where did you send it?" Soren asked.

"Land of the condemned." Zeke replied, and Sadie jumped forward.

"Seriously? How many pain levels is she going to pass through then? Her crushed legs, the bullets on her upper body, her injured head and face, then the mark of death and now land of the condemned? Father I can't love you less." She hugged him, and the man laughed.

"Now, shall we deal with the newly released family member?"

"Of course." She shrugged, leaving with get father.

Soren and Azura didn't move.

"How was the slave able to open the door?" Soren said.

"You're as curious as me, aren't you? But my Zeke doesn't look bothered, and that's somehow. These days, I can't help but think he's keeping things from me." Azura replied.

"What are you planning to do with Lilith?" Soren asked.

"She's joining the family, and she's getting married to Ryker secretly very soon." Azura faced him.

"Joining the family? Mother, are you drunk?" Soren frowned.

"Son, joining the family doesn't always mean joining the family." Azura smiled. "We Millions have a long history of using people and manipulating them, we're just gonna use her too, as usual." 

She left, and Soren followed her.

Jude climbed the pinnacle, squatted and touched Shayla's blood with his fingers. He rubbed it, his jaws clenching again.

"Land of the condemned? That dreadful place."

--

[Palace Prison]

Cheyenne woke up to find herself sitting on the bare ground with her back on the wall, her suit bloodstained and her hair scattered. She's barefoot too.

After she was thrown out of the window earlier, she had lost consciousness, and she's only just waking up right now.

She spranged up, rushing to the thick metal bars to grab them.

She pulled stubbornly, kicking and even slamming her forehead on it, but no amount of powers can make it open. This prison was sealed with Azura's powers too, and so far it's Azura, no one can open it, so how was Shayla able to open the basement door?

That question made her pause, and her eyes went still.

"Is she really not the partner? Who's she if not?" She muttered, getting herself together again.

She resumed slamming her body on the metal bars, and they pierced her skin, giving her huge marks.

She summoned her nails, and when they grew, she went down and attached them to the ground concrete, trying to dig, but she couldn't.

Azura's seal is too strong, and she was thrown back. She tumbled hard.

"Darn it!!!" She grunted, wanting to stubbornly continue, but then the metal bars opened, and Granny Jojo came inside.

"Granny?" Cheyenne stood with heavy breaths.

Jojo looks guilty as she approached her, she couldn't stop it.

"How were you able to lift the seal and enter?" Cheyenne asked.

"Azura is my daughter, and this sealing technique was taught by me during her power training, so yes...I know how to open the seal." Jojo explained.

"You know?" Cheyenne came closer. "And you're aware that your youngest granddaughter was locked up, but you didn't save her?"

"Listen, I was just as selfish as the rest of the family before Ryker came here. When Pearl was born blind, Azura wanted to kill her, but I didn't want to lose a grandchild, so I suggested the lockup cos fighting against Azura would be suicidal, she's younger than me so she's stronger." Jojo explained.

"I guess you're not any different from the rest then. Y'all are sick in this family, and I'm not sorry to say. Now, what have they done to the slave?" Cheyenne asked breathlessly.

"You suddenly like her now?" Jojo looked up.

"Anything that gives Lord Ryker the slightest comfort is mine to protect! She's warm, and she has to stay alive!" Cheyenne snapped.

"She was shot and sent to the land of the condemned." Jojo said.

Cheyenne gasped, eyes popping wide. "Land of the...what!"

--

[Main Castle, Twins Room]

The sound of gloomy qin playing saturated the room, it's dark melody giving the room more sadness to drown in.

Pearl sat on Sadie's bed with the qin in hand, she's the one playing with her eyes widely open.

She's smiling again as her melodies flew out of the instrument, and her fingernails are riding effortlessly on the black strings.

She was about to hit the last note when the twins barged into the room, their anger getting bigger.

"My qin?" Soren scoffed, snatching it from her.

"On my bed?" Sadie added, using her leg to push her away from the bed.

Pearl fell on the ground, and the parents came in.

"You're going back into the basement. We already got disgraced by your revelation, and this disgrace can't continue." Azura said.

"That's right, youngest daughter. You should count yourself lucky that you got to taste outside life for a bit, now it's over." Zeke smiled.

"Is that really necessary?" Jude came inside with pocketed hands.

"Shut up!" Sadie snapped.

"I'm not going back in there, never! Especially now that I have a good-looking reason to stay outside!" Pearl spat, her blind eyes glaring.

"And who gave you the right to say a word you unwanted stain!" Azura snarled.

"My last granddaughter isn't going back to the basement." Jojo said, entering the room.

She took Pearl, hiding her behind herself.

"Mother" Azura spoke.

"You shouldn't do this." Zeke said.

"I said no one is locking her up again. So what if the citizens are disgracing y'all for her existence? Still doesn't change the fact that the Millions family are the rulers of the city, so retract your wings and leave her alone! She'll stay in the chambers with me." Jojo rushed, pulling smiley Pearl out of the room with herself.

"Should we just kill your mother?" Zeke faced Azura.

"You better not think about it, I'll take care of her myself." Azura glared.

The twins left the room together, and they went straight to the servants quarters to get Xena.

--

[West Palace, Midnight]

Kasia just finished sewing in the cut part of Astor's ear, so she sat tiredly beside him.

The kids are with them in the lobby, including Kade who came here to stay the night.

"I thought you said they'd be streaming Shayla's punishment live." Faye said.

"That's what I heard, but it got cancelled. The Millions Family are probably boiling right now, so they don't even have the time for that." Kade shrugged.

Winona is in one side, crying profusely like she just lost a husband. She's not wiping the tears, so they kept entering her mouth, and she kept licking crazily.

"When is Winona gonna stop crying?" Kade muttered.

"She likes the slave, and now that she's dead, imagine the pains, so let her cry." Faye replied, and Kade nodded.

This bastard sister of his knows how to make trouble. Winona likes her? Sounds sour even to the ears.

Well, she's dead now, so nothing more to worry about.

Blaise is alone in a corner of the lobby, and he's trying to teleport to his room but it's not working.

It's midnight, so it's normal for powers to disappear in this west Palace.

"And you both had to go make trouble with the Granny?" Faye scoffed, facing Astor.

"We wanted to try our heaviest best, but she ended up wounding Astor." Kasia replied.

"She told us to find out why we lose powers at midnight first." Astor said.

"Exactly!" Blaise stood from his corner, surprising everyone.

"Do you both even think? I'm so ashamed to even say I was birthed by Kasia cos the thinking faculty is dry!" 

"Blaise!" Kasia stood.

"You will not talk rudely to your mother!" Astor stood too.

"Oh shut up Astor!" Blaise slammed. "You do not have the smarts like my father! If my father was still alive, he'd have solved this mystery since long ago, but you being with my mother is making her look dumb which she's not! You're nerfing her!"

With her mind, Faye threw the flower vase on the table at Blaise, hitting him on the face and wounding his jawline badly.

Blaise fell back on the chair he stood from, and Kade quickly rushed to hold him so he won't lose consciousness.

"Faye?" Astor and Kasia gasped.

"Faye" Winona wiped her tears and stood, looking at Faye shockingly too.

"Did you forget? Only my father changed his surname to this family surname, not me, so he's the only one carrying the midnight curse with you all, not me!" Faye spat.

She faced Blaise who bleeding already.

"So you better watch how you speak to my father, and stop calling your dog by my name, or I kill that dog and feed you with the raw flesh!" She threatened and went inside.

"That was... insane." Kade chuckled.

Astor and Kasia exchanged glances, and Astor quickly followed Faye upstairs.

Winona and Kasia took Blaise to go take care of him.

--

[City~A Lone Mansion]

The twins chose this place as their sin spot tonight because the owners are on a trip to the human world, so the mansion is unoccupied for now.

They got down from their car in front of the building, they smiled at each other and went to the trunk where they dragged Xena out from.

She's gagged and also blindfolded.

Soren took the lead to the house, plucking the fire extinguisher from the outside wall.

He crashed it to the side window, breaking it down to create the entry space. He threw Xena inside the house then entered too.

Sadie jumped in with them, and in two more minutes, they've already messed up the house.

Loud music blasted as they took turns to stab the couches, break the chandeliers and shatter the plates in the kitchen.

They messed up the clothes in the bedroom wardrobes too, and they broke every drink in the cellar, but they left three bottles which they took one each from.

They were drinking as they came back to the large living room where Xena is on her knees.

Her blindfold and gag are still there, and she looks overly scared as she trembled obviously.

"Cheers!" The twins clinked bottles over the music, and Mercury came in.

"Y'all didn't tell me it was time for the fun! I was missing out!" She shouted, grabbing the third bottle from the ground.

She popped the champagne and drank her fill, then she noticed Xena on the ground.

"What's she... doing here?" She asked, slowly facing the twins.

"Remember what you said earlier about not caring if Soren rapes her? We're manifesting it." Sadie smiled.

The bottle fell from Mercury immediately, and she swallowed.

"Soren, get to work." Sadie ordered, and Soren started unbolting his belt.

Xena shook her head on the ground, swallowing over and over and over as she heard her fate.

She has managed to stay celibate all her life, she can't lose it this way. Not to a monster like Soren.

She could feel him getting closer. She has to do something.

Sadie's eyes stayed on Mercury, and Mercury managed to smile.

"I was serious, there's no big deal." She muttered, shrugging, though she's dying inside.

Soren better not touch Xena with his thing...he better not...

She gasped when she suddenly felt a sting at the back of her neck, and before she could turn around, the twins made the same gasp sounds.

Bees.

There are bees in the mansion.

They multiplied, stinging venomously till the cruel bullies started screaming, running around the room and trying to get the hives away.

Surprisingly, even their powers seemed to fail them at this point, they could only scream and run, and it's worse cos Sadie has fear of bees.

Xena stood from her knees, removing her gag and blindfold with irregular breathings.

The bees aren't attacking her since she's their summoner anyways. She called them with her mind, thanks to her animal control ability.

She ran out of the mansion, away from the chaos, but before she could get to the sidewalk, she was pulled back, and she screamed when she saw Soren's face.

It's swollen in different places with the beestings, and he the ree swells look hella painful.

Xena quickly went on her knees, begging...

"Please...I just...I just wanted to keep my celibacy intact. I'm so sorry, I know I was wrong but—"

"Run." He cut her off coldly, causing her to blink.

"Run before I change my darn mind!!" He yelled.

Xena stood and ran off without looking back.

Soren hooked his belt back, looking at the mansion. Mercury and Sadie are still screaming inside, his ears are getting loud waves of it.

He has always wanted to stay celibate till death, and he wasn't gonna let Sadie ruin that, so Xena's move worked in his favor too.

He went back into the mansion, casting a spell to wipe the memories of the two girls.

--

[Clouds Castle]

Oddly, Ryker has been curious.

He's far from Luminaria right now, so he's not expecting to hear Shayla's voice as usual, but at least, there should be signs.

His ears should itch from lack of her voice. They do anytime he's far from the palace, so why not now?

Should he have given her the Channeler Shell?

This worry isn't something he does on the norms, but he kept getting the underlaying feeling that something ain't right.

He's sitting alone in his mountainside room, and he has fused the heavenly energy of this place with his own, trying to search for Xena's twin again with the conjunction, but he can't even concentrate a little bit.

"Sage!" He called, and Sage rushed in.

"My Lord?"

"When last did Cheyenne communicate with you?"

"When the say was still bright, she communicated and told me how warm the slave was. That was like twelve hours ago." Sage reported.

Ryker went silent.

"Do you have suspicions?" Sage asked, but Eternus entered with a tray of alcohol before Ryker could answer that.

Sage left the room.

"I still can't believe you actually wounded me during the duel yesterday. Your obsession with blood got thicker." Eternus sighed as he set the tray on the table.

"Since you won't eat, then drink. That's less stressful." 

But that same second, the tray fell from the table cos Ryker actually hit it away. The alcohol wasted away.

"I'm not interested in eating, not interested in using my mouth for anything that's not talking, try to process the words one by one!" Ryker snapped before he knew it.

Eternus didn't even look at the wasted alcohol, his eyes settled on Ryker's livid ones.

"You're angry, why? Is something the case? You were talking peacefully with Sage before I entered." He softly said.

Ryker stood like a giant, but Eternus blocked him.

"What's the reason for this visit in the first place? You won't tell me anything, but you expect me to tell you truths! Why are you pretending not to know where Xena's lifeline is? And why are you still letting Xena suffer in the East Palace kitchen when you have enough power to take her out!"

"That offer landed on her table too many times, but she kept declining. She insisted she's not leaving the kitchen if I do not save her father with her, and you know that's the most impossible thing in my existence. The only thing I owe him is death! If Xena wants to suffer with him, who am I to block that" Ryker declared, still angry.

"Then why are you still in the palace yourself? You'd have escaped if you wanted to. What are you planning?" Eternus asked again.

"Your questions doubled, makes me wonder what you're actually up to." Ryker smirked, and the curiosity on Eternus face slipped.

"It's just curiousness with nothing else at the base!" Eternus said, but that wasn't enough to save him from the slash he received on the chest.

He wasn't fast enough to dodge it, and the hit flung him to the ground where he held his aching chest.

He summoned his sword, but Ryker waved it away. The weapon entered the ground, getting stuck.

Eternus held his chest, looking up at Ryker's livid face.

"You hurt me on purpose during the duel yesterday just to weaken me?"

"Glad you know." Ryker nodded, walking closer with burning gaze. "You shouldn't have lied to me. You've been my confidante for years, and I know your habits. Your eyes are gloomy whenever you're saying the truth, but they're shiny when you tell your pranking lies, like you did yesterday. How dare you lie to me!"

"Ryker .. " Eternus stood, but he fell back to the ground, his weakness spreading to his legs.

"Is she, or is she not the partner? You only have to answer yes or no. Is someone hiding the mark? You only have to answer yes or no to that too, or I'll have to be the first demigod to kill a seer!" Ryker demanded, all softness going into extinction.

"My Lord!" Sage rushed inside, breathing hard and heavy.

Ryker's eyes flicked to him, and Cheyenne entered behind him, panting and sweating.

Merely seeing the bloodstain on her suit, Ryker knew everything is wrong. 

"I had to find my way here. She was taken after she let Pearl out of the basement, Azura condemned her legs with her thunder, she was shot with the rot barrel, she injured her head and Lilith also marked her before she was sealed in a coffin and sent to the land of the condemned." Cheyenne explained without breathing.

"Land of the condemned?" Ryker muttered, getting robbed of all existing patience.

Cheyenne took a deep breath, and Ryker stepped back.

"Stay here with him, you both. He's not supposed to be able to stand before I return." He said.

"Are you going to go to the land of the condemned?" Sage came closer fearfully. "That place holds a darkness blood can't quench, and you know it. Even the powerful tyrant supreme who entered to seek power some centuries ago...he lost his soul completely and hasn't returned. You can't take that risk. Also, we all know you need the pass from Zeke to enter, and he'll never release it."

"How dare you compare me to a foolish tyrant supreme?" Ryker rolled eyes arrogantly.

"My Lord, it's very ris—"

"Hold your peace, Sage. Trust him." Cheyenne cut him off, and Sage swallowed.

"Watch him." Ryker said, then he vanished by the door.

--

[Luminaria, East Palace]

The palace is silent as it's 2am presently, so everyone is asleep including the servants and slaves, but the lights are brightly on in every corridor and halls.

Faint footsteps pressed on the sleek floors of the main castle lobby as a pair of clean bare legs marched silently till it arrived at the long staircase which it climbed.

A barrel hung from the hand of the person, smelling like death.

The walk was intentional, but with obvious hint of lethal darkness.

The space between the two feet at each step is measured, and it stayed as ominous as that till he arrived on top of the stairs.

The first door he opened was the twins room.

It opened with a swing, and he wasn't surprised to see them on their separate beds, sleeping soundly.

He cocked the gun, slid his finger under the trigger and pulled twice, targeting the twins legs.

Deafening cries of agony fell out of their mouths, the venom of the bullets waking them up from their beauty sleeps.

Blood covered their sheets in seconds, and they fell off the bed, lifting the room temperature with their cries.

With satisfaction, he left the room, going down Jude's room, but Jude isn't there, so he moved to the chamber.

The twins cries accompanied him till he got there.

Granny Jojo and Pearl are sleeping, curled up like a happy grandmother and grandaughter in bed.

Jojo sensed the presence, so she shot up in bed, her eyes coming wide under the lights.

"Ryker, it's not what you think. I couldn't fight for her because —"

"Shhh" Ryker hushed her, blasting the bullet into her own leg too.

Her cries wake Pearl up, and the moment she sat up blindly, Ryker gifted her her own bullet.

Jojo and Pearl's blood mixed on the sheets just like the twins, and Ryker's toe ring glowed, happy to witness the blood and tears.

"Ryker!!!" Azura and Zeke barged into the chamber, and when they saw he had shot Jojo and Pearl too, they looked traumatized.

Where did he even get that barrel gun from?

"Mother!!" Azura shouted.

"I like that you love death so much, enough to court it! You don't need Xena anymore? Is that it? How the hell did you even enter the castle without my notice! I'd notice the entrance of the smallest ant even during my sleep." Zeke spat, both in shock and surprise.

"I like that you love underestimating me so much, and it's so beautiful to watch." Ryker groaned, keeping the gun intact and that frosty face.

"But I don't have that storytime in hand as at now. Drop the pass to the land of the condemned, or I take your blood too. And don't think this is your barrel gun. That rot one is your foolish version, but this one sucks blood. I'm sure you see my toe ring shinning, meaning it's sucking. Drop the pass, or everyone I shot dies in five short minutes!" Ryker spat.

"Ryker Morozov!" Azura yelled.

"Correct. You don't take my only warmness and expect me to sit still like a moron!!!" Ryker growled...

"The pass, or everyone dies."

CHAPTER 27&28

My plan: to post throughout this week. Y'all can muddle that by messing with the engagement anyday. I won't hesitate before going off.

Let's make up for the lost days and cover up this week, y'all.

Reply All Comments Drop Urs Like and Massively Share. Complete requirements before 7pm tomorrow, or no chapter.

★

Even after the unfunny warning and the short time limit, the evil couple still stood there, staring back at Ryker like he just said a language they didn't understand.

Despite the high agonizing cries of Granny Jojo and Pearl, they didn't move, and the threat is far from fake cos as the seconds counted, their bleedings got heavier.

As for Pearl, she might be crying and bleeding like crazy, but somewhere inside her, she's glad that the bullet came from Ryker.

It came from HIM, and she's going to cherish this bullet for the rest of her life. To her, it's his first gift to her.

She could smell him, his dark doom scent with a mixture of wet petals scent from his spring pool.

She could feel him so bad, and even as she bled, she got aroused.

Now two minutes are gone, and both Jojo and Pearl herself already lost two litres of blood and still pouring.

Ryker stood there, looking like a calamitous foreshadow of death beside the bed. His robe is slightly bloody from all the blood he has spilled tonight, and his eyes are still that shade of unquenchable anger.

The barrel is still with him. He's still not relenting as he glared back at the couple.

Royal guards ran into the room—three of them.

"The twins, they lost consciousness already!" They reported.

"They...did?" Azura gasped.

"Blood! There's blood everywhere!" They said, their fears rubbing off on the couple.

"Zeke! The pass!" Azura said.

"What are you talking about! We're going to bow?" Zeke faced her.

"You heard me, the pass!!" Azura yelled.

Zeke looked at Ryker, and he could feel his own pride getting thwarted as he spread his palm forward.

The pass appeared in form of a black card with a white skull drawing.

Through the air, he sent it to Ryker who caught it and vanished from the chamber immediately.

--

[Land Of The Condemned]

Darkness. Darkness. Darkness.

Nothing else is here except hovering shadows of wraiths 

There's no structure, no form, no water, ground or plants or stones. It's just empty. A void space.

Ryker appeared in the air, his brows curving in a tight knot as he took in his surroundings, trying to get his bearings and have a feel of it.

Shayla was sent here in a coffin, so it's either she's somewhere in the air, or the wraiths got a hold of her.

If it's the second one, then it'd be harder to get back cos the wraiths here are spirits of the people he killed—innocent and guilty, and their resentment against him is so strong that he's already feeling it in his core.

He tried using his ears, but nothing came. She might be unconscious, but at least he should be able to hear her faint heartbeats.

He got nothing, so he moved forward, swirling with the cold air.

"My Lord!" Shayla's small voice alerted him behind

He sharply turned around.

His toe-ring shone bright, providing the needed amount of light.

Shayla stood there in the air, smiling her usual bright smile at him. Her clothes are clean—free of blood, and her hair is well kept—silky.

"My Lord, did you come for me?" She smiled again, her full lips parting as she stretched her arm forward.

"Here, let's go back."

Ryker moved to her, giving the hand a stare before rolling his eyes back to her face, carefully.

"Is there a problem, My Lord?" She talked again, and Ryker decided to answer that one with his dagger.

He pulled it out of nowhere, stabbing her chest with the blade and making sure everything went in.

She shattered to white ashes, giving away the disguise as she disappeared with the air.

It's not her, just her image that's formed by the resentment of this place to fool him.

Ryker looked at the dagger, then forward again. The knot between his brows grew, and the more he moved, the colder he got.

It's like someone is increasing the level of the coldness here, and he's not surprised.

This place hates him, so the wraiths are going to use his greatest weakness against him—the cold.

"My Lord!" Shayla's voice interrupted his search again, and he looked beside him to see her.

She looks same as the first one, but she's not smiling. She's crying.

"Please take me out of here! Please please... it's too cold in here...I want to go home, i—"

That was the end of her sentence cos Ryker threw his dagger to her chest, and she blasted to ashes just like the first image.

Now it's getting annoying.

Whatever game the wraiths are playing here, there has to be a stop to it. He's a patient god, but this is his breaking point.

He grabbed his dagger again, but the wraiths began whispering in his ears.

Misleading, discouraging things.

"You'll never be able to find her."

"You shouldn't have come here."

"How could you enter a land full of resentment from people you've killed."

"You have no idea what you just entered."

The more sentences he heard, the colder he got. Ice carpets are starting to form on his skin, but that wasn't enough to stop his search.

If anything, he got more adamant.

He's not supposed to get wounded here. If he does, the resentment seeps deeper into him, but he doesn't care about that at the moment.

He used the dagger on himself, cutting his palm and splashing his own blood around.

Sensing his blood, the place took form. Grounds appeared, rivers mountains, trees and forests, skies.

A red glow of light peered from the open sky too.

Ryker landed gently on the ground, concentrating on his ears again.

He heard faint heartbeats, and he traced it to one of the caved mountains.

Entering, the cave, his steps halted.

Sitting pointedly on top of a wooden pike is the coffin, but there are wraiths surrounding it.

He could see their shadows crossing and mingling on the coffin body, and they started talking again.

"Thanks to your sweet blood, we had a meal after centuries of starving, why not give us more?"

"Do you even know what wounding yourself here means?"

Ryker looked at his palm. It's not healing like his other wounds.

"Of course it won't heal, you shouldn't have gotten wounded here, Ryker!" A husky wraith voice said.

"Too noisy!" Ryker snapped, moving with the wind to get to the pike. 

He was attacked by the shadow wraiths, but he fought back heads-on, tumbling and turning. 

His wounded palm seemed to be drawing strength from his chest, and it's exhausting him fast, so he cut himself again.

He didn't splash the blood like the first time. He brought the cut to his lips and sucked on his own blood.

"You're as unbelievable as they say!"

"Using your own blood to fight your exhaustion? You're a stubborn one, Ryker!"

"You might be fine now, but you'll never be able to get out of it when you leave this place! Two wounds means more agony for you!"

"Even your demigod status won't save you! Ryker Morozov!"

The voices of the wraiths got louder as Ryker sucked his own blood till he was content.

His ice grew in insurmountable sizes from the ground. The insane coldness and the ice water sent the wraiths away. Their shadows escaped the cave on their own.

Ryker finally had access to the coffin, but just as he was about to open, someone else popped out of a corner in the cave.

Jude.

"I knew you'd come, and here you are. Thanks for chasing the shadow wraiths away, but she's mine to take home." He muttered, his eyes sheening with both desire and superiority.

He dashed Ryker with a hit, strong and powerful.

Ryker knowingly waited for the hit, allowing it to touch him. He didn't even shake, but he could feel the energy of the FrostOrb in the hit.

"You absorbed power from the FrostOrb." He said, looking up at Jude who threw him a scoff.

"I wanted to take you as a brother even when you aren't really my brother, but you never wanted it. And that's why you gave me headache that night when I was with her at the bleak corridor!" 

"Now that I think about it, I should have killed you. It's less painful than headache." Ryker replied simply, voice running deep.

"What!"

"You Millions Family are never pure or kind. You're either greedy like your parents, hateful like the twins, pretentious like Jojo, or lustful like the blind Pearl. I've been waiting for you to share their sins for long, but I never knew you have your own different sin. Mad Desire for what doesn't belong to you." Ryker listed, his lips and chin so wet with his own deeply red blood.

"Doesn't belong to me?" Jude laughed. "Buying her with your own money means nothing. She belongs to the family, and I am part of the family, so if I say I want her, I'll get her. I am the one who healed the wound you gave her on the tummy. The twins once broke my legs just because they know how I feel about her, and I'm the same person who covered her with my expensive jacket when she slept on the bare ground of the Slaves Hall corridor! Tell me, what have you ever done? You only know how to hurt and kill and shed blood!" Jude snarled, breathings getting heavy.

"That's a pretty long list" Ryker smirked arrogantly. "Do you want me to go on my knees for thanks?"

"Get out of my way, I'm taking her. There are several nonhumans cities apart from Luminaria. I'll take her to one of them and we'll live happily till her heart opens for me." Jude said, and Ryker nearly laughed.

Slowly, he moved out of the way, paving it for him completely.

"All yours." He nodded at him.

Jude threw him a glance as if measuring his sincerity, then he looked at the coffin, knowing Shayla is waiting inside.

He sped there, but before he could even touch it, he was thrown out of that environment.

He rolled down the pike, landing on the cave ground, and from there, he lunged his own attack, but his green flame barely touched Ryker.

Ryker leapt to the air and touched the coffin lid with his leg.

He whipped around as the lid opened, lifting from the coffin and going down the cave to slap Jude's face fatally.

The wood broke on his skin, and two nails got stuck on his head and chin.

He went down, his body falling into the cave dust.

Ryker landed again, lowering his lashes to peek at the foolish male.

"Thinking you can tackle me after absorbing power from my FrostOrb was your first mistake. Such weak plans, it's boring to watch." He said as if Jude could hear him.

He looked back at the coffin that's now open, and his coldness nearly slipped from his face when he saw Shayla's condition inside.

Her skin is complete dead purple in color. No whiteness anywhere, and he could clearly see the imprints of her broken bones under her skin.

Her wounded face and forehead. Everything looks bad.

Angrily, he went down the pike and removed the remaining nails from the coffin lid. 

They're much, but that's just what's needed. He sent them all to Jude's body, making sure his bones got pierced to the marrow.

That barely calmed Ryker, but at least it did something.

He brought the coffin down from the pike and carried Shayla's body out of it, settling it down.

According to Cheyenne, Lilith marked her for death, but he's not seeing any mark on Shayla's forehead right now.

Could her warm aura have wiped the mark?

He waved that curiousness away for now, deciding to heal her bones first.

Placing his palm above her body, he began fusing her with his energy, and that made his ice act up more than it's already doing.

Ice carpets are even forming on his fingers, stopping the healing process, but he slammed his hand on the ground, breaking the ice and continuing the healing.

Under his eyes, her bones returned to their original position, and her legs straightened back, then he moved to the bullets on her upper body. 

Cheyenne would have removed them better with her nails, but the journey from here back to Luminaria is long, and the time here is faster, so if anymore time is wasted, Shayla might fall into coma, and it's dangerous with such bullets.

He summoned a sharp ice spike, and thanks to her unconsciousness, she won't feel the intense pain.

He inserted the spike under her chest skin, digging out the bullets.

It was easier than expected.

He moved to the head and facial wounds next. By now, the ice has formed on his whole body.

They're on his lashes, there are shards of it on his lips.

He's shivering internally, and he can't use her warmness right now cos she's unconscious. 

Despite his weakness and freezing iciness, he healed the wounds.

Everything. 

Including the ones on her back and hands.

He swept her up from the ground, into his shaky arms.

He was grunting, growling in a low tone as he walked out of the cave, his legs trembling on the ground.

He needs the warm spring pool right now. NOW.

Actually, even the spring pool won't help this iciness at all.

It has never escalated to this level before, and he knows it's because of his palm wounds.

The shadow wraiths were right about how he'd suffer, and the suffering is already starting even when he hasn't even left this place.

He walked past Jude's body on the ground, and ice were falling off him to the ground as he left the cave.

He held her tight in his arms, throughout.

--

[Luminaria, East Palace]

The moment Ryker vanished some hours ago, the bleedings of everyone stopped, and the palace physicians were called to remove the bullets from everyone of them.

When Pearl's bullet was removed, she collected it from the physician and hid it in her pocket.

She wasn't joking about cherished this bullet. It's hers.

Every member of the family is at the Starr balcony, and there's silence.

Lilith is present, too.

"I have just one question, and this is it. Why is Xena still alive? The moment that bastard shot everyone, you should have cut his sister's lifeline!" Sadie spoke up first.

"Killing his sister means he'd kill us all and leave this palace to wreck more havoc! That can't happen yet, especially now that we're close to finding his partner. He called her his only warmness" Azura replied.

"His partner?" Soren frowned.

"Shayla, it's very obvious." Lilith said from the rails, her hatred for Shayla higher than before.

"What! Shayla? How?" Sadie stood.

"I felt her warmness when she came to take me at the basement, I started suspecting from there." Pearl said.

Granny Jojo stayed silent.

"But she doesn't have the mark. He always checked for marks in his Groove. If she's really the partner, she should bear the mark on her back. It's impossible" Soren said.

"I guess he doesn't know. For once, someone actually kept something from that bastard. He visited the clouds castle, Eternus must have said something, which is why he came back to shoot everyone." Zeke said.

"Then make it quick. He has gone to the land of the condemned to get her, right? Take her and extract her FireStone from her, merge it with his FrostOrb and share it among us, make us become unconquerable demons and kill the two of them. Why wasting time?" Sadie said.

"She doesn't even have the mark yet, so calm down. He went to the land to take her, and he'd be weak by the time he arrives. We attack in the morning, take her and make the mark appear on her first before proceeding with the rest of the process." Azura explained.

"Cool." Sadie nodded.

"And I need to change his sister's lifeline location. Something tells me the bastard knows it." Zeke stood.

"You have skills in hiding things, how would he ever be able to find out?" Soren frowned.

"It's hightime you all stop underestimating him." Zeke said.

"I'm going back to sleep, it's just 5am." Sadie said, facing Soren.

Soren followed her out.

Zeke left next.

Pearl stood and wanted to walk out on her own, but Lilith held her.

"You are?" Pearl asked.

"Lilith the banshee, a new member of this family." Lilith said.

'New member? Fool' Pearl thought.

She didn't shoo her away though. She allowed her, and Lilith took her.

Sadie and Soren don't like her, Jude dislikes her too, so it's safe to make friends with the blind one.

After they left, Azura went to Jojo wh's still sitting.

"You loved him more than your real grandchildren, but look...he shot you too." She laughed at her face.

"That bastard is as cold-hearted as his ice, so I dunno what you were expecting. He likes no one in this family, stop your delusions, he'll never see you as a friend! You should come back to your senses, return to your family." She touched Jojo's arm.

"Your grandchildren needs you, mother. And I need you more." 

Jojo looked up, and Azura smiled before leaving the balcony.

Meanwhile, inside the twins room, Sadie feels uneasy, and there are questions in her mind.

She's under her bedsheets, and she kept rolling around in bed, unable to get back to sleep.

"Ren." She called.

Soren turned to her on his own bed.

"Yes?"

"After Granny took Pearl away from our room yesterday, did we go somewhere?" Sadie asked.

"No. We went to sleep directly." Soren replied instantly.

"Are you sure? We didn't go breaking into the unoccupied mansion with Mercury?" Sadie asked.

"We did nothing, just sleep." Soren said, pulling the quilt over his head.

"Why do I keep feeling like something happened and I can't remember?" Sadie muttered.

But then... Soren will never lie to her anyways.

She pulled the quilt over herself too, and sleep enveloped her.

--

[Next Morning, Slaves Kitchen]

Old Chef is cooking the family meal in the castle kitchen, but Xena is here, cooking the slaves breakfast with tears in her eyes.

The only moment she stopped crying was when the twins took her to break-in to the unoccupied mansion, but after she escaped, her tears has resumed.

When she was a kid, her only friend was her twin who eventually went missing, but then the heavens sent Shayla as a new one, and now this ruthless family took her too.

Who'd follow her to the next audition now? Who's going to dance with her or talk freely to her?

She was wiping tears with her arms as she washed the serving plates, and she didn't even know when Old Chef entered the kitchen.

"Xena, there's a news!"

She didn't turn.

"It's about Shayla! I heard Ryker returned last night, and he went to that dreadful land to bring her back." He broke the news, and she turned, hope filling her eyes.

"You don't mean that, yeah?"

"I heard he shot every member of the family with a weird gun just to get the pass." Old Chef further explained.

"What!" Xena's eyes popped.

--

[Heartless Tower]

Shayla could feel the sun behind her tight lids, the softness beneath her small body, and the painless state of her flesh and bones.

If this is hell, then hell is not as painful as they say at all. She feels at ease, and her skin feels new on her flesh. Even her lids are eager to open.

This isn't hell, and this isn't paradise either. Is there bed in paradise?

She's definitely in a bed, so...

Her eyes opened so fast, and she sat up to see she's really on a bed.

Soft, large one. She's in the middle like a small kitten, and the room on its own is as spacious.

Jeweled walls, golden grounds, the ceiling tiles are surely sapphire with crystals. The shine from everything is binding, and she had to cover her eyes slightly with her fingers.

This feels like paradise, and she can't even point where exactly it's located, but her own is more shocking.

Apart from her healed body, she's wearing a long but cute pink dress which covered her knees, stopping below it. It's short sleeved, so she can see her fresh arms.

This can't be her own skin, right?

She checked her foot too. It's usually so rough, but now it's like a newborn baby's. Even her palms and fingers.

She smells nice too.

What's going on?

She rushed down from the bed, wearing a fluffy shoe she saw beside it. She rushed out of the room, and surprisingly, the corridor is just like the room. The gold, sapphire, crystals and Jade. Diamonds, too.

She kept blinking in unexplained shock, but she stopped when she saw a mirror by a part of the unending wall. Her eyes widened properly at her own reflection.

No scars on her face, her lips are more luscious and pink than before, and her hair looks treated. Silky.

It looks longer too, and fuller.

What's going on?

Fear licked the back of her neck, and she moved forward, breaking into a penguin run. She stepped into the first room she saw after a three minutes sprint, and her shock doubled up when she saw Cheyenne.

The room looks like a kitchen.

It's actually a kitchen, and Cheyenne is cooking. She's with a knife, chopping green cabbages.

Her eyes met Shayla's, and Shayla froze in place.

Yeah, this is hell.

So far wicked Cheyenne is here, then it's definitely hell.

With two long strides, Cheyenne got to her. Shayla pressed her body to the wall trepidly.

With empty, dark eyes, Cheyenne raised the knife to her neck...

"D-don't" Shayla stuttered, her breaths seizing.

Cheyenne's lips curved with intentions only known to her.

She dropped the knife and returned to the cabbages she was slicing.

"Considering what My Lord went through before he could get you out, I should be killing you right now, but you're covered by his grace, so I dare not touch you. Lucky you." She said.

"What he went through? I'm not dead?" Shayla went closer to her, suddenly finding her boldness.

"Don't ask me questions, Tiny. I won't answer you. Take your questions to Sage. He's the nice one." Cheyenne replied without looking at her.

She's so focused on her cabbages.

"Tiny?" Shayla blinked, using her fingers to slip her smooth black hair behind her ear.

"That's what I'll be calling you from now on. I'll never call you by your real name so get used to it." Cheyenne replied, still not looking at her.

"I'm not...tiny." Shayla said.

Cheyenne finally looked at her, and there's a smirk hanging loose under her lips.

"Put your wrists together and give them to me." She ordered.

Shayla did that, and Cheyenne held her two wrists with just one hand.

"I can hold your two wrists with just one hand, and you're not tiny? You should look in the mirror. Your stature is so small, but thankfully, you have a slightly pretty face, so it's all good, Tiny." She said.

"Fine, but where are we?" Shayla asked curiously, shifting her gaze to the cabbages.

"I told you, I won't answer your questions. You don't even know your own identity." Cheyenne replied.

"My own identity?" Shayla asked.

"Do you know Lord Ryker's identity?" Sage asked, strolling into the wealthy, shinny kitchen.

He sat on the kitchen golden island, and Shayla faced him.

"You're...."

"Sage." Sage nodded.

"Oh...Sage. Nice to meet you properly." She smiled.

"You haven't answered my question." Sage said kindly, the sound of Cheyenne's cabbage chopping filling the kitchen. She's doing it so fast like she's really good with the knife.

"Yes. I know Lord Ryker is a demigod who carries the millennial ice inside him, and he owns the FrostOrb. He has the tattoo of a frozen sword in the middle of his back." Shayla said.

"Then do you know you're a demigoddess who carries the millennial fire inside you? And you own a FireStone. You have the tattoo of a burning fire in the middle of your back, but it's not showing yet." Sage explained.

"Me? Demigoddess? Fire sword? What's that?" Shayla said in confusion.

"Stop wasting your energy, Sage. It's useless, she won't understand." Cheyenne said from her side.

"Where are we?" Shayla asked.

"Heartless Tower." Sage asked.

Shayla staggered, eyes coming wide again. Her ears can't even believe.

"What! How come? How am I inside...." She gasped.

"Listen!" Cheyenne dropped her knife, losing all patience.

"The Millions Family threw you into the land of the condemned, a place inhabited by the shadow wraiths of everyone Lord Ryker has killed. I mean their spirits, so the place is full of resentment which could hurt him internally. Still, he went in there to look for you!" She snapped.

Shayla's eyes widened again, her chest thundering too.

"In there, getting wounded would only increase his chances of getting weak, but he cut himself once to search for you, and twice to control his exhaustion. He found you, healed your wounds and broken bones and got rid of your bullets! He brought you here when he was covered in ice!" Cheyenne continued.

Shayla swallowed.

"Yeah... Lord Ryker was covered in ice completely when he arrived here, and his palm wounds has allowed the resentment of that place to get into him, so he's internally wounded. He's currently soaking in his spring pool." Sage said softly.

Shayla shook her head.

"No. Why would he do..."

"He's always cold, you're his warm partner." Sage cut her off.

"Part...ner?" Shayla stuttered, not knowing how to process all these. The info dump is too much.

"Why do you think he allows you to hold him? Are you slow?" Sage asked.

"Of course she's a slow retard, anyone who lived all her life in a cage would be dull." Cheyenne rolled eyes.

"Be kind" Sage nudged her, but she rolled eyes again.

"I'm not slow. I just don't know much, but I promise you don't have to tell me twice. I'm a fast learner so I grasp things fast." Shayla said slowly.

"Great" Cheyenne shrugged, pushing a plate of salad to her.

"Eat, and we can talk about what's next." She said, handing her a fork.

Sage gave her a stool, and Shayla sat, still looking so out of it.

She took the fork though, but she was only able to eat one forkful out of the rich salad.

She can't even taste what she's eating on her palate and buds.

"Can I...see him?" She asked, looking up at the two knights above her.

Cheyenne's reply was curt "No."

"Yes." Sage said.

"We left Eternus at the sky castle, and Lord Ryker's order was to hold him down. We need to go back to the sky castle." Cheyenne explained.

"But he needs her to calm down his ice that's acting up." Sage said.

"The spring pool is enough for that. We can't take her to the Groove without his permission, and he's not conscious enough to give it." Cheyenne said.

"He has been in the pool since 6, and this is 8. Has he ever soaked for two hours before? I've changed the hot water close to twenty times, but his ice kept freezing it. He's still shivering, Cheyenne. If the Millions Family decide to attack like this, then we're cooked." Sage said.

Cheyenne looked at Shayla, and Shayla stood.

"Please... If he's in that condition cos of me, I should at least help. Please, please." She said.

"So bothersome, follow up." Cheyenne scowled and led the way out of the kitchen.

"Let's go." Sage smiled at Shayla, and her face brightened up.

She followed them.

There's a path to the Bamboo Groove behind the tower, so they just walked that way.

Cheyenne and Sage didn't get there with her though. They left her at the entrance, and she entered by herself.

This is a place she once nearly got killed when Lilith tricked her to enter. Now she's entering for a different purpose, and this entry is authorized.

She found the spring pool fast, thanks to the fact that she has entered before.

Ryker sat beside the pool wall, eyes closed and chest bare.

His arms are inside the water with him, not spread like the other time.

She can't see the frozen sword tattoo behind him cos the ice covered it, and it covered every visible part of his bare upper body too.

He looks frozen completely just like Sage said. A blue halo covered the spot he sat in.

"Moy Vladika" Shayla mouthed with urgency, lifting her legs. She rushed to the pool entrance.

She slipped her fluffy shoes away and dropped herself into the water. 

It's madly cold at first, but it didn't last a second.

She moved forward, walking in the pool. It reached her chest as she's not so tall.

The water did large swirls, reacting to her intrusion. It made loud rumbling noises and popped blue bubbles as she moved close.

The blue halo thickened, but Ryker never moved, till she got to him.

At first, she didn't even know where to touch or start from.

A simple touch won't solve this problem. He's far too frozen that it's scary to her.

She lifted her hand to touch his pretty face.

The coldness she felt made her gulp. 

How's he holding all that in?

There's only one way, and she didn't even think twice before going for it.

She slid her hands under her dress, lifting it up above her waist.

Slowly, she peeled the dress away from her body, leaving her in a thin white underwear and lace panties.

Their skins will be closer this way.

She moved closer again, taking his head and gently bringing it to her shoulder. She wrapped her arms around his body.

She wrapped her legs on him underwater too, rubbing her fingers on his skin, dragging it slow and steady. She rested her lips on his neck and placed her chin on his shoulder.

Her eyes went shut, too.

"Moy Vladika...can you feel me?" She whispered in a hush, moving her lips up to his right ear.

"Hmm? Can you? I'm here for you. I won't leave till you stir. Give me a sign." She whispered again.

Her arms went tighter around his body, and her fingers caressed slower, gentler.

The water went silent around them, just the sound of her soft whisperings and her fast breathings.

"Please... Cheyenne will hate me again if I leave this place without you waking up. Lord Ryker..."

He's getting warm. She could feel the change in his skin temperature.

She pressed her thumb to the back of his neck, drawing the rest of her fingers down his smooth face.

"Open your eyes... open up. Please."

Her lips moved back to his neck, then it pressed down his skin again, warm and tender.

"Wake up... Pretty demigod." She added to her soft whisperings, and at that same moment, she felt a slight stir from him. 

It was almost nonexistent, but she felt it. It was there, and she felt his lashes stirring, too.

His breath fanned her skin suddenly, and before she knew it, his large arms curled wholly around her body.

A cold gasp spilled from her mouth.

"You're always so loud, Firefly." His deep voice echoed through the waters.
