★

Was that the wind?

Definitely has to be the wind, cos ain't no way Ryker would speak that casually to her after she entered his sacred pool.

His large arms is even around her, too. He hugged her back? Why isn't he tearing her apart instead?

Last time she entered this Groove without even stepping into the pool, he killed her if not for the entrance of Cheyenne, now she entered the pool and he's talking this simply?

She unwrapped her legs from his body, and her arms fell from his skin. She slipped her shoulder away from the weight of his head, moving away from him.

She made sure there's a large gap between them before she looked up, her eyes catching the deep darkness of his own.

He looks the same, but the ice is gone from his skin. Now that she's seeing him up close, she wasn't wrong about the most attractive parts of his body.

His fingers are hella fine. Also, his skin is richly clean. That's expected from someone who stays cold and iced most of the time. He's unconventional.

When she realized how long she has been ogling shamelessly for, she pushed her wet hair away from her face, and her face went down.

"I heard you were sick, and I heard my warmness could help, so I offered to come share it with you. Cheyenne and Sage allowed me in here, I didn't come without permission so don't hurt me like the last time."

She could hear the tremble in her own voice as she talked.

"I was sick?" Ryker's voice attacked that statement immediately. She looked up to see the glare on his face, and it's obvious he took offence.

Is that not the right word?

"I...I heard you were freezing. Yeah, freezing." She quickly corrected.

"I might have to give you the list of words to use and to not use around me, Firefly. I'm never sick, even in my weakest moments, no one in this city or beyond can defeat me. I'm not some useless furry puppy." He continued, and Shayla found herself smiling inwardly.

Of course, that's more like him. He's very arrogant, and he doesn't even try to hide it.

She snapped out of t when she remembered she's wearing only a thin underwear and lace panty.

Her eyes went down her chest, and she let out another gasp at what she saw.

Her small nîpples are puckering through the thin material of the underwear.

She looked around to find her dress floating not far from her, and she walked there quickly.

The pool whirled turbulently around her till she arrived, but before she could pick up the dress, it drowned.

She dipped her hand underwater, but she found nothing.

"Where did it go?" She asked herself, her breathings irregular as she dipped her hand underwater again.

"I've never been a fan of lousy colour pink, Cheyenne must be out of her mind to pick out this dress for you." Ryker's voice said behind her, causing her head to shoot up.

He's with the wet dress, holding it up and giving it a stare of disapproval.

How did that even get to him?

"That's mine!" Shayla pointed. 

By now, the pool water has plastered her underwear to her skin, the whole shape of her breàsts has been carved temptingly on her chest.

"I know," Ryker faced her. 

She gulped, covering her chest with her arms.

"You were all-over me some moments ago, whispering intimate things to wake me up, and now that I'm up, you're suddenly shy?" His head angled, a smirk trying hard to pull his lips down.

"That was because you were freezing! Now that you're alright, I need my dress back." She pointed her palm forward, using her second hand to brush her wet hair back.

Darn, she'd have to find a band to tie this stupidly long hair. She's not used to long hairs. 

When we was still in the Dark Room, her head was shaved every year, so her hair has never grown longer than shoulder level, but now it's reaching the middle of her back which she's not comfortable with.

"Here, come get it." Ryker pointed the dress at her, water dripping from it back to the now steamy pool.

She looked at the dress, then at his mischievous face. No one knows what he's thinking with that foxy head of his, it's hard to pinpoint.

Nevertheless, she moved to him, but he dropped the dress back in the water before her hand could touch it, and he grabbed her wrist instead.

She was with him again in another before she could talk, and she had to use her hand to push his chest so her own won't slam on it.

She looked up, their eyes meeting with a ferocity she can't plot.

"Did you wonder how your wounds healed? How your face lost all scars, how your hair regained healthiness and how you have so much vitality coursing through you?" He whispered, surprising her.

Ryker is whispering?

"I...yes. I was—"

"Don't stutter, it's boring. Aren't you always so bold with me?" He interrupted, his hold on her fragile wrist so tight that she feared it might break into two.

"I was told you healed me, and I was planning to say thank you." She said, looking up at his face again to hold eyes with him, boldly.

"Good. Get stronger, your warmness is becoming insufficient. You have to get stronger and reveal your fire mark on your own. Accumulate more warmness." He told her directly.

"Am I really...your partner? Is that why I didn't freeze when everyone froze last time? Am I a demigoddess like Sage said?" She blinked.

"Does it sound good to your ears?" He stares deeper. She blinked again.

"I just thought...I should ask."

She looked down, but his second hand went below her chin. He held it firmly like an egg, forcing her face back up.

"You look at me whenever we're conversing." He said seriously.

"Yes sir...I mean...yes, Moy Vladika." She said, unable to fight her own nervousness.

His hands on her wrist and chin is making her heart pound with fear.

A smile lingered on Ryker's lips at her silliness, but Shayla didn't get to see it before it dissipated.

"You're to stay in my tower from now on. You can't go out without Cheyenne or Sage, and the only person who's allowed to come see you is Xena. I'll make an exception since you both formed a rapport."

She smiled brightly. "Really?"

"And stay away from every member of the family, including Jojo." He said.

"But she's not bad, she's—"

She stopped talking when she saw the unrelenting glare on Ryker's face.

"I'll stay away." She said quickly.

He let go of both her chin and wrist, and she quickly took her dress from the water. She slipped it on easily.

At the same time, they both heard a loud crashing sound from the tower, their eyes went that direction.

"What's going on?" Shayla muttered.

"Go back to the tower, and stay in the room you woke up in." He instructed.

Shayla didn't argue.

She just nodded and walked out of the pool, slipping her shoes back on before rushing away.

Ryker made sure she was already so gone before lifting his left palm to look at the two cut wounds.

Shayla's warmness might have healed his freezing coldness, but it did nothing to heal his internal wound, and that's why the cuts are still there.

The crash they heard earlier means the family is here to tackle him in his weak state. 

He expected this.

He walked out of the pool, grabbing his robe jacket from the side.

--

[Tower, Center Space]

There's just Sage and Cheyenne in front of the Millions Family clansmen.

Azura and Zeke are the only direct family members among them. There's no children or Granny Jojo.

"Are you both willing to die in place of your master?" Azura said proudly, raising her chin and shoulders.

"Attacking when he's weak just shows how feeble you are. Why not come when he's on all prowls?" Sage spat.

"Handover the partner, and this fight won't happen. We might take you both in as one of the royal guards since you're from reputable family of Knights anyways, and as for Ryker himself, he'd be dying with his partner the moment we split their FrostOrb and FireStone." Zeke smiled, his pride matching Azura's.

"Such heavy arrogance, and you both are still drawing power from his FrostOrb to survive. Whatever is making you think you can actually take the partner away from this place today has to be the most foolish thing ever! It isn't even the start of the heat yet, so stop getting ahead of yourselves. Two old fools." Cheyenne insulted.

Azura flashed a sudden thunderbolt at her, and Cheyenne was flung to the wall, but she stood her ground, wiping the slim line of blood from the middle of her lips.

"Is that all you've got, Azura? Your thunder ability that's useless unless you eat souls? Pathetic." She laughed.

It was Zeke who retaliated to that speech. He plowed at the metal wall behind Cheyenne, and the metal went in, pulling Cheyenne inside the wall, but Sage intervened.

He dragged Cheyenne away from the closing wall and rolled the two of them over the sleek ground.

The wall lost Cheyenne, so it took one of the clansmen instead, drawing him inside before closing up like a mouth.

"There's a mark of death on the partner's body, I'm sure you've seen it, so it's either you hand her over and let her die for a good cause at least, or you both and your master dies with her." Azura declared.

"Funny how you think a demigoddess aura will bow to the mark of a lowly banshee." Sage laughed. "She might not possess the mark yet, but she's not going to bow!" 

He removed the bracelet on his wrist, and it grew longer to his rope. 

Cheyenne's long nails grew, too.

"Well then...as you wish." Zeke nodded at his wife and the rest of the clansmen. 

They attacked the knights, two against a large number. 

The fight was tough, relentless.

Thunder cracked from Azura.

Grounds broke with Zeke's powers.

The rest of the clansmen stood in a circled formation, protecting the couple from the hits of the knights.

It's Azura against Cheyenne, Sage against Zeke.

Azura sent the third thunder strike to Cheyenne, and she laughed when Cheyenne fell on the ground again, getting wounded on the ribcage.

"What was your motivation before you decided to face me?" Azura asked, the mock so heavy.

"The fact that you won't be leaving this place with the partner. Moron!" Cheyenne groaned, pushing herself up from the ground and leaping to the air so fast.

Azura did the same, summoning her thunder again, but she wasn't so lucky this time.

Cheyenne's nails met her back, the nails venom sank deep into her flesh as it peeled.

She staggered in the air, blood soaking her robe.

"Azura!" Zeke yelled from the ground, but Sage didn't give him the chance to leapt up.

He threw his rope at Zeke's neck like a noose, pulling it from behind and nearly taking his head away from his neck. The rope left venomous imprints on his neck.

The clansmen strengthened their formation, and Azura fell from the sky to the ground.

She gave Sage a merciless dash, and he unwillingly let go of Zeke.

Zeke sped to Azura's side, both conjoining their abilities. Their eyes lost all white, it turned dark with the lids, and the skies rumbled angrily.

"They're going to conjure acid rain?" Sage muttered.

"Cowards!!" Cheyenne yelled, but none of them ran.

The rule is not to ever run no matter how hard the trial is.

Help will always come, and help indeed came cos the dark clouds suddenly brightened.

The weather cooled down,  Azura's thunders stopped. The tower turned silent except for the rumble coming from the dark skies.

"My Lord?" Sage and Cheyenne chorused, and he answered with his domination appearance.

Ryker stood midair, looking casual in his bathing robe which is getting blown by the morning air.

The moment he landed on the ground and waved his hand, the acid rain started, pouring only in the clansmen side of the tower.

They didn't escape. They began melting away like plastics in scorching blaze. 

"What do you think you're doing!" Zeke yelled.

"Exactly what you were planning to do to my knights before I arrived. You don't like tasting your own cup of tea?" Ryker smirked.

"You abomination of nature!" Azura spat, summoning a strike together with her husband.

Ryker dodged, but it wasn't fast enough. He got slightly hit, and his head lurched forward weakly. His lips turned slightly pale.

"Lord Ryker!" His knights chorused fearfully, but he dispelled their worries immediately.

He turned back, throwing a pile of thorny ice at the couple.

It split into two, going for each of them. Azura escaped her share, but Zeke didn't.

The ice thorn delved into his belly, and he went down, rolling bloodily on the ground till a pillar took the weight of his body, stopping him.

"Zeke!" Azura slipped to him, bending beside his bleeding body to hold him.

"Zeke!!"

He was already bleeding profusely, and he's not even talking despite the fact that his eyes are open.

Azura looked behind and saw every clansmen they brought had been melted down by the acid.

She bit herself hard before looking at Ryker who looks madly unapologetic.

"This isn't over, Ryker Morozov." She told him in a tone that says it really isn't over.

She carried Zeke's body and vanished into the morning.

"Their clansmen were too much, I think. And that's why they decided to come waste the lives of some of them here." Sage said, taking a look at the melted bodied on the ground.

"My Lord!" Cheyenne turned back with him, and they ran back to Ryker who's still standing where he is.

"My Lord, are you ok?" Sage asked, his concern glued to his eyes.

Ryker held his chest, and their eyes widened when he spat out blood.

"Why is it still like this? The ice is gone from your skin!" Sage said.

"Must be the internal wound. That one won't heal unless his palm wounds heal." Cheyenne said.

"Then we have to act fast and find solutions. No one can know he's wounded internally till we solve this. Else, they'd take the opportunity to do their deeds." Sage said.

"How weak do you think I really am?" Ryker faced him with a angry glare, and his head went down.

"I don't mean it that way, I'm just too worried. If they take her, then it's over." Sage said.

"No one's taking her. She can step out once a day, and Xena is allowed to come visit her here after thorough inspection." Ryker said.

"Understood." Cheyenne said.

"Do you really know where Xena's lifeline is?" Sage asked, and Ryker's hard eyes met with his own.

"Eternus had a heart to heart chat with you both?" 

"We just want to know the truth. If you know where her lifeline is, it means we can actually leave this palace without issues, but you're not trying to do that, which means you're after something in his palace. What could that be?" Cheyenne asked.

"Later today, we're visiting Liza, her mother." He said, ignoring her question.

They went quiet.

"And no one is to interrupt my rest session. I'll be at the Phoria." He said.

"Not even her is allowed to come see you?" Cheyenne asked.

"I said no one." Ryker stressed hard on the last word.

"And clean this melted mess." He pointed at the acid rain victims before vanishing off.

"Heard that?" Cheyenne faced Sage.

"Not even Tiny is allowed to come see him at his Phoria, cos I know you. You'd want to send her there at any slight opportunity." 

"I thought you were starting to like her?" Sage scoffed.

"She's my master's partner, so I'm still trying to like her. The process is still ongoing." She replied, walking off.

"Can you be less intimidating?" Sage went after her with a smile.

--

[Shayla's Room]

Before Shayla even arrived back in her room earlier, her pink dress already dried up, thanks to her body warmth.

She sat in her new room, following Ryker's instructions. 

She was there throughout the fight, and though she didn't witness the fight, she knows Ryker won, which is why she's still here, breathing.

She's by the window of the room, and just like the room at Granny's place, this room has a wind chime by the window too, this one is pure gold. 

There's a really fancy dreamcatcher too, and she hasn't been able to stop touching both. She's in love with them.

She has been trying to calculate just how many billion treasures are in this room alone, talk more of the whole tower. It's too wealthy.

What kind of job does he do that he's as filthy-rich as this? 

She just doesn't get it.

Also, if she's the partner, does that mean they might get entangled romantically in the future?

That thought made her slap herself, and she blinked back to the present.

Romantically? With Ryker? That god looks like he doesn't even know what romance is, and her?

She has no idea too, what would she probably know?

She sighed out and left the room, using the door she sighted at the left exit. She found herself in an orchard, and couldn't blink as she checked it out. There are various fruits trees.

Apple, plums, tangerines, even berries.

If she had a long stick, she'd have loved to have an apple, but she didn't want to overdo, so she gave up.

Luckily, there's a swing chair in the middle of the trees, she sat on it and began swinging herself softly.

She didn't last at Granny's side, what if she doesn't last here too?

What if one day she does something to really annoy  him, and he hurts her so much or even kills her?

That thought made her hold her chest. The imagination of his anger is dangerous, and it made her scared.

He told her to get stronger, that her warmness is becoming insufficient.

Does that mean she's supposed to be warmer than this for him, but her hidden mark is stopping that?

The mark is really doing a good job hiding her powers. Who'd have known she's a goddess.

Maybe she'd ask Cheyenne for power practices? That's if the wicked lady would even agree.

"Shay!!" The unmistakable hyper voice broke into the orchard.

She stood from the swing chair.

"Xena!" She shouted back.

Xena sprinted towards her, and her eyes turned wide when she saw her.

"Wait...are you real? Are you really my friend? What happened to you!"

"What do you mean? Is there something on my face?" Shayla touched her face gently.

"You look mad different! From the hair, to the skin, to the dress. You smell nice too! Did you get reborn?" Xena said, her shock palpable.

Shayla smiled.

"It's your brother...he did some things and I woke up to see myself like this. They said I'm his partner."

"I heard about it in the palace and I was doubting, so it's real? Omg!" Xena  jumped twice.

"There's a lot of stories" Shayla said.

"Tell me everything! And I have plenty gists too!" Xena sat with her on the swing chair.

"Gists on?" 

"I nearly got raped." Xena said.

"What!"

--

[Main Castle, FrostOrb Hall]

Azura set Zeke down on the table when she arrived.

The ice thorn is inside him, and it's not even melting. It's not gonna melt unless Ryker decides to let him go.

Zeke isn't gonna wake up till it melts.

Despite the fact that Ryker went to face shadow wraiths in the condemned land, he's still that strong? Was the wraiths resentment not enough to weaken him?

She's trying her best to understand how he works, but it seems he's smarter than everyone here, and it's not making any sense.

They took him from his mother the day he was born, they killed his mother and brought him here with his father and sister. They watched him grow powerful, so why is he still so closed off and unrideable?

Why is he still hard to grasp?

She looked at the FrostOrb, and she felt a sharp tang of pain from her back, remembering how Cheyenne's nails wounded it.

She cussed under her breaths, then she healed herself with the orb, casting out the venom.

She healed Sage's rope venom from Zeke's neck too, but that's all she can do for him.

He can only lay here and absorb power from the orb so he won't fall in coma. She'd have to find away to bring him back to consciousness.

His Channeler Shell shone in his pocket, and she brought it out...

"Who's it?" She spoke into it, but no voice came. The shine on the the shell stopped.

"Who was that?" She muttered.

"My Lady!" Two guards came inside, holding Jude tight.

There are several nails on his body, and on his face. He's barely alive.

"Jude!" Azura nearly fell. "Jude!!"

"He appeared in front of the castle and lost consciousness. Judging by thick the dark aura surrounding him, he probably went to the land of the condemned using his soul as collateral to enter." Lilith said, entering the hall.

"What!!" Azura staggered.

--

[Granny's Chamber]

Jojo sat in front of her mirror, but she's not doing anything.

Not communicating with her shadow or trying to learn techniques from her reflection.

She's just lost in a haze, and her mind is refusing to leave how Ryker had shot her last night.

She's well aware that she hasn't completely earned his trust. 

Ryker doesn't anyone.

He doesn't even trust his knights, so why would he trust her, a Granny of the Millions Family?

He works alone, and his plans are only known to him.

The kindness he showed her were all fake with no crumbs of sincerity underneath.

But one thing is for sure, she has never taken him for granted, and she really loves him sincerely more than her grandchildren.

She'd never betray him. Staying still and not fighting for Shayla was because her fight would have amounted to nothing.

She's not even as strong as the twins, not to talk of Azura and Zeke.

"Granny?" Pearl came out of the inner chamber, leaning on the wall to keep her legs steady on the ground.

Jojo turned around to her.

"I heard from some passing guards that my parents were defeated by Ryker. Isn't it so sweet to hear? Their defeat?" She said.

"Weren't you boldly providing information about his partner at the family meeting?" Jojo stood.

"That's because I want her gone. You don't even understand me." Pearl scoffed, finding a chair with her hands. She took her seat.

"You want her gone, so you lied that she took you out? How were you able to get a phone to upload yourself on the lumi web that fast if not that you've been planning this for a long time, and she's just a tool you wanted to use!" Jojo said.

Pearl stood back.

"Lilith said you once took that slave in here like your daughter, are you seeing me like her replacement? A slave replacement? I am Pearl Millions, might be blind but still a member of this family! Did you forget?" She seethed.

"Pearl!" Jojo snapped.

"I want the demigod! If it means sacrificing my soul to get my sight back just to see that handsome face and kiss his fingers, then I'd do it! You don't even know the arousing wet dreams I get about this man so shut up if you have nothing beneficial to say!" Pearl shouted.

"Oh...girl. You've gone insane." Jojo muttered.

[City, Vega Mansion]

It's 6pm, and Mercury just finished dressing up.

She's going to be visiting the Lumi park with the twins and several dancers from the ballet school will be coming, it'd be fun.

Been a while since they last had fun together, and she's eager.

She dressed to kill, making sure her skirt is shorter than normal. Her makeup is hot tonight, too. And her bóobs are popping out of her bra cups. Many necklaces rested on it, and her earrings are big.

All for Soren.

She sprayed herself, and she had a look in the mirror, making sure it's all too much before leaving the room.

By the door, she got a call from Sadie, and she picked excitedly.

"Hey, bestie. Are you at the amusement park already?" 

"Yeah, and I think they're going to turn it to a party or something. Be fast, I and Soren are already waiting. Also, the idiots from West Palace are here... I'm talking about Blaise and Faye. I'm not seeing Kade the bastard anywhere but I'm sure he's coming. You know the night will be fun already, don't forget the ashes." Sadie said all at once and hung up.

"Yes! I've not had fun since long. It's finally time." Mercury gleamed, walking past the study where her mother Esme is standing.

"Your dress is everything. I love it." Esme smiled.

"Thanks mother, don't feel lonely without me. Will be back before daybreak." Mercury kissed her cheek before exiting the house.

Esme sat on the sofa, her right leg going over the left one as she took a glass of wine, staring at her Channeler Shell on the table.

"Why waw she the one who talked on his shell earlier? Is he ok?" She wondered worriedly.

[East Palace, Heartless Tower]

After talking for what looks like forever and ever, Xena was ready to leave the tower at past 6 to go cook dinner, and Shayla was rumbling as she prepared to take her leave.

"I'm sure there are lots of things to play with in this tower, it's full of treasures anyways. You'll see me tomorrow morning." Xena said.

"Fine, tommorow morning it is." Shayla let go of her hand.

"By the way, you haven't mentioned your twin sister's name before." Shayla said.

"Rumi. Her name's Rumi, and we look exactly the same, except she has blonde hair." Xena replied, then she left the tower.

"Rumi? Xena? Doesn't sound like twins name though." Shayla said to herself as she returned inside.

She could feel the emptiness and silence immediately.

If Cheyenne or Sage are in here, she'd feel the aliveness instead, but this place feels dead right now, meaning even Ryker isn't around.

Where did they go?

She took a tour around the visible parts of the tower for five times, and when she got tired, she went to the kitchen and picked a huge bar of osmanthus candy from the freezer.

She has no idea what it is, but she opened it and ate one. She liked it immediately.

She came back outside, sitting by the orchard and eating the candies one by one, melting the taste in her mouth.

But most importantly, where did Ryker go? This loneliness is heavy.

If his face is around, he'd surely be his intimidating self, but at least he talks to her, so is it safe to say she wants him here?

If he's still not back by the time she eats every of this candy, then she'd go have her bath and sleep. So she waited.

By the door.

[City, Kings Mansion]

The news of Shayla's survival hasn't reached the city. Everyone is still under the belief that she's dead, but Liza could feel her healthiness.

She's her mother anyway, and there's a thin string connecting them together, so she could feel that she's fine, and that's why she's able to eat this evening.

She had a small sip from her vinegar tea, and her phone screen lit up on the table. She picked it up, it's her manager.

"Yes?"

"The voting poll for the most beautiful woman in Luminaria is out tonight in preparation for the Feast, and as usual, Esme Vega is the first to register before others. Do you want to contest with her again this year?" The manager asked.

Liza paused, looking at her flashy pink nails.

She's the first woman to start using this adult flashy nails style, but Esme stole the inspo, and she's shamelessly claiming rights.

Esme is a thief, not only good at stealing her styling ideas, but also good at stealing men.

She's in a secret affair, she can't just prove it yet. She's very sure Esme is dating someone's husband.

And this year's contest? Maybe it's time to make a difference.

"Register for me, I'm going against her again." She told the manager and hung up flat.

The moment she dropped the phone, Kade appeared on the chair beside hers.

He has been upstairs, dressing up for the amusement party.

He took her teacup and was about to have a drink from it when three other presence joined them in the house, and they both rose to their feet, looking overly stunned.

Ryker and his knights stood by the door, their presence so powerful that Liza quickly went on her knees, interlinked her fingers in a respectful kowtow.

"My Lord."

"You already know why I'm here." Ryker spoke calmly, but there's a lotta danger under that tone.

"Will you start talking, or should I give you a reason to talk? Cheyenne can can flay your facial skin and hang it on your gate to dry." he continued.

Cheyenne sighed behind him.

"And Sage can snap your neck without lifting a finger." He said. 

Sage smiled, and Liza gulped thrice—one after the other, her fingers are badly shivering.

"I don't have to touch you to torture you, and you know, but I just thought of awakening your brain." Ryker sat on the edge of a couch, staring down at the woman on her knees like she's an ant he could end her life under his shoe soles.

"My Lord!!" Kade suddenly fell on his knees too, his eyes full of respect like he just met his mentor.

"The 150 billion slave is her daughter, and she's your partner! Mother hired a spell caster to hide her mark! I've been wanting to meet you to tell you this just so you could kill her, but when I heard she has been sent to the land of the condemned, I was overjoyed that the slût is finally gon......."

Kade couldn't say more, cos he bit his tongue, but it wasn't a mistake. Ryker made him do it.

He was bleeding from the mouth before he knew it, and Sage grabbed him by the hair, bringing his face to the dinning table where he slammed it down on the glasswares.

More blood, but Kade couldn't even cry. In fear, he covered his mouth with his palm.

"Fuckwit! You do not interrupt him when he's talking!" Sage crashed Kade's head on another set of glassware, harder.

"Please.... please!!" Kade begged loudly, finally finding his voice. His blood cascaded into his mouth.

Liza is still down, trembling.

Ryker is still sitting, calmly. Cheyenne is still behind him, impatiently.

"I really hired a spell caster to hide her mark cos I was scared! I was scared you were going to kill her the moment you knew her identity! She might be a product of rape, but she's still my daughter. I'm sorry...I didn't mean to offend your lordship!" Liza talked, shaking her head as she fearfully looked at Ryker's poker face.

"The spell caster, who was it? Who cast the spell to cover the mark?" He asked without stress.

Liza lifted herself slightly on her knees, her damp palms going to her laps to stay.

"I haven't seen her since then, but her name was Rumi. Rumi Morozov."

[Kings Mansion]

Ryker and his knights left about a minute ago, and the lost peace is starting to crawl back into the abode.

Liza is currently smoothening her nightwear. She's thankful that he didn't kill her.

For someone known for his blood-spilling kink, she's lucky to still be alive.

She strolled down the living room and had a seat, at the same time she's still trying to calm her shaky nerves.

She could still feel him in this house after he's gone. Just how strong is his goddamn presence?

Kade is still by the ruined dinning table, trying to heal his broken head, but no amount of energy is enough to do it. It's like Sage poisoned the wound, so it won't be easy to close.

He kept sniffling as he wiped blood from the surface of the wound, repeatedly with a piece of cloth.

"He doesn't want her dead? How!!!" He suddenly yelled, dumping the piece of cloth.

"If he kills his partner and steals her FireStone to merge with his own FrostOrb inside him, that's the only way for him to attain stage 3 of his powers! That's the only way he'd become truly untouchable!"

"That is the only way for him to ascend to the Nine Heavens and live forever as a Celestial Being! You once told the story so I know it! But what? He's not interested in killing her? He's not interested in that insane power? It's a lie! There must be something stopping him. He must be planning something, it's a damn lie!" 

He was panting as he spilled the words out of his itching throat, his tongue and head bled more.

"Save your strength, Kade, your wounds won't heal unless you calm your arteries and veins, you're poisoned." Liza cautioned from the living room.

"Zip it, mother! How do you expect me to move on from this?" Kade stalked to the living room, pulling her up from the couch.

"I've been waiting for this day all my life, waiting to meet him face-to-face and tell him who the wretched partner is, but I got disappointed. There has to be another way, I and your daughter can't coexist, especially now that she'd be known as the demigoddess. I can't take that!" 

"Do you only hate her because she's your stepsister? Or is there another reason to your blind hatred for her when she did absolutely nothing wrong to you?" Liza said.

Kade plowed her back to the couch with a very rough push. Her toes got wounded by the soles of his boots.

"Kade..." She looked up at him, her eyes streaked.

"If one day it gets out that the product of rape is my stepsister, you have no idea how my pride would be hurt! She won't be the reason I lose my status in this city. If the demigod won't kill her, I'll do it myself!"

With that declaration, he picked the bloodstained piece of cloth back from where he had dropped it, then he stormed out of the house.

Liza rearranged her hair, letting out a sigh. She turned on the humidifier on the table and relaxed on the couch.

'Rumi, you better hide well. You can't lift the spell till the day we both discussed. You can't let your brother catch you till then. This is the only way I can get my power back, and the only way my daughter can survive on her own' she thought.

She shut her eyes.

[East Palace, Heartless Tower]

Ryker descended with his knights at the entrance, and as they walked through the hall railings together, he he received questions as expected.

"Why can't you lift the mark cover  spell by yourself?" Sage asked.

"I was about to say that. We now know why Rumi has been running, and there's a great probability that she might be another villain. You should lift the spell yourself." Cheyenne added.

"Each god to his own power, I have power over my ice, not her fire.
I haven't ascended to the Nine Heavens, there's still limitations to my powers." Ryker replied without stopping his walk. Their steps followed behind him.

"You have no power over her fire, but Rumi does?" Sage asked.

"Rumi didn't seal her powers," Ryker stopped to face them at this point.

"She only covered the mark, but unluckily, that mark is the key to her powers. Without it's appearance, she's just an ordinary mortal with slight warmth." He explained.

"Then finding Rumi won't be easy. I dunno why she agreed to cover the mark in the first place, but something tells me it's not a good reason." Cheyenne said.

"More reason to protect the partner. There are too many eyes on her." Sage nodded.

"Return to your quarters for the night." Ryker ordered.

They didn't argue. With synchronized bows, they left the hallway.

Ryker resumed his journey to his Phoria. That's the name of his resting place in this tower.

His walk came to a sudden halt when he saw a scene by the door of the orchard.

Shayla sat there on the treasure polished ground, body leaning against the wall and her hand holding the osmanthus candy pack. 

She must have fallen asleep while eating them, and thanks to the warmness of her body, the candies are already melting.

Ryker completed his stride, stopping in front of her peaceful image. 

He stared down at her for longer than he could calculate, then he took his seat beside her.

He stared ahead for just one second before his eyes returned to her own, and he smiled faintly.

He took the melting candy gently from her palm. It has left grease on her skin, and it's glossing.

He set the candy pack beside himself, making sure it made no noise before he returned his gaze to her again.

Even in her sleep, he could hear her stomach rumbling, meaning she didn't eat dinner yet.

That rumble made her lashes stir, and her eyes fluttered open, drowsy at first but not for long.

They brightened up so much when she saw him, and she smiled instantly. "You're back"

"There's a bed in your room" he replied without taking his eyes away from hers, and she blinked once.

"I didn't sleep here on purpose. I was waiting for you cos I felt lonely alone here, and I didn't know when I slept off." She said with a little bite to her bottom lip.

"Are you angry about it?"

"That's a feeling I'm not ready to share tonight." He replied, pointing two fingers forward.

Additional lights came on in that hallway immediately, showering them both with more brightness.

Shayla stood, staring up at them with pleased eyes. The lights seemed to lighten her mood even more, and her happiness showed in the way her lids refused to flutter.

"They're so beautiful. I've never seen many lights at once all my life. You're so cool, My Lord."

"What's cool about excess lights?" He replied from where he's sitting, and she looked down at him.

"It's cool! You might have spent your days in total brightness, but in the dark room cage, there was no light. In the slaves hall corridor, there was only bleak dull lights. At Granny's place, she didn't like lights at all, so I'll cherish this one." She said, returning her eyes to the lights.

Ryker stayed quiet, he just watched her walking around and trying to touch the ray of lights which is impossible, so she chased with soft laughter.

Blue fireflies blinked into the hall, and Shayla stopped her ray chase, wondering where they came from.

They got so many that she could feel their blinking lights bathing her skin, a beautiful addiction to the numerous electric lights.

She faced Ryker who's still sitting there, and she smiled as she went back to sit beside him.

"Did you do it, the fireflies?"

"Who else would summon such beauties in this city?" He replied, and she smiled widely.

"So arrogant, it's a flex at this point" she muttered.

"I heard that." he replied, and their eyes met for the first time this night.

"Sorry, but you're really a bit arrogant, but it's cool." She said, maintaining their eye contact.

"Is everything about me cool to you?" He questioned.

"Yes" she replied without thinking.

"Even this?" He turned his face away, and by the time he looked at her again, his face is completely bloody.

"No!" She gasped. "Not this one!"

She shifted back once, but his hand grabbed her wrist.

He pulled her closer, making sure she's staring directly at that blood.

"Do you hate blood?" He asked, feeling her fear on his own skin.

She's starting to shake again.

"Yes... there's nothing... appealing about blood anyways." She stuttered.

"I told you..." He let go of her wrist and slid that arm around her waist, pulling her closer than before.

Her breaths hitched, and his second hand held her whole chin. Her lips flattened in the process.

"Do not stutter when you talk to me, Firefly." He said, voice gravelly.

"I'm trying...but the blood." She said gently, her chest pounding.

The blood wiped from his face, and she took a deep breath.

"What job do you do? Why are you so madly rich?" She asked straight.

"Where is that question coming from?" He smirked, letting go of her waist but still holding her chin.

"Curiousness." She replied in a crooked voice, her chin resembling an egg on his palm.

Her stomach rumbled for the second time, and she held it, her eyes drooping to his own.

"I don't have to cook, I'll just take fruits from the orchard. But there's nothing to pluck with." She said.

He let go of her chin and stood, making his way to the orchard.

"Are you going to get me some, My Lord?" She went after him with a short run, her forehead nearly colliding with his back.

Ryker only had to use magic, and the fruits fell from the tree in obeisance. She wanted apples and plums, and she got those two in large quantities.

Her hands are full of them as she sat on the swing chair, eating gently.

Ryker sat beside her, again. 

He just watched her eat, and the one time she looked at him, her eyes caught his palm. The cut wound is still not healing.

"Why!" She quickly grabbed the hand, spreading it to have a look.

She left her apple between her teeth as she checked.

"Just eat, and go to bed." He said, taking the palm from her, but she grabbed it again, holding it tight inside her own palm.

"My warmth might not do much for the wound, but it'd do something." She said, refusing to let go.

Ryker just let her hold on to it. She returned to eating her fruits with her second hand.

She ate three apples and two plums. She was on the fourth apple when that one fell from her hand.

She fell asleep again.

Ryker's eyes went back to her, studying her shortly.

"Durak." He muttered before lifting her from the swing chair into his strong arms.

The lights in the orchard were going out as he walked out of it, and when he arrived at the corridor, more lights kept coming on till he got to her room and set her in bed.

He closed his eyes, activating profound sight before opening them again, and he saw it.

Her FireStone.

It's inside her—right below her heart, but the fire burning on the body of the stone is small.

No wonder her warmth is minimal. That darn mark has to appear to activate this FireStone and her powers fully.

It's hightime he took Rumi's search serious. That troublemaker can't keep ruining things.

[Luminaria Amusement Park]

Just like Sadie predicted, it's a full-time party.

Each time a party happens involving dancers from the ballet school, it always ends in bloodshed and fights.

And that's exactly why the twins came. Things has been rowdy in the palace lately, blowing off steam here won't be bad.

Sadie is by the drinks stand, talking with the vendor who looks genuinely scared.

She dropped the small dose of dead ashes on his table, and she gave him a commanding look.

He picked it up.

"Drop that inside Kade King and Blaise Spectre's tea when they come for drinks." Sadie ordered.

"Yes, Grand Princess." The vendor bowed, and she gave him a nod before going back to join Soren and Mercury who're sitting under a large umbrella beside a merry-go-round.

Mercury is sipping soy milk, so she picked her own bottle too, stabbing the foil cover with a straw before starting to drink.

"Are you both like obsessed with soy milk?" Soren said.

"The day I became besties with Sadie, we drank it as our friendship bond drink, so we're obsessed." Mercury shrugged at him, wishing he'd just take a look at her chest, but he's not even looking at her face.

He looks bored here.

Sadie went out of her way to explain everything, and by the time she was done, Mercury's mouth hung open.

"You mean to tell me that slave is still alive, and not only that... she's the demigod's partner? And he really shot everyone just to get the pass to that place to save her? On top of that, he left Grand Supreme Zeke unconscious?" 

"Exactly, that's why I need to kill someone to drown out my stress. That bastard is stressing everyone out in the palace, and there'll be no stop to it till the Feast. I think father has a plan." Sadie shrugged.

"I'm so sorry bestie, must have been so hard." Mercury rubbed her shoulders, and Sadie sighed.

"I can't wait for the Feast. By the way, second audition for the newbies is tomorrow, and I heard from mother that two particular masked girls stood out during the first audition last week. It's possible that those two would be at the center." Mercury said.

"What!" Sadie slammed her milk bottle on the table.

"Your mother said only one person would be at the center, why changing tunes?" Soren talked, lifting his head from his phone.

"I dunno, she said the two are just too excellent to separate. She looked so thrilled when she was talking about them, and I can't even change her perspective. If we aren't at the center of the ballet, then what's the use of the Feast?" Mercury said.

"As if the happenings at the palace aren't enough, now I have to bear the pain of seeing someone else at the center? Bloody hell..." Sadie held her head with two hands.

She stood from her chair, the music pounding in her veins angering her more. "I need someone to punch. Soren, call the Old Chef. Xena has to come here right now." 

"I deleted his number, mistakenly." Soren replied, and Sadie took his phone, smashing it on the table with the rage of a storm.

"Why would you delete something that important? Why!!" She yelled, attracting attention from the people around them.

"Sadie, calm down." Mercury stood, bringing her back to her seat.

Soren was silent, so Sadie took a deep breath.

"I'm so sorry, Ren. I was just too angry." She apologized.

"It's ok, I'm used to your rage." Soren shrugged.

"They're here, the West Palace folks and Kade." Mercury suddenly said. 

Their heads moved with hers at the same time.

Faye, Blaise and Kade are sitting not far from them under a different umbrella. Seems they're just arrived cos they have no drinks yet.

"We only have to wait for the bomb." Sadie smiled evilly, signalling at the drinks vendor.

--

"What happened to your face? Who battered it this way? You look like a serial killer's experiment." Faye said as she settled with Kade and Blaise.

Blaise brought his dog along. He placed it on the table, and he kept his distance from Faye. That's what he has been doing since Faye wounded him with the vase that night.

It's very obvious Faye is more powerful, she has just been hiding it, and he won't want to fall victim again

"The demigod came to our house, and his male knight did this after I informed him of the 150 billion slave identity." Kade said.

"His male knight? You mean Sage?" Faye blinked.

"Why would Ryker come to your house in the first place?" Blaise asked.

"Because my mother is the slave's mot—"

Kade stopped himself before he could go further. He looked at the step-siblings and sighed before changing narrative.

"I dunno why he came to our house, probably to talk to mother about something confidential."

"And you told him who the slave is? Who's she?" Faye asked.

"His partner, the one he has been looking for." Kade replied.

"What!!!" Faye and Blaise chorused, sharing glances before looking at Kade again.

"You don't mean that, meaning the slave is still alive?" Faye gasped.

"Can someone get us drinks first? My throat is killing me." Kade sighed.

"I'll be right back." Blaise stood to go get the drinks, leaving Faye and Kade on the table.

"Since you seem more powerful than Blaise, why not try healing my wounded face? My tongue is wounded too." Kade said.

"Your wound is poisoned, how do you expect me to heal it?" Faye scoffed.

"You can tell it's poisoned just my glancing at it?" Kade blinked. "Who exactly are you?" 

"Faye" Faye giggled.

"Stop playing with me." Kade scoffed.

Blaise returned with the drinks, and as expected, he didn't buy for Faye.

He got three cups. One for Kade, one for himself, and one for his dog on the table.

Faye rolled eyes, getting her purse and taking out some nyx notes to go get her own drink.

As she's standing, the guys are drinking from their cups.

The moment she took a step away from that umbrella, she heard the guys coughing.

"What the hell is going —"

Her eyes widened, and her words died when she turned around to see the two of them crying blood.

The dog on the table is crying blood too, and that one is already laying flat. It looks dead.

The money fell from Faye, and she stumbled back to them fearfully, grabbing their shoulders with one hand each. She shook them.

"Blaise! Kade! The hell! Guys!!"

Meanwhile, under the opposite umbrella, the twins are already smiling, but Mercury is laughing loudly, they're enjoying this show.

"Now this is what I needed for a good night party!" Mercury clapped.

"Kade looks good with that blood in his eyes." Soren smiled.

"Blaise, I told you I was going to get back at you for using your stupid red hair on my eye, didn't I? Now is that payback time, enjoy!" Sadie palmed her cheeks.

Faye looked at them, her eyes widening more.

They definitely did this, and whatever poison they used must be so potent if it's as powerful as this.

She looked back at her own side, gasping, her eyes stretched again. The dog is already dead on the table, and the guys are already foaming from the mouth, ready to cross the bridge.

People gathered around, watching their last moments with smiles.

Everyone hates Blaise cos he's from the West Palace.

No one likes Kade cos he's the arrogant Liza's daughter.

It's all balanced.

"Guys!!!" Faye yelled.

"Just give up!" Sadie shouted across.

"In your dreams, witch!" Faye yelled back, positioning the dying guys on the same angle, side by side.

She looked at the surrounding crowd, but she threw away the shyness and attached her lips to Blaise's own.

She sucked out the poison from him first, and Blaise breathed back to life, then she did the same to Kade, sucking out his poison with a kiss too. 

She spat out the poison in form of two small scorpions.

The guys stood to their feet, looking shocked and spooked at the same time, their eyes never leaving Faye.

Even the crowd are shocked, especially the twins and Mercury.

Sadie could hear her own breathings, and her eyes are crazily wide.

"What just happened?" Soren muttered.

"Who exactly is this bîtch, Faye? What is she?" Mercury said.

Faye grabbed her purse and rushed out of the park.

"Faye!" Blaise and Kade went after her instantly.

Sadie swallowed.

[Same Night, East Palace~Soul Hall]

Lilith went into the city at dusk to kill people and bring their bodies here.

Azura absorbed the multiple souls into Zeke and Jude's body, all in a bid to wake them up, but it's just not working.

There are twelve dead bodies on ground, all rotten and soulless.

After the twelfth attempt, Azura dropped on a sofa.

"You shouldn't give up." Lilith said beside her.

"You shouldn't even be here now, considering the fact that your darn mark didn't work on Shayla." Azura replied tiredly.

Lilith attacked him, bringing him to the ground with her scream.

"In an hour." She whispered magically to his face.

The guard went still, and Lilith stood, going back to stand beside Azura.

Granny Jojo came in, and Azura raised her head.

Jojo looked at the twelve soulless bodies on the ground, then her eyes went back to Azura.

"Your mirror technique, mother." Azura came closer. "Communicate with your shadow in your mirror and find solutions. Zeke and Jude can't keep sleeping like this!" 

"I don't want to." Jojo smiled.

"You're welcome to try" Jojo replied, leaving the hall.

"I spent my years in the basement training myself. I might be blind and have no magic, but I have strength, and I can kill you if I want, so you better not lust over Ryker. I was in my room at the chamber when I heard some guards whispering about your likeness for him." Pearl said.

Lilith peeled her hand away from her neck, breathing hard.

Pearl pulled her back roughly.

"And?" Lilith frowned.

"What!" Lilith gasped.

Pearl held her tighter.

"You can't say no. I know you know where. You're a banshee, so you know things. Tell me where!" Pearl forcefully demanded.

Lilith stayed iron-faced for a minute, then she smiled.

"We need a soul shifter for that. There's one in the ballet school, meaning we have to visit the school tomorrow." 

"Cool, I'm ready." Pearl said.

[Heartless Tower, Knights Quarters]

Not only the use of ropes or nails, but they're mastering the art of flying and levitation.

They're both in the air, striking each other relentlessly.

By the door, Shayla stood, peeping inside the room.

She has been watching since the start of this practice.

She's moving her hand, trying to follow their moves.

Two fingers if you want the strike to be mild, but full five fingers for a brutal hit. Fold the five fingers to choke without touching the person, and glare to seize voices.

They all look simple, but she doesn't even have the power to blend her new knowledge with.

"No wonder I've been sweating, what are you doing here?" Cheyenne came to her, sweating so much.

"I was only watching, by the way, can I do the power practice with you guys?" Shayla asked.

"Yeah, it's 10. Since we can't go for the second audition anyways, I came to spend time with you." Xena said.

"Who said you can't go to the second audition" Ryker's deep baritone voice entered the hall.

He approached with ease.

He ditched his robe today, he's wearing a loose shirt and smooth slacks, and he looks as divine as usual. Even more.

"Moy Vladika." Shayla smiled, his presence affecting her mood more than she could control.

She had woken up to meet herself on bed this morning, and it's definitely his handiwork which she doesn't want to question.

Can't be Cheyenne. Impossible.

Can't be Sage. That's somehow.

So it's definitely him.

"She can go for the second audition?" Cheyenne and Sage came out of the practice room, both looking confused with the suddenness.

"Prepare her dress." Ryker ordered Cheyenne.

Cheyenne looked at Shayla before nodding. "Yes, My Lord."

Ryker looked at Xena, and Xena smiled.

"Prepare something for her to wear, too. Sage." Ryker said.

"Me?" Sage blinked. "Fine, I'm a dress expert, lucky you!" 

"Come here." Ryker took Shayla's hand, trying all his best to be gentle.

"Where are we going?" She asked as she followed him, using her second hand to hold his robe.

"To get you bathed." He replied, and she pulled him to a sudden stop.

"You're not... bathing me... right?"

"Stop your wild imaginations, it's not time for them to come to pass yet. Don't put ideas in my head." He said coldly, and she gulped.

"Go wash up. Twenty minutes, Cheyenne will be with you." He said clearly.

"Ok. I will." She detached her fingers from him.

"No ok. Use your full words." He said.

"Yes. I will." She smiled before rushing back into her room.

Cheyenne and Sage went to the treasure clothing room, and they both got busy with picking out dresses for the girls.

"I can't get used to his mode of working. What the hell is he planning again by suddenly agreeing for her to leave the palace." Cheyenne said.

"Definitely something big, we'll just have to wait. He won't make a move without a tangible reason." Sage smiled.

[Black Swan Ballet School]

School isn't as silent as it was last week.

How Faye sucked poison out of Kade and Blaise so easily,, and the two masked dancers who're likely to be in the center of the Feast ballet.

It's a hot Monday.

Neither Kade nor Blaise or Faye are in school, but the twins and Mercury are in school already.

They're waiting for Faye to come and explain, and they're tapping their foot in front of the school building.

Also, the newbies who got picked for the second round of the audition are here too, so it's a full school.

At 12pm on the round dot, two cars skidded to rough halts in the park.

The air became so acidic, the cold weather turned very hot. Tension covered the atmosphere.

Sadie approached the crosswalk cos Faye is standing on the other side of it, but the moment she made to cross, another car slid into the park.

A deep blue model, built only for the owner, fuckin new. 

Sadie almost got hit, but Soren teleported to get her out of the way, and they both tumbled to the ground.

The car stopped right in front of their fallen bodies, and there was silence for a bit of second.

Dead silence.

And then...the car backdoor flipped open...

A pair of shinny diamond-designed heels stepped out, in style.

Soren helped Sadie up, bringing her back to her feet.

Their four eyes are bulging in astonishment, but their smirks are unaffected as they awaited who's gonna come down from that car.

No doubt, today is the deathday of whoever it is.

Even Faye and her two boys are all attentive, wondering who's brave enough to almost knock the BIG Sadie down.

The students are all where they are, just that their attention has shifted to the luxury car, too.

"Who the hell is that?" Mercury joined the twins. "Whoever it is is begging for a fast death."

But the sight of the heels that emerged from the car made their morale drop at their feet.

The diamond-designed heels is nothing like ever seen before, and the reflection from the shinny stones caught the soft sun, sending sparks across the school walls.

Most students had to close their eyes quickly, and some covered their eyes with their spaced fingers at the risk of going blind.

Out of the car, came Shayla.

Simple but elegant in her bright yellow dress which shared the same shade with the glow of an angry fire. It's so bright. It's free and backless, but there are interconnected ropes at the low back, so her skin is sparingly out, and it's glowing.

Thanks to the tip of Cheyenne's sharp nails, her ears were pierced, and for the first time in her life, she wore earrings, small sapphire ones though.

Her other jewels are minimal, but they're all too luxurious that it feels illegal to see them for free. Her face matched the pristine glow of the rest of her skin.

From the other side of the car, Xena came out, looking pretty too in her off-shoulders black dress and enough jewelries to show.

Soren blinked slowly beside Sadie. Sadie is still not able to close her mouth.

Mercury looks so out of her wits, sudden anxiety descended on her as she began rubbing her own neck abnormally till it turned red.

"The slave?" Faye muttered.

"She's really the partner?" Blaise said, not blinking at all.

Kade is swallowing invisible lumps and saliva, he looks like his whole air supply system got stolen.

Actually, the girls stole the air of everyone. No one could voice out.

It got much worse when the driver's seat of the car swung open, and the alpha himself came down.

Ryker.

He took Shayla's hand, and her smile blossomed, his coldness calming her nervous nerves as he climbed the front staircase with her, entering the school building.

Her heels kissed the ground repeatedly with the rich crispy sounds.

As Xena followed behind them, her eyes caught Soren's from the crowd, but she was quick to throw her face away before entering building too.

The stolen air of everyone returned, and the sun dipped back into the clouds as if it only came out for the just completed scenario.

"Ok, that was insane!"

"Finee, she's pretty as hell. I can still smell her perfume!"

"Money can transform a duck to a peacock."

"The shoes are enough to buy all of my family mansions, like twenty of those big arse buildings."

"And the jewels? I'm gonna die. Does he manufacture them or what?"

"The richest man in this city with many treasure chests, it's not supposed to be shocking."

"But I'm still shocked, look at the car too. Who built that?"

"And the second girl? She's hot."

"That's his chef sister, Xena."

"You must be kidding!"

There are no stop to the shocked compliments and admirations. It grew noisier as the minutes whiled.

No one even noticed Sadie's anger.

"What just happened?" She muttered, finally finding her voice. Her pale face turned to Soren.

Soren couldn't talk.

"I'm still in awe, too. Have you ever seen me as speechless as this before?" Mercury said.

Sadie's eyes went down to her feet, and suddenly, she doesn't like the heels she's wearing anymore.

She took them off and flung them away to the crosswalk, then she snatched Mercury's phone.

"No slave or undeserving nameless thing can come here to disrupt the hierarchy or lower the bar! No amount of jeweling or panel beating can wash away the fact that she's a slave! I'm calling mother!" 

"For?" Soren asked.

"If the bastard insists on dumping her here, then Mercury, your mother will have to close this school down. Two boss can't rule an empire!" Sadie snarled venomously, storming into the school building too.

"Soren, what do we do?" Mercury faced him.

"Follow her lead. She's always the one with the rules." Soren replied, and they both followed Sadie.

"Damn, why am I somehow loving this shit." Faye muttered from across the crosswalk. There's a lean, sweet smile on her face.

"Let's see how the Diamond 3 are gonna cope with someone richer and with a more powerful backup. Sadie is so going to hang herself. Hot pepper coming." She clicked her tongue, nodding.

"What was that last night?" Blaise shifted closer to her, and her smile disappeared.

"I don't think you're in anyplace to talk to me. Your mouth stinks." 

"Keep telling that lie to yourself. You kissed me last night!" Blaise shoved her to a car, but she pushed him away, glaring hard.

"Yes, and I kissed Kade too, both to suck out your poisons." 

"You spat the poison out in form of two scorpions, and somehow, nothing happened to you. Look, I've seen series of insane powers in this city, but I've never came across your type before. What exactly are you!" Blaise demanded, and Faye laughed.

"I love that you're curious about me, and there's more to come. I know you hate getting curious, so you might just die while at it. And don't you dare touch me again." She gave him a deadly eye roll and left.

Blaise leaned on the car, his head is in shambles as he tried to figure it out, but nothing is coming.

He glanced at Kade who's still standing where he is.

"Is the slave entry shock still in your body?" He asked.

Kade didn't reply.

"Kade!" Blaise nudged him.

"Don't touch me!" Kade pushed him off, then he ran into the building.

Lilith jumped out, and she helped Pearl out too.

[Director's Office]

The news has reached Esme even before Ryker arrived, so the moment she saw the door opening, she stood to her feet. Lots of her staff teachers are around her.

Ryker entered with Shayla and Xena, and Esme gasped when she saw them, but she gathered herself together, knowing it's unadvisable to use forbidden words around him.

"What's going on, Lord Ryker?" She asked politely with a inept smile.

Ryker didn't let go of Shayla's hand, and Xena planted herself on the other side of him.

"It's a shame you don't recognize your two best dancers from the last audition. Were they wearing masks?" Ryker said, intentionally giving his sarcasm away with nonchalance.

The folds that appeared on Esme's forehead are a proof of her shock. She stepped out of her desk, eyes wide as she accessed the two girls.

"You two are...what!"

"Nice to meet you properly. You said it yourself that we must come." Shayla said.

"Here we are, ma'am." Xena added, and Esme gasped.

She quickly requested for their submitted forms, and her teachers slid her laptop to her on the table.

"You both applied under..." She faced them. "Fake names?"

"That was due to the circumstances. If we registered with real names and came unmasked, you'd have chased us off." Xena said.

"No, young girls. We don't do that here. That alone is enough to disqualify you both, you literally deceived the school! Also..." Esme smiled at Ryker.

"We don't take lowliness here, impossible. One is a palace chef, and the other one is a sla—"

Esme was already clutching at her own neck before she could finish that last word.

Her neck felt a real choky congestion, runny veins danced on her throat, mapping the skin. Her eyes turned till white filled it up.

She threw up water.

Her knees clashed with the ground, and her clutch at her throat got tighter. She's shaking, writhing, sweating and getting red.

Shayla's eyes went to Ryker. As she expected, he's glaring at Esme. He's the one doing it.

"Ma'am!" The teachers shouted, but none was bold enough to go closer for the fear of getting choked too.

Ryker got that laptop from the table, went two steps to Esme and brought it down to her head, crashing it there.

It broke to shatters, and Esme bled from the middle of her head, the redness stained his hands.

Shayla gulped, Xena did the same.

The teachers are still standing clustered in a corner of the office.

"Thinking you can exchange words with me is forbidden, talk more of actually doing it. A slave?" Ryker smirked, pulling her up by her hair.

Esme's shoes left her feet on their own, and her lips quivered.

"Don't use that word around her, or you'll be in a lidless coffin." He said, darkly with poisoned tongue.

Esme nodded, and he let her go.

"Direct the second audition as you should. Do your job, that's all I ask. And oh...if she gets bullied, or touched... I'll have to send your daughter to the dark room as an indigene slave. First of it's kind, how's that sound?" He said.

"What!" Esme's head went up, eyes wide again.

"You heard me, Esme Vega." He called her full name to knock that in harder, and she quickly nodded.

"Listen, Ryker. The mark is connected to her heart, maybe if you show mercy and compassion, the mark would appear even without Rumi. Spend time with your partner, buy her valuable gifts. Try to be warm even if you can't—"

Ryker already vanished with the air before he could finish up.

"You're dying by the way, no one survives a stab from my dagger. But luckily for you, your soul won't die." His voice echoed in the cold air.

"Never met a demigod as stubborn and ruthless as him all my life." Eternus sighed, sitting back in the boat. His bleeding never stopped.

And like Ryker said, he died on that boat. His body disappeared, but his soul travelled into a new body.
