[Mirror Maze Party]

Shayla has never attended a party all her life, and this one being the first, she has plans to make the most of it, except that she won't drink.

Cheyenne had picked out clothes for her and Xena. Hers is a beautiful robe which covered her body well, and she likes it.

She has always wondered how Ryker feels in those black robes of his, and finally she got to feel it tonight, though her own robe is pink.

She's skipping her feet on her white sneakers, the decorative jewels on her hair dancing with her moves as they entered the mirror maze.

Music pounded the grounds and walls, fellow ballerinas from the school milled everywhere.

Noises even went above the trap music at a point.

It's full, and one could see their own multiple reflections on the irregularly mirrored walls.

"Whoa, it's ai beautiful in here..." Shayla turned around when they entered the inner part of the maze which is less crowded.

Her reflection on the mirror is like ten, and she chuckled.

"You look extra happy, apart from beating the bullies, what else happened?" Xena asked beside her.

She's wearing heels, so she's taller. Even naturally, she's taller, she looks like a real older sister.

"Yeah... something else really happened." Shayla smiled.

"What happened?" Xena came closer curiously.

Shayla looked around to make sure there's no one close, then she leaned in to Xena's ear.

"He kissed me" she whispered.

"Wait, what!" Xena gasped.

Shayla quickly covered her mouth. "Don't be too loud."

Xena nodded, and Shayla took her hand away.

"He did? Ryker?" Xena said, batting her lids in disbelief.

She brought out cookies from her belt bag, giving Shayla one pack since they've been warned never to eat or drink anything here.

"Hmm! Smells good!" Shayla inhaled the cookies, closing her eyes in over-exaggeration.

"You're so cute" Xena laughed. "You like the kiss that much?"

"Yeah" Shayla blushed, and Xena pulled her cheeks fondly.

"Hey, heroine!" Faye appeared, butting into their conversation.

Xena quickly pushed Shayla behind herself protectively.

"You're from the West Palace." She said, and Faye smiled.

"Yeah, chill. I don't bite. I'm Faye, and i want to thank Shayla for giving us a show today. Mhua!" She blew kisses, and Xena made faces.

"Thank you..." Shayla said behind.

"Yeah, by the way be careful. Prince Dyke from Eclipseria is here, and I heard he doesn't show his face to just anyone. He's only gonna appear to anyone he chooses, and no one has the right to decline him. Anyone he chooses, goes to Eclipseria with him." Faye explained.

"Like kidnapping?" Shayla asked.

"If you put it that way, have fun! The game starts in ten minutes." Faye waved and left.

"That sounds like a problem already, Prince Dyke?" Xena muttered.

Shayla came out behind her.

"He'd probably chose girls from wealthy families with connections to royalty. We're safe, let's go deeper and check out the maze well before the game starts." Shayla said.

"Sure" Xena replied, but just as they were about to move, she got a slithering feel.

She stopped, turning around sharply.

Her twin bond is flaring up. She could feel it in her core.

Rumi is close. 

She's here somewhere.

She rushed down that lane, turning left and right, but it's empty. No one.

Did she feel wrong?

She squinted, looking left and right again. Still nothing.

She blew it off and turned back, but Shayla is nowhere to be found anymore.

"Shay?"

"Shay!" She ran forward.

--

The maze must be a magically planted one, cos one minute, Shayla was following Xena, and in another minute, she found herself in another part of the maze.

She turned around, her robe flowing with her, but instead of Xena, her head knocked into a hard chest.

The scent of strong smoke that hit her immediately was thick and sleep-inducing.

Her eyes went drowsy.

She closed it and opened it, trying to fight the sleep, but it won anyways.

Her cookie nylon fell from her as her arms went down weakly, and the cookies pieces scattered around the mirrored ground.

Her eyes closed, and her head fell against the hard chest.

Two of the decorative gold and crystals in her hair fell off, and her lips opened slightly.

"Moy...Vladika." She muttered before the sleep went deeper.

She slept off completely.

The long veiny hand of the person curved around her slim waist, and the second hand came up to her face, his big thumb pressing her lips.

"Found the one." His gravelly voice said, belonging to the distant prince.

Prince Dyke of Eclipseria.

His eyes flashed sharp green, and the beam enveloped them both.

He vanished with her.

Xena was colliding into bodies as she ran around the maze, searching for any face that looks like Shayla's.

She could feel sweat pooling in her armpits, wetting her feet.

Where's Shayla?

The split images of herself in the mirrors was another confusing shit, she fell so many times.

The game has started, and everyone she's coming across are wearing masks, struggling to hide before the maze hunters gets to them.

But despite their masks, Xena was sure she'd recognize Shayla if she walked past her.

First, Shayla carries the warmest, gentlest scent ever, and it's easy for her to sniff out.

Second, she's wearing a flowing pink robe, and there's no other person wearing a robe.

Thirdly, she has much treasures in her hair...gold and the rest.

With all of these, Xena expected to fish her out of the masked crowd easily, but as the minutes chimed farther, she got more scared.

Shayla is still nowhere.

Now the maze is madly filled. Ballerinas are everywhere, and she could barely walk since it's tight.

She stumbled on legs and fell down again, and she was shocked to see where she fell at.

On the ground, there's a crystal pendant which she's very sure was in Shayla's hair.

Did someone take Shayla?

She pushed herself up, folding the pendant in her palm as she looked around again, the music seeming to be pulsing at the back of her mind.

Then it dawned on her...the distant prince. Prince Dyke.

He better not be the one.
He better not.

She pushed some ballerinas out of her way and rushed out of that part of the maze, then she felt it again.

Her twin bond.

She felt it stronger than the first time, her skin hair stood one by one.

She froze.

"Miss me?" A velvety voice whispered in her ear from behind.

Xena turned around with a sharp twist, and her wide eyes caught a mess of blonde hair, walking away from her in the crowd.

"Rumi" she muttered, pushing off more ballerinas.

They cursed her and nearly chewed her out, but she was already out of that angle.

She ran, going after the blonde, her heartbeats quicker than her steps till she entered a restroom.

The window is wide open when she got inside, but Rumi isn't in.

Xena climbed the window, jumping down the sill since it's not very high.

Getting outside, she saw no one.

No Rumi. No fellow ballerina. Not even the wind.

The place is silent.

"Rumi..." Xena muttered emotionally again, feeling like crying. "Why are you doing this?"

A piece of paper landed on her head, falling at her feet.

She quickly picked it up, opening it to read what's inside.

I MISS YOU TOO, TWIN SISTER. 

Xena stared at the note till the letters blurred, remembering the last time she saw her was the day their mother was killed when they were still three year olds.

How has she been living since then?

Who took her?

Where has she been?

She sniffled, folding the note on her palm too, together with Shayla's hair pendant, and that was when she remembered Shayla is missing.

She remembered she has a phone too, so she opened her belt bag to get it, but unluckily, both her phone and Shayla's are in the bag.

"Darn it" she cussed frustratedly.

But a smell lingered in the air faintly—the burning smell of cigarette.

She looked at the side of the building, adjacent her is Soren.

He sat on a windowsill, one leg up and one leg down with a cigar stick hanging between his lips.

Seems like he didn't see her, so Xena just ignored his presence and ran back into the maze building.

It's clogged, so she can't even enter anymore. She held her head with two hands, crying.

"Oh my Shayla."

"Hey, Xena!" Faye appeared with two cups of mojito, intending to hand her one, but seeing the tears on Xena's face, she dropped the cups.

"What's going on?"

Blaise appeared from the crowd too, taking off his ghost mask from his sweaty face.

"Everything ok?" He asked.

"Shayla, I think the distant prince took her." Xena replied.

"What!!" Blaise and Faye chorused, and Blaise couldn't stop blinking.

"Are you serious? I mean...why would anyone in his right senses take HER?" 

"I dunno..." Xena sniffled.

She proceeded to dialling Ryker's number with Shayla's phone, but the line is only making a straight sound.

Nothing else.

"Geez...I'm scared. What if he's hurting her right now? What if he touches her inappropriately? She gets scared easily and..." Xena cried again, unable to finish her sentence.

"Hear me out, Xena. Be calm." Faye said, rubbing her palm on her chest.

"Cool down, and let's think this through." 

Xena hiccuped, wiping her tears.

Faye dragged Blaise one step away from Xena.

"What?" Blaise said in confusion, sweeping his sprawled red hair away from his face.

"The demigod once saved you, it's your chance to make him realize you're worth it." Faye replied.

Blaise released himself from her.

"Wait... you're not actually thinking of going to Eclypseria to get her, right?" He said.

"What else? Seems like the demigod isn't available, we can play our parts and get on his good book. We need him on our West Palace side, this is our chance." Faye replied.

"Are you crazy? They're very territorial in Eclypseria, they don't allow strangers inside their palace! Do you even know how brutal Prince Dyke is? He once killed a mother, left the body outside till maggots infested it, then he fed the daughter with the maggots." Blaise said fearfully.

"And?" Faye scoffed.

"Are you even listening?" Blaise blinked.

"I'm getting impatient." Xena said behind, and Faye faced her with a assuring smile.

"We're visiting Eclypseria, please tell me you have a useful ability." 

"I can...summon animals." Xena nodded.

"Cool, let's move." Faye replied.

"But this is..." Blaise grumbled, and Faye dragged him by the ear, pulling him out of the maze.

Xena rushed after them.

[Director's Office]

Zeke is still inside with Esme.

There's a bedroom in the inner part of her office, and they're both laying on it, only in their underwear.

Esme's head is on his chest.

"So you're gonna leave the demigod to continue his deeds till the Feast?" Esme said, lifting her head to glance at him.

"I told you, there's a plan for the Feast, you only have to do your part when it's time. After the feast, everything will go back to it's place. By then, there'll be no demigod, no demigoddess, no Azura. Just me, you, my kids and your daughter. We'll make the happiest, most powerful demon family, Esme." Zeke explained.

"You're so sweet" Esme smiled, rubbing his moustache. "I'm winning the beauty contest again, isn't it?"

"Of course, Liza is an annoying pest who won't just give up easily. She should have known her place by now." Zeke sighed.

"And didn't you say your mother Tara is coming to the palace soon?" She drummed her fingers on his chest.

"Yeah, she'll become the new Palace Matriarch. Jojo is due, just as stupid as her daughter." Zeke sneered.

"I can't wait to meet my mother-in-law." Esme smiled.

The telephone rang on the bedstand, and they both sat up in bed.

"Who's that?" Zeke frowned.

"My secretary, must be something important." Esme shrugged as she took the phone.

"Yes?"

"Prince Dyke made his pick already, ma'am." The secretary reported.

"Already? That was fast. So...which wealthy family daughter did she pick?" Esme smiled.

"Not the expected, ma'am."

"What do you mean?" Esme got serious.

"The slave, Shayla." The secretary dropped the bomb.

"What!!" Esme gasped, dropping the telephone.

"The slave got chosen by your Prince Dyke." She broke the news to Zeke.

"Impossible," Zeke smirked.

"My secretary won't call if it's not true. She got taken!" Esme snapped.

"Must be a joke, I mean...of every girl in this city, it's her?" Zeke laughed.

"Why are you taking this as a joke? She has our needed FireStone inside her, she's the goddess, so her getting kidnapped by that prince is a no!" Esme glared.

"Stop taking this the hard way, Esmeralda." Zeke smiled, standing from the bed.

"Prince Dyke and his father aren't interested in power, they're only addicted with money and women, so he has no concern with her identity. He only wants sèx, and he probably took her cos he recognized her as a virgin." He explained.

"So...what now?" Esme stood in front of him.

"I'll leave her to him for a week and let him have a fill of her, then I'll take three maidens to him as replacement to get her back." Zeke said.

"You have plans for everything, don't you?" Esme smiled.

"Isn't that why you're stuck with me?" Zeke replied.

"I'd love to see Ryker trying to get her back within that one week. He might be a demigod, but he hasn't merged with his partner yet. I bet there are limitations to the barriers he can break." Esme said, walking to the room cellar to pour them drinks.

"And trust me when I say... Prince Dyke is a greater bastard. He's mad." Zeke grinned.

"Ouu," Esme faced him. "This would be interesting."

[Mount Zhitomir]

The skies at the other parts of the city is dark dark, but this part of the sky is swirling with silhouettes, rainbows, and incessant flashes. 

Also, the wind here is harsh, so harsh that it's threatening to push away the bowl of blood. Lilith bad to hold the bowl down.

Mercury is currently holding the blood-soaked bell, and as she rang it, blood splashed forward from it, hitting Pearl's body on the chair.

Pearl is already unconscious. 

Lilith had to close her eyes so her own soul won't get wounded in this exchange.

The irregularities in the sky got broader, stretching beyond the mountain. 

Mercury's mouth is moving fast as she chanted inaudible words, then she dipped the bell into the blood again, ringing for the fifth time.

Pearl screamed back to life on the chair, so loud that it reached the skies and propelled a wide streak of lightning.

The sixth and seventh bell ring sounded from Mercury, and that was the last straw.

The blood bowl broke to pieces, letting out the blood.

Blood flowed around the mountain, going under Pearl's chair and soaking her bare feet.

Her screams turned earth-shattering, and her neck went to the side, seeming to have cracked.

Her body went still.

The rowdy skies cleared at once, and total darkness took over.

Lilith opened her eyes, and Mercury dropped the bell. They both looked forward to see Pearl is still sitting on the chair, not moving.

Satisfactorily, Mercury smiled.

Pearl's soul is definitely inside a pig down the mountain already, so this body is empty.

Lilith is smiling with joy too. Pearl is definitely dead, she put the death mark on her body anyways.

They marched to the chair together, and when they still didn't get a movement from Pearl, they looked at each other.

"Are you sure your soul shift worked?" Lilith asked.

"Not sorry, but her soul is in a pig, actually. I never had plans to help." Mercury smiled.

"Oh wow! That sounds refreshing to the ears, considering the fact that I marked her for death too. Her soul is dead, so whichever pig it entered would die too." Lilith shrugged.

"Why am I suddenly liking you?" Mercury grinned.

Lilith grinned right back, their cruelty matching, but their mini jubilation came to a screeching end when they heard sudden movements from Pearl's chair.

At the same time, their faces went down, and they were shocked to see Pearl's hand is up.

She removed the blindfold from her eyes and stood from the chair, opening her eyes to the new world.

Her smile shone brighter than summer sun, and her pretty features appreciated it.

She looked at the dark sky, at her bloody surroundings, and then at the two shocked girls in front of her.

The smile on her face disappeared.

"Truly, I spent so many years in the basement, but I didn't spend it doing nothing. I have my own secrets that has been keeping me alive till today, and before I can die, you'll have to do more than a lowly death mark or trying to send my soul to a pig. Actually, you can't even kill me, I can't be killed. I'm Pearl Millions, remember that name in your nightmares." She glared.

Before they could both reach her, she teleported from the mountain.

Lilith and Mercury stopped, both exchanging glances.

"No!" They both gasped.

[Treatment Haven]

The alchemists are all gone now.

Sadie sent everyone away to go look for Soren for her.

It's just Sadie and Kade on their beds, then Liza who's beside Kade.

Kade is already asleep, but Sadie still looks as angry as ever in her bed. She has refused any further drugs. She's bleeding, but she doesn't care.

"You'll never stop being a hateful little bîtch, will you?" Liza spoke to her directly.

"Shut up, or I won't mind killing you with my last breath." Sadie said through her teeth.

Liza scoffed, but she stood when she got a suspicious notification through the air.

She stepped out of the Haven, squinting when she saw a female with a messy wave of blonde hair disappearing down the hall.

"Rumi?" She muttered, noticing there's a note sitting by the door.

She picked it up, reading inwardly.

FROM NOW ON, YOU'RE ON YOUR OWN. I'LL LIFT THE MARK COVER WHENEVER I'M IN A GOOD MOOD, AND THAT'S COMING SOON.

Liza clenched her fists after reading weird for word.

"That whore, I knew she'd play dirty, but don't worry...I'm not very easy either. You won't ruin my plan for me." She whispered.

Soren whisked past her in front of the door. Before she could say a word to him, he already entered the ward.

The moment he entered, Sadie stood skeptically from the bed.

Apart from her Haven gown, her bed is also soaked with her blood, but she looks darkly determined to wound Soren, so her bleeding means nothing to her.

She grabbed the porcelain potpourri pot beside her bed, rushed to Soren and brought the pot down to his head, crashing it violently.

Blood showered Soren's face, rushing from his wounded head as the shattered pieces of the pot flew around.

"How could you leave me alone at the cafeteria! I mind-linked but you still didn't come! How dare you leave me to get humiliated!!" Sadie shouted, her pale lips nearly tearing apart.

The break and her scream woke Kade up, and even as wicked as he is, he couldn't hide his shock.

"The hell?"

"Grand Princess Sadie?" Liza muttered, entering the haven witch shock-filled face.

Sadie looked at the two of them, then her eyes went back to Soren who's still bleeding like crazy.

Her eyes widened, she realized what she had done.

"Oh my devil... Ren." She covered her mouth in shock.

"Ren...it wasn't...I wasn't... thinking..."

Soren took off his jacket and wrapped it around his bleeding head, then he walked out of the Haven.

"Soren!!!" Sadie went after him, but she barely got to the door before her wounds tore up again.

She fell on her face.

[West Palace]

"I hope Blaise and Faye are enjoying their maze party, how blissful it is, to be younger." Kasia sighed, standing by the glass wall of the upper floor.

Her arms are folded, and she's staring out to the dark sky, wearing her brown nightie already.

Astor's arms went around her from behind.

"Nothing beats a dark sky." He said, and Kasia chuckled.

"Ew, don't do that when I'm here at least." Winona said behind them.

She's sitting on a sofa with pile of books in front of her which she's taking turns to study with her reading glasses on.

"How's the making of the fake FrostOrb going?" Kasia asked her.

"Easier than expected, should be done within days." Winona shrugged.

"Good, and I'm glad you're over your likeness for the slave girl." Astor said, and Winona scoffed.

"It's not over! I'm only this cool cos I'm sure she'll still be mine after we take the city back, don't be deceived by my cool." 

"That's more like you" Kasia laughed.

"Did you know that several centuries ago, the elders of this family did something brutal to a snake woman?" Winona suddenly said.

"What do you mean?" Kasia faced her. Astor looks equally interested.

"I guess you don't read our family journals in the Archives enough." Winona stood, raising up the particular book.

"Centuries ago, elders of this Spectres family brutally murdered a homeless snake woman and her two children, just because she gave birth on their valued farmland." She said.

"The heck?" Astor gasped.

"12:00am"

"12 midnight?" Kasia muttered.

"Yeah, exactly." Winona nodded.

"Then the midnight curse..." Astor looked at Kasia.

"Oh hell." Kasia mumbled.

[East Palace, Main Castle]

Pearl pushed the main door open, her bright smile returning to her face when she felt the authenticity of her newly gained sight.

She's not even happy enough, she needs to start jumping or rolling on the ground, but maybe it's not time for that yet.

She stopped when she saw Jude who's still kneeling in front of the burner, body so red with the fire scorch.

She pulled him up from his knees, and Jude's eyes widened.

Azura sealed him there on his knees, so how come?

How was Pearl able to pull him up.

Then he saw Pearl's rolling eyeballs, and he gasped.

"You're...?"

"Seeing you! My sight is back! I told you!" Pearl laughed.

Azura came down the staircase, intending to visit the Soul Hall, but...

The moment she saw Pearl alive and moving without getting help, she was stunned.

"Pearl?" She blinked.

"Yes, mother. I guess I'm eligible to call you mother, now that my sight is back with some power to crown it." Pearl laughed at her face, going up the staircase.

"Pearl!!" Azura's voice echoed behind her, but she's too deep into her excitement to even answer.

She entered Jude's room, and Jude barged in with her.

"What's going on!" He drew her back swiftly, every part of his body demanding a quick answer.

Pearl broke free. "I can see now, so don't shove me around or I'll kill you!" 

"What exactly is going on?" Jude asked calmly, and Pearl smiled, fanning herself.

"Nothing much, I told you we'd be working together... didn't I? Now all you need to do is calm down and wait for me to settle down. We'll talk tomorrow morning." She said and left the room, leaving him more confused.

Next, she went to Granny Jojo's Chamber, running around the old woman's bed excitedly.

"Too bad you're in coma and you won't be sharing in my good news, and that's why you gotta wake up soon! You're one of the people I want to announce to. You just have to see me using my eyes, you have to!" She gushed, touching Jojo's still face.

"Wake up soon, Grandmother, you deserve to see my eyes working." She grinned, leaving the chamber after a few more squeals.

She assembled maids, planting herself between them to give instructions.

First, she has to smell like Shayla. She needs that warm gentle scent.

"Send words to the perfume warehouse. I need something gentle and warm for spraying. I don't care if they make thousands bottles, I need everything they can get their hands on before daybreak." She ordered.

The maids dispersed, but one of them stayed and came to her.

"Grand Princess, I heard the prince of Eclypseria took a bride, and it's the 150 billion slave ." She gossiped.

"Seriously?" Pearl jumped.

"Oh my goodness!! Good news!!"

[Eclypseria, 4am]

Faye arrived with Xena and Blaise since an hour ago, they had flown with the air.

It was stressful to travel in the air with Xena who has no levitation ability, so they spent more time than required on the journey, and it was even harder to find the palace.

Eclypseria is not half as wide as Luminaria, but their architecture isn't straightforward at all.

There are too many corners and pits, and there are too many homeless people roaming the streets, diminishing the lights.

Faye is on the roof of an old house with the other two, and from there, they're staring at the Eclypseria palace.

It's like the only modern building in the whole city, completely lighted up and so damn tall. Painted black and gold, it looks terrifyingly beautiful even from afar, they could already see rich guards flanking each tower.

Unlike the Luminaria Palace, this palace is heavily guarded, and the gate looks like something that'd take days to climb with tearing thorns on top of it. It looks unapproachable.

The worse part? There are dead bodies hanging on the gate at the moment. Bloody dead bodies.

"I think we should just go back, please. I don't want to die yet." Blaise faced both Xena and Faye.

"Are you sure you even have a dîck?" Faye rolled eyes. "You should seriously start wearing skirt."

"Xena, you're not scared too?" Blaise faced her.

"I'll do anything to get Shay back. Not only is she like a little sister to me, but also... Ryker might just hate me if anything happens to her, so no...I'm not scared." Xena replied.

"That's my girl..." Faye smiled, standing from the roof.

She grabbed Xena's hand, jumping down with her.

Blaise jumped down beside them, too.

"If I die here tonight, oh Satan...don't even try to take my soul." He said.

He plucked out some of his red hair strands and folded them in his right palm.

Xena summoned some cruel bees, and thousands of them converged behind her, humming loudly.

They faced Faye, expecting her to showcase her powers too.

She smiled a villainous smile, then her body began changing.

Scales appeared—white shinny ones which caught the city lights.

Blaise and Xena shifted back as she turned bigger, scaling out completely. 

Her clothes fell to the ground.

She became a snake. A large, beautiful white snake which towered above Xena and Blaise.

"Hell." Xena muttered in shock.

"Don't be too shocked, she's a snake girl...a serpent." Blaise said.

"Oh..." Xena sounded.

They focused back on the palace, and their journey started towards it.

For the first three minutes, the journey went smoothly, and they're counting the seconds it'd take for them to arrive at the gigantic gate.

But then,...the city lights went off.

Every. Single. One.

They stopped on their tracks, and a cluster of arrows flew towards them from the south.

"I knew this wasn't a good idea. They knew people would come to try to save her, it's a trap!!" Blaise yelled, pouring his red strands away since it's useless now.

Sensing that the bees are useless too, Xena summoned bigger animals... Black wolves.

The wolves jumped across the skies, taking the hits of the arrows instead, but then another cluster of arrows came from the north.

This time, Faye leapt up, using her hard scales to take the arrows. They bounced off her scales, and the arrows dug into the ground instead.

But in the process of bouncing the arrows off, one of the blades entered her beyond her scales, and she came back to the ground, landing heavily.

She returned to her human form, losing consciousness.

"Faye!" Xena and Blaise rushed over to her.

Xena covered her naked lower body with her coat. Blaise used his jacket on her lower body.

"The arrows, I think they're poisoned." Blaise muttered.

As if to confirm that, they received their shares immediately.

Arrows met their backs, and they both went low, eyes drowsy.

"I knew this was a senseless move. These people are good with sleeping spells here." Blaise muttered sleepily.

They fell on top of Faye's body, losing consciousness too.

[Human World]

From the company, back to the glass mansion, Ryker hasn't stopped trying to open the gateway.

It's been eight hours now.

Currently 1pm in the human world, but nothing is coming forth.

He has slashed his own palm close to fifty times, adding blood to his ice to strengthen it, but no matter what, the gateway just won't appear.

He's pale in the face and on the lips as a result of blood loss.

Worse part is that; this is the human world, so every blood he wastes is going to count.

His icy body won't protect itself like it does in Luminaria, so his blood won't get replenished.

The more he lost, the weaker he got, but he's not stopping.

He's getting covered in ice again. 

Lashes, brows, lips, feet and palms. He's freezing up, but he's still not pausing.

Eternus is at a side of the glass, watching silently.

Cheyenne and Sage are in another area, and Cheyenne hasn't felt guilty like this all her life.

She hates herself right now.

"I told you, you should learn how to be calming down at times, Cheyenne. You shouldn't have come back." Sage said, looking frustrated too.

"I know, the blame is all mine." Cheyenne replied guiltily.

Ryker cut his wrist for the 51st time, dropping his dark blood on the building ice again.

It's still the same. No gateway, nothing.

He brought the dagger to cut for 52, but Eternus stepped in.

"No. You won't hurt your body any further when I'm here" he snatched the dagger from him, but the dagger flew out of his hand instantly.

It went back to Ryker.

"Oh...I see it only stays in the hand on it's master, but seriously you need to stop." Eternus said.

Ryker stabbed his wrist again.

"Ryker!" Eternus yelled in an alarming tone. "It's just four hours till sundown. By then, the gateway opens by itself and we'll leave!" 

Ryker said nothing, he continued dropping his blood in rivulets, looking rigidly iced on his spot. 

He's breathing cold steam out of his thin nostrils, and there are small pieces of ice in his hair too.

"You can't bend the rules again. No matter what you do, it won't open till sundown. You've killed a lot, Ryker, so even the heavens won't work in your favor. Wait till sundown." Eternus said.

"Fûck sundown." Ryker growled, deep and low before bringing the dagger to his own chest.

He stabbed deep.

"Fûck! Boss!!" Cheyenne and Sage let out conjoined screams.

[Eclypseria Palace, 7AM]

Shayla might have passed out suddenly, but her brain was sharp enough to process the fact that she was kidnapped by the distant prince, so the moment she opened her eyes to the light of the day, she knew she was in a different city.

The lightning, walls, ceilings, atmosphere and mood is all different. They smell like evil, hostility lingered thickly in the air.

She's on a bed. She could feel it beneath herself. She sat up very fast, her hair covering her face.

Every single jewelry inside her hair is gone, so she had to start brushing her hair back nervously with her fingers.

She inhaled the smell of smoke again, just that this time, she didn't fall asleep. She looked around the bedroom—not as big as Ryker's, but there are guards by the open door.

She could see their back views.

She brushed her hair back again with trembling hands, and her fingers hit a small pin.

There's a single hairpin left inside her hair. They must not have noticed it cos the hair fullness covered it.

She took the pin in her palm, hiding it under her robe, then she heard royal greetings by the door.

The guards bowed their heads respectfully as the prince himself entered.

Prince Dyke.

Shirtless and so bulgy with his veiny large muscled arms and legs. His hair is in a buzz cut, and his nose looks sculptured as it's overly pointed. It could stab.

His other features are just as sharp, giving his standoffish face too much dangerousness.

She shivered more at the feel of his cruel vibe, but she made sure to hold the hairpin tight under her robe.

Her tongue felt heavy inside her mouth, her veins shook in her body.

Prince Dyke stayed wordless till he sat beside her in bed. 

She quickly shifted back, gulping.

"Why...did you take me? There are other girls. I am...nobody." She muttered, voice dragging.

"You're nobody?" Prince Dyke smiled, the sight so bone-chilling. "I heard you're the demigod's partner, so you're far from nobody. Besides..."

He raised his hand to her face, tracing his pointer finger lustfully on her fragile cheekbones.

His eyes stayed as sinful as iniquities, brimming with impurities.

Shayla trembled, bending her body back. She feels like ants are crawling all-over her with that single touch.

"D-don't... touch...me that way." She stuttered, bones tightening.

"I like my brides fragile, untouched, virgins. I like innocent females with small breakable bodies like yours." He whispered, stopping his finger close to her lips.

He was about to press it when Shayla pulled her hand out from under her robe. She rammed the sharp hairpin into his neck.

She didn't even wait to see if it penetrated or not. The moment he groaned, she jumped down from the bed, running out of the room.

By now, the guards are gone from the door, so she was alone as she ran down the polished hall, her steps heavy against the sleek floor.

She just ran with no idea of where she's heading in mind, she needed to get as far away from the cruel prince as fast as possible.

But she wasn't born lucky.

Luckiness has never worked in her favor, cos just as she was about to cross to a barricaded corner, she was blocked by Prince Dyke himself.

Her blood turned hit inside her, and she stopped with a gasp.

And it's more scary cos the wound she gave him with the hairpin is now gone. No blood, no injury on his neck.

It's like she didn't even stab him cos he's already healed.

Shayla was only able to take one trembling step away from him before he tangled his hand into her hair, dragging her back to himself.

His merciless face clashed with hers, and she began crying.

"I'm sorry... please. I just wanted to—"

"I wanted to see how smart you are, so I left that one jewel in your hair. I must say you're a smart one, but no one gets smart with me and goes scot free." He dragged her to her knees in that cold hall.

"I was going to wait for our wedding night before doing this, but since your hand is smart, let's see if your mouth is smart too." He cackled, pulling his pants apart with a flip.

Shayla felt her blood pressure rising, heat rising and cold rising at the same damn time. 

Tears ran down her eyes too.

More tears. Loud cries. Sniffles.

He freed his enormous cóck, and her chest squeezed, her eyes widened.

She felt hot sweat running down the middle of her back.

Guards, Maids and the numerous wives of Prince Dyke...all of them assembled in the hall, ready to watch his madness again.

His cóck bounced up and down in front of Shayla's mouth, and her streaming tears entered her mouth.

"Please no...no. I don't want to!"

"It's not a choice, my bride," Prince Dyke fisted her hair so hard her scalp stung with pain.

He shoved her face closer to it like some cheap slave.

"It's a command. Now open your mouth and suck your my cóck!"

Shayla's heart sank when she saw a white stuff leaking out of his cóvk.

She might not know what it means, but she knows it's not good. If she gets forced to take that into her mouth, she'd fall sick. 

She doesn't want to have contact with that thing even in the slightest way ever.

The sting coming from her scalp nearly ran her mad as Prince Dyke shoved her without mercy again.

His cóck was just an inch away from entering her mouth when another presence entered.

It's the king of Eclypseria himself—Prince Dyke's father, looking as lustful as his son, if not more.

The moment he set eyes on her, he dropped the bottle of alcohol with him for a guard, and he rushed closer

"Father?" Prince Dyke suspended his cóck, smiling at his supportive father.

"Been a while since you brought something like this." The King murmured, his craving eyes not dropping from the kneeling female.

Shayla has stopped the tears already, but she's hiccuping, and she still looks half-traumatized.

"Something like this?" Prince Dyke laughed, standing beside his father. "You mean slim and clean?"

"I mean slim, clean and innocent-looking. You know I have a kink for those three combos." The King replied, gradually smiling.

"So what? You have plans?" Prince Dyke faced him. "I heard she's the popular ruthless demigod's partner"

"You mean Ryker Morozov? The bastard who slaughtered how many people again? How were you able to get his partner out of Luminaria?" The King asked, looking impressed.

"I'm your son, father. Trust that I have my feats too. I'd love to see just how ruthless he is, maybe he'll really be able to break the thousand year old stranger-restricting barrier of this palace." Prince Dyke sighed, his eyes falling back on Shayla on the ground.

"Alright," the king cleared his throat. "Take her back inside, there'll be a strip banquet at 10, she'll be the main strip object. After that, there'll be a public orgie show too, featuring I had my son and her. I'll take her from the front, Dyke will thrust from the back. In one word, we're ruining her beyond repair. Get your eyes ready, you're about to witness a one-of-it's-kind banquet." 

"Take her into the corner room, keep the door locked tight." Prince Dyke ordered.

Guards came for Shayla, lifting her off the ground from each side. Her small struggles were tantamount to nothing, she was helpless till she was thrown into the corner room.

After the door was locked, she used the first one minute to study the room.

There's no electric lightning like the room she woke up in. The only light in this room is coming from a burning fire in the middle of the room. It looks like a holding room for sinner servants, and it stinks.

Shayla stood, and her hands went to the door, slamming as if she could break it.

She was controlling the sniffles from her nose as she threw her body at the door repeatedly, and as she did, her back flared up with sudden pains she can't explain.

Her hands fell away from the door, the corner of her eyes throbbed with the sting, stretching to her whole body but mostly concentrating on her back. It didn't stop slashing her back.

It's like wildfire is burning in the middle of her back, or like wild animals are feasting on her.

She kept the pains inside.

She really, tried to keep it, but as she watched the burning fire in the corner of the room, the pain got unbearable, breaking through her endurance.

She cried out, rubbing her back on the door till it bled, staining her pink robe with redness.

She was gnashing her teeth, pressing her fingertips on her skin as the pains settled deeper, burning her.

She cried out again, going on her shaky knees.

She brought her arm to her mouth, attaching her teeth to it.

She began biting herself to reduce the pain, and her hot tears fell on her skin.

It's better to get beaten with the broomstick.

It's better to break stones at the slaves labor camp.

It's better to go back to the dark room cage.

Everything is better than this hellish pain that's about to split her into two.

Only one face is appearing in her head even through the pains.

She could see it clearly in her skull, and she's wondering if he'll come for her again. If he'll come fast and get her from this hellhole.

She attached her teeth to her arm again, biting herself repeatedly as the fire went blur in her peripheral.

[Outsiders Prison]

Blaise's palm met his own skin with a smack. He got rid of the mosquito biting him, and he stirred on the straw he's laying on.

For the second time, his palm met with his skin, killing the second mosquito.

"Who are these bloodsuckers? Geez." He muttered, feeling the rough irregularities on his skin from the straw beneath him.

He touched with his fingers, and his eyes opened fast. 

He sat up to see he's really sleeping on dry straws in the Eclypseria palace prison.

Xena and Faye are both beside him, they're both awake too, both staring at him with unreadable eyes.

"What is going on? This isn't a prison, is it?" He spranged up.

"What does it look like? A suite? A manor?" Faye scoffed.

"How long have you both been awake? You guys should have tapped me awake!" He talked again, all while still facing Faye.

"If we had tapped you awake, would you have proffered a solution to this problem we're in?" Faye frankly said.

"Please, Blaise. Just sleep back, consider this a vacation."

"Vacation? In this godawful place? Yunno you have no right to act like the almighty now, right? You suggested this rubbish, and when I went against it, you called me a coward and dragged us here, now look... we're on a prison vacation! I've never been in a prison all my life! Even when I was arrested at the East Palace, I was in a pit, not a prison!" He yelled.

"And?" Faye yawned.

"And?" Blaise stepped into her space to grab her by the jacket. He locked her neck.

"Did you just say and? Are you serious? I'm Blaise Spectre, prison should be one of the places I shouldn't even step the threshold!"

Faye pushed him off, dusting away her jacket as if he's a plaque.

"I suggested to come cos I wanted to help you and your mother get on the demigod's side, so stop trying to guilt-trip me!" She shouted.

Blaise nearly grabbed her jacket again, but Xena quickly stood from the ground and got between them.

"Seriously? Is this how you both behave at the West Palace always?" She cautioned.

Faye sat back on the floor, Blaise held the prison bars, pulling at it.

"Don't try to use strength, it's useless. We can't use our powers in here." Xena said.

Blaise faced them, looking more terrified.

"What!"

"I can't summon animals, Faye can't scale out, and you..." Xena sighed. "Your hair has changed to dark."

"What!!" Blaise touched his hair in shock.

"We only have one saving option right now,that's if the demigod comes at all." Faye said from the ground.

Xena looked ahead the bars, smiling a little as she inhaled the palace aura.

"I wonder what they're currently doing to Shay" she mumbled.

[Luminaria, East Palace]

One slap from Azura made Lilith fall on her knees at the wide lobby. It echoed through the walls, and Lilith held her cheek.

Now it's just few minutes till sundown, and his mind is working faster than the clock in counting the damn minutes.

Eternus and his knights are ready behind him.

At one minute before sundown, he waved his arm. His suit left his body, getting replaced by a black robe.

Exactly sundown, the gateway finally appeared on it's own, and he led them inside.

They descended the ladder back to Luminaria.

The moment they entered, Ryker felt slight rejuvenation in his system, but it wasn't enough to relieve his ice.

He stayed frozen till they arrived at the ballet school.

Cheyenne led them to the mirror maze, and he didn't have to wander before seeing Shayla's golden hair pendant under one of the mirrors.

He picked it up and brought it to his nose, sniffling the aura.

He could still smell her gentle warmth, but apart from that, there's a thick scent of smoke lingering.

His lashes shifted up.

The last time his nose caught a smell of this kind was when Eternus took him to Eclypseria.

"Smells like Eclypseria." Eternus said behind him, confirming it.

"I heard the Eclypseria prince was going to be at the party to pick a bride." Cheyenne spoke up, and Ryker turned back immediately, his already frozen eyes gleaming frosty blue.

"I trust Faye, she's the instigator." Astor added.

Winona was silent.

Ryker, too.

All that's in his head is bride.

That word—BRIDE.

Seriously? The more he thought about it, the larger the rage expands, the higher the anger, the broader the ice enclosing inside him.

"My Lord , I think you should go soak in the spring pool first. It's dangerous to go to Eclypseria like this." Sage said as tiny ice cubes fell from his body to the mirrored ground.

"The pool can wait." Ryker declared, voice grim as he turned his posture.

He vanished from the maze, together with his knights and Eternus.

[Eclypseria Palace, Corner Room]

Her eyes her drowsy, lashes barely up as she was taken through the wide hall where Prince Dyke's wives are all waiting, looking at her with pity.

There are kids too, so many kids belonging to Prince Dyke himself from his numerous wives.

Some of them are even breastfeeding.

Shayla's throat feels parched. Water. She needed water, must be the pain on her back causing that.

Her pale lips looks as hurtful as the rest of her face, and she was already dreaming of death when one of the wives suddenly pushed the guards away from her.

"Run!!" That wife screamed at her.

Shayla stood to her feet, not leaving the opportunity hanging at all.

"Go south, you'll find a forest. Walk under the shadow of the leaves to avoid getting seen." Another wife said 

Shayla ran down the hall, and when the other guards tried going after her, the remaining wives stopped them.

Shayla looked back when she got to the end of the hall, seeing the struggling wives all because of her.

She resumed her run, and though her back was killing her, she dashed out of the main palace walls.

The guards at the other side of the palace came after her, and that was when she knew the thing about being unlucky was real.

She's really an unlucky one.

She went south like the wives told her, but on that south, there are stationed guards too.

She was grabbed, yet again, and this time, she wasn't let go till they arrived at the banquet arena in the open roof of the palace.

There are ministers, kingpins, palace royalties and royal bloods, the king and Prince Dyke himself.

"Thinking you can escape this heavily guarded palace wasn't so smart, my bride." Prince Dyke said beside his father where he sat planted 

Shayla was plunged down in the middle of the eagar old men, and most of them are already lowering their pants, planning to jerk off to her strip session.

"Start the show, don't waste anymore time. We're all busy people." the King ordered, picking a flute of alcohol to pour down his lust filled throat.

Shayla looked up at the pool of desperados, her fingers trembled again on the ground where she placed them.

Can't Rumi just take pity on her and release that mark just this once?

She values her nakedness too much, it's the only thing she has left.

Her pride.

She has only let her princess part show in the presence of one man whose face isn't among this bitter crowd at all.

She'd never do this, she can't. They can just kill her instead.

So she kept sitting there, and the pain from her back slashed through her again, causing her to scrap the ground with her nails.

"Ahh!!" She groaned, propping on her knees and palms, sweating.

"Start the session!" Prince Dyke ordered strictly, hitting the chair with his thick callused hands.

"Your Grace! There's a problem!" A guard came inside with a run.

He whispered into the king's ear, and it sounded like something calamitous cos the king spranged up from his chair.

"He broke the palace barrier?" 

"What's going on?" Prince Dyke stood too, and the crowd of oldies started standing one by one when they sensed the shift.

"He's close, barrier broken. City in ruins." The King said.

"He really did?" Prince Dyke gritted his dentition.

He went to the arena and dragged Shayla up with her hair again.

[City Of Eclypseria]

The people who're still alive right now are definitely lower than twenty, and those twenty are still owing death their lives.

This massacre is more than the one at Mount Zhitomir. This is crueller.

Bodies lay on top of each other, all innocent and unaware of why they even died, but Ryker wouldn't care about anything at the moment.

Buildings are freezing up, crashing and falling at his cold feet.

People are losing their runs, spitting blood and falling to their demise.

Heads flew away from necks, bodies flew away from necks.

He didn't even have to touch them, each step he took, bodies dropped, the blood flowed.

His steps are quiet, unheard as he walked through the ruins of the city, the sounds of falling buildings a playing loop behind him and around him. He was insatiably craving.

His robe nearly dragged the ground, but the tips are touching the ground, and the fabric is drinking blood.

His face is freezing already, getting worse by his chest stab and wrist wounds, but that won't be enough to put a stop to his bloodthirst.

Cheyenne, Sage and Eternus are on the bridge which slashed by the city. 

From there, they're watching the extinction, unable to do a thing to stop him.

"Their stupid palace barrier should have opened by now." Sage said.

"It opened the moment we entered this city. Who are they kidding with that crap?" Cheyenne scoffed.

Eternus chose to stay silent, just staring at Ryker's present state.

The last body dropped beside Ryker in the bloody streets, and his blue eyes casted down the female corpse.

He couldn't smirk even if he wanted to, the ice held his already bloody face in place. He reeks of blood.

The knights jumped down the bridge, and Eternus joined them.

They landed in the palace, meeting the old men who escaped the arena by the gate.

They're the first people to taste death in that palace.

Ryker didn't even touch or try to kill them. He walked past.

Cheyenne and Sage took care of them. Even Eternus summoned his sword, joining the slaying.

Bodies began falling again.

Getting inside the palace, the wives and children are already converged at a corner, quivering in fear.

Their heads are all down, on the ground. They're too scared to look up

Ryker's bloodthirsty condition didn't give space for mercy.

It has never did.

He nearly stabbed them with spikes, but he heard whimpers of Shayla, and his head snapped to the left.

He super sped there, entering the portal he came across since he could smell her scent inside.

Right inside the portal, Shayla sat, looking drained, tortured and in deep-rooted pains.

Bloody back, pale lips, her face had few cuts.

"Shayla." His deep voice dropped.

Two steps run, he was with her. 

He went on a knee, cradling her small teary chin in the middle of his palm, staring deep at the details of her face.

He boiled inside despite the ice.

Shayla smiled dryly, raising her hand to press his own hand harder to her red face, focusing only on his eyes.

"It hurts, like hell." She whispered, sniffling and hiccuping.

"Tell me, everything." He replied, his whisper deeper but calm.

"He wanted me to have his thing in my mouth, but I narrowly escaped..." She hiccupped. "I was nearly made to give them a strip session too." 

The blue in Ryker's eyes switched. Redness swallowed the irises.

"No!!" Her voice screamed suddenly.

A flat sword entered Ryker's body from behind, coming out on his chest.

He bled from the mouth, his blood spluttered on Shayla's face.

"My...My Lord." She stuttered, blood curling. "Moy Vladika!!"

Ryker fell on the dusty ground, his blood colouring his lips.

Prince Dyke and his father are behind. Prince Dyke is the one holding the sword, wet with Ryker's blood.

"I expected more from the demigod. Samurai sword was enough to take him down?" He laughed.

"You pulled the words right out of my mouth." The King joined the laughter.

"No..." Shayla dragged herself to her knees. "No!!"

She was already crawling to Ryker's body when Prince Dyke stalked to her.

"You'll never be free from us, my bride. Now you're ours. You belong to me and my father! Ours to fûck! I'd like to feel the satisfaction of fuckin a demigoddess." He boomed.

Talking was one part, but getting to her was the craziest part.

The moment he stretched his hand to her hair to drag her up, that hand was pulled, and the hand fell out of his body, flatly to the ground.

"Ohhgghhhh!!!!!" Prince Dyke howled like a wounded wolf.

Tears poured out of his eyes as his blood flowed.

The King stepped back, horror registering deep on his face.

Shayla looked forward to see Ryker's body isn't on the ground anymore.

She looked ahead, and her breaths clogged madly when she saw Ryker is already with Prince Dyke.

He's the one who did the arm pull, and right now, his five fingers are allover Prince Dyke's face.

Eyes redder.

Aura darker.

Energy punishing.

"Even at my weakest," he growled. "No one...I repeat...no one can take my life unless I willingly let it go."

"Ahhhgh!!!" Prince Dyke cried louder than his vocal chords.

Shayla stood, sniffling and unable to move.

"You shouldn't have touched her, not even a hair strand on her head!" Ryker spat fiercely.

And with that said, his fingers dug deeper into Prince Dyke's face.

He tore the skin on his face away.

"Dyke!!!!!!" The name rolled out of the king's tongue with horror, tears streamed out of his eyes.

"Dyke!!!!!!" Another scream spilled out, louder with more depth as if he had just realized.

Shayla was breathless behind. 

Momentarily, she forgot the pains from her aching back. Her back was on the wall before she knew it.

When she was in the dark room, the guards threatened to tear off her whole skin anytime she made a mistake, and she always thought it was an empty threat.

What human would tear the skin of another human completely?

But it wasn't empty after all. Someone just did it in front of her, he even pulled out the arm...

Everything done singlehandedly.

The cries of the king got louder and crazier, like he had just lost his only son which is true.

No one would live without a facial skin, but it didn't end there.

Ryker's eyes found Shayla, and she held her breaths again as he conjured a spooky ice thorn on his palm.

He held eyes with her as he threw the thorn to Prince Dyke's middle.

His cóck dangled out of his body, and Shayla fell on her butt, covering her mouth with her palm.

She let out her breaths, they're shaky and hot.

Prince Dyke's incomplete body crumbled heavily on the ground, his blood filled the portal.

"You're really a bastard as they say!  An uncircumcised bastard!!!" The King grabbed the sword, standing from where he sat on the ground.

He pointed the sword at Ryker who looks nonchalant even with the blood on his face and fingers.

"I never tried to beat the allegations. Always a bastard, always will be." He spoke with intentional slowness which angered the king further.

"I'm willing to send you to hell." He spat, rubbing his index finger ring on the sword blade.

The blade caught fire, burning bright and filling the room with yellow illumination.

"Take it!" He threw the sword at Ryker's way, straight.

Ryker didn't move out of the way, he saw it coming but stood there.

Shayla raised herself from the wall, rushing over to the end point.

She nearly got in front of him to take the sword pierce, but Ryker buckled his arm on her waist and pulled her away from the point.

With that done, he faced the point himself. The burning sword stayed in front of his eyes, and the shadows of the burning fire swirled in his eyes, but the sword wouldn't move closer.

Slowly, the fire on the blade went off, thanks to his ice.

"Next time, use a more powerful fire. But too bad, there'll be no next time." Ryker said, directing the weak blaze back to the starting point.

The King couldn't even escape his own sword. He wasn't able to take a step away before the sword pierced him, carrying him to the wall.

The sword stabbed his body to the wall, and he went limp there, his blood drawing irregularly on the wall to the ground.

Shayla's body went rigid beside Ryker again, she swallowed emptiness multiple times.

Ryker's eyes shifted back to her, and she did the swallow thing again.

"What were you trying to do getting in front of the sword like that?" He questioned.

"I carry the FireStone inside me, I thought if the sword pierced me, it wouldn't wound me. I just didn't want to sit down uselessly." She replied.

"You will not do that again." He instructed, holding her waist tighter as if to drive the point home.

"But you were—"

"Shayla!" He snapped.

"Yes...I won't do it again." She muttered in a low voice.

Ryker lifted her robe sleeves when she noticed she was cringing, and when he saw the bite marks, the anger he thought was reducing started building again.

"My back was aching, and I had to bite myself to keep the pain minimized. It's not very painful, you're here now so it's fine." She said with a little convincing smile, but he could see through her smile.

Through the pains.

He waved his two fingers over the bite marks, but they refused to heal.

He dragged them over her facial cuts too.

Still the same.

He's unable to heal her.

Must be because of his exhaustion and the coldness inside of him. She's currently wounded and weak too, so if he absorbs her warmth right now, she'd suffer backlash.

He'd have to use the pool, and she'd have to rest well before any healing process can work.

He lifted her into his arms, and her arms went locked behind his neck.

He carried her out of that portal, and they met bodies outside.

Every single soul on the palace is also dead, bodies are scattered across the marbles.

He could see the sign of Cheyenne's nails, the sign of Sage's rope, and the sign of Eternus sword on the bodies.

He met them close to the banquet arena, they're now six—plus Xena, Faye and Blaise.

Cheyenne had found them in the prison during the slaughter.

"Shay!" Xena gasped, looking relieved when she saw her.

Shayla smiled at her from Ryker's arms, and she returned it.

Ryker's eyes went behind the arena, dissatisfied to see there are still people alive—the helpless wives and their children.

He dropped Shayla gently, and he was about to freeze them to death when Shayla got in his way.

"No! They tried to help me escape! They're innocent, you can't kill them!" She brought his hand down, but he took it from her.

"Please... please please don't kill them. Please..." She pleaded in repeat, holding his hand again and looking up at his eyes maybe she'd catch some pity or mercy.

There was none.

"I never leave witnesses." He groaned and took her out of the way.

He froze them. To death.

He didn't even bother doing the shatter, they all stood frozen in their spots like statues.

"No!! No!!!" Shayla covered her mouth as she fought the tears stinging her eyes.

"They did nothing wrong... nothing." She sobbed, shutting her eyes.

"Has your brother always been like this?" Blaise whispered beside Xena.

"I swear I'm not breathing." Faye muttered.

Xena was speechless.

"I wouldn't care less if he decides to visit the non-human city closest to this one to kill additional people." Cheyenne said.

"Of course, you're just as cold-blooded." Sage replied.

Eternus is looking at Ryker with eyes that says he has lost all hope of redemption for him.

He doesn't see this god growing a love thread anytime soon.

No. That's not happening with this heartlessness.

Ryker tried carrying Shayla again, but she shifted back, shaking her head.

He looked at her, and she shook her head again.

The fear of him that was already reducing...it came back in folds, her eyes are full of it.

He didn't care about it though, he forcefully carried her in his arms, and he could feel her body shivering against him as he caged her there.

They vanished back to Luminaria with the rest.

Even Faye and Blaise followed them to the East Palace in the tower.

Eternus too.

Ryker dropped Shayla on her bed in her room, and when he reached for her hair to touch, she shifted back, sniffling and hiccuping.

"The second Granny is here. Apparently, she arrived since morning, but she has been resting in her chambers. She only just stepped out this night, and she's already showing what she's capable of." 

"Things like?" Ryker sighed.

"Killing a guard because he was too fat. She went to the slaves hall and killed ten slaves, Note; they did nothing to her—she drowned them in the face-eating well. Also, she poured hot water on your fath...i mean, Old Chef. She poured hot water on his hands because he served her cold tea." Sage listed the atrocities.

"And?" Ryker sounded.

"She's currently eating dinner with every member of the family at the royal dinning." Sage said.

"She was meant to come play her part. Let her be, I heard a lot about her psychopathia, and I'd love to witness more. Bring Xena to the tower." Ryker replied and kept the shell away.

His eyes went back to Shayla's head on his chest.

She's already asleep, but that won't stop him from taking her where he has in mind, so he brought the bike back to life again.

Even in her sleep, her arms instinctively wrapped on him. 

She held tight till he arrived at his destination.

[East Palace, Castle Dinning]

The dinning is silent, not even the sound of cutleries on plates can be heard. Everyone ate in silence, not daring to make any pauperism sound like Granny Tara had tagged it.

Granny Tara sat at the head of the table, looking as dark as ever. Her snake is on the table in front of her, and the serpent is eating with her on the same plate.

Despite Sadie's overbearing cranky personality, she's trying her best to keep herself together on the table for the sake of her favorite Granny.

Jude looks like he's in-between. Not happy not sad about this visit.

Soren looks obviously unpleased. He didn't even try to hide it.

Pearl looks just as intrigued as Sadie. She's even smiling as she ate.

Lilith looks invisible on her seat as she had to stretch her spoon to reach the table. She's more focused on the food than the new granny.

Her wedding dress is done, ready for the wedding in two days time.

She dropped the phone and focused back on her food.

The snake faced him, Jude shifted back on his chair.

"Of course." Lilith smiled.

Soren didn't reply, he just drank some water and relaxed.

Soren stayed still like he's deaf.

"Soren Millions." Tara called his full name, but he stayed unmoved.

Sadie folded her fists angrily.

Azura smiled, resuming her meal.

At the same time, Xena entered the dinning to deliver more soup cos Zeke ordered it.

"And who said you could enter at this time? Who said that!" Tara stood angrily, throwing her snake at her.

Xena's eyes widened, and as the snake neared her, she tried changing it's direction by communicating with it, but the snake is Tara's soul pet, so her ability didn't work on it at all.

The snake was almost at her face when Soren stood, hitting it away. 

The snake gave him a bite on the wrist before returning to the table, but he kept the bite secret like it was nothing, like he was ok.

Sadie kept sitting, thinking of brutal things to do to Xena.

Should she just cut her n1pples away and wear them as earrings?

She stabbed her steak angrily with her fork before leaving the dinning too.

Jude and Pearl left the dinning together too, and Lilith left with her own plate of food.

"I noticed a strange energy around him, that was the real reason I spoke to him last." Tara said, sitting back.

"What strange energy?" Zeke asked.

Tara faced him.

"That child is our major weapon in the future, Zeke. He's going to be what we need most, so we can't let anything come close. E.g, the Old Chef's daughter." She said.

"Anything you say shall be done. You're my only confidant apart from Esme." Zeke smiled.

[City, King's Mansion]

She didn't wait long. Just five minutes later, Liza entered the room with her phone to her ear.

She's speaking to someone.

"Rumi, I respect you a lot as the demigod's sister, I respect you cos your spells are strong, but you're starting to make me think you're unworthy of that respect. What was that letter? You're going to stop working with me and unveil the mark cover anytime you want? I paid you!!" Liza shouted.

"Maybe if you pay more?" Rumi replied from the other end.

"I've been paying you for close to 23 years now, and I'm saying no. I won't give you more money for your drugs addiction you junkie! You do not mess with me!" Liza threatened.

"Should I ring it that you're her mother then? Kade hates her, so the moment it gets out that she's his half-sister, he'd hate you more than you can handle. Should I shake the tables and raise the stakes?" Rumi laughed.

Faye slithered back down the wall, crawling out to where she scaled out at. She changed back to human form and dressed back up, smiling as she rushed down the silent road.

[West Palace]

She took Blaise's juice cup and downed it all at once.

"Also, Shayla's mark isn't coming out yet because it's covered by someone called Rumi, and to lift the cover, Shayla has to—"

A gunshot sliced through the air, interrupting the peace and quiet.

The bullet came directly for Faye, and she quickly jumped out of the way, landing on the nearest couch where the second gunshot sounded at.

Faye tumbled, escaping that one too, and when she landed back on her feet, she landed beside Blaise who immediately covered her.

All mouths are wide, especially the parents and Winona.

Faye was panting, and at the same time, they all looked up at the railings where the shooter stood like the devil's vice.

Rumi.

She leaned her elbows on the railings, staring down at everyone with a sick smile on her face which looks exactly like Xena's.

Except that her own hair is bright blonde.

A cartridge gun sat in her right hand, and her manicured fingernails are tapping the trigger carelessly.

The smell of gunpowder is still in the air, thickly.

"I missed two times...I must admit you're fast, Faye." She said, voice velvety and high.

"I put spells around the house of my clients, so I get alerted anytime there's a gecko glitch. You really didn't think you'd open the secret I've been keeping for years, did you?" She grinned, raising her legs to climb the sharp edge of the railings.

She stood there with her heels.

"X..ena?" Blaise muttered.

"Nice try, it's Rumi." Rumi winked.

[A Flower Island]

Shayla didn't expect anything less from the god of ice and treasures, but at the same time, she didn't expect something this splendour.

The moment she opened her eyes earlier and came down from the bike with Ryker, she was shocked to see their location changed completely.

They're in a flower island which she hasn't heard about before, and there are every flower you could think of, covering the grounds, slopes and valleys. Also, there's a flowing river beneath the island, surface clustered with flower varieties like the main island itself.

There are snacks, fruits and there are playthings too, a fake gun and a real arrow, cartridges.

"Wow! This is beautiful!" She gushed, skipping her legs down the island in a small dance.

She nearly stumbled into Ryker's legs, so he had to stop and let her do her silly dance away.

Her hair was jumping with her as she leapt, and she finally fell when her boot git stuck in one of the flower stems.

"Shayla!" Ryker went down to pick her up, and she smiled brightly at him.

"I'm just too thrilled to see this, what's the name of this place?" She asked, rubbing his fingers.

"No name yet, I created it when you were asleep in the afternoon." He replied, and she looked back at the island with a blink.

"Really? No name yet? Can I name it then?"

"As you wish" he walked to the table to sit on it, and she immediately stood between his legs.

"Pink Island?" She suggested.

"Childish." He replied.

"But pink flowers are so many here...is pink your favorite color?" She asked, hanging her arms on his neck slowly.

"I don't have a favorite color, but pink is yours." He replied, looking up at her with a smile.

"Gosh...I love when you smile." She touched his dimples fondly, and that made him laugh.

"When you laugh too." She added, and he held her waist.

"Do you love flowers?" She asked.

"I prefer roses, but you love flowers." He replied.

"How did you know?" She blinked.

"Should there be a how?" He moved his head, and she took her bottom lip in, blinking.

"Cute" he touched her nose before picking the fake gun from the table.

He threw it to her, and she caught.

"It's heavy!" She gasped.

"Of course, death is heavy." He replied, getting behind her to raise the gun with her.

Together, their fingers slid under the trigger, and he dragged it with her.

Instead of bullet, fireworks exploded from the barrel, cracking in a loop across across the moonlit sky.

"Not a gun? Whoa..." Her mouth and eyes widened in awe as she beheld the supernova beauty.

"Of course it's not, you'd probably faint here of it was a gun." He replied.

"Gun is scary, of course I'd be scared" she stated.

"Scared of guns when you have a FireStone burning inside you? Peak." He said, placing his chin on her hair and inhaling the scent of her shampoo as they watched the still cracking fireworks together.

Occasionally, he peeked at her happy face, the sight making him calm.

"Will I become ruthless like you when I get my mark?" She suddenly said, not pulling her eyes away from the skies.

"You don't want to?" He fingered her hair gently.

"I want to remain a soft girl forever and only bite back when necessary. I just want softness only." She replied sincerely.

"We'll see about that when the mark appears soon." He set his hand on her tummy, pressing the skin.

Shayla picked a strawberry from the table, taking a huge bite then offering him to take a bite too.

"Not interested." He frowned, slightly pushing it away.

"Oh...sorry." She said.

Her expression fell flat, but she tried to hide it which failed.

She was about to lower her hand when he took it suddenly, and she gasped out.

He brought her hand, to his mouth to take a small bite from the strawberry.

"Happy now?" He asked, chewing slowly.

Shayla smiled with two fast nods, watching him chew with intrigue.

The breeze turned colder, suddenly feeling like it's going to rain, but it's not rain. The weather is probably respecting Ryker's cold aura.

Shayla moved to the other side of the table, check-in out the rest of the food with glee.

"Hmm! Osmanthus candy!" She picked it up, unwrapping one into her mouth.

She picked the small plate of cake and sniffed it close to her nose, her expression funny as she looked at Ryker across the table.

Ryker's smile was small as she took a huge bite from the cake, eating...

"Delicious..." She shook her head slowly, closing her eyes to savor.

She dropped the plate and came to him, jumping on him suddenly.

Ryker quickly held her laps, keeping her safe as her face rested on his neck where she chewed the rest of her cake.

"You smell so good, it's addictive. Can't even name the scent but I like it." She said, sniffing his neck.

"You're a simp." He chuckled smugly, placing her butt on the table.

"What's a simp?" She asked.

"Won't tell" he said.

"I see it through your eyes, it's something bad." She replied.

"And?" He inched closer.

"I...." She smiled. "I don't know." 

She was only able to say that much before he pressed his lips tightly on hers, devouring her face.

She has no idea why her chest kept slamming, but she held his face, her thumb rubbing his ears, and his hand flattened at the back of her head.

The kissed him back with sweet softness, their pointed noses rubbing which she has come to like a lot.

Suddenly, something foreign surged inside Ryker, shaking him.

His eyes opened, and his lips separated from hers.

The absence of Ryker's lips made Shayla's lashes separate, her eyes opened, catching his own.

He looks the same, but there's a small tilt below his right brow.

"Moy Vladika, are you ok?" She asked the first question that came to mind.

He kept looking at her as if there's something hanging on her face.

The moonlight shattered between them, brightening the island more, but she has no business with that beauty right now.

Something isn't right.

"Huh? Are you...ok?" She asked again, lifting her palm to his forehead to touch gently.

"But your temperature isn't too cold" she muttered confusedly.

Ryker took her hand away, sitting back on the chair. He gave her smallest finger a slow caress before letting it go.

"Explore, it's all yours." He said point blank.

Shayla did her slow blink. "But you suddenly broke the...kiss." she drawled carefully.

"What? You wanna continue?" He leaned in sharply with that smug expression again.

Shayla stepped away before he could reach her. A cute chuckle filled her lips as her steps fell afar.

"No. I'm just worried, you seemed to be zoning out." she said, subtly biting the inside of her mouth.

The NO was a lie. 

None of their kisses has lasted as long as she wanted, and that hurts.

Maybe she's the greedy addicted one, or maybe he's the one not giving enough, but all she knows is that once his lips separates from hers, she'd be wanting more, and more.

Ryker lowered his hand, taking a cup of alcohol from the table, but he poured it away without drinking.

No idea why, but the smell of food and drinks just have a way of irritating him, but he ate the salad from her the other time, and he took a bite from her strawberry earlier without getting that irritation.

She's really effortlessly making him do these things.

A smirk formed on his lips, and his mind zoned to what happened some minutes ago. 

The foreign surge inside him. 

Last time he felt this surge was 200 years ago, he got so weak without a reason, and he had to go into seclusion for a year. 

The surge is like a premonition of bad things, meaning he has to heighten his carefulness.

He looked ahead, and he squinted a bit when he saw Shayla inside the island river already.

The water reached her knees, not very deep.

She's looking at the river with the stare of an huntress, and he sighted what she's hunting for—a octopus.

She has been chasing it around the water for minutes now, but her warmth is driving the sea animal away from her.

She went down to catch it again, and Ryker secretly stretched two fingers forward, freezing the octopus.

Shayla was finally able to catch, and the smile that rubbed on her face afterwards was priceless.

She looked up, raising the animal as she walked out of the water.

Her boots are completely wet with the water, even the down parts of her jean pants. They're all dripping as she came back to him with a little run.

"Look, I got it." She said, breathing heavily and dangling it.

"Messy, are you gonna eat that?" He frowned.

"No! It's just fun catching it. When I was young, I had no friends or playmates, and I've always wanted to go on play outings to the park, but all I got was beatings and ill-treatment from Ma, so don't be mad at me if I get messy sometimes. I guess my inner child is still breathing." She said, face going down.

"Who said you could go sad?" Ryker talked.

She looked up quickly. "You're really not angry about it?"

"You're cute when you play childish." He replied seriously, and she smiled wide with a huge blush to crown it.

"What are you going to do with that?" He asked, looking at the octopus. "Kill it?" He lifted his hand.

"No! Everything isn't about killing please! I'll return it to the water!" She quickly rushed two steps back to the shallow water.

She threw the octopus back inside, and as she rushed back, Ryker waved his arm.

Their chic outfits changed to robes. Black and pink respectively. On the waist of Shayla's robe, there's a tiny scent bag hanging there.

"Whoa! The more I know you, the cooler you turn out to be, Boss." Shayla said, jumping a bit so her robe flowed with the air.

"Don't call me that, or I'll really kill you." He deadpanned, walking ahead of her.

Shayla was skipping her legs behind him till she got in front of him.

She faced him, walking backwards.

"Why can't I call you that, Boss?" She intentionally asked with a warm teasing smile.

He didn't reply, so she stopped walking, till he walked past her.

"Boss, you really don't like the name? Huh?" She went after him again, holding the tip of his robe with that tip of her fingers.

Ryker smiled secretly, but Shayla saw it, and she chuckled, staring at him with with eyes full of admiration.

Suddenly, Ryker stopped, facing her.

She let go of his robe tip.

"Get a different name." He said.

"Boss?" She replied.

"I'll seriously kill you" he glared, his deep voice tickling her ears.

"Moy Vladika?" She said, getting serious too.

"No. You have three days to form one, your highness." He replied, taking her hand and pulling her over the bridge.

"Three...days? That's too small." She complained.

"I don't care." He replied, caging her to the bridge rails suddenly. Their lips almost touched.

Her breaths hitched, and she expected something steamy again, but Ryker just teleported with her.
