[West Palace, 11PM]

Everyone is sitting down.

Kasia and Astor on a couch.

Winona on a single couch.

Faye and Blaise on another couch.

Rumi is sitting on a stool in the middle of everyone of them, and there's a permanent smile on her face.

Her gun is still in her right hand.

On the left hand, she's eating a gumbo soup which she scooped from their kitchen.

"You better decide who's gonna die first, cos if I finish this soup without any decision from y'all, I'll kill everyone using the same bullet. It'll go from one body to another." She said, bringing another spoon of the soup to her mouth.

Faye is exchanging signals with Astor, using her eyes.

Blaise is communicating with Winona and Kasia too with his body.

"And if you think this is an ordinary cartridge gun, then you're wrong." Rumi continued, raising the gun.

Her badass smile spread to her eyes as she stared at the gun like her priceless treasure.

"I cast a spell on the bullet, one can kill at least seven people at once. It doesn't matter if you're Eternals or not, it'd cut your lives short." She stood, dropping the soup plate.

She faced Faye, stretching the gun to aim at her.

"You shouldn't have gone to my client's house, Faye.

"Shoot me, if you dare!" Faye spranged up suddenly.

Rumi didn't waste time after hearing those daring words.

She fired.

The echoing sound of gunshot blasted through the silent air, causing everyone else to stand up.

"Faye!!" They shouted collectively, horror and terror accumulating.

Faye fell on the couch, her shot chest bleeding to the ground, but she made no sound, just there like a dead weight.

"I've lived for hundreds of years, and for those years, I wrecked havoc everywhere I went. They know my history in every non-human city I visited. Eclypseria, Solaria, Vekkar, Kelara! just name it! I kill families and murder friends to shred attachments, and you...a mere snake... you wanna act as a stunt in my way? You're way too full of yourself." Rumi scoffed.

But her expression experienced a breach when Faye suddenly stood from the couch like she wasn't the one who just got shot.

She brought her palm to her mouth, vomiting the bullet on it.

Rumi's eyes snapped.

"A mere snake?" Faye smiled too, psychotic and dark. "That's where you're mistaken, bîtch. I'm a white viper, and your spelled bullet is a poison I can easily neutralize. I've been eating poison since long ago, so you're the one who should be scared, cos I show no mercy to your kind!" 

"Thank you Faye!" Blaise hailed from the other end of the room.

Just one second, Faye transformed, her snake form nearly took over every part of the living room.

Winona disappeared into the wall.

Blaise's red hair shone in waves.

Kasia and Astor stood side by side, and they successfully trapped Rumi in the middle.

Unluckily for her, the gun only has one bullet, so it's useless now.

She grabbed their mop stick from the ground, bringing it to her knees to break away the woolen part.

She kept the stick, defending herself with it from the whole family, but she's no match.

Winona bursted out of the wall behind her, giving her a hit at the back of the neck which sent her flying to Blaise.

Blaise's hair strands messed with her eyes, but she was able to smack Blaise with her stick twice before she went down flat.

From there, Kasia and Astor mind-lifted the center table, bringing it down on her body. 

The table shattered to bits on her body, sending glasses across the lobby. Rumi's blood flooded the middle, and now it's Faye's turn.

She crawled aggressively to that middle, wrapping her tail on Rumi's body. She threw her off that angle, and Rumi's body travelled to the railings where she hung like a rag.

She spit hot blood to the ground, and she jumped down the bloody railings with heavily bruised body.

From there, she went out of the top window, bursting out of it. She left with the breeze.

Faye transformed back, and Blaise quickly took off his jacket to cover her naked body with, but Winona stopped him.

"Close your darn eyes, pervert." She rolled eyes, snatching the jacket from him to go cover Faye with.

"But I covered her in Eclypseria, nothing I haven't seen." Blaise muttered.

"That was a great warmup." Kasia smiled at Astor.

"I know, right?" He chuckled.

"All thanks to Faye" Winona said.

"Of course" Blaise nodded.

"To get her mark back, she has to travel back to the past, into her own memory." Faye said calmly.

"I'll text Ryker. He rarely checks texts, so let's hope he sees this." Kasia said, getting her phone.

--

Rumi was staggering as she walked the last street that leads to the city. Her arms are on the wall, dragging with her heels on the ground.

Her blood was drawing on the wall, and she's still vomiting blood.

The wounds on her body still bleeding.

She entered a alchemist shop, and the alchemist stood shockingly at her sight, wondering where she got thrashed so much.

"Painkiller herb" she ordered, leaning on the counter.

She held her bleeding rib.

The man gave her a strange look before taking out a small bottle which he handed to her.

Rumi drank it all at a go, wiping her lips with her palm.

"That'd be two thousand nyx." The alchemist said.

Unfortunately, those were his last words.

Rumi stabbed his neck with the rim of the bottle, and she exhaled as his bloody body dropped at her feet.

She got a whole pack of the painkiller herb before stumbling out of the shop.

Her pains lessened already by now, and she can walk without holding walls, but her mouth still tastes metallic with blood.

Cracking her neck, she pulled out a small phone and dialled a line.

"The secret got out, and knowing how desperate Ryker is, he'd want to make her travel back in time into her memories to get the mark back, and that's where I'll wait at. I'll kill her inside her memories." She said.

"How exactly did the secret get out?" A low voice asked from the other end

"Faye, the stupid snake from West Palace. She's stronger than I think, can't believe my spelled bullet failed on that bîtch." Rumi stood upright.

"Get me everything you can find about her origin. If I'm gonna kill her, I'll have to rummage through her background first." 

She hung up and poured another bottle of the herb down her throat, then she dipped her hand into the back pocket of her trousers.

She pulled out a small picture of Soren, staring at it as she walked into a dark boulevard.

--

[Next Morning, King Mansion]

Liza started her day with money packaging.

She was sweating as she threw the wads of cash into a tall black bag inside her workroom.

She has vowed to never give Rumi even a stipend anymore, but she can't let her ruin her plan now that the Feast is close. 

She can't risk it.

She threw the last bundle into the bag and was about to zip it up when she saw Kade standing by the door, staring at her with spite.

Had she forgotten to shut the door?

"Kade... you're ready for school?" She ineptly said, smiling.

"The more I see you, the more I wish you weren't my mother. Why did you have to give birth to Shayla too? Why!!!" He yelled.

"Kade—"

"I'll go in search of additional powers wherever it is. I only have to wait for the right time and opportunity, and find a way to create a rift between her and the demigod." He threatened and left the room, shutting the door with a loud slam.

Liza ignored his rants and zipped up the money bag. She got her phone and dialled Rumi.

"Money is ready, let's meet at the park as planned. I have all you need to keep your mouth shut!" 

"Deal's off, the secret is open." Rumi replied bluntly.

"What are you talking about!" Liza demanded.

"Faye from the West was at your mansion last night. She's a slithering viper snake, and she heard our conversation." Rumi explained.

"What!!" Liza sat on the bag, tension taking over her body. "Now what? What's next?"

"I'll wait inside the memories for Shayla, and I'll kill her." Rumi replied.

"Rumi!" Liza stood immediately.

"What? Shocked? My brother Ryker deserves to spend his long life in freezing cold. When I helped you put the spell on her, I had no intention of ever removing it, so it is what it is. I always have a plan A, B, C for my shit, even plan D and E. I'm always prepared, and I'll end everything myself." Rumi sighed and hung up.

"Darnnn" Liza growled, running out of the room.

--

[Vega Mansion]

"Miss, your congee is ready. I added meatballs like you wanted." The only maid in the mansion said as Mercury came out of her room.

Mercury ignored the old woman.

She just walked to the dinning, had her seat and started eating.

"Baby, you're ready?" Esme stepped downstairs, looking beautiful in her fitting skirt and feathery fur blouse.

"The Feast ballet practice gets harder today, so I have to be in school in time to balance my bowels." Mercury gave her a peer.

"Proud of you, you're as hardworking as your mother." Esme smiled, settling beside her.

"Tell me Shayla and Xena aren't really gonna be at the center." Mercury said, her fake lashes battling in the middle of the dark mascara.

"What center? Trust me it's just a matter of time, wait till the feast." Esme scoffed. "And how was the meal I helped you with? You didn't tell me if it was good or not."

"Sadie poured it away, she didn't let Soren eat it." Mercury explained.

"I don't understand." Esme gave her a quick stare.

"It was for Soren." Mercury admitted.

"Why would you cook for Soren?" Esme frowned.

"Cos I love him, isn't it obvious? Don't be slow." Mercury dropped her spoon, taking her jacket and bag from the maid.

"I'll go first" she smiled at Esme and left the house.

Esme blinked thrice, trying to get it all together.

This isn't happening. At all.

"No. It's just infatuation. She's going to get over him." She told herself.

Her phone hummed, and she was already smiling before picking it up, knowing it's definitely Zeke.

Sweetheart: Mother wants dinner with you tonight. Pick the menu, and mother will pick the time.

Esme typed a reply immediately.

[East Palace, Back Field]

A power training has been ongoing since daybreak.

Everyone obeyed the call of Granny Tara including Zeke.

The only people who aren't here is Azura and Granny Jojo.

Soren seems to be focused again, and that made Sadie so happy.

They're still winning against Jude, but Jude's energy level has increased so much that he's nearly on par.

Jude's nature is insanely strong, and that's why glasses break when he holds them too tight.

Seems he has been practicing in secret, cos his improvement is shocking.

Lilith is by one side of the field, drilling a hole on the ground with her scream.

The hole is already wide, she's currently working the depth.

"Careful with the ground, it might just swallow you." Pearl walked past.

"Now stop talking like you know so much about grounds." Lilith replied.

"Of course I know nothing about grounds, you're closer to it than me." Pearl replied scornfully, and Lilith gritted her teeth, unable to do anything since she's at the base of the hierarchy chain here anyways.

"I should be killing you for putting your mark on me, but nah... you're still somehow useful for the family, so I'll let you go. Newsflash for you, I'm getting married to him secretly tomorrow." Pearl said.

Lilith's eyes widened "What!"

"Yeah, feel free to wail about it. Y'all don't know what I am,, but I'm a patient person. I'll patiently wait for y'all to figure out my big role." Pearl grinned, but her grin wasn't permanent.

It wiped off in a whim, and she gave Lilith a heavy strike. Lilith rolled into the pit she had dug.

Granny Tara bursted into a impressed laughter up the field.

She's standing there with Zeke who's also grinning.

"This is the great child your wife nearly killed?" Tara scoffed. 

Pearl was laughing beside the pit too, resting her palms on her knees.

Jude suddenly knocked off the twins, they both landed with a loud thud to the dry field.

Sadie's ankle sprained.

That's the first time Jude actually brought them down.

"The hell?" Zeke faced them.

"Soren has a sting on his wrist from Granny's snake bite! The venom is slowing him down and if he slows, it affects me too!" Sadie complained.

"You got bitten by my snake? And you didn't tell?" Tara went closer, appalled. "Kiss the snake, it'd suck the venom out of you, else you'd get drained completely."

"I'd rather die" Soren rolled eyes and waltzed out of the field.

Sadie looked at Jude before running after Soren.

"You did well, big brother." Pearl pat Jude, leaving with him too.

Lilith crawled out of the pit with her hands and knees.

She has so many scratches and cuts. She threw off her socks and left the field angrily too.

"Soren is now very different, madly so." Tara said.

"Not really, he has always been a little bit empathic. I mean ... He's the same person who cried when his pet rabbit died when he was 8, but Sadie already killed that empathic side of him, dunno why it's arising again." Zeke sighed.

"Keep an eye on the Old Chef's daughter, I'm telling you." Tara insisted.

"Morning here..." Granny Jojo's voice broke into their discussion.

Mother and son turned to see Jojo coming with Azura, side by side.

"Oh...it's the mirror watch." Tara said, clearly ridiculing Jojo.

"Oh...it's the wife of the snake." Jojo replied, using the same tone.

"I see you're finally awake from coma." Tara said as Jojo halted in front of her.

"And I see you're finally awake in the brain after it slept for donkey centuries." Jojo replied savagely.

Tara attempted to pull out her snake for a bite, but it's unavailable, so she just kept glaring at Jojo.

Jojo glared back.

"Is this really necessary?" Zeke faced Azura.

"Your mother can't be the only Matriarch here, sweetheart. You want power struggle? I'm giving you just that." Azura faced him too.

Zeke snickered.

[Heartless Tower]

Shayla is all ready for the ballet school, even Xena is here, ready too.

They had eaten the pancakes made by Cheyenne together,but now that it's time to leave, she's finding it really hard cos she hasn't seen Ryker this morning at all.

Last she saw him was last night when they left Pink Island together and he dropped her in her bedroom to sleep.

She kept looking at the blue hallway for any sign of him when she was eating at the balcony, but he didn't show up till she was done with Xena.

Sage said he's in his Phoria, and whenever he's in there, he hates being disturbed, so she really can't see him before going to school.

There are places she still can't enter in this tower, he still limits her accesses, and she understands...

He's not used to her intrusion yet.

Cheyenne took her and Xena to the garage, and she looked at the direction of his Phoria again before getting in the car.

Cheyenne drove them out.

Meanwhile, in the Phoria, Ryker sat in the middle of a fogged water which has sweet-smelling roses scattered around it.

This water is different from the one in his Groove.

This one is for subtle relaxation, he only uses the Groove when things get intense with his ice.

He wore a white bathrobe, but his chest is completely bare, and his eyes her closed as the treated fog simmered into his white skin.

He revealed his right hand after a while. Shayla's earring sat on it. He tilted his head as he rubbed the gold with his fingers.

It fell from her ear again when they teleported last night, and he just kept it. She's clumsy without even realizing, and it's entertaining.

He revealed his second hand.

On that one, he's holding the neck of a snake—Granny Tara's snake.

"Took you long enough to come say hi after entering this palace." He said.

The snake stretched out it's tongue to sting, but Ryker froze it's neck, so it stopped moving.

He threw the serpent on a low white rock not far from the fog.

"Idiot." He muttered.

The visitor alert sounded by the Phoria door, and Ryker left the warm water, fog oozing out of him as he walked barefoot on the ice ground till he got to the door which opened without him touching it.

Sage came to view.

"Your phone, it kept buzzing in the bedroom." He offered.

"From the human world?" Ryker asked, face flat.

"No. From the West Palace, it's about the goddess." Sage replied.

Ryker took the phone, and the moment he opened the message, he looked up.

"Her her back... from school." He ordered, and Sage left to do that.

[Black Swan Ballet School, Elite Practice Hall]

Music rolled in from the blinking multicolored walls, heat wafted in the room from the wild energy of ballerinas, tunes curling with their smooth moves.

The hall breathed with life, thumped with ambitions, feels like a universe on it's own.

Xena and and Shayla are at the center 

Soren isn't far from Xena at the left, and they're going to make some moves together.

By the left, there's a new girl beside Shayla too.

At the near back, there's Mercury and Sadie who look murderous, they did the dance with spite.

They both hate the sight of Soren near Xena, and at the same time, the sight of Shayla in the exact middle.

Shayla and Xena might be the center, but Shayla has more spotlight, it's a obvious fact.

She's making her moves too easily. She dances with her heart and mind, her body just interpretes beautifully.

And she's wishing Ryker was here, maybe she'd do better than this? She really wants those frosty blue eyes to be watching her right now, but he's probably still in his Phoria.

Xena and Soren's body touched again, and their eyes met as their moves synchronized.

She clearly saw the small swell mark on his wrist as a result of the snake bite, she wondered if he's ok.

He's not looking at her at all. He's too focused on his dance to care.

Esme and she rest of the teachers and co-judges are in front of the room.

They might not say it, but their eyes are full of admiration for Shayla in the center.

Even Esme can't deny it.

Her flexibility is peak. 

Her body is too soft that it looked like it'd break whenever she moved her legs up.

The first dance practice session ended at 10, and immediately Esme dismissed them, Pearl entered the hall, causing whisperings from the resting dancers.

"The former blind one?"

"Don't tell me she'd be here too"

"I've heard about her, she's cunning!"

"But she's pretty!"

"Pearl Millions here. Let's get along!" She waved at the ballerinas.

Sadie and Soren left like they don't even know her.

Mercury followed, and she's still looking shocked as she walked past Pearl.

Shayla and Xena were making their exit too when Pearl blocked them.

"Move!" Xena glared.

"I have nothing to say to you, move out of the way." Shayla said.

"But I have something to say to you," Pearl chuckled, leaning closer to her ear to whisper...

"Do you know this is actually my second life? I only reincarnated this time. When I got my sight back at Mount Zhitomir, I got memories of my first life back, and guess who was my dear husband in my first life?" 

Shayla went rigid.

"Yes. It's exactly who you're thinking, that irresistible god."  Pearl whispered again.

"Goddess!" Sage entered the hall.

"Sage?" Shayla moved.

"I am to take you back home" Sage said.

"Huh? Why?" Shayla asked.

"It's about your mark. Come, no time." Sage replied.

Shayla followed immediately, Pearl's words heavy on her mind.

When she got to the door, she looked back at Pearl, and Pearl winked cunningly at her.

She left with Sage eventually.

Cheyenne appeared beside Xena. "I'll be your guard then." 

"About last night, I just don't want to leave father—"

"I'm not talking to you, Miss Xena. I'll only guard you." Cheyenne cut her off, and Xena nodded.

They left the hall.

[East Palace, Heartless Tower]

Seeing Ryker after missing him all morning should be blissful, but Shayla couldn't even thinking about anything except Pearl's words.

She's inside her room, laying in her bed presently.

Sage and Ryker are inside with her, and they're saying things about how they're going to send her back to the past to get her mark back, but she's just following with her head, not her mind. Her mind is somewhere else.

"You're going to follow her into your memories? No, My Lord. I'm never allowing that. You both haven't bonded, so you going into her memories with her will wound you and send you to sleep for years, you'll have to go into seclusion and it's dangerous. You might even get lost in there." Sage said, and that snapped her out of her thoughts.

"That's really going to happen?" She sat up.

"Yes, goddess. I believe in you, I trust you're not weak. You can do this alone so he really can't follow you when you both haven't bonded." Sage explained.

"There'll be villains of the past, she can't do it alone." Ryker insisted.

"I'll do it alone." Shayla replied, and he faced her, eyes meeting.

"Trust me, I'll try my best." She assured, reaching for his robe to hold gently.

"Your best isn't enough...give it your all, and get it." Ryker deadpanned, sounding like a threat.

"Yes, I will." She replied, laying back on the bed.

"You're going back to the day your mother saw your mark. What were you doing?" He asked.

"I was bathing in the rain" she replied.

"You'll stop yourself from entering the rain that day." Ryker said, and she nodded.

"Yes" she quickly corrected.

"I was four years old, and it was a week before I turned 5" Shayla said, shutting her eyes.

Ryker placed two fingers on her forehead, waiting for a second before a blue beam flashed.

Her soul followed the beam, travelling with the blue light into her memories.

             {Shayla's Memories}

Shayla landed back in the living room at Liza's mansion.

The weather is already blackening outside. Clouds are already gathering, meaning it's going to rain soon.

The rain will soon fall.

Shayla pulled her eyes away from the open window, and her younger version ran out of the storeroom.

Her 4 year old self, wearing that faded singlet and black knickers.

She looked so eager to bath in the rain already, and she began praying for the rain to fall.

"There are marks on my back from Ma's beatings. Maybe the rainwater can calm it down a bit." She stood by the window, watching the clouds, impatient for the downpour.

Shayla climbed the couch with her quickly.

"You can't bath in the rain. You shouldn't take off your clothes." She whispered.

Little Shayla turned around, wondering where the voice is coming from.

She's not seeing Shayla.

"Who was that?" She mumbled.

"It's me. I'm from the future. You can't bath in the rain, else your Ma will sight your mark and cover it." Shayla whispered again.

"Who are you?" Little Shayla replied, jumping down from the couch and looking around.

"You can't see me, but I see you" Shayla replied, wanting to touch her, but she can't.

"She's a bloody lair, you should bath in the rain. Don't you love it?" Another voice popped out.
 
Shayla was utterly shocked to see Rumi entering the house. She recognized her with her blonde curls 

"Rumi?" She muttered in disbelief.

"Welcome to your death, demigoddess of fire." Rumi smiled eerily.

"Who are you both? Why am I not seeing anyone?" Little Shayla turned around again on a spot, her confusion running deeper.

"You have to go bath in the rain, now!!" Rumi pushed her.

"Stop it! What exactly are you after!" Shayla got in Rumi's way.

"I'll still kill you, don't rush and get out of my way!" Rumi pushed Shayla out of her way.

Shayla fell on the sofa, and Rumi faced little Shayla again.

She tore off her knickers.

"What...why are you tearing my clothes!" Little Shayla stumbled, looking scared.

"Tear off your singlet too and enter the rain, or I kill you!" Rumi threatened.

Little Shayla swallowed.

"Don't!! You can't go inside!!" Shayla yelled, picking two glass cups from the table.

She threw them at Rumi, but Rumi easily waved them away.

Little Shayla is already taking off her singlet by now, and that was when Shayla got it.

Rumi put her under a spell.

"No!!" Shayla rushed forward, but Rumi dragged her back, falling on the sofa with her.

Shayla picked one of the broken pieces of the cup, shoving it into Rumi's eyes.

Crap. It didn't even enter.

"We're inside your memory, Shayla. You're the only one who can get brutally hurt you fool!" Rumi laughed, flipping her away.

Shayla rolled off, and before she could stand, Rumi already kicked her in the face, slapping her three times one after the other.

Shayla's lips bled, but still...she stood.

By now, little Shayla is already stepping outside, about to enter the rain.

"Mini me!!" Shayla yelled, flipping the door open to go out.

Rumi pulled her back again, slamming her head on the wall.

Shayla bled all-over the face, tasing it in her mouth too.

Her eyes burned with tears, and she slumped on the floor where Rumi held her down at.

"Mini me... please..." She struggled against Rumi's unyielding hold as she watched little Shayla entering the rain. At the same time, Liza appeared.

"Where's that rape product?" She muttered, opening the door to step outside too.

Shayla's eyes widened on the floor, going beyond their limits.

Rumi's smile widened, but just as Liza's hand touched the door to open, she remembered what she's cooking in the kitchen.

"My porridge!" She gasped, rushing back inside.

At the same time, the rain pouring outside got replaced by a ice shower.

Big ice chunks fell from the sky, sounding heavily on the roof.

"What's going on?" Rumi muttered confusedly.

Little Shayla rushed back inside, and that small body merged with Shayla's grown body.

The wounds on her face closed up, and the blood wiped off on it's own. Her body felt the blazing change.

It was so high that she collapsed, but before she could fall, another presence entered the memory.

Ryker's.

Her body fell into his right arm, and his deep blue eyes glared up at Rumi who's already walking backwards.

"Bastard!" Rumi cursed, opening the door to escape, but...

Shayla's gold-rimmed eyes opened at that same moment, her aura infusing with the air.

So hot. Mad hot.

Her hand went up angrily.

The burning millennial fire erupted, catching Rumi by the door.

A golden hue from the fire hit the walls, and the subsequent destruction was so intense that it broke the walls, causing them to crash down. It brought down the living room completely, sending dust to the now open air, but Rumi's wound was minimal since they're still inside Shayla's memories anyways.

She was able to maneuver her way out. She dashed out of the memory through a thin door.

Shayla wanted to slump back afterwards, but shockingly, she swayed. There's no arm holding her anymore.

Ryker is gone, and she has no idea where or how. Wasn't he beside her just now?

"Your Lordship?" She whipped around, stepping on the broken bricks of the walls and glasses.

"Your Lordship!!" She yelled again, getting really really scared.

Was Sage really right? Did he get lost in her memories?

Or...did he fall into a deep sleep already?

She put her fire into use, but the moment she sent a flame to the open sky, it came back as a crash, ruining that memory completely.

Crap. 

She can't even control the fire yet. It's so destructive that it's giving her backlash too.

"Ryker?" She called his name, shifting helplessly on her sweaty feet as the memory kept crashing, pushing her out forcefully.

"Ry?" She shortened it, fighting against the destruction. It's like using poison to fight poison.

The more fire she used, the harder the destruction, till everything crashed completely, and she was thrown out of the memory.

--

[Luminaria, Rumi's House]

The small house sat on the downside of a hill, so it's covered, not easily found out just like Rumi wanted.

Inside the house, Rumi is on a camp bed in her small bedroom.

She looks deeply asleep, but there are scanty signs of burns on her skin—arms, legs, neck and back.

She jerked awake with a loud gasp, shooting up, her eyes flew open.

The burns started stinging immediately, and she lost her mind within that one minute.

She jumped down from the bed, rushing to a stool in the room corner where the painkiller pack from last night sat.

She was shaking as she unpacked it and began drinking the bottles one by one. She even poured some of the herb on the burns, but nothing is curing it. Nothing is working.

She entered the bathroom, opening cold water on herself, but it's all useless, again.

She ran out of the bathroom, falling by the door and rolling on the ground, crying out properly.

"Fuvkkk!!!!! Argh!!!!"

"Darn it I can't breathe!!!"

"I can't...geez!!!!"

She cried in successions, crawling back to the bed where she got her phone, dialling the number again.

"What now, Rumi?" The low voice hit the phone speakers.

"My soul entered her memories to kill her like I said, but Ryker interrupted. She merged with her younger self., so she got her mark. She used her fuckin fire on me! By the time I woke, my body has burns from her fire! I'm dying!" Rumi shouted.

"What!! Are you daft? Why would you let her fire even touch you? You don't know how dangerous that thing is? Her flame might even be more dangerous than Ryker's ice are you stupid?" The voice scolded.

"Remove the maybe, it's really more dangerous I said I'm dying here! And don't you dare blame me! I said Ryker appeared in her memories and ruined things!" Rumi yelled, going back to the bathroom to stand under the sprinkling shower.

It barely did anything, but it's better than the bedroom heat.

"Wait...it was in her memories, you weren't supposed to get hurt no matter what." The voice said in wonderment.

"This fire is gold. Not yellow, it's gold! I think she has started growing a kind of affection for Ryker, and that's why her fire is crazy like that." Rumi said.

"Oh..." The voice croaked.

"That's not the reply I hoped for, give me a solution to these burns on my skin! I can't seduce Soren with a skin like this, and there's no way I'm dying. I'm not dying without making Ryker pay for killing mother!" Rumi spat, venom oozing from her deliberate choice of words.

"I know nothing about the millennial fire burn remedy, for real." 

"What did you just say? Am I gonna suffer till death?" Rumi gasped, looking at her arm. "Oh my gawd the burn is spreading!" 

"Really? That's bad. I'm not sure about this, but...try applying his ice?" The voice suggested.

"His ice?" Rumi frowned, remembering that when they were in the memories, Ryker actually rained ice on the roof.

"Wait...ice fell at Liza's mansion when we were inside the past memory, is it possible that ice would still be there in the future?" She asked.

"Be fast, Rumi. Maybe you can still find a chunk of it, I can't lose you when things are just getting important." The voice dropped.

Rumi ended the call, running out of the house with madness.

The burns on her skin kept stinging mercilessly and spreading speedily.

"Ry?" Came his name from her mouth for the third time, and that was when his eyes went up.

She has never seen Ryker Morozov shocked before, but he's currently looking like that.

"Continue your meal," he said, voice sharper than blade edges.

Her heart skipped beats.

"But you don't look fine, and you promised to eat with me and—"

"I'll be right back." He took her hands away from his face, and before she could hold him again, he teleported out of the balcony.

She teleported too, going after him.

He entered his Phoria, and the door went shut loudly on her face.

She folded her fists, resting her forehead on the door.

"If I'm not really JUST a partner, then you don't have to leave me hanging everytime something happens. Tears come out of my eyes so easily whenever it's about you, and you're taking advantage of that too much" she sniffled, hitting the door once.

"Ryshenka! Ry!"

She could burn down this door with her mind if she wants to, but she just doesn't want to cross that boundary yet. Maybe he needs space?

"Goddess?" Sage's voice made her turn around, and the tears fell from her eyes easily.

"He's just in there, he's not dead." Sage came closer.

"But something is wrong, I know it." Shayla replied, wiping the tears.

Sage took her away from that building, going back to the middle balcony with her.

She sat and drank some water to dilute her worries, and Sage settled down with her.

"We're traveling to the human world later today." Sage broke the news.

"Ah... really?" Shayla drawled.

"But you're going with us" Sage said, making Shayla look up quickly.

"Are you serious?"

"His lordship said it himself that we'd take you" Sage replied, and that brightened Shayla's mood a little bit.

Sage took Shayla's phone from the table, doing a few clicks on the screen before handing it to Shayla.

"Have a look at the screen." He gestured, and Shayla's eyes hit the screen.

He actually clicked lumi web for her with the search entry–Lantern Night Yearly Feast.

"The Feast that's coming next week?" Shayla said, eyes brightening up more as she watched it.

"Yeah, it's colourful... isn't it?" Sage smiled, and Shayla returned her stare to the lit screen.

Everyone wears robes during the feast...its an historical stuff which starts with alcohol parade from morning till evening, followed by the lantern hanging ceremony by the city bridge at early night, followed by the real feast in the palace at late night.

It's a whole lot of mood, and Shayla was smiling as she watched the recap from last year's.

"It's indeed colourful, now I get why everyone is so excited about it" she muttered, replaying the video.

She's aware that Sage is trying to cheer her up with this, so she kept smiling even if it's fake.

Her mind hasn't left Ryker for once.

"Back with the flowers, I had to visit every florist shop in the marketplace." Cheyenne groaned, entering the balcony. 

She dropped her weight on an armchair and waved her hand on the table. The flowers appeared with the white pots, but they're not all flowers. There are many blue roses too.

"The roses are beautiful!" Shayla instantly picked one, bringing it to her nose to sniff.

"Smells too nice too, Ry said he prefers roses to flowers." She said.

"Blue roses to be precise." Sage offered.

"Wait...so this is exactly his preference?" Shayla asked, and the knights nodded at the same time.

Five minutes later, they're already at the orchard with her to plant them.

[Phoria]

Ryker still hasn't stopped getting the memories. They kept flooding in with force, and the more he remembered, the angrier he got.

The more he wants to kill.

Shayla and Jude were really his gardeners in his first life several centuries ago, and Pearl was his wife.

Winona from the West Palace was Pearl's personal maid?

That made him smirk again, but the smirk didn't last... another memory stuck, and in that one, he clearly saw himself getting married to Pearl, both wearing red robes and tying the knot, kowtowing to each other.

Seriously?

Whenever Shayla made the slightest mistake with his garden of roses, he didn't only slap her, he chained her up, and Pearl made her slice cucumbers under the dim moonlight till she nearly cut off all her fingers.

Jude tried to beg for Shayla, but he got chained up with her.

"What a joke" Ryker muttered, his mark glittering angrily on his back, beyond his clothes.

He sent a strike to the walls, creating cracks and dents and slits. 

The Phoria shook violently on the inside s, and most of the white rocks by the shallow pool broke into two.

That was enough to stop the sour memories from flooding in.

He stormed out of the Phoria.

[Orchard]

Shayla has barely planted two rose stems when Xena joined them.

Shayla didn't allow her to touch the roses though. She handled the planting of roses all by herself, so Xena planted the flowers.

Cheyenne and Sage stayed behind, they're both on the branch of a apple tree, watching the girls from there.

Sage's stomach groaned with hunger, and he faced Cheyenne.

"What? You don't know the way to the kitchen?" She scoffed.

"You're too strict, even with me. I dunno who I should fear more between you and his lordship." He joked, and she chuckled.

"You should have cooked out breakfast with Goddess's own." Sage said, wanting to remove a leaf from her hair, but she was faster.

She removed it herself, so Sage's hand went back down.

"I can't cook our food with hers, you dummy. She's our second boss, literally. That's disrespectful. Her menu is special and exclusive." Cheyenne explained.

"Then you could have cooked our food separately." Sage said.

"I planned that initially, but then he announced that we're going to the human world—Russia. Don't you miss Russian dishes? We'll eat when we get there, so manage your hunger you glutton." She replied.

"Glutton? You need to watch your mouth, Cheyenne" he pointed a finger at her, and she laughed.

"Why did he enter his Phoria though? Thought we were going to Liza's place." Sage wondered.

"I dunno, but I have this lingering feeling that something ain't good" Cheyenne sighed.

They returned their eyes to the girls planting again.

Shayla is done with the last rose stem for now, but Xena still has two flower roots to plant.

Shayla continued squatting in front of the rose field, smiling as she stared at them. They're too beautiful.

"You look obsessed with the roses" Xena whispered beside her.

"He likes them, so I like them too." Shayla replied, and Xena chuckled.

"Are you trying to build your world around him?"

"Why not?" Shayla drew closer to her. "If he's here, I'll be here." She said, demonstrating by placing two fingers together tightly.

"Are you in love? No...I don't even have to ask. You look like you're madly in love or something." Xena whispered.

"Shh" Shayla quickly placed a finger on her lips.

"I dunno, that's just how I feel. Also, I got my fire." She whispered.

"You did? OMG!" Xena gasped.

"Yeah, Rumi was hired by my Ma to hide it with a spell. She used the spell to trap the mark in my young body, so I grew up without the mark. I went into my memories to take it back, thanks to the West Palace people who gave us Intel." Shayla explained, still hushing.

"Rumi? My twin?" Xena gasped. "Why would your mother hire Rumi, and why would Rumi...do that?" 

"I dunno, but it's obviously not from a good mindset." Shayla replied.

Xena went silent for a beat, remembering the nite Rumi dropped during the maze party, then she returned to her bubbly self.

"I'm happy for you, Shay. Can't wait to see the fire." She aid genuinely.

"Thank you, and don't tell anyone." Shayla quickly said.

"Of course, by the way I met Soren by the attic last night and woke his rabbit for him. He stopped Granny Tara's snake from biting me, and we kind of started talking from there, though he's really hard to get along with." Xena told her.

"I've heard about the granny's antics, and that stony faced prince? Wow, that's almost unbelievable. You like him?" Shayla whispered.

"Noo... likeness don't just happen that suddenly, we've only just started clicking, and I can't rely much on his mood cos his sister is a devil. Also, he's a part of the Millions, I can't risk that, Ryker would snap him in two." Xena replied, and Shayla nodded.

"Right."

And that was it. That little break in their chat made her remember what happened with Ryker again.

"You ok?" Xena asked, but before Shayla could say a word, Ryker entered the orchard.

Cheyenne and Sage jumped down from the tree they're sitting on.

"Are we leaving for Liza's place, your lordship?" Cheyenne asked, falling in step beside Sage.

But Ryker didn't spare them a simple glance. His face stayed as flat as ever till he arrived at the new rose field.

He cast a hard glance to the field, and every rose went frozen, blasting to dust and getting destroyed.

"Ry!" Shayla gasped, facing him with wide, hurt eyes.

"No roses, especially blue." He muttered authoritatively, turning back and leaving them confused.

Cheyenne and Sage had to exchange glances, not understanding a thing.

Even Xena looks too stunned.

"Ry!" Shayla blocked his way, breathing heavily, the hurt still so obvious in her expression. It even got bigger now.

"But you love roses,you love blue roses." She said breathlessly.

"Not anymore." He replied without any softness, making her arms fall with her face.

Ryker left the orchard, his steps as cold as his face, and when his knights followed, he sent them back.

"Shay" Xena held Shayla quickly, getting in front of her.

"Don't take it to heart, he's probably in a foul mood." She consoled.

"Come here" she proceeded to pulling her into a hug, but Shayla couldn't even hug her back.

"Can you...go to the park with me?" She suddenly asked.

Xena broke the hug. "Of course...let's go right now."

The breeze turned so hot and fast, carrying dried leaves around tombstones and grave surfaces.

A sign that the spirits answered.

Lilith inserted the ring into her own finger, and she kissed the red document, imagining it as Ryker.

"This has to be the craziest thing I've witnessed all my life, a secret wedding without the groom?" Jojo said from where she sat.

[Mount Zhitomir]

Pearl hasn't stopped kissing her ring since the end of the secret wedding.

She hasn't stopped giggling or admiring the beauty.

Old Chef has been sent back to the palace, but Zeke and Jude are still present. They both have their hands in their pockets as they waited for what Pearl has to say.

"Your identity? Now that the wedding is done...why not speak up and stop wasting time, daughter." Zeke said.

Pearl gave her ring one last kiss before standing from the stone she's sitting on. Her white dress have several blood blotches from her thumb drippings.

"In my first life, I was the fire goddess, and that's why I was his wife." She said.

"Then? What happened? Y'all died in your first life, and that's why y'all reincarnated in this lifetime, right? Shayla reincarnated as the new fire goddess, and you reincarnated as what?" Zeke asked.

"Queen of the underground. I reincarnated as the underground goddess." Pearl replied, and their eyes widened.

"If you're the underground goddess, then your partner should be the air god." Jude said.

"There's no air god. The heavens didn't give me a partner in this lifetime, and that's why I have to take back what's mine from my past life... Ryker." Pearl declared.

"Since we were all in the past, why is it that only you and Ryker would remember that past? Why can't I and Shayla or Winona...why can't we remember the past too?" Jude asked.

"Cos I and Ryker were the main characters in that past life?" Pearl smiled, rubbing her ring. 

"Why did you both die back then?" Zeke asked, and that question made Pearl's face twist.

She turned her back on them.

"I killed everyone." Pearl replied, looking at her palms.

"But we reincarnated this time so I could correct those mistakes." 

The mountain clouds suddenly went black, air colder than whiplashes and sand getting carried around in whirlwinds.

Pearl turned around, smiling. "He's here. I knew he'd come."

Zeke and Jude vanished down the mountain immediately, and Ryker appeared as predicted.

"Took you long enough, husband." Her smile widened, not minding the dark mad anger roaming about him.

She went closer, and his palm snagged her throat, gripping it tight and nearly breaking it into two within that short second.

His head tilted, lips flattening and red streaks lining his eyes, his breaths so cold, hitting her face and freezing some pores.

The whirlwind caged the two of them in the middle, and some of the blowing sand entered her eyes, but despite the thorny sensations, she didn't shake.

"I guess you finally remembered? That your partner of this lifetime was your gardener from your last life, and I was your wife!" She spat, letting him choke her hard till blood crawled out from under her tongue.

"Shut up!" He squeezed harder. She vomited blood on his face. He pushed her out of the whirlwind, her shoes dragging on the ground, carrying debris and pebbles till he arrived at a stone where he slammed her back at, and she spat more blood.

"You still look mad hot when you're angry, husband." She laughed, her teeth bloody.

"You want death, and that's why you made a marriage pact with...I guess you used the Old Chef's blood." Ryker said, voice hitting deep.

"I just wanted you to remember what you did to me back then! I wanted you to remember how you refused to bond and how you cheated!" She shouted, raising her right hand.

On the smallest finger of that hand, there's a red thread hanging on it, but instead of connecting to Ryker, it's dragging on the ground.

Her love thread.

"You left my love thread hanging, you refused to grow a love thread for me to connect with it as your partner. Instead, you were lusting after your female gardener you heartless bastard! I hate you!!!!" Pearl yelled, tears pumping out of her eyes.

  {Centuries Ago, Ice-god Mansion}

The mansion is located on the pointed top of Mount Zhitomir, the largest mansion in the whole of Luminaria which has every sign of life scrapped away from it. 

It's always snowing on that mountain,  always freezing cold with the lowest temperature in the whole city.

The mansion looks lifeless and heartless like it's owner, painted black with equally black rims.

House of the millennial ice god where he lives with his wife Pearl who's the millennial fire goddess.

And their two gardeners Jude and Shayla, plus Winona who's Pearl's personal maid.

A god  is expected to grow a love thread for his partner. After they bond through intimacy, they complete the bonding with the thread, but none of it is happening.

No intimacy between the two gods, and the love thread? Only Pearl grew it. Ryker grew nothing.

He actually rejected Pearl as his partner at first sight, but he only respected the heavens law by getting married to her and letting her stay with him in his mansion.

It's a loveless marriage.

And that's why right now... Pearl is trying to hold him in bed, but he kept his distance.

It's a king-sized bed with too much space between them, and he kept trying to hold him which she made impossible everytime.

Pearl couldn't keep her hands to herself, so she tried touching him again, and that was when he snapped.

He got down from the bed and threw on his robe jacket, then he started exiting the bedroom.

"Look," she raised her hand, showing her love thread.

Ryker still didn't turn.

"You prefer spending your time in your rose garden, punishing your gardeners than actually spending time with me! You won't even touch me! What have I even done wrong!" Pearl continued ranting.

"No rule says we're going to die if we don't bond. Drop the wedding ring and leave." He finished.

"Cos you're fake!" Ryker snapped, getting more angry as he came closer.

"Ryker!!!" Pearl fell on the ground, holding her head as she cried out loudly, profusely.

"What are you talking about?" Pearl looked up, eyes so red.

"The female gardener, Shayla." Winona said.

Pearl stood, skeptically.

"What do you mean Shayla? He hates her more than me. One single mistake with his roses, he slaps and chains her up with the male gardener! In fact, she's chained up right now at the torture room." 

"That was all show, the open tortures were all to fool you! You should visit the torture room one of these days." Winona said.

Pearl bursted out of the room immediately, and she started a fast run to the torture room.

She met Jude by the way, and he looks all sore and bloody. 

"Where's your co-gardener?" Pearl questioned.

"Shayla? She's still in the torture room. I'm always released faster than her." Jude weakly replied.

Pearl gasped.

She increased her speed to the torture room faster than thunder.

She got there and slowed down her steps, leaning on the door since it's slightly broken in the middle.

Clearly, she saw Ryker inside the room with Shayla.

She's totally naked! And Ryker is healing her wounds.

Pearl went cold by the door, her chest constricted tightly.

With sweet care, with insane gentility and intense softness, he healed Shayla's wounds.

Afterwards, he brought her to his laps and made her sit there.

He covered her with a shawl, and her arms went around him. He kissed her nose, then her lips.

It wasn't deep, but it was soft, and as the sounds entered Pearl's ears, she nearly went crazy.

"See? I was right." Winona appeared behind Pearl by the door.

"Sorry I had to hurt you openly and heal you in secret everyday." Ryker whispered in a soft voice.

"It's fine, at least you spend time with me, but can you heal Jude too?" Shayla's tiny voice replied.

"No. He likes you, and it's too obvious." Ryker replied.

"But I don't like him." She replied, kissing his throat, then his lips, longer than the first.

"It doesn't matter, liking you is a sin he'd pay for...for the rest of his life." Ryker replied possessively.

"Why would your wife's family steal the goddess mantle from me and give it to her though?" She asked.

"Cos they're corrupt? And you're not rich." Ryker replied, cupping her breàst under the shawl.

"And I can't get the mantle back?" Shayla asked, looking up at his face.

"No. Once it's in her body, it's in there. You can only get it back if we die and reincarnate." He said.

"Reincarnation?" She asked.

"Yeah, in the next life... you'll get the fire goddess mantle back, and we can end the thief once and for all." He replied, smiling down.

Shayla smiled back, tilting her neck up. His face met hers, and their lips smashed, the kiss so deep.

And that was Pearl's ruination. She lost it by the door.

"Cheater!!!!!!" She shrieked, bursting the door.

                 {Present Time}

She ate her fill of the breakfast, and after she was done, she rested and then followed the knights to the practice room in the mansion.

There are unlit candles sitting in thin plates everywhere, then there are so many unlit fireplaces too.

"The practice is for me?" Shayla asked.

"Who else? You burned four guys cos you can't control your fire yet. You need to learn control seriously." Cheyenne replied.

"They touched Xena inappropriately, and though I feel bad for doing that, but they were being scummy." Shayla explained.

"Can you stop mentioning Xena?" Sage said behind her.

"What exactly did Xena do?" Shayla asked.

"We don't wanna talk about it" Cheyenne said.

Shayla sighed, looking at the array of candles and fireplaces.

"I know you're a lazy bone who only wants to spend time with boss and slack off, but you can't escape this." Sage said strictly.

"I know," Shayla said, going closer to the middle of the room.

"Good. Boss can manage his ice, and that's why he can freeze and unfreeze anytime he wants. You should also be able to put out your fire if you're not in the mood to kill a scum completely." Cheyenne said.

"You should close your eyes now, and try putting fire on these candles one by one." Sage said.

Shayla's eyes went shut, and she dragged in a breath, focusing.

Every single candle caught fire. Even the fireplace lit up, burning bright.

"Oh...well. She'd burn everyone in Luminaria. At this rate, you'll never be able to control." Sage muttered.

Shayla shut her eyes back, determined to put out the fire one by one, but she felt a slight drop underneath herself.

She understood immediately, and she moved to Cheyenne.

"Want anything?" Cheyenne asked.

"Period. My period." She whispered, trying not to sound embarrassed.

"Come here, I'll get you pads." Cheyenne pulled her out of the room.

[Moscow, Frost H2O Headquarters]

It has been one product showcasing to another since yesterday. There has been no rest since the products are detailed and very much.

Not only the Russian ones, but his branches from the other parts of the world are also sending in samples for his knowing eyes to check out.

He's on the third showcasing today, and as he sat in the middle of the large glittering room with his treasure team and shine men, he looks as different as he always looks whenever he's in a suit.

It's orange color this time, but the trousers are the usual loose, and he's extra smart, hot.

His eyes aren't blinking under his fallen dark hair strands as he accessed the jewelries rolling in through the show glasses one after the other.

"That one's too small"

"Get that one off the glass"

"Not that one... it's too bleak"

"Replace the sapphire with jade."

"Get that one across the branches."

"Reduce the crystal on this one"

Those were his unending comments as he scrolled past the items, and as he picked and dropped, his active team followed the orders.

The showcase hasn't even reached the middle when he felt the mark on his back glowing.

It doesn't only glow when he's mad, it also glows when Shayla bleeds, so he was alerted immediately.

Confirming his suspicions, Cheyenne's call rang on his phone immediately.

He raised an hand, and that was enough to stop the shine team.

They paused the scrolling glass, and Ryker picked the call.

"Tell me she's not really bleeding, or I'll make you and Sage bleed times twenty of whatever she bleeds." He deadpanned.

"She's bleeding, but it's her period, and I dunno...i think it's because she just got her mark. Her temperature went high the moment the bleeding started, and she has vomited the breakfast she ate cos of irritation. She's very sick, Boss." Cheyenne reported.

Before she could even finish speaking, Ryker was already on his tall feet.

"Your...baby?" The leader said in confusion, having the same confused face as the rest of the thirty members of the team.

Ryker looked back at him over his shoulder, smirking.

"Oh...you wouldn't understand what that means. I mean...My Girlfriend." He finished him completely.

[Glass House]

His presence did everything.

Also, you have a kind, soft heart and fragile, small body, and the angry fire contradicts that, so it'd act up anytime you bleed or witness anything emotional." He explained, touching her cheek with the back of his fingertips.

"Yeah, I'll help you." He said, bringing his wrist up.

His dagger appeared on his palm, and he gave his wrist a cut.

"Ry!" She gasped.

"Drink my blood, you need it to replenish your lost blood and regain your stamina." He said.

She shook her head immediately. "No. Cheyenne said there's blood transfusion, and I can take the blood of the same blood group—"

"You. Are. A. Goddess, Shayla. You're not human, and you won't find your blood type anywhere just like I have no blood type too, but we can supply each other. We're partners anyways, so drink." He brought the wrist to her mouth, but she continued shaking her head, refusing.

She held the wrist, stopping it from coming closer to her mouth. His blood dropped on her face.

He closed their middle space, caging her face in the middle of his palms.

He kissed her, and she smiled widely into it before shutting her eyes, jumping up his body to wrap her legs around his waist.

With one hand, he held her waist, and he held her lap with the second hand, occasionally moving her hair away as the kiss deepened, going past mouth to mouth.

They're literally kissing each other's souls, feasting on their goddamn minds till they couldn't breathe anymore.

Breaking the kiss took a lot of effort, and they both look bright as their faces met again.

He smiled beautifully, and she held his face with hand hand, planting deep kisses on his dimples.

"You not smiling since we started this practice, it's a punishment?" She asked, and he pushed her hair away from her face before replying.

"Real one. I know how obsessed you are with these dimples." 

She laughed, and he joined her, their lips connecting again in a short kiss.

He carried her out of the room, and they went to another room where they met the knights.

Cheyenne is sitting behind a jazz drum set, drumming so good while Sage just sat behind a shelf, watching as her only audience.

"It's done?" Cheyenne stood immediately.

"Obviously," Sage smiled, getting on his feet too.

"Are we returning now? I'm sure it's 10pm in Luminaria right now, meaning it's two hours before the feast day." Cheyenne said.

"Not yet, I'll show this clumsy girl here around the city before we'll return. One hour, we'll be back." Ryker replied.

They didn't even border to change clothes. Ryker took her to the airfield at the back of the property, and they entered the helicopter together.

He made sure there's enough goodies and snacks inside for her before the pilot took off, driving the helicopter to the sky.

Shayla was stunned beyond words, and she just leaned on the glass window, staring with wide eyes at the vast country of Russia beneath them.

"This is like a bird" she blurted, and Ryker chuckled behind her.

"Don't be silly, it's nowhere near a bird. It's an—"

"Helicopter, Cheyenne told me." He cut her off. 

"But it flies like a bird" she added, making him chuckle again.

"Are you intentionally funny, or you don't even realize how funny you sound each time?" 

"I'm just saying what comes to my mind, don't criticize me" she said with a slow blink, coming back to him. He drew her to his laps, and she sat there, feeling the rubbing of his boner on her butt throughout.

But she didn't comment on it. About how she likes it, or about how it's soaking her up.

She just sat, eating her snacks of cookies, lollipops. Fruits and more.

Her love for sweet things needs to be studied at this rate.

As she munched, she had a view of the city each time the pilot lowered to another altitude.

Russia is vast, and beautiful.

Human world is as beautiful as Luminaria, or even more beautiful, but that's not a topic for today.

Normally, Ryker's hands would be all-over her body right now.

He loves physical touch a lot, just like her, but he's not doing anything. Not touching her, so she looked at him.

He's staring at her, but he looks like he's spacing out.

"Is it about Xena? I heard she did something." She muttered.

"Not today, Shayla. Just enjoy this ride, peacefully." He said.

"Ry, you promised to never shut me up whenever these things happen. You can't just think alone." She persisted, and his hand found her face, rubbing tenderly.

She leaned into that hand, holding it to her face.

"I know, and I didn't forget, but not just now. You'll find out when we arrive at Luminaria in an hour. Don't ruin your sky picnic." He said, and that gave her the needed assurance.

She leaned into his chest, and before she knew it, his mouth sealed hers.

She melted into his laps like she's boneless, eyes going shut as she fisted his black shirt.

[Luminaria, 11:50PM, A Bar]

Rumi's earpiece is into her ears, blasting loud jazz as she drank from a large, heavy cup of beer.

She's the only one on that bar corner, and there are several portions of coke on the table which she's taking turn to sniff into her nose with a straw.

The white stuff stained her nostrils so much, and she's hitting her own head so much.

The atmosphere around the bar is just as festive as the one in the other parts of the city.

There are laughter of kids from the park, screams of vital youths, whisperings of the adults and elders, explosives fireworks in the sky and the festival fireflies.

Shops are still open, doing bonanza sales of robes for the poor Luminarians. Balloons, even bangers are sounding repeatedly.

It's a whole lot of happenings at the same time, but Rumi gives no fûck about what's going on outside.

She's all focused on her drags.

She was on the the fifth drag when the person she has been expecting settled in front of her.

She didn't look up.

"We were supposed to meet since Saturday, but you didn't fufill your promise." She said, sniffing.

"I got a burnt head, and I had to cater for that, daughter. Don't be mad." The voice replied.

Rumi looked up, eyes highly watery.

She took in a deep breath, smiling before wiping her tears of highness away. She cleaned the white stuff from her nose too.

"Anatoli, aka, Old Chef, my loving, caring father." She hailed.

Old Chef grinned, resting his arms on the table and leaning forward.

"You get more interesting everyday, dear daughter." He laughed.

Rumi ordered another beer for him, and they clinked their heavy beer glasses, but they didn't drink yet.

It's one minute before 12pm right now, one minute before the feast day.

"His birth killed mother, so even if it means going to the pit of hell to buy death for him, he has to die. Ryker had to go at the Feast." Rumi said.

"And luckily, your sister is always defending me. Somehow dumb, but she's on my side. Once Ryker is gone, the three of us can live together happily." Old Chef nodded.

His smile mirrored Rumi's smile, and they raised their beer cups.

Old Chef's smile grew wider.

His head remembered how he had intentionally slapped Azura's butt that fateful night just to get her attention and get his wife killed.

It was all his plan, from the very beginning.

He grinned out wickedly, shinning his disarranged teeth.

At exactly 12am, he clinked his beer glass with Rumi's.

The festival city bell sounded from the city tower, ringing across the whole city in deep echoes, signifying the start of the feast.

"Happy Lantern Feast, father!" Rumi shouted excitedly.

"Happy Lantern Feast, daughter" Old Chef smiled, and they both brought the beer cups to their lips, drinking like the best partners in crime.

Fireworks burned across the skies in sprinkles, sweet-smelling multicolored flames mixed with the air, the smell of alcohol and midnight snacks wafted.

Lantern Feast began colourfully.

Rumi dropped her beer cup after she drank everything.

Old Chef followed suit, but there's still alcohol below his own cup.

"You're still very good with holding your alcohol." He commented.

"I'm not lightweight like my twin." Rumi rested an elbow on the table.

"Just assure me one more time that your tears are going to work." 

"He has no idea about my poisonous tears, Xena is unaware too. I intentionally did a soliloquy about it in the kitchen one day when I was sure Zeke was listening, and since then, I've been expecting him to use the tears, and he didn't disappoint. Last week, I was made to cry inside the spring pool." Old Chef explained.

"And?" Rumi listened raptly.

"And I'm sure he's not even aware he has been poisoned. He still hasn't grown a love thread for his partner, so there are limits to his capacities. Today makes it the eighth day since the poisoning, and today is the day the poison explodes in his body. He'd disappear like he never existed." Old Chef explained further.

Rumi leaned back into her chair.

"Do I need to visit the temple and offer coconuts to Buddha to thank him for making you my father?"

Old Chef smiled, taking a small sip from his glass.

"And again..." Rumi got serious. "The blood pact you signed, how does that even work?" 

"He might be a demigod, but I'm still his birth father, and a drop of his blood is still in my system which i can never bleed away unless I use it against him. Pearl knows that, so she made me sign the pact with my blood." Old Chef said.

Rumi sighed.

"Right, but Liza... Shayla's mother, she has Shayla's drop of blood in her body too!" She suddenly gasped.

"Of course, and I'm guessing she's going to use it against Shayla today. Everything is working in our favor, Rumi. We're the best partners ever" Old Chef laughed as he drained the remaining beer in his glass.

"Let's remain the best father-daughter duo." Rumi cheered.

"Absolutely." Old Chef chuckled, his intentions murky.

They parted ways at 1am, and Rumi dragged her hoodie over her head before stepping out of the bar.

He stopped in the middle of the children's park, looking back at the bar he just came out of.

He had a quick flashback.

  {350 Yrs Ago, Morozov Household}

Anatoli arrived with a full bag of blood from the alchemist shop, and he looks exhausted as he got down from the rusty taxi. 

He held the bags of blood close to his chest, entering the house.

But he was greeted by the loud cries of the twins from the top pavement, so he rushed there, meeting another one of his wife's traumatic scenes.

His wife—Dominika, is standing on the sharp edge of the top pavement, stomach so big as if she's carrying ten babies. 

She's preparing to jump down, and the three year old twins—Rumi and Xena, are crying under the pavement.

"Dominika, don't move. In fact, don't shake!" Anatoli warned, gently dropping the blood bags on the ground without taking his eyes off the pregnant woman.

This is the fourth time she'd be attempting suicide this week.

First, she stabbed herself in the stomach, but the knife didn't even affect the child.

Second, it was poison, but the poison only made the baby kick in her belly.

Third, she drank gasoline, but it was like water...it did nothing to the baby.

"Why shouldn't I jump? This baby is going to ruin us all, Anatoli! Nothing works on him! Not poison, not harmful liquids, not sharp objects! All he does in my stomach is drink blood like a beast! You spend all our life savings on blood always! And now we can barely feed the twins! I don't even know if it's a baby! I dunno what's growing inside my stomach!" Dominika cried.

Despite her tears, her eyes were on the blood Anatoli brought, and her throat was drying up with hunger.

"So far that special baby is in your belly, nothing will kill you even if you jump down, Dominika. Come down." Anatoli spread his palm for her.

"Special?" She gasped. "Did you just say special? There's nothing special about this blood drinker! He's just—"

Anatoli already pulled her away from the edge before she could finish.

Her swollen feet landed on the ground, and the twins stopped crying.

"Calm down, Dominika. Just calm. Breathe." Anatoli said.

Dementedly, she broke free from him and went to the blood bags on the ground, taking one and drinking directly from the pipe.

The blood dripped from her mouth as she drained. It stained her chest, fingers and protruding stomach.

She took another bag, crying as she started drinking too.

As she drank, the twins stared in pure horror, hiding behind their father.

Anatoli clenched his jaws, watching her with impatience.

'Drink as much blood as you want, wife. I'm going to kill you the moment you give birth to that child anyways.' he thought.

[Vega Mansion]

Esme planned to be the center of the parade, she knowingly left her mansion very late.

She's rocking better robe than Liza's this year, and she's so proud of it, impatient for when Zeke would take it off from her body tonight.

She's becoming the new Palace Madame tonight.

It's historical, but she's going in a car. No one can stop her anyways.

Maids were carrying her long robe behind like a wedding dress as she walked to the car, but her phone suddenly beamed.

Her smile cracked.

It's Mercury.

"Baby, are you enjoying the parade without me?" She picked.

"The Mirror Maze, be there in five minutes, and you're to come alone, or your daughter goes off." A husky voice said.

Esme's eyes popped wide.

"I dunno who you are, but if you dare touch my daughter! If you dare to—"

The line went flat, and she gasped, throwing the phone to the ground.

She jumped into the car, closing the door on some of her robe material.

She drove off on her own.

--

[Mirror Maze]

Mercury is tied to the ceiling, but the rope is on her ankles instead of her neck, so her body is upside down.

The maze is hot, reacting to the sun outside, so she's soaked with sweat.

"I swear if you touch a hair on my head, my mother will kill you!!! Do you even know who my father is? You have no idea!!!" She yelled psychotically.

"Ain't no one getting freaked with that dry threat. Your Pa is dead, and that's why your mother is a husband thieving bîtch so shut up!" A harsh voice creeped in.

Mercury's hearts skipped.

She recognizes that voice even if she's not seeing the face from her upside down position.

"Madame Azura?"

"You're sharp," Azura replied from where she stood beside the wall.

Granny Jojo is sitting on a chair beside her, and the old woman looks as determined as her daughter.

"Why the hell would you abduct me? I'm best friends with your twins and I did nothing wrong!" Mercury cried.

"Did you not hear when she said your Mama is a bîtch who's having an affair with Grand Supreme Zeke?" Granny Jojo said.

Mercury recognized her voice too.

"Wait... Grandmother Jojo? Please tell me it's not you what have I done!!!" She yelled, feeling like her heart is sitting on her throat.

She vomited the cream puffs she ate cos her stomach got upset so fast.

"And did you just say my mother is having an affair with the Grand Supreme? Find another lie you old fools! Let me go!!" She screamed.

Azura pulled the rope on her ankles, and Mercury swung left, right without stop.

Her eyes turned wildly, her ankles peeled as the ropes digged in, her hair swept the floor, and she vomited again, crying.

"Let go of me!!!"

"Mercury! Mercury!!" Esme's voice came from outside.

Mercury gasped.

"Mother!!" She yelled in tears.

Esme ran inside,gasping.

"Oh my goodness! Baby!" She started running to her.

Azura appeared with a thunder-charged metal which she pointed at her with sharp aim.

Esme stopped, frowning.

"It's you? Madame Azura?"

"Yes, the woman you share a husband with." Azura said, pushing the metal closer to her head.

Esme's eyes went to the right, and when she saw Jojo sitting, she laughed out suddenly, the sound so shameless and high.

"So...this is a mother and daughter thingy? Aren't you both ashamed? I'm dating your husband? So what!! He met me by himself, proposed an affair to me and I got on the wheel with him cos I'd be insane to not actually accept! I mean...my husband has been dead since long, and I needed a new man to cure my longing! He chose me over you not only because I'm more useful, but because I'm also prettier! You can't even compare to me, shameless old dog!" Esme spewed.

Angrily, Azura slapped the metal on Esme's head.

Esme flew to the ground, but the thunderbolt didn't work on her... She looks fine.

Azura's eyes widened.

"You have nothing but this useless thunder ability which you stole from your idiotic mother, and you think it'd work on me? Do you even know who I am? Azura, do you?" Esme spat.

She stood from the ground, laughing derisively.

The metal fell from Azura, and Jojo stood from her chair, looking too stunned to speak.

Mercury is still crying on her hanging rope, her body dangling side to side.

"Talking about usefulness, what else did you do for my sweetheart Zeke except giving him children like a baby making machine? What else!!" Esme demanded to her face.

Azura couldn't speak, but she's shaking.

"I knew you'd soon get suspicious, and my secretary would be your surest bet, so I told him to tell the truth whenever you came. I expected this! You're nothing but a tool and a fool to my Zeke, wake the hell up!" Esme yelled.

That was followed by a thunderous slap from Azura, and it resounded as it landed hard on Esme's face.

But Esme is a rage-baiting bîtch, so she didn't retaliate.

She just laughed, and Azura slapped her again.

"Slap all you want, fool! Zeke would massage it for me with a good fûck tonight on your bed!" Esme spat.

Azura's hand stopped midair, body shaking more than before.

Esme's red eyes didn't blink, and Jojo's eyes are still wide.

Mercury's tears kept pouring.

"That was some entertaining shit-show," a deep voice bounced down from the maze top metals.

The eyes of the three women went up, and they were dumbstruck to see Ryker standing there, looking like the worse of evil with Sage beside him.

"Ryker" Jojo muttered.

"Bastard" Azura said.

Esme took the opportunity quickly. She dropped Mercury's rope from her ankles and released her.

She carried her and ran out of the maze.

Ryker moved from his spot, feeling that stabbing pain from his chest again, but he didn't care.

He descended down, landing in front of mother and daughter.

Sage used the staircase.

"You have no business being here!" Azura said, and she received a brutal strike from Ryker immediately.

Her body whacked on the mirrored wall, breaking it and pushing her into the mirror ruins.

A thick chain from nowhere wrapped on her neck like a collar, choking her.

"Zura!" Jojo gasped.

"You turned 700 today, and there's a century-old belief that if you kill a 700 year old Luminaria on her birthday, her memories will be filtered out of her offsprings head. Meaning, your kids will forget they ever had a mother." Ryker muttered, each word infused with vengeance.

"Ryker...no." Jojo gasped again, getting where he's heading with this.

Azura was already spitting blood beside the wall where she's chained.

"I intentionally made you witness Esme's real identity as Zeke's real woman, Even in death, you'd never rest. Your spirit will keep roaming the afterlife and you won't ever find peace" Ryker continued.

The words left his mouth so easily, and he strolled away from his spot, looking at the two women through his lowered dark lashes, his steps careful and calculated.

"Are you doing this cos of your mother?" Azura gagged.

Ryker stopped on his tracks, retracting the one step he was about to take.

"Your father Anatoli touched me first! Also, I was still madly in love with Zeke back then, that's why I told him to kill the man who touched me with his family, but then we got to your household and saw your wife who has just birthed you, the ice demigod...we couldn't resist the thirst for power!!" Azura cried, pitifully.

"Anatoli knew about your wickedness and your pick me dedication to Zeke, and that's why he approached you in that bar. He picked you for his plans, and you did just what he wanted cos you've always been a wench, which is why I'm starting with you! You're on the bottom of my list, and today is your last living day! I'm dedicating today to your death." Ryker smiled mercilessly.

"Happy 700th birthday, Azura Millions." 

He stretched his palm forward, and an ice sword formed on it, so long and sharp and pitiless.

"No... Ryker no!!! You can't kill my only daughter! You can't!!!" Jojo went on her knees.

Her matriarch crown fell, letting her grey hair loose.

"Ryker you can't!!! Ryker!!!"

Ryker kicked her out of the way, and she slumped back.

She pulled out her mirror, but Ryker returned the mirror flash to her. She went blind, and deaf.

"Mother! Mother Mother!!!! Mother!!!!!!" Azura cried hauntingly by the wall, shaking the maze with the painful depth.

"You don't even know what's coming for you, Ryker. I'm sure you're already feeling it!" She rushed, but if that was an attempt to stop him, it failed woefully.

Ryker's ice sword pierced into her chest, and her blood splashed his face, wetting him with satisfaction.

Azura's body went still on that wall, her blood flooded the ground till it soaked Granny Jojo's body.

The chain on her neck pulled her up till she got to the ceiling, and a quick decomposition happened.

Her body experienced decay very fast...on the spot.

As the body parts decayed, they fell to the ground, one by fuckin one.

Hands, legs, head, bowels, stomach components.

Everything fell off till only her heart is hanging on the ceiling with the ice sword in the middle.

Ryker smiled eerily, the sight so terrifying with that bloody face.

Sage looked at the body on the ground, the heart hanging on the ceiling, then at Ryker's unfeeling face.

He nearly peed on himself.

She limped towards Granny Jojo, and at the same time, she was looking up at Azura's heart on the ceiling.

"Does my husband take classes on how to kill brutally or what? Geez! I wanna puke!" She leaned on the wall, held her stomach and coughed, but she only retched.

Nothing came out.

She took pictures of the rotten body and hanging heart and sent them to the Grand Supreme with the message...

"Your Wife Is Dead Finally, You Must Be Very Happy, Evil Man!"

[Wellness Haven]

Despite Mercury's insistence that she's fine, Esme was adamant.

She brought her here anyways, and the alchemists are currently doing a wellness check on her.

Mercury kept her blank eyes open throughout, looking like she's tired of life. She didn't even blink once.

Esme stood by the wall, phone in hand as her fingers rushed to text Zeke quickly.

Esme: Sweetheart, your worthless wife found out, and she abducted my daughter. I dunno what she was thinking, but I got Mercury back anyways. Ryker arrived unexpectedly, and I'm not sure if Azura is still alive. He seemed to be targeting her.

She was expecting Zeke's reply when the alchemists got done with Mercury's health check.

"She's fine, no internal damages done, but get her the calming herb bottle from the outer shop when leaving, she seems to be in a state of shock." The alchemists reported.

"I'll do that," Esme nodded, going to Mercury. She sat beside her.

"Baby, I think you should go home and rest till the night munching. The parade will soon end so you don't have to go back, and you don't have to attend the lantern hanging ceremony too. Just rest." She persuaded, stroking her hair.

"Get your hands off me," Mercury muttered, still not blinking.

"Baby?" Esme drawled.

"I said get your hands off me!!!" Mercury yelled, throwing her hand away and standing from the bed.

"So this is it? The reason you kept telling me to get over Soren was because you're in a relationship with his father you shameless wh0re!!" She screamed, not minding the alchemists around.

"Mercury!" Esme rose.

"How could you, just how!!! When did father die? Just last year, his ashes is still fresh in the urn inside my room, and this year you're already in an affair with Grand Supreme—the father of my bestie, the father of the boy I love, the husband of the mother of the two of them! Have you no shame? You know how much I like Soren so why are you doing this to me! Why!!!"

Mercury was shedding real tears as she said the hurtful words, and Esme was confused for a while.

She had to send the alchemists out with a snap before replying.

"Mercury, listen to me. Your father is dead, and i have nothing in this city except the ballet school. It was already falling apart when the Grand Supreme proposed an affair to me! I couldn't say no! I'll become the Palace Madame, and no one in this city will be able touch you or look at your face without bowing. Zeke is going to adopt you, and you'll become a Princess like Sadie. Don't you want to be in the same level as Sadie?" 

She persuasively held Mercury's arms as she poured out the words, but Mercury shrugged her off.

"No!!! I don't care about the benefits! Do you know how much I struggled before Sadie could become my friend? That girl liked nobody except her brother, and she's the reason Soren became my friend too! And one thing about Sadie? She can hate!!!"

"She hates too much, you think she'd buy me wrapped gifts if she finds out my mother snatched her father? She'd kill me! Now let's talk about Soren, you think there'd be a future between us if you become his father's new wife? You never think about me, it's always you... selfish wh0re!!!" 

"Mercury!!!" Esme grabbed her again.

"What!!!!" Mercury yelled, pushing her away before laughing crisply like a incurable maniac.

"You taught me to get what I want even if lives have to go with it, so don't expect me to thread carefully on this. I hate you!!!" She spat saliva at her and walked out of the haven.

"Oh..." Esme gulped, turning around confusedly on her spot.

"Mercury!!"
