[Parade]

Since Jojo and Azura went missing, Zeke and Tara sat in the same carriage.

The parade is almost ending though, time is past 3pm. It'd end in an hour.

"Why didn't you go after them? They're probably up to something corny." Tara spoke up.

"No matter what they're up to, it'd only hasten my plans. I planned everything so meticulously that every move would result in my victory. Don't sweat about it." Zeke replied, and his phone buzzed.

Pulling it out, he frowned when he saw the text from Lilith.

The number isn't saved.

Before he could start trying to decipher it, another text came in from Esme.

He smiled after he read that one, then he read Lilith's text again, understanding what it means.

"Azura is dead, I told you it'd work in my favor. I was planning to get rid of her today, turns out the bastard fid it for me." 

"It's for revenge, and he obviously started from the bottom of the list if he killed Azura first, who's next?" Tara faced him.

"He won't be able to carry on, he's dying." Zeke grinned, and the old woman sighed.

"But he's cruel as he should be, he killed her on her 700th day? I bet all her offsprings forgot about her already." Tara sighed.

"Except Pearl, that witch is a demigoddess anyways," Zeke sucked his teeth.

The clamour around their carriage suddenly went down, signifying the entrance of someone important.

Tara and Zeke looked back, and they weren't amazed to see Ryker entering the parade.

Azura's blood is still on his face, and his focus is unknown. He's just staring at nothing in particular.

His eyes are straight as he walked the parade grounds. Everyone bowed in fear, moving out of the way and creating a large walkway for him in the middle like he's the king.

"Look at the huge fear on the people's faces...it's annoying." Tara muttered.

"More reason he has to go. I own this city, people can't keep worshipping him." Zeke sighed, relaxing.

"The pain he'd be feeling right now is insane, but the bastard knows how to mask pains, too much." 

Ryker's eyes met with his own inside that carriage, and both of them refused to look away.

It's like a deadly staring competition, but Ryker's eyes are a warning of casualties, dark and grim.

"Bastard," Zeke muttered, looking away first.

[Shayla's Carriage]

Since the end of the fight, Xena was taken away by Soren.

Cheyenne stayed back in the carriage with Shayla.

Cheyenne has been busy on her tablet, but Shayla has been struggling inwardly with the change she's experiencing. Her blood is becoming hot—too hot to stay inside her body.

She's boiling inside.

It started since she saw a blood thumb print on her arm earlier during the fight. 

What could it mean?

"I knew it, of course he'd take the opportunity to sell his ugly treasures." Cheyenne scoffed, her eyes still buried inside her tablet.

"What's that?" Shayla asked.

"Oh...it's a scum in Russia. His name is Caspian Yusluv, he owns a jewelry conglomerate too. Rich, but his wealth is only half of his lordship's. He's not a competitor at all, but he loves inserting himself into the the game."

"The day we lost billions in Frost H2O cos his lordship came home to attend to you, this scum took the opportunity to distribute more newly developed treasures to his own branches, so his net worth went up, and he's bragging about it in the human internet." Cheyenne explained, bringing the tab closer.

"Wanna see a picture of the scum?" 

"No no no. I don't want to see anyone's face." Shayla quickly rejected, and Cheyenne turned off the tablet, focusing more on her.

"Are you ok?"

Before Shayla could answer, they felt reduction in the parade noise, and their eyes went up.

Everyone's bowing, stepping out of the way and taking their presence away from the carriage body.

"He's here," Cheyenne said, getting out of the carriage.

A second later, Ryker came in, and Shayla couldn't stop staring at his bloody face as he settled beside her.

"Who died in your hands this time?" She asked, resting her head on his chest without minding.

"Azura," he replied, and she looked up quickly.

"Her?"

He nodded once, looking down at her face. His fingers are already in her hair, caressing.

"You started your revenge on the family already?" She asked.

"That's what it looks like" he replied, and she sighed soundlessly, snuggling closer to his chest.

His ice should be helping her reduce the sudden hotness inside her, but it's not doing anything.

She kept it hidden though, not wanting to give herself away and worry him again.

"What happened when I wasn't here?" He asked suddenly.

"Nothing much, but Cheyenne and Xena fought. Xena was bleeding, Soren took her away." She narrated.

"Good." He replied.

"What's good? The fact that she bled?" She muttered.

"And the fact that Cheyenne handled her. If I did, she'd do more than bleed." Ryker replied.

She lifted her head from his chest.

"Cheyenne said she called you a bastard, and she said you shouldn't have been born cos your birth killed your mother Dominika. She's overprotective of your father." 

Ryker went silent, he just frowned, and it showed in the middle of his full eyebrows.

"Are you seeing something about your father that she's not seeing?" She asked, taking his hands to rub.

"Don't call him my father." He corrected.

"Won't do it again," she said softly.

"I found out he killed his wife, and I still dunno why. He must have found out that killing me with the godslaying sword would give him half of my powers, and he wants that power." He explained.

Shayla stayed calm, but she's still rubbing his fingers.

"The godslayer sword is within the Millions Family, that's why he targeted Azura that night. She'd have killed him directly after he touched her, but he intentionally cried that he had a pregnant wife at home. He already knew Dominika would give birth that day, the plan was clean."

"Azura and Zeke took him to the household where they met Dominika who just gave birth, and seeing me—the child, they grew greedy. Like Anatoli wanted, they killed the wife, and took me away with him and Xena to the palace." 

"Wait...so the main reason Old Chef is a cook in the palace is because... he's looking for the godslayer sword that's hidden in the Millions Family?" Shayla gasped.

Ryker nodded once.

"And I'm guessing he hasn't found it, and you haven't found it too." Shayla muttered, eyes softer than usual.

"Zeke knows the whereabouts of the sword, I'm still investigating." Ryker replied.

"And Rumi? Where did Rumi go that day. Why did she disappear when Zeke and Azura came to kill Dominika?" Shayla asked.

"Under investigation, but it definitely has to do with Anatoli too. Someone high up is working with him in this city." Ryker replied.

Shayla blinked. "Your father is...wicked." she muttered.

"No one's kind-hearted in this city, Firefly. Don't mistake it...We're devils, including me." He replied, but she shook her head, holding his face.

"You're not as wicked as they think. You're kind to me at least." 

"I kill innocents, Shayla. My hands are soaked with their blood, and I'm still going to kill more, and I do not feel guilt. I am exactly the bastard like they call me." He whispered, lowering his eyes.

"Still doesn't mean. You were built to crave blood, and I get you even if others don't." She muttered.

"So...if worse comes to worse and you have to kill the innocent with your fire sword, you'd do it?" He asked.

Shayla went quiet, and her eyes closed as if charmed.

Ryker smiled, pulling her head back to his chest warmly. She fell asleep.

"Remain innocent, simp." he muttered, taking one of her lollipop to his mouth, but it's too sweet, so he just swallowed without eating it.

He held her tighter. The pain inside him is threatening to shred him to pieces. It feels like it's ripping his inner organs apart, one by one, but he kept his cool.

Whatever this pain is, he's aware that he doesn't have much time left.

Meanwhile, outside the carriage, Sage is gulping the third bottle of water, and Cheyenne is laughing at him while rubbing his back.

"We've witnessed many brutal deaths throughout our years of working with him, you should have gotten used to it."

"You won't understand, Cheyenne. He unlocks new ways of killing everyday, and I'm already used to it, but this one... Listen, he stabbed her to the wall with his ice sword, chain-dragged her to the ceiling where her body decomposed and the parts fell off one by one till only her heart was hanging there. Fuck!" Sage drank another bottle.

Cheyenne only laughed more, but she didn't stop rubbing his back.

"I wish I was there, that sounds sweet, c'mon, it's Azura."

"You share the same brutality tendency with his lordship" Sage said.

"Proudly. If any other person except him had killed Azura, then trust me he'd kill the person who killed her. Azura's death was meant to come from him. It's all good." She shrugged.

The smell of walnut pie blew over, and she turned around to see it's available in one of the street stalls.

She kept staring, wanting it but decided to not satisfy her cravings. To her, staring is enough.

"I'll get it for you," Sage said when he saw how hard she's staring.

He was about to crossover to the other side when Cheyenne held him back, and their eyes met.

"I told you, do things because you want them, not because I want them. You're not my servant."

"What do you mean? Buying you a walnut pie doesn't make me your servant." He blinked.

"Buy it, and I won't eat." She replied, and he pulled back secretly hurt.

[An Exclusive Haven]

He dropped his qin, facing her properly.

"Have you been brainwashed?"

Xena stood too. "What do you mean brainwashed? Your mother is evil, but if someone was trying to kill her, you'd still fight for her, won't you?"

"What mother?" Soren frowned.

She has been following them around.

[7:30Pm, Lumi Bridge]

The second part of the feast has started.

City people are standing by the bridge to hang their lanterns on the joy trees on top of the bridge while making joyful wishes alongside.

Some are floating their lanterns on the joy river below the bridge.

Anyone applies.

The bridge is packed, and though Xena came with Soren, he didn't even let her hear what his wish was.

He just whispered it before hanging his lantern on a branch.

Xena did the same, wishing for freedom for her and her father, and wishing for more smile from Soren.

Not far from them, Sadie stood alone, not bothering to hang a lantern.

She's just staring hatefully at them.

Sadie didn't move.

Sadie faced her at this point.

"What mother is dead?" She glared.

Faye and Blaise came with Winona, but Kasia and Astor have gone to the palace already to prepare for the night munching since it's the most important part of the feast.

[Joy River]

While most people choseto hang their lanterns on trees, Ryker chose the river.

He's on a boat with Shayla in the middle of the river, they're surrounded by the floating lanterns of the people on top of the river.

They're both trying to fold their own lanterns currently.

Ryker intentionally slowed down, so she was the first to finish folding.

"I finished before you!" She raised it.

"As you should," he replied gently, finishing his own too.

With her mind, Shayla put fire in the middle of the lanterns.

They closed their eyes to make wishes, but Ryker opened his eyes after a short while.

He faced to her, staring hard at her as her lips moved, her wishes coming out in a whisper which he's hearing.

As he listened, he's making his own wish with his own mind too.

There's no time anymore.

When Shayla opened her eyes, they both set their lanterns on the river at the same time, and it floated away.

"And it's done! Happy Lantern Feast, Ryshenka!" She gushed.

He pulled her to his laps, and she blinked slowly, wondering why he seems so low-spirited.

He hovered his palm above her left wrist, and a madly beautiful crystal-bead bracelet appeared on that wrist. Color blue and gold.

"It's...pretty." she muttered, touching it with utmost care as if it'd break.

"I formed it from frozen starlight and molten emberstone, a combination of our essence." He smiled.

She brought it to her lips to kiss, liking it more.

"Thank you, Ry."

She could feel coldness on that wrist, oozing from the crystals.

"Wait in the rain," he whispered, stroking her soft cheek.

"What...what do you mean?" She muttered in confusion.

"Happy Lantern Feast, Firefly." He muttered.

She managed to smile, but she just knows something isn't right.

With the fire burning inside her, and with his underlying tone.

"Ry?" She whispered.

Her eyes turned seductively drowsy as she searched his own.

Slowly, he captured her lips, the kiss so deep and ravenous, like he wants to pull out her soul and chew.

Their hardest kiss ever.

She returned the hard energy, with no idea of what's going to come after.

She just kissed his addictive lips like she owned it.

[East Palace, 9pm]

The palace munching started since thirty minutes ago—the last part of this feast.

But the last thirty minutes was used by the guests to make their entries, so it's just starting properly now.

The night munch doesn't only include people from Luminaria, there are Grand Supremes and Palace Madames from other non-human cities, and there are important wealthy families too and travelers.

Also, folks from the West Palace.

The East Palace is too full, and one would have to wait for the front coast to clear up before you can walk past a particular lane.

Esme and Liza entered at the same time. Though, their robe materials are equally sophisticated and with the glitters, their rivalry showed just the way it does yearly. 

They rolled eyes at each other as they made their dignified entries.

Winona came in elegantly with other Lumi beauties who'd be partaking in the beauty contest.

Granny Tara is already seated on the matriarch throne inside the main feast hall, and Zeke is seated in the center of his court on his throne too.

The moment he saw Esme, he stood and went to take her up the throne with him.

The woman couldn't stop smiling. It's the best day of her existence.

"I guess I'll open the munching with this announcement," Zeke said, drawing the attention of the thousands of guests to himself.

The hall turned dead silent.

"Azura died this morning, killed by the demigod." he announced.

Shocked whisperings started in the court immediately.

"Madame Azura?"

"Oh my gawd!"

"Just like that?"

"Wait... things are aligning. Don't tell me Esme Vega is the new Madame"

"Right. Esme Vega becomes Esme Millions today. She becomes the new and rightful Madame beside me. Her word is law, and the same respect you accord to me...give it to her. Royal wedding comes after this Feast." He commanded, holding Esme by the waist.

The silence in the hall deepened, the shock eating the people up.

"Welcome home, Esme." Granny Tara stood, holding up her wine flute.

Everyone else did just that.

"Welcome home, Madame Esme!!"

The chorus was so loud that it breathed through the palace walls.

Esme hugged Zeke affectionately.

"I love you so much, Zeke."

"I love you, Esmeralda." Zeke smiled cunningly.

The Feast continued as if Azura wasn't part of this city in the first place. As if she never existed.

There's food everywhere, and the aroma is enough to make anyone hungry, so everyone's eating while waiting for the ballet performance.

Mercury is in the crowd, glaring hatefully at Esme beside Zeke.

Old Chef is somewhere in the crowd too, wondering where the godslayer sword might be.

In the middle of the feast, Ryker entered, walking the middle of the court with composure, his eyes settled solely on Esme and Zeke.

The hallwent silent again, and those that were eating had to drop their cutleries or chopsticks to watch what's going to really happen.

Liza kept checking her time, waiting for when Shayla would strike.

Astor and Kasia are at the back of the royal table, and they immediately sent Winona out of the court.

Getting to the middle, Ryker smiled, so threatening yet painful.

"Congratulations on becoming the new palace Madame, Esme Vega." He said, his deep voice echoing with something unclear.

He picked a cup of stinging alcohol and raised a toast.

Esme, not wanting to drag this too far, took a cup too.

"Thank you, Ryker Morozov," she smiled, standing to raise the glass.

Granny Tara was already awaiting the main show.

Zeke stayed calm.

Old Chef is expectant as fûck at the back of the court.

Cheyenne and Sage entered at the same time, both breathing heavily.

Esme and Ryker lifted their glasses to their lips at the same time.

Instead of drinking the alcohol, Ryker vomited blood into the cup.

Blood mixed with the alcoholic liquid, and his chest slashed into two without a sword or knife.

His blood flowed, so much on the court ground. He started freezing from his feet to the top, very quick.

The cup fell from him, and everyone stood to their scared feet.

"Your Lordship!!!" Cheyenne and Sage began running to the front.

[Changing Room]

Since the ballet is the first entertaining part of the feast, the ballerinas are here, all dressed and waiting for when they'd be called in for their performance.

Winona entered the wall, leaving for the FrostOrb room, and they went back into the room.

Shayla finished pinning her leotard and stood, searching for Xena with her eyes. She found her beside the door and went for her.

"Xena,"

"No. I'm not talking to you. Cheyenne probably filled your naive head with lies about father already" Xena turned away from her quickly.

Shayla went to her front.

"Your Pa is a bad man. Ry wouldn't say something bad about someone if the person isn't really bad. You need to be cautious, your Pa is not who you think he is." She said.

Xena scoffed.

"Listen, Shay. Ryker would do anything to make me hate father and come to his side, lies included. You're the one who needs to wake up and see! He's evil!"

"Yes he's evil, but he has been showing you kindness, and he even put you in the ballet school with me—"

"Oh, that?" Fine! I'll forfeit this performance and leave the school! Don't try to school me when you don't know anything" Xena rolled eyes, taking off her leotard.

"Xena!" Shayla snapped.

"Don't shout at me!" Xena snapped back, shocking her. "I won't let you slander my father. We're friends, but don't go too far. The fact that Ryker is fuckin you doesn't give you the right to be his hunt dog too." 

Shayla's arms dropped, her eyes widened. She stepped back once, twice, thrice, unable to believe.

Xena gulped, obviously regretting what she just said, but she just walked out of the hall.

Shayla hugged herself.

It's like that harsh statement brought back the hot sting inside her.

She left the hall too, rushing into a washroom where she squatted behind the door, sweating and panting.

The hotness inside her got worse, evil thoughts brewed in her ear.

It's definitely the thumb print on her arm during the parade. That's why she's feeling this insanity.

"Ahhhmm!!!" She groaned, going from squatting to kneeling down.

She blocked her ears with her palms, but that wasn't enough to fight the voices.

Her body burned, embers appeared on her chest and neck and arms. 

Sweat. Flooding sweat.

Her back mark shone, so bright that it outshone the electric lightning in that washroom.

This over-bright shine isn't only because of her pains, it also means Ryker is bleeding. What's going on?

Why would Ryker be... bleeding?

She tried to stand, but it's like something pushed her back to her knees....a wicked weight which crushed her, causing her to cry out.

The embers on her skin spread, and the voices resumed again.

"Go out there, kill everyone at the feast and kill Ryker last. Ascend to the nine heavens alone and become the only demigoddess alive!"

"No!" Shayla growled.

"No!!" She repeatedly, scratching the marbled floor with her short nails.

Then...Liza's cunning image appeared in front of her.

"Do it!" She persuaded.

"No! Leave me!" Shayla pushed her.

Liza vanished, but she appeared at her back.

"Do it!" She whispered in her ear.

"No!!" Shayla screamed, turning back.

Liza laughed loudly, screaming.

"Do it!!!"

"No!!! Leave my head!!!! I'll never kill the innocent! I'll never lay a hand on Ry to hurt him! Leave my head! You're just an imagination, you're not real leave me!!!!!!" Shayla screamed till blood crawled out of her nose.

Stretching an arm forward, she burned Liza's imagination to the ground, then she was able to stand.

She flung the door open, meeting Kade there.

"You actually overcame the effects of the blood pact?" He said shockingly. "Just how powerful are you? How deeply have you fallen for the demigod?"

Shayla grabbed him by the neck, hauled him up and flung his body to the side of the hallway as if he weighted a feather.

Her eyeballs are still rimmed with gold-red, and the embers are still burning viciously on her white skin.

She stepped Kade's body as she left the wall.

She walked past Winona who's now holding the real FrostOrb. She has gone to the FrostOrb room to exchange it with the fake.

Shayla didn't look at her, didn't see her. She's more focused on where she's going—to find Ryker.

She burned down a part of the hall wall, entering through the space.

In front of the court, there's Ryker, but he's already frozen inside his bloody ice. He's now a pillar of ice, and everyone is watching like it's some kind of paid show.

Only Cheyenne and Sage are trying to help. Cheyenne is using her nails on the ice, but she's only hurting herself. 

Nothing his changing.

Sage is beating the ice with his rope, but it's even more useless than Cheyenne's nails.

Shayla rushed forward, pushed them out of her way and hugged the ice, conjuring more fire in her body.

The fire engulfed her completely, burning and razing with fury till her ballet outfit got burnt away, leaving her in just the singlet and tights.

She kept hugging the pillar of ice, melting it with her fire, so fast and effective.

As the ice melted, water filled the court, and most people had to climb tables and chairs so the water don't swallow them.

In just one minute, Shayla finished melting the ice, but there's no Ryker inside. It's like he melted with the ice, and her madness started right there.

"No....Ry. No." She muttered, looking at her own burning hands.

"No...no...no!! Ry!" She knelt inside the ice water and started searching with her hands as if he's inside somewhere.

She acted mad for twenty minutes straight, searching the water.

"Ry.... Ry!!!!"

Tara exchanged glances with Zeke and Esme.

Old Chef exchanged glances with Rumi who's inside the crows.

The ballerinas rushed into the court at this point, and their eyes when they saw that's going on.

Xena gasped.

"Mother..." Sadie remembered.

"Where's mother?" Soren asked him too, their eyes wide.

Meanwhile, the West Palace folks are already rushing out of the East with the FrostOrb.

Cheyenne and Sage fell on their knees too in the middle of the water.

Shayla suddenly stopped searching the water.

Her eyes came up, and the golden intensified when realization hit her.

He's gone.

"Ry!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!"

That scream flushed hot tears out of her eyes, and at the same time, the East Palace caught serious fire, burning bright with the command of the pain in her mind.

"Fire! Ring the fire alarm!!" The a guest screamed as everyone began running for their lives, pushing each other crazily by the door, fearing death, but some are already getting burnt, unable to escape the rage.

As if possessed, Shayla suddenly pushed herself to her feet, ice water dripping off her body.

Her fierce eyes darted to the only person in the court she's aware is capable of this.

Zeke.

In a whim, she super-sped to him.

Esme blocked her, but she gave her a bite on the neck till her flesh pulled off. 

"Mother!!!" Mercury howled.

Shayla landed in front of Zeke, and he couldn't even move even if he wanted to.

She slapped him thrice with her angrily burning palm, blazing.

Zeke's cheek burned, cooked, before tearing off to the floor.

She's not on the throne anymore, she's now by the window of the throne room, staring out at the beautiful bright night.

At the moon sitting in the sky, and at the stars playing and twinkling around it.

A glad smile adorned her face, and it never faded. It kept widening.

"Everything happened just like I predicted, that's enough reason to pop some alcohol, but not just yet..." She stepped away from the window and went to the bone wall clock to check the time.

"Perfect! I have to go now...he needs me now more than ever!" 

"Go where?" Jude appeared behind her, and she turned with a giggle.

"Go to him, you heard me right." 

"Are you playing me? Is this a sick game?" Jude came closer.

"Concentrate!" Pearl snapped at him.

Jude clenched his fists, face tightening.

"Don't ruin things with your impatience, and listen to me," she brought her voice down before leaning to his ear.

She whispered inaudible words for a minute before stepping away.

 Jude nodded. "Understood,"

"Good. Now wait till I tell you to move. You have plenty of time. Just guard this palace till I'll be back." Pearl smiled, walking out briskly.

Now it's Jude's turn to stay by the window, and before he knew it, he was smiling at the stars.

--

[Luminaria, Granny Jojo's Household]

Thanks to the fact that Azura shared some valuable secrets with Lilith before her death, she knew to take Granny Jojo here after dragging her unconscious body out of the maze earlier.

This household is called Lannister Household, and thankfully, they all like Jojo here, so they took over immediately.

They've been taking care of her by themselves...both the young and old members of the household.

Lilith just stood at a corner, watching as they tried to bring back Jojo's eyesight and hearing ability.

Looks like she can't even talk cos she's just staring at everyone like a dummy. Ryker did her very bad.

The household have been busy with her since she brought her in the afternoon, but there's still no improvement, and now it seems they're all starting to get tired.

Things won't get better unless Ryker himself says so, and Ryker himself is gone totally.

Lilith looked at the ring on her finger, smiling creepily.

"I love you, Ryker, but sometimes...your evilness drives me too crazy." She muttered, looking back at Granny Jojo's immobile body.

"You have to get better, Jojo. I have only you by my side now. My banshee ability won't buy me survival."

--

[Royal Wellness Haven]

Esme and Zeke are both on treatment beds, side by side with alchemists administering herbs and healing powders on them... anyone they could get their hands on.

Esme is conscious, neck bandaged since it's the only wounded part of her body, but Zeke is still unconscious.

His burnt cheek looks so hideous, and they can't even cover it with wools or plaster cos it'd only get worse.

The neck veins got rejoined, but his neck is still swollen, it it's bandaged. That part is still very much easier than the cheek.

Healers have been going from pillar to post, trying to heal the cheek, but it's not effective.

Nothing is effective.

Esme kept ordering them not to stop, so the healers aren't stopping even when they're all exhausted shaking and losing energy. They kept trying.

No changes. Nothing.

Zeke's burnt cheek remained the same—ugly and black.

"Stop!" Tara suddenly ordered.

"Granny they have to continue!" Esme jumped down from the bed.

"I said stop!" Tara ordered the healers again, and they gave up.

"Granny..." Esme faced her.

"Don't you get it?" Tara scoffed, looking at Zeke's body on the bed.

"It's Shayla's fire, so it'd never heal unless with Ryker's ice, and Ryker's ice is nowhere cos he's gone!" Tara slammed the words to hit deep.

"But his face can't stay burnt like this for life...it can't. My husband can't stay with that scary face." Esme said.

"Unfortunately, he'd have to stay with the scary face forever. In fact, it'd help plant his fear in the mind of the people, cos that burn wound is never going to heal." Tara said.

Esme started crying.

"Stop the tears!" Tara snapped.

Esme slapped her palm over her mouth, sniffling.

"His burnt face means nothing. In the end, we'll overcome everything. We started this, and we'll finish it. His face will be the last thing we focus on. For now, focus on your godslaying ability. Very well." Tara instructed.

"But the twins...they won't like me." Esme said.

"Don't worry about them, Zeke will handle them when he wakes up. The twins are minor cases. The fact that they're already friends with your daughter will make things easier." Tara explained.

"And the West Palace? They took the FrostOrb." Esme said.

"I told you, don't be bothered by these nameless things," Tara responded, sitting beside Zeke's bed.

"They're like ants, too easy to eradicate. They're the same people who lose their powers at midnight. Zeke will wake up latest tomorrow or in two days, we'd wipe out everyone of them and take back the orb. It has always been ours."

"From now on, things gets dirtier, we have to be careful though." Esme said 

"I know, that's obvious. This city gets darker, and more dangerous." Tara sighed, looking at Zeke.

At the door, Mercury stood, gummed to the wall as she listened to everything.

Now she hates her mother more than before. More than anything.

--

[Next Day]

Morning refused to come. 

The day didn't break at all.

Since the chaos at the East Palace yesterday, there has been no morning.

People only calculated the daytime with their wall clocks and watches, everywhere has been dark.

It's like a curse, and it's spreading like a plaque throughout the city.

Also, the heat has been unbearable. Everyone has been feeling the scath from Shayla's fire.

It never stopped.

Cheyenne and Sage took Shayla to her Fire Palace since it's the only available place right now.

After she used the fire to break the chaos, her palace opened on its own.

She subconsciously showed them the way yesterday after they took her out of the burning palace, and they've been here...the hottest place in the whole of the city with fire burning on the walls and volcanoes cracking under the rock segments.

The only places free from fire is the rooms, but it's so hot, and no matter the air conditioning, they're sweating very hard.

They both had to wear only singlets as they used hand fans to blow air on Shayla in bed, contributing breeze.

She has been unconscious, but saying one name from her sleep.

"Ry"

That's all she has been muttering, and each time she said that, they fanned harder.

Seems the bracelet on her wrist is helping to keep her heat in check cos whenever it shone, her sweats reduced a lot.

Her eyes didn't open till the 12th hour, and the knights dropped their big fans, waiting till she calmly took in her bearings.

She sat up quickly, looking around again, but the only people she's seeing are Cheyenne and Sage.

"Goddess," Sage whispered.

"Your ladyship," Cheyenne added, their eyes full of worries.

"Ry, where is he? Where's Ry?" She asked, looking traumatized.

They both went silent, they just exchanged glances.

"It's not true...no." Shayla shook her head quickly, her tiny voice shaky and emotional. "It's all a dream, right? What I saw about how he melted away. It's all a dream. Please say yes."

"Goddess, this isn't the time to be emotional." Sage said.

"No I don't want to hear that! He has spoiled me too much that I can barely do anything without him, and now he wants to just leave like that? Can you even hear yourself? It's not making any sense!!" She cried, trying to get off the bed, but Cheyenne held her back fast.

"You can't exhaust yourself now. You should recuperate gently and conjure all the energy you can. You have so many trainings to do, too. War is approaching, and we can't be caught unprepared. Many people will come for you." She said.

"I don't want anything else! I just want him! What do you mean he's gone? I don't want to be fine please just bring him to me!!! He's not gone! He's never gone! He can't be gone! Never!! He's alive and well!! He can't be gone!!!"

Sage helped Cheyenne to hold her back too, and as she cried, the fire under the volcanos and firewalls burned brighter, producing more heat and messing with her head, snapping at her control.

"Ry... Please come back! Please... Please!!! Bring him back to me!!"

--

[City Tower Edge]

The twins couldn't think of a more suitable location.

This place has to be the best—closer to the dark sky and far from the low ground.

They brought Xena here since yesterday, plotting ways to punish her for her brother's sins.

Sadie is the one thinking about the ways and dictating.

Soren is the one writing them down.

Sadie is strolling the edge with her evil smile intact, her black boots marching the ground with ominous clicks which is like music to her ears.

It's like this chaos brought Soren back to her, and she can't ask for more.

Soren is seated on a high-structured chair, his pen kissing his note repeatedly as he jotted Sadie's suggestions.

He looks like he backpedalled.

Looks like the old Soren who has no pity in his eyes and just does whatever Sadie wanted.

He stopped jotting at the last suggestion, and Sadie faced him.

"Done?"

"All done," he stood from the chair, handing her the book.

They faced the same angle by the wall where Xena is seated like an enslaved captive on the floor, hands and legs still tied, eyes still blindfolded and mouth still gagged.

Sadie made few strides to her, squatted in front of her and stripped her mouth gag away.

"Ple...se... don't...kill me." Xena begged and coughed shakily, snot crawling out of her nose.

All her life, she has never trembled this hard, it's like the fear is germinating with their choky presence. Her body is shaking with the wall, and her teeth is clashing.

She could taste the pain on her tongue even when it hasn't started, and she could taste her own fear on her dry palate.

"Do you want to know a truth?" Sadie said with a cruel cackle, the chains on her boots clanking as they dragged the cemented ground.

"Your brother didn't only kill our mother because of revenge. In fact, revenge isn't the major reason why he killed her." She whispered.

She tapped Xena's cheek with two fingers, her black painted nails catching the glow of the fire burning in the middle of the tower.

"Your lifeline which father stole...it was hidden inside mother's core, and to get it back... mother had to die. The bastard killed mother majorly to get your lifeline back, so your lifeline is back to your life core." 

"Wh... what?" Xena stuttered, body going pale with shock. A hot line of sweat ran down her backline.

"Oh... Soren she's shocked." Sadie clapped once, laughing as she looked back at Soren.

"Of course she'd be. She's a bîtch who'd die for her father," Soren replied dryly.

He's currently beside the burning fire on that same edge.

He pulled out three branding irons from the fire at the same time.

The metals are already red with the fire ember, and they looks like the pit of hell already.

"Ou! That looks delicious! Can't wait for her to taste it!" Sadie howled, facing Xena who's already crying.

Her tears crawled out from under her black stuffy blindfold.

"Aww, you're just a girl." Sadie laughed loudly, the sound dark like the persistent night.

Soren walked towards them with the three metals swinging in his hand.

On the three irons, SLAVE, SLAVE, SLAVE was written on each one.

"Can we start?" He said emotionlessly.

Sadie stood by his side, madly pleased with this new him.

"Of course, brother." she said, glancing at the book with her.

"First on our sweet beautiful list is branding her as our personal slave, and you see..." She faced blindfolded Xena at this point.

"These triplets branding irons are from hell, so just like my father's face can't heal, this mark won't be able to wipe from you... for life..." She said, wickedly grinning.

"No... please....don't... Don't do that to me!!!" Xena shifted, but Soren's cold grip landed on her, holding her in place.

She froze, peeing on herself.

Sadie laughed again, taunting and more wicked.

Then it came, painful than hell and abyss and bitterness combined.

The three hot irons kissed Xena's forehead at the same time, and smokes oozed out as her flesh burned with the three SLAVE brands, taking over all of her forehead.

Blood streamed out of the little holes on her skin, her tears boiled hotter than lava, more pee trickled out of her stinging pûssy, and her mouth opened wide.

"Ryker!!!!!!!"

[Two Weeks Later, Fire Palace]

"In one....." Sage counted.

"Two......" Cheyenne joined in.

They're both in the Palace Radius where there's a vast area of land, free for practices.

They had taken the last two weeks to  design it perfectly for Shayla's practices, and just three days ago, it came out just fine.

They sat on top of a stone with black whistles to their mouths, each one of their legs on top of a smaller stone.

They've gotten used to the insane heat here, it's not even a problem anymore.

Shayla stood at the center of the ground, holding her fire sword which she had summoned out of herself since three days ago, and it has been working with great usefulness for her practices.

Her aesthetic changed, but not too much. She's still a pink-obsessed girl, just that she mixed it with the colour of her fire—gold.

Most of her robes are like that, and her hair? It seems to be growing longer everyday even without Ryker here. It's longer beyond her butt right now, and her jewels didn't decrease from it. In fact, the jewels multiplied than before.

She looks serious, and focused as she gave the mountains and small volcanic rocks around a hard prickling stare.

She looked back at her knights over her shoulder, and the two got the signal immediately, standing to their feet.

"Go!" They chorused, blowing their whistles at the same time.

Shayla leapt to the air, levitating better than anyone she has ever witnessed till she's at the top of the mountains where she wielded her sword professionally, rubbing the blade with two fingers before sending the fire sparks to the mountains.

The mountains split, pouring lava out of their middles and brightening up the fire and flying sparks.

She tumbled, rolled, twisted and whisked herself in the air before landing another thud from the sword.

A lone dry tree stood on top of the mountain with few leaves on top of it. She leapt to it, touched a branch with her toes then whisked back.

The few small leaves on the tree fell off, and she chased after them, gently moving with the waves of the hot air till she was left with just one by her sharp sight.

She landed on the ground while still targeting that one with her sword, and in the middle of the other leaves, she stabbed that particular leave with the sword tip which happened to be in front of Cheyenne's face.

Cheyenne didn't move, didn't blink even when the burning sword blazed in front of her eyes.

Shayla stayed in that position for a minute, and the fire burned away the little leaf before she lowered the sword, sighing.

"I'm tired," she muttered, dropping the sword and sitting down.

Cheyenne bursted into laughter.

"You'll never stop this laziness, would you, your ladyship?" She said.

"At least, she's very good now. She's taking her practices serious." Safe replied, bringing her a cup of strawberry juice.

Shayla took it and gulped everything, licking her lips after.

"But when you were still at the slaves labor camp, I heard you were the most hardworking," Cheyenne sat in front of her.

"Of course I was hard-working, there were whippings and pressure from the supervisors, but here... nothing like that. Why should I have sleepless nights cos of people I can handle with one snap of my finger? Please, just leave me alone with my laziness." Shayla sighed tiredly, and now it's Sage's turn to laugh.

"Stop laughing!" She pointed at him with a cute frown, and he covered his mouth quickly.

"Why not try extracting your FireStone?" Cheyenne suggested.

"You think it's a good idea?" Sage said cautiously.

"She summoned her fire sword already, so why not call out the FireStone too? His lordship's FrostOrb was out of his body the whole time, and he was even more powerful with the orb outside his body." Cheyenne shrugged.

"Come to think of it, she hasn't been sleeping at nights since two weeks ago, maybe removing it will actually reduce her inner heat?" Sage said.

"Is that true?" Shayla looked up.

Cheyenne and Sage nodded.

Two minutes later, they're both standing some meters away from her already, and her eyes are closed.

Focusing on the inner energy again, conjuring the inner stone and taking advantage of the hot environment, she summoned.

Few hand movements, and a few more whimpers, she was able to really call it out.

From her core, the FireStone fell into her palm. Round, gold, and burning brightly. So beautiful.

The golden glow from it was everywhere in that palace, and it nearly outshone the light of the day.

Cheyenne and Sage came closer, each by her sides as they stared at the FireStone on her palm together.

"I hate to admit, but this damn FireStone is more beautiful than his lordship's FrostOrb." Sage said.

"I disagree, his lordship's FrostOrb everyday everytime for me." Cheyenne replied, and Shayla huffed.

She bit herself on the finger, dropping her blood on the stone. The fire burned brighter, glowing up.

"Fineee...it's so fuckin pretty like the owner," Cheyenne sighed.

"Right," Shayla nodded, but suddenly, she pressed it to her stomach, returning it inside herself.

"Why?" The knights chorused.

"I can't risk it being outside, these greedy freaks might just make plans to snatch it just like they did the FrostOrb." Shayla sat back.

"Wait ... You still think that orb the West Palace stole from the East Palace is the real FrostOrb?" Sage scoffed proudly.

"It's not?" Shayla blinked.

"If his lordship was that negligent, then things would have gone sour since long ago. Also, if the Millions Family were really drawing power from the real FrostOrb, then his lordship wouldn't have been able to kill Azura that easily." Cheyenne explained.

"Lots of things that I still don't know," Shayla muttered.

"I'm sure his lordship kept it from you for a reason." Sage said.

Shayla sighed, nodding twice.

"Morning broke yesterday after two long weeks of darkness, and what's happening in the city today?" 

"It's the traditional royal wedding between Esme Vega and Zeke. Also, it's the takeover ceremony at the West Palace." Sage provided.

"Do you want us to go waste everyone at the wedding?" Cheyenne said.

"No." Shayla flatly said.

Cheyenne sighed.

"We don't move unless they move first, I'm telling you." Shayla turned serious. "Don't do anything without telling me first, and if you have useful ideas, feel free to tell me."

"I understand," Cheyenne complied, head going down a bit.

"By the way, I and Cheyenne will visit the human world later today. The headquarters showcase is today, and we're to represent boss if he's not available." Sage said.

"I know, I have somewhere to be too. Be careful, you both." Shayla stood.

"I'll go soak in the cold pool before I sweat away my blood." She said.

Her robe was slowly dragging the ground as she proceeded into the entrance at the other side.

"The fact that she has to soak in the pool for almost five times a day is something else." Sage muttered.

"You should even be thankful that she has that bracelet on her wrist that's slowly fighting the heat. If she doesn't have it, then maybe she'd be soaking like ten fifteen times." Cheyenne said.

"But she's actually enduring better than I expected." Sage nodded.

"She's stronger than she looks, but it's just this spirit of laziness." Cheyenne replied, and Sage chuckled.

Their chats paused when then sensed a visitor around.

After exchanging knowing glances, they both vanished off, reappearing at the major entrance of the palace—the burning gate.

Liza stood in front of the gate, holding a bottle of liquor and a greedy smile on her face.

Even when the knights arrived, she didn't look at them. Her eyes stayed on the burning golden gate, and her smile turned bigger.

"I've been planning to come for a visit since two weeks ago, but unfortunately, morning didn't come till yesterday. I miss her so much that it hurts. Go back inside, and tell her I'm here... waiting." She said, finally looking at the knights.

"I feel power oozing from you, meaning you got your powers back. Wenches be doing the most unexpected." Sage sneered.

"Whatever made you think you can come here and demand to see her and get to see her is just as foolish as your face. You're the most pathetic person in the whole of this city, Liza Kings." Cheyenne grinned mockingly.

Liza drank from her liquor bottle, maybe trying to drown her anger.

"Tell Shayla King to come out here! I have to see her! My daughter doesn't hate me, you both can cry all day about it. Get me inside, or get her out here!" Liza ordered.

"Shayla King?" Cheyenne scoffed, walking closer to the woman. "Say that again. Call her by your dirty surname again!"

Liza went silent, but she smirked and started pouring the alcohol in her bottle to the ground.

"She's his lordship's partner. Love thread or not, wedding or not, weather he's alive or not... she's automatically Shayla Morozov. Unless you want to disappear with her fire, keep your wishes unspoken." Cheyenne threatened.

By now, Liza has finished pouring out the alcohol in her bottle, and she put it to use immediately.

She broke it with he'd mind, and she nearly shoved the jagged edges to Cheyenne's face, but Sage was faster.

With his rope, he tied Liza in a roll, lifting her and flinging her to a different part of the mountain where she nearly rolled down.

Liza stood as if she didn't just fall, sending a crack to the mountains beneath them.

The mountains cracked in the middle, almost sinking them inside, but Sage was faster again, dragging Cheyenne with herself to another side where they sent conjoined hits to get.

Liza fell out of the way, not letting it cool down before returning the hit to them. Cheyenne didn't let Sage have the upper hand this time, she blocked it, but Liza is really really strong.

Cheyenne staggered, and a little trail of blood come out of her nose.

"Darn it! Cheyenne." Sage muttered.

"This is only just a warm-up. Next time, I won't be so nice. I have a wedding to attend today, so I'll return tomorrow, and you better be ready to bring my daughter to me by then, or you'll do more than a little bleeding." Liza said arrogantly, vanishing away.

"Cheyenne, you ok?" Sage held her, but she broke free.

"How many times do you want me to tell you to stop doing things for me? Why were you taking the first hits alone?" She confronted.

"Cheyenne i—"

"If I didn't act faster to take that last hit, you'd be the one bleeding in the nose right now you know that. Why always ready to do anything for me at any cost! What is wrong with you!!" She shouted angrily.

"Cos I love you!!!" Sage yelled back.

"But I don't!!" Cheyenne spat, pushing his chest hard.

Sage lurched back, swallowing hard multiple times.

His eyes grew watery, his palms sweaty. 

He suddenly started feeling the stings of the fire environment again.

"Focus on yourself, Sage. Grow your skills, not because of me, but because of yourself. Do things because you really want to do them. Our families abandoned us half-dead before his lordship found us and saved us, focus on making your family pay for what they did to you and stop. Stop your feelings. I don't plan to ever fall in love, not even in the next life." Cheyenne declared.

The tears finally fell from Sage's eyes, but Cheyenne didn't care.

Sage quickly wiped them though, with his fingers.

Cheyenne wiped the blood on her nose too.

"Let's go, we have the human world to be at." She said, leading the way down the mountain.

Sage followed grudgingly.

[City Tower]

Xena us still here, yet to be saved by anyone. She has lost hope completely, and all she does is take the twins daily punishments like a pathetic little slob.

She has no idea what's going on in the city or how her father is faring. 

No idea how Shayla is doing now that Ryker is gone.

No idea if Shayla is still mad at her for what she said to her.

No idea if Ryker is really totally gone just like that.

She's crying as she leaned by the wall of the edge, still tied up and still blindfolded.

The only free part of her body is her mouth. Her blindfold is always wet with her tears, and it's already reeking of tear smell.

Also, her branded forehead doesn't even feel like it's healing. Her lifeline is back to her, so her animal ability should grow sharper, but it's like it even got worse cos she could do nothing about the houseflies hovering around her.

They're very many, cos part from her forehead brands, she has been on her period since yesterday, and her blood has been soaking her skirt up. She smells so bad.

She has even forgotten what the sun looks like, or the moon, or daylight.

The clicking of heels on the cemented ground made her freeze.

No need to even wonder, it's very obvious who owns that walk.

Sadie.

She could smell her choky perfume even before she got close, and Sadie clapped as she approached.

She wore madly tall black heels, a mini skirt and a black hoodie. A arm bag is on her shoulder, and she looks slightly different cos she cut her hair, so it's now in a bob, but her goth appearance is still there.

All black.

"Yunno what? Today is my father's wedding to his new victim, so I thought of coming to say hi before I'd leave, but heck ... You're a mess. You smell like shit!" She plugged her nose with her fingers.

"I can't even come close, I guess Soren would faint if I had brought him. Look at the swam of houseflies, ew!" She waved her hand to hit one of the houseflies away.

"There'll be plenty of food at the wedding, so don't worry... I'll bring leftovers for you and plenty bones, but before that...why don't we play a little?" She smiled, lifting a bucket of water from the ground.

She poured it all on Xena, then she broke the bucket with her heels before picking a horsewhip hanging on the wall.

She brought the whip down to Xena's body mercilessly, whipping her in repeat like a sinner animal.

Her skin peeled, and her blood started wasting again.

Xena cried till her voice seized, and her body went limp on the ground.

She fainted.

[Carnegie Hall]

The historical hall is the wedding location of Zeke and Esme's wedding, and the theme is also historical.

Robes and whatnot.

Zeke woke up a week ago, and he has been slowly recuperating in silence since then.

When morning finally came yesterday, he ordered this wedding to happen today. Esme didn't even complain despite his ugly burnt face.

She agreed to the wedding immediately, and here they are.

Only exclusive guests got invited. Just members of the royal family and the clansmen.

Over twenty clansmen died during the collapse of the East Palace, but they're large in number, so there's still a lot of them left, and they're all here, filling the space.

Liza attended too with Kade by her side. As they entered the hall, Kade looks calm—very calm.

Since Liza got her powers back, he has been cautious and obedient around her, and he's even the one carrying her back.

The hall is filled with the guests already, but Zeke and Esme and Granny Tara aren't in sight.

They must be in the bridal or groom room, fitting into their dresses.

The twins entered the hall, both looking as dark as ever.

They're the only ones wearing modern in the historical call. Soren wore a leather jacket on a loose jean, and he held his qin, intending to okay for the new couple.

But surprisingly, they're not alone. A girl around their age came in after them, and she's dressed exactly like Sadie. She looks just as wicked too.

Her name's Rivera, Sadie's new sidekick which she replaced Mercury with, and she looks extra.

"I thought you said you were gonna bring your rabbit, Soren." She said, getting beside Sadie.

"He's slightly sick, he's resting in his home cage." Soren replied, that emotionless face so hard.

The whisperings of the guests started getting to them by now...

"Aren't they too radiant? Their mother died two weeks ago."

"And their father is getting married to their best friend's mother?"

"I heard Zeke's face is incurable and now ugly."

"Zeke?"

"What else should I call him? So far the FrostOrb isn't with the Millions Family anymore, then he's not the Grand Supreme."

"What a dramatic family, is that really the end of the East Palace?"

"I heard they're rebuilding it though."

"What use would that be? They'd be below the West Palace now anyways."

Sadie was boiling as she listened to the whisperings, but she kept her fists balled, controlling her anger cos Zeke specifically warned them not to cause ruckus today.

"Those runny mouths should go in the pit. Shut up y'all!" Rivera shouted at a point when she git fed up with their whisperings.

"Ignore them, I'm not in the mood to kill today," Soren said.

"Look who we have here!" Rivera pressed back when she saw Mercury coming, dressed in a robe.

Mercury's eyes went to Soren first, then Rivera, then Sadie.

She stopped walking, and Sadie walked to her with a smile.

"Welcome to the family, Mercury Millions. I bet you've been dying to bear that expensive surname." 

"I'm still Mercury Vega, I'm not gonna get adopted by your father! I hate my mother just as much as you do right now, so don't try to make me feel guilty!" Mercury spat.

"Do I care? And you have no say in these things, Mercury. Your adoption documents got finalized already. After this wedding, you and your prostitute mother becomes a part of this family. She seduced father, so she should be ready to bear the brunt." Sadie glared.

"Let's go, Rivera." 

"Yes, baby!" Rivera held her arm.

Mercury was looking at that contact as the girls left her.

Soren walked past her too...

"Soren..." She grabbed his arm, but he pushed her off, and she fell on the ground.

"You don't touch me, or I'll change my mind and kill you and your shameless mother!" He threatened before leaving.

Mercury stood and rushed into the available corner to cry.

[Couple Dressing Suite]

"You must be beauty-drunk," Sadie's voice said behind her, and she quickly turned, facing the door to see  Sadie entering.

"What? It's my father's wedding with you, why would I stay home? I need to witness the start of your hell. You have no idea what you're stepping into." Sadie chuckled.

"Stop saying rubbish, and wait at the main hall." Esme replied.

"Sadie!" She gasped shockingly.

His heart matters, so far he loves her, it's all good.

"I'm sure they're currently doing their takeover ceremony. But don't worry, they takeover the city today, and we snatch it back tomorrow. Let them taste superiority for some hours." Zeke replied, leaning in to kiss her.

His burnt face has a specific smell, but she didn't mind. She kissed back.

"Are you both gonna spend the rest of your lives in here? Guests are waiting." Granny Tara said by the door.

The new couple left the suite, walking down the aisle of people together, accompanied by both friendly and hateful eyes of the guests.

Soren is playing his qin aggressively in the front, Sadie's arms are folded on her chest as she stood beside Rivera, both glaring.

Mercury's eyes are red from tears.

Liza is clapping so loud in the middle of the crowd, and Esme wouldn't stop rolling eyes at her.

Shockingly, Granny Jojo is in the hall too. Lilith brought her, and no idea if she's healed or not, but Zeke was smirking as he stared at the old woman.

Granny Tara on the other hand looks like the happiest in this hall. She didn't stop smiling till the couple got to the front and kowtowed slowly to watch other.

The new arrangement is now ready. The battle can now start.

Not just the West Palace folks and the clansmen, but there are new additions to their harem like slaves and servants and able-bodied guards.

Faye and Blaise and Winona are smiling like fools as Astor and Kasia went forward together to receive the royal endowment.

Since Kasia is the head of the family, she's the one who got crowned as the owner of the city.

She got crowned as the Grandmistress while Astor stayed beside her as the Palace Eminence.

Anatoli zipped it up and carried it.

[Pink Island, 7pm]

Last time she came here...she came with him, but now...

Now...

She let tears out of her eyes before she knew it, and she sniffled.

"Cheyenne and Sage said I need to move on and live the best, and I agreed with them, but I only try to keep it all together whenever they're around. If I'm alone like this, I can't stop sadness from crawling in." She said to herself, pouting.

She stopped talking, sniffled, wiped her tears and drank from her alcohol. She softly kissed her bracelet, then she brought his embroidered handkerchief out of her robe pocket to sniff into her nose.

"Or I'll just...get another man. I'm pretty enough to bag any man in this city yunno?" She sniffled again.

Then a cold wind blew past, carrying a voice she'd recognize even in the noisiest pit of abyss...

"Before any other man would touch you, he'd die off like an unlucky withered flower." 

Shayla spranged up, eyes wide as she looked around desperately.

"Ry?" 

"I know it's you so stop speaking from the air and get out to me you scum!" She cried, wiping her nose.

[Clouds Castle]

"Greetings, your ladyship." He bowed.

"You know why I came. He's still breathing somewhere, right? I can still feel him everywhere and it's driving me crazy. Earlier,I heard his voice at our island and —"

"Your ladyship, I'm a seer who sees the future, I dunno about lives and deaths of gods." He cut her off.

"But you're close to him! You should know these things!" Shayla raised her voice, and he sighed.

"I can't even see into your future tiger, Shayla. All I see is darkness which has never happened before. Darkness usually means hopelessness, so maybe it's really the end of the way for the two of you. It's better to end it now than to wait for that dark future, so maybe it's all for the best." 

Shayla went quiet.

"You should leave as fast as you can, goddess. Your warm aura isn't fit for this foggy place, you'd suffer backlash if you stay too long." Eternus continued before starting to go inside.

Eternus stopped, but he didn't turn back. He could hear the breaking in her voice.

"Please...I know you know something. I miss him so much and it's messing with me every time. The cold spring isn't even doing anything. I have to take five baths daily and it's killing me. Whatever you know about all of these, just tell me. I miss him, very very, too much." She said.

"If you both grew a love thread for each other already, this would have been simpler, but in the absence of it, there's nothing I can offer. Go back to your palace, goddess. Your aura isn't compatible with this place." Eternus replied and teleported into the castle.

He waited till he was sure he couldn't feel her aura around the castle anymore, then he opened the deepest room in the castle.

Inside the room, a FrostOrb sat majestically inside a prism glass, so beautifully crafted, shinning radiantly. But the shine is confined in the glass cos it's several divine layers.

It's Ryker's real FrostOrb.

"You better come back fast, I'm tired of guarding your orb for hundreds years plus, you heartless demigod." Eternus smiled a little.

It's 8pm now, so it's been fifteen hours, and he's at the verge of losing it when Pearl returned.

She was smiling as she rounded the ice bowl, watching him inside.

"Good. You can come out now." She said after a while.

She barely finished talking when Jude jumped out of the bowl, shivering inside his own frozen skin.

"Go now. Take the compass, it'd lead you to her fire palace." She handed him a compass.

"I might have been busy with Ryker since two weeks ago, but I took my time to search for her palace too, just for you. You're my minion after all." Pearl smiled.

"So you came just to give me this compass?" Jude said, his bones cracking under his flesh.

"We've been doing it raw since two weeks ago, we want to try something new this time." She said.

Jude rolled eyes, and she chuckled.

[Fire Palace]

Eternus was very right when he said Shayla's aura contradicts the aura of the foggy clouds castle.

Normally, soaking in the cold pool calms her down after a while, but it's not like that this time.

She has been inside for some minutes, but there has been no improvements.

Her own skin feels like her enemy. She's literally cooking from the inside, and the water turned mad hot already.

She stayed in the middle, hugging herself with her arms. Her pink bikini seems to be hurting her skin as it's starting to burn too.

Soon, her whole skin will catch fire. She could feel it.

She raised two fingers underwater, changing the water to cold, but a minute after, the water boiled again, reddening her skin.

"Too hot...too too...hot...ahh..." She whispered, clamping her arms around her body again.

The water boiled, as if trying to cook her for dinner, and this time, she even lacked the energy to change it.

She's starting to go inert.

Is this how Ryker felt when she was absent from his life for more than 300 years?

The pain? The torture? The grit?

She whispered again, hot steam rising from the water and implicating the air too. She screamed out...

"Ry...is this like a punishment? Huh?" She whispered, letting go of her arms.

They went sidewards, and her body went limp in the water.

She was already inert when a new entity jumped into the boiling pool.

Jude.

Thanks to his icy body, bypassing the fire gate was easy, and now that he's inside the boiling pool with her, he feels nothing too.

He easily swam to her and cupped her up in his arms, sweeping her hair away from her face.

"Fûck, I missed you." He kissed her forehead first, then her hair.

"Don't worry, I'm here for you. I'll make you feel better. Much more better than you've ever felt." He whispered, collaring his arms around her in a slow drag.

Their bodies collided underwater, and as he tried to share his body coldness with her, he felt strange.

It didn't take long at all.

It happened so fast, like a spell.

Like an ant is crawling from his feet, so he stayed still, feeling the sensation of the torturous crawl till whatever it is arrived at his belly.

He gently let go of Shayla, settling her body down beside the pool wall.

He got out of the pool to see what it really is that's crawling on him, but he saw nothing.

No ant, no insect.

But something is crawling inside of him. By now it's at his chest, and that was when tragedy struck.

He felt his heart getting slashed, and he swayed back, holding the pool wall as his head went down.

His second hand held his chest that's already bleeding, and his body trembled with fear, and the venom of death that's already lacing him.

He looked at Shayla in the water, and he grunted, getting what exactly is happening.

Hex.

Ryker must have put a forbidden hex on her that any other person who touches her intimately should die.

"F...u....ahhhh!!!!!!!!" He growled, his voice shaking the lava beneath the top mountains.

"You're going to be a bastard till...till the end... Ryker Morozov.... even in your absence....ahhhhh!!!!!!" His hand slipped away from the pool edge, and his back landed on the ground.

He didn't bleed from the mouth, ears, eyes or nose.

The blood only soaked his chest, and his eyeballs bulged out.

The agony was short, but painful than anything he has ever tasted.

His eyes closed on their own, embracing the poisonous claws of sudden death.

He died, flatly.

[Minutes Later]

Shayla stirred in the pool, eyes peering open as slowly as ever.

Had she passed out?

She groaned, touching her forehead with two fingers to feel her temperature. 

It's gone down already.

"That's strange," she muttered, throwing her glance around.

She could feel the aura of another person in here. 

Though it's not very thick, but it's still lingering.

She leapt out of the water, landing on a white stone beside the side.

Her sword appeared in her right hand, she positioned it rightly, her eyes sliding to everywhere she could look at inside the pool chamber.

Water dripped from her bikini, sounding tap-tap on the ground.

"Who's there?" She demanded, pointing the sword forward. 

Fire erupted on it's body, reflecting on the pool surface.

"Who's in!" She asked again, firmer and more authoritative.

It's definitely not Cheyenne or Sage—they're currently in the human realm. Also, her nose recognizes their scent, it's definitely not like this one.

She lowered her sword when she couldn't smell the aura anymore, her shoulders dropped.

The person was already out of here even before she woke, but who?

She sighed a small bloodstain at the other side of the pool.

She leapt there and rubbed her fingers on the blood, bringing it to her nose to smell.

"It's not an animal, smells like royalty... Jude?" She recognized, standing up quickly.

"Why would Jude come here? And why would he bleed?" She muttered, walking towards her bedchamber entrance already.

"How did he even get past the fire gate and volcanoes?" She wondered aloud again, glancing back.

The fact that he only bled this little means he's ok, and maybe whatever hurt him is enough punishment for breaking into her palace.

She gave up on thinking about it, went into her bedchamber and opened her closet for a change of clothes.

The first one she sighted was her leotard and leggings and the tutu skirt—ballet.

She misses ballet crazily. It's something she once dreamt of going to the human world to settle down and do, but that was before she learnt about her goddess title.

Now it seems highly impossible. 

There's still a lot to do, and she hasn't even grown the red love thread.

Why is the thread not growing when her heart already belongs to....

Her thoughts were cut short when she felt a drip under herself, and she gasped. Her eyes widened.

"Period! Not again!"

She quickly fished out pads and dressed up, hoping it won't be severe like last time, but who's she kidding?

It's even worse.

She endured it all alone without shedding a tear.

As she bled, she drank red tonic to replenish some of the lost blood.

Just like the other time at the glass mansion, the fire around her burned with rage, as if angry at her.

Alone, she persevered through the toughness.

It took over all her night.

He looked back again to see if Rumi is still far from him, and luckily, she's still walking. She seems lost in the scenery of the dessert.

"A friend," Anatoli replied, but of course she didn't buy that lie.

She put a obeisance spell on him immediately, and Anatoli went still.

"Who were you on the phone with?" She asked again.

"A friend, from the East Palace. He was one of the chefs who came from the capital to cook for the Feast." Anatoli lied through the spell.

"Let's go"

Before they knew it, handcuffs were pulled out.

With his mind, he replicated it. The pin became five, matching the number of security men.

Sage brought the pin out of his pocket, handing it to her.

[Luminaria, New Millions Family Penthouse~Mercury's Room]

Don't worry, the reconstruction of the East Palace will be done in a week, and we'll move in. Also, we're getting the FrostOrb back from the West Palace today, so be happy. This city is still ours."

Mercury looked up, her red eyes resembling a junkie's.

"I'm doing all of these for you," Esme stroked her hair, but Mercury pushed her away suddenly.

"Stop using that excuse every single time you dog! You're doing nothing for me! You're doing everything because you're stupidly in love with Zeke, that smelly-faced man whose face gives me nightmares and makes me wanna puke! Your ugly husband!!" Mercury snapped.

A sizzling slap was what she got for saying that. 

Esme landed a slap on her face, and she started crying again.

"Come out here, even if it means forcing the food into your mouth, you have to eat! You can't die on me!"

Esme dragged her out of the room, and they went past the twins at the large hall.

Sadie leaned by the wall, smiling.

Soren is beside her, empty-faced.

Rivera is with them, and she has a large grin on with those scary big eyes of hers.

She spent the night here, actually.

Esme dragged Mercury till they got to the dinning where Zeke and Granny Tara are sitting already.

She forced her ass to the chair, brushing her hair back.

"Eat with your new family, it won't kill you!" She spat.

Mercury remained seated, but she made no effort to eat.

Esme went to sit beside Zeke.

The twins and Rivera joined the dinning shortly.

"Don't worry, you'll get used to your new father soon," Zeke told Mercury, and she glared resentfully at him.

"True, you'll get used to your new father soon. Eat your meal, Mercury" Sadie said tauntingly.

Rivera grinned, filling her mouth.

"I forgot to ask what family you came from, Rivera?" Granny Tara said.

"Oh...faes. I'm a dark fae." Rivera replied, showing her black large wings for a minute before retracting them into her back.

"Cool, very cool. Hope you're ready to fight the battles of this family with us though" Zeke said.

"She's a supportive friend," Soren talked. Granny Tara nodded.

"I'm home!" A voice hollered by the front door, pressing the doorbell repeatedly.

"I recognize that wishy-washy voice" Sadie spranged up, rushing down to throw the door open.

Pearl is indeed there, with Jude's body inside a lidless casket.

She dropped the casket by the door, smiling bright.

"Jude!" Sadie gasped.

The rest of the family rushed to the door except Mercury, and when they saw the body, their eyes popped.

"I get that he was a useless member of my family like his mother, but still...this is much." Zeke said.

"Ow! Father! Your face is baked!" Pearl said mockingly.

Zeke only threw her a thorning glare, and she scoffed.

"Jude..." Soren muttered.

"This is cruel," Rivera commented.

"Who was it? Tell me who?" Granny Tara demanded.

"Shayla, it's her." Pearl said.

The twins exchanged glances.

[Fire Palace]

Shayla's pains went down today.

According to the message she received from the knights, they'd be spending more time in the human realm cos a disturbance came up, so she got herself busy with practices.

Her dark long hair was flowing beautifully with the air like a paid artist as she moved in a clean sway, backwards, counting her steps till she got to the end of that lane and levitated, sending fire to a tall fat mountain.

The mountain obeyed without holding back. It opened up with a scream, pouring out lava before clashing, then it shattered.

Fire splinted everywhere, and she didn't feel like she even did anything as she came down, watching the ruin she made alone.

Her nose caught the smell of a visitor by the gate. She moved there and waved the gate open, stepping out to see Liza with a motherly smile.

She came with Kade, and despite the fact that he's so full of fear for Liza right now, he didn't hide his hate for Shayla. It showed in the way his lips curled, but Shayla barely even noticed him.

"Look at my baby! Look at that! If anyone else gave birth to you in this city, then it'd have been somehow, you only deserve to be my daughter even in the next life." Liza said proudly, moving forward.

Shayla stayed still, but her eyes are on the ground that Liza is stepping.

"I know, right?" Kade said hatefully. "Now that you own your palace, you eliminate people anyhow. You even killed Jude Millions? Praises."

"What do you mean Jude?" Shayla frowned, looking up.

"Don't be shy to admit in front of us, baby. Jude Millions died by your hands, the news is everywhere and the Millions Family are threatening to revenge especially the twins, but who cares? You're above them anyways." Liza replied, still walking closer.

Shayla still didn't move.

"There's only one way to become untouchable, and that's if you kill your partner first before he kills you. Ascend to the nine heavens alone, and you'll get the final powers alone. Have you ever imagined what it's like to be the most powerful being in the world?" Liza continued talking.

"And I'll become the mother of the unstoppable goddess, and you'll—"

The rock they're standing on suddenly broke in the middle, and Liza quickly shifted back, unable to move forward anymore.

"Next time you come here to spill nonsense, I'll burn you to ashes. And about Jude? I still don't know what you're talking about. Scram!" Shayla spat, voice cold and sharp.

She turned back and started walked into the gate.

"Your friend Xena is held captive by the evil twins at the city tower. I guess she's dying today." Liza said before the gate closed behind Shayla.

"Bîtch," Kade muttered.

"Quiet, you don't expect her to give in to us that easily. She's probably still having flashbacks of how I broke her arm as a kid, or how i made her eat blended raw pepper for dinner, or how i removed all her upper teeth and blended them for my pet hens." Liza smiled.

Kade looked at her, smirking at the wickedness of this woman.

"We'll keep coming back till she gets over it. Let's go!" She said, disappearing down the mountain with him.

[City Tower]

As expected, the twins came here to vent on Xena.

Shayla killed Jude, and Xena is Shayla's best friend, so it's balanced.

Rivera came with them, already holding a whip.

Sadie removed her own horsewhip from the wall hanger.

As for Soren, he sat calmly and just watched the show.

Xena started crying again, her period blood smell is so terrible. The whiplashes nearly killed her.

Rivera and Sadie were as cruel as the whips, they were relentless.

Soren held his rabbit in hand, stroking it's fur and watching the beatings unfold.

But it didn't last this time around. There was a disruption.

Shayla landed on top of that tower, and merely seeing Xena's pitiful state, she gasped.

"The heroine is here to save the best friend?" Rivera laughed.

"Get away, her fire is dangerous." Sadie pulled Rivera out of the way.

"Are we just gonna let her take her?" Rivera argued adamantly.

"I said her fire is dangerous! No cure!" Sadie snapped.

"Xena!" Shayla marched forward, but she was blocked by Soren.

She stopped, eyes lifting slowly with rage. Strangely, she feels a darker energy from him as if he's a new person entirely.

"Get away from this tower, you aren't touching her..." He darkly declared.

"Dry joke" Shayla replied, charging forward.

Fearlessly, Soren did the same, and without waiting, Shayla threw her fire forward.

"No! Soren no!!" Sadie screamed behind.

The blaze hit Soren's chest, but swear to the devil... nothing happened to him. He stayed fine.

Shayla's eyes widened for a beat.

It has never happened before.

Why is her fire not working on Soren?

Her state of shock gave Soren a leeway, and he was able to send her a strike which hit her in the face.

She whipped back with uncontrolled roll, but she leapt off the ground, touched the wall with her leg and tumbled swiftly in the air before landing back on the ground.

"Soren is actually... strong?" Rivera muttered shockingly.

"I'm in shock, too." Sadie muttered.

This time, it was Soren who charged forward first.

Shayla pulled out her sword, deciding to not use the fire this time.

She charged, too. Dragging the sword on the ground and sending sparks flying till she got to Soren.

Soren was shocked.

She stabbed him on the arm before levitating across him in the air till she landed beside Xena on the ground.

She swung the sword twice, and Xena's ropes got cut.

The ones on her legs, wrists, and the blindfold on her eyes.

Everything cut off.

Shayla pulled Xena up.

Rivera and Sadie are already running to Soren who's bleeding madly.

Shayla left Xena, determined to get there before the girls.

Her levitation was faster. 

She landed in front of Soren, wanting to stab him in the stomach and end his life completely...

"Stop!!" Xena screamed behind her.

Shayla's eyes snapped, turning back in shock.

Xena stood there, rooted.

"Stop, don't kill him." She repeated.

"They tied you up here, torturing you since days ago, and you're..." Shayla's voice broke with utter disappointment, "Xena you're actually pleading for his life? You would have died if I didn't arrive!"

"Don't touch him," Xena insisted, crying. "If you still consider me a friend and a sister, don't kill him.

By now, Rivera and Sadie has gotten to Soren.

They took him by each side, and Rivera flapped her wings, flying them out of the tower.

Xena swallowed hard, and Shayla turned away from her.

"Shay..." Xena started.

Shayla walked fast to her, held her by the arm and teleported with her, back to her Fire Palace.

She dropped her at her cold pool.

"Bath inside, my aura is in the water, and it'd heal your wounds." She said, still not looking at her.

"Shay, please look at m—"

Shayla already vanished before she could finish those words.

She reappeared at the lumi bridge and pulled a bottle of light alcohol out of her robe.

She was sniffling as she drank in total sadness, wetting her throat as the tears sprang out of her gold eyes.

"Where are you? Ry? Where are you hiding? I need you back right now, it's not even funny. Is it till I run mad?" 

She sniffled again, draining from her small alcohol bottle.

The skies suddenly rumbled, and she looked up. The skies have changed, from blue to ash.

A raindrop touched her arm, and she looked down at the cold dot, sniffling.
