[West Palace]

Enlargement has happened here.

Since the takeover happened yesterday, things have changed and still changing.

The main castle is currently undergoing a widening project, it's becoming bigger.

The dark room auction is tonight, and it's sure they're getting the slave, so they're preparing wads of cash in their home bank.

Also, a meeting is ongoing with the city committees. The city affairs got transferred here, Kasia is handling it.

Astor is in the meeting with her, so just the kids are at the lobby.

Winona's glasses are off, and she's currently cat-walking in style.

"Grand Princess Winona!" Faye hailed.

Winona flipped her hair. "Clock that!"

Then, Faye stood beside her, cat-walking to the right.

"Aw, Grand Princess Faye" Winona winked.

"I mean...yes!" Faye chuckled.

Blaise stood from the couch he's sitting on, whistling with his mouth.

"Grand Prince Blaise," the girls chorused.

"I'm about to burst!" Blaise replied, and they laughed out.

"Royalty is so delicious!" Faye said dramatically.

"I know, right?" Blaise rested his elbow on her shoulder without realizing, and she didn't even shoo him away.

"Grand Prince, Princesses! There's a problem!" About five clansmen rushed inside.

"What problem?" Winona asked, and the problems immediately came in to show themselves.

Zeke, Granny Tara, and Esme.

"Ew, that ugly face. You've gotta be the most grotesque thing I've ever laid eyes on." Faye sneered.

"I knew they wouldn't rest, East Palace people and their annoying consistency." Blaise clicked his tongue.

"I heard you lost Jude to the merciless hands of Shayla, and you're here to what? Fight?" Winona smirked.

"Congratulations on your wedding to Zeke, Esme. Lest I forget." Faye said.

"Are y'all done?" Granny Tara smiled eerily like a tomb breeze.

"Welcome to the Powerful West!" Kasia said, stepping into the lobby.

"We've been expecting y'all" Astor added, emerging behind her.

"Losing powers at midnight means nothing, we have the FrostOrb so it's better you take your leave before we paint the new castle extension with your blood!" Kasia snarled.

"We didn't wait till midnight before coming, and that's because we're going to show you that even with the FrostOrb here, even with your rat-like powers, y'all are nothing. The FrostOrb belongs to the East, and this city equally belongs to us! Today, we take back what's ours." Zeke gnarled.

"This time, we'll even take this West Palace from y'all and chase everyone of you into the cages! We'll control both East and West." Esme declared.

"Come on, fight!" Kasia declared back.

And it started—five against three.

Faye scaled out.

Blaise's hair glowed.

Kasia and Astor stood together, and Winona took position behind them.

A great rain started outside, beating the roof and thrumming the ground, causing a furious flood across the city as the fight started too.

It's Kasia and Astor on Zeke alone.

Esme on Winona.

Faye and Blaise on Granny Tara.

Faye wrapped her tail on Tara so many times, but the old woman kept slipping free.

"I once had a pet snake, so I damn know how to tame serpents like you!" She punched Fate in the throat.

Blaise returned the thick punch to Tara's gut, flashing her with his hair at the same time.

Tara fell back with blinking eyes and loud coughs, and Blaise quickly went to Faye's side.

"You ok?"

The snake nodded, and they faced Tara again.

As for Esme and Winona, Esme is clearly having the upper hand.

As a godslayer who has been practicing the godslaying ability for months, she's good with combats.

Winona's wall-walker ability didn't help much. As she entered, Esme pulled her out.

She has been absorbing powers from the FrostOrb since last night, but Esme is extraordinarily strong.

For the fourth time, Esme kicked Winona to the ground.

Her body broke with the wooden sofa, distributing wood all-over the lobby. and she bled much.

Zeke isn't overpowering Astor and Kasia.

In turn, Kasia and Astor aren't overpowering Zeke, too.

There's no winner, no loser.

Strike collided with strikes, hits with hit, and blasts with retaliating blast.

"You've gotten stronger, Kasia. Congratulations." Zeke smiled through the hot fight.

"Thank you, rotten face!" Kasia blasted him ghastly in the face.

His facial burn opened, creating a foul funky smell.

Zeke was angered, and he shoved her in the face, too—harder that she did, so hard that her life trembled.

Astor saw it coming—he could have stopped it, but he didn't.

Kasia flew to the wall, crashing inside with bloody force.

At the same time, a thunder blasted the skies from the rain, and the FrostOrb room opened upstairs as if listening to an unknown order.

The orb rolled out of the room, and all eyes widened, but Zeke grinned.

"You know your owner, orb. Let's go home." He tried drawing the orb to himself, but the orb didn't listen.

It rolled faster, faster till it got to the railings.

From there, it fell.

Zeke flew there, widening his arms below the railings to take it, but he was late—the orb slipped off, going below his large arms. 

It fell on the ground, shattering to smithereens.

--

[Lumi Bridge]

Shayla still stood there, crying and drinking in the rain.

Rainwater mixed with her alcohol, but she didn't care.

It's thundering hard and angry in the skies, but she didn't care.

Flood is going past her legs, pouring down the bridge, but she didn't care.

Rain thrashed her body till she couldn't feel the thrums anymore, till she started feeling cold despite her warm aura.

She lifted the alcohol to her lips to drink the remaining one, but she lowered it when she felt something beneath her feet.

Her eyes went down slowly, and they widened when she saw what's going on.

Ice.

The flood below her feet—it's freezing, turning to ice.

She's now standing in blue thick ice.

The bridge froze, too, railings freezing like the ground did, but the rain didn't stop.

"Wait for me, under the rain." The words he said on the boat on the feast night echoed in her skull.

The alcohol bottle fell from her, breaking on the icy ground, her face went up desperately fast.

A black umbrella covered her head.

She felt his presence even before she saw his face. It hung thick in the air, ground, all-over the towering atmosphere. The scent of doom and petals, aura so cold that it made her bones shake, countering her warmth.

Her heart responded to it with unhealthy, fast beats, echoing in her ears which are currently getting beaten in the shells by the raindrops.

"Ry," his name echoed from her lips the moment her eyes caught his pointed chin, and that sped everything up till her eyes reached his full face—his eyes, staring down at her. She could see the dark emotions written all-over them.

Her heart just slammed harder, rattling her ribcages.

"Ry" came his name again, as if she couldn't believe what her eyes are seeing.

"Miss me?" He smiled, and when she saw those dimples, she reached for his face, touching them and sniffling.

"Are you... real?"

"Sorry I kept you waiting, Shayla." He muttered, voice as deep as always.

Her insides responded to the deepness, too.

It's indeed him, her partner.

She jumped on him immediately, not sparing any second anymore.

Legs and arms went around him, and he dropped the umbrella, his arms going wrapped around her, too. The small body he missed.

Her face went to the crook of his neck to stay, and her sniffling continued as the rain wet them both.

She didn't say anything, didn't breath anything to his ears. She just stayed there like a baby, as if wanting to eat him, or alternatively enter his skin.

Ryker stroked the back of her neck, his nose resting on her skin too, her warm scent awakening his mind and soul, unravelling his thirst.

He feels alive again, his blood began running in his system, again.

He didn't try making her talk, didn't bother opening her mouth. He knew she needed as much of his presence as possible, so he kept it like that.

He vanished with her.

[West Palace]

The breaking of the orb felt like a dream, surreal.

Everyone is at a standstill now. Even the wounded ones who were on the ground already stood.

Their shocked eyes stared at the shattered orb below the rails.

Zeke looks the most shocked.

He prepared the most for today, and now it's...

"What exactly is going on?" Winona spoke up.

Faye transformed back to her real form nakedly, and Blaise quickly went down beside her, covering her with his large coat.

"Why...did the...why did the orb shatter?" Esme stuttered.

"This probably isn't the real orb! They still have the real one inside!" Granny Tara accused.

"Oh shut up, Tara! You already act brainless, don't make it worse by talking brainlessly too!" Astor attacked her.

Tara's fists curled.

"Feel free to go inside everywhere in this West Palace. Enter the tunnels and wall vents, there's no orb anywhere. The one we stole from you is the broken one, meaning the orb was never real in the first place!" Faye barked from the ground.

"Correct!" Blaise supported.

Kasia chuckled suddenly, facing Zeke who's still at a loss for words.

"Can't talk anymore? You've been drawing power from the fake orb for how many years? 300+?" She laughed loudly, holding her side.

"The more I get to know this demigod, the more impressed I am. Why would women not want him? I mean...he's everything. Why would men not bow? He's everything once again! He fooled the great Zeke Millions." Astor laughed too.

Zeke's eyes are still on the shattered orb, and truly, he looks defeated, looks fooled and deceived.

"Sweetheart, say something.." Esme came to his side.

"Zeke, you can't keep quiet forever!" Tara said by his other side.

Without replying any of them, Zeke stormed out of the palace.

Esme looked at Kasia and Winona before exiting after him.

Tara glanced at Astor and Faye with warring eyes before leaving too.

Kasia fell on the ground voluntarily, gasping out with her bleeding mouth.

"Fake orb? For more than 300 years? How exactly did it happen? All our efforts wasted in vain?" She gulped with shock as she spoke.

"How has the orb been energizing the Millions If it's fake?" Blaise said from the ground.

"Ryker wets it with his blood. His blood has been supplying it with power, I think." Winona said, sitting sprawled on a survivor couch.

"His blood supplied them with energy for that long, now imagine how it's gonna be if it was the real FrostOrb, unlimited power." Astor said calculatedly, exchanging a secret stare with Faye.

"We're missing the real problem here. We have to get the real FrostOrb before the East gets their hands on it." Kasia said.

"It doesn't have to necessarily be the FrostOrb" Astor said craftily.

"What do you mean?" Faye stood from the ground, dragging the coat tighter to herself.

"Shayla. She has a FireStone which holds as much power as the FrostOrb. We either get the FrostOrb, or FireStone." Astor clarified.

"But getting either of them would hurt the owners, aren't we on the demigod's side anymore?" Blaise said.

"Demigod's side?" Astor scoffed. "That bastard will never support anyone that's not himself. Read my lips." 

"Right. From now on, we stop trying to get the demigod's attention cos it's obvious we'll never have it. We'll start fighting for ourselves no matter who gets hurt." Kasia declared.

"Supported, I'll support any motion at all at this rate." Winona said from the couch, giving Astor a suspicious look.

The hit Kasia received from Zeke during the fight, Astor could have counterattacked to stop it, but he didn't, and Winona saw that.

She looked at him again, battling questions in her head.

[Fire Palace]

Shayla was right about the pool water. Xena has been healing since she had her bath inside.

Her body wounds from the whiplashes are gone, but the slave brandings on her forehead didn't heal.

She left the pool after draining the water she used, and surprisingly, Shayla's bedchamber is open. She entered, gasping at how wide, luxurious and rich it is.

She imagined herself with these riches for a second, and she smiled.

Shayla was once a slave who was bought and then became a goddess, so why can't she achieve better too?

She shook her head and entered her closet, gasping again. She covered her mouth.

There are different hangers, wardrobes for different type of outfits. Robes, modern womanly wears, casual, sèxy night-wears and lingeries.

Footwears like slippers, sneakers and heels shoes, each with treasures attached to them. Even the slippers are luxurious.

Then the jewelry glasses. They're insane, and Xena swallowed hard.

She didn't get to enter Shayla's room at all when she was still staying at the Heartless Tower in East Palace. 

Is this how her suite was like back then too?

Did Ryker prepare all of this riches for her before his death?

She swallowed again, picking a soft furry gown to wear.

She quickly left the closet before she'd choke to death with the luxury.

She put on the gown and sat in front of Shayla's bedroom mirror, checking herself out as she brushed her hair after using some of the hair supplements.

She smiled before she knew it when her mind remembered a flashback.

            [Weeks Ago, City Tower]

It's the day Xena was captured by the evil twins —the Feast day.

They brought her here, tied up and bonded. She was dumped by the wall side, and Sadie was determined to kill her that night.

"Her brother killed mother, it's only right if she goes to hell too. Soren there's machete in the storeroom, right? Get it for me, I'm going to butcher her and cook her flesh, I swear to Satan, I'll eat her flesh!"

Sadie started going down the tower, obviously to go get the cutlass, but Soren blocked her.

"What? Don't tell me you're going to start defending her again! Apart from mother's death, you saw what Shayla did to father's face! Father might be a scum for chosing Esme, but he's still father!" Sadie glared.

"Killing her won't be entertaining enough," Soren sighed, backing the wall nonchalantly.

"What do you mean?" Sadie frowned.

"Let's keep her here, and torture her till she actually dies when she won't be able to take it anymore." Soren explained.

                  [Present Time]

Xena smiled at her reflection in front of the mirror.

The slave brandings on her forehead means nothing to her right now.

What matters is that—the torture was for her own good. Sadie would have killed her, but Soren stopped her by suggesting the torture which is why she's still alive till now.

Soren is still redeemable and soft. If she gets to spend more time with him, she'd change him.

Besides, he has potential cos it seems Shayla's fire didn't work on him.

She smiled again, rising from the vanity chair.

Luckily, she found a mobile on the foot of the bed which she took.

Knowing her father's line offhand saved her. She dialled him, twice, but he didn't pick.

If he was ok, he'd have been searching for her and would have probably found her at the city tower no matter what cos of their daughter-father bond.

The fact that she's not sensing him anywhere only means something bad happened to him.

She positioned the phone between her fingers again, typing...

"Pa, please be fine. Where are you?"

Sending that, she left the room to go find food wherever it is in that palace

[Human Realm]

Cheyenne and Sage are still on the run, chasing father and daughter with no win.

When they arrived at the overpass, Cheyenne sensed them ahead.

They chased further, but they lost sense of the hairpin scent.

Rumi must have noticed the chase, cos by the time they arrived at the end of the road, no Rumi, no Anatoli.

Cheyenne and Sage got out of the car, both panting.

"They can't be this fast without any help. This is human realm, not Luminaria where they know everything to do." Cheyenne said.

"You mean Caspian is helping them?" Safe faced her.

"It's just what I feel. Might be true, mightn't be." Cheyenne backed the car door, her eyes catching the city billboard.

Caspian Yusluv is there, modelling for his own company—COLD-HEARTED—that's the name of his fucked up company.

"First time I'm seeing that dickface on a billboard, he's probably dancing around his castle right now." Cheyenne muttered.

Caspian didn't spend one minute on the billboard before Ryker's face took over. 

He's not even modelling, he just leaned on an elevator wall in the picture, wearing a red suit and dangling male earrings.

BACK AGAIN was sprinkled on top, and it never left the billboard.

Cheyenne and Sage exchanged knowing glances immediately.

"He's back!" They chorused.

They nearly got into the car, but they stopped themselves, encapsulating their excitement.

"No. We have to find father and daughter first and console the families of the dead securities." Cheyenne said.

"Right, but boss..." Sage groaned.

"Get in the car, don't be a whinny kid!" Cheyenne scolded, and he immediately slipped inside the car.

--

Truly, father and daughter had help, and that's why they didn't get caught.

They were running on the tar when a car suddenly crossed them.

A powerful-looking man entered the car with them, and based on what they've been hearing about him, they couldn't possibly turn him down.

They'd surely be needing him.

Rumi covered her hair with a cap already since the hairpin scent is coming from there.

They're out of the express now, and they're going on with the direction the stranger is providing.

He sat at the back of the car, and Rumi stole glances at him seldomly.

He's too much. 

Rings on all ten fingers, multiple hoop earrings on both ears, he even pierced his lips, nose , and the top of his brows.

His tattoos are madly dark.

His eyes are heterochromic, and his presence filled up the car.

"You have something to say to me, pretty girl?" He talked, referring to Rumi when their eyes met through the front mirror.

"You're Caspian Yusluv, the popular competitor." Rumi said.

"Next time, don't use the word competitor. I'm here to take Ryker's place, you could call me the right one." Caspian replied arrogantly from the back, voice sinful.

"And I know you both..." He continued, leaning forward suddenly.

"Anatoli Morozov, and Rumi Morozov. There's a twin at Luminaria, Xena Morozov."

Anatoli stopped the car immediately, and they both looked back at the man who's now laughing dangerously, his nose ring glittering.

"You know Luminaria? You're not human too?" Anatoli asked.

"I'm human, just a different kind of human who knows everything he's supposed to know. I have spies in Luminaria though." Caspian laughed again, touching Rumi's cheek.

"But you...you're hot as fûck" he bit his lip.

Rumi slapped his hand away, and his laughter stopped.

"Careful, you don't slap my skin like that, doll." He muttered.

"How come you have spies in Luminaria?" Anatoli asked.

"You Luminarians travel to the human realm using the descension ladder. Eligible humans can also travel there using the ascension ladder, you didn't know?" Caspian gasped dramatically.

"But only few special humans like me know about it." He continued.

She wanted us to find you and Rumi for her, also provide for you and your family, and in return, she gives us everything she knows about Ryker," Zeke said.

Anatoli smiled amusingly first, shoving his face down. 

This daughter of him might be useful than he imagined.

She actually stole her way into the Millions Family?

He smiled again, looking up.

"Who am I to say no?"

[Blossoms Temple]

Granny Jojo and Lilith could already hear the chanting of 'Amitabh' from priests and monks as they came down from the car they came in.

The temple is the oldest and farthest in the city backwaters, and if they'd get anything at all, it'd be here.

Lilith was holding Jojo's bag as they stared at the stony cemented steps leading up to the mountaintop temple, and Lilith is already tired even without climbing.

"Let's go," Jojo told her.

"Yes, ma'am." Lilith sighed, following her lead.

It was more hectic and tiring than she expected. By the time they got to the top ten minutes later, they were both out of breaths, and sweaty.

Lilith sat on top of a rock, dabbing her sweaty face with an hanky.

Jojo left her there and went inside the prehistoric temple. She hasn't even stepped in fully when one of the monks blocked her.

"Are you here for the godslayer sword investigation?" He asked.

"How did you..." Jojo frowned.

"There are legends, granny. There are prophecies, and today is destined to happen." The monk smiled.

Lilith joined them.

"Come with me," the monk further said, leading them.

[West Palace]

Rumi didn't leave earlier.

After she made the investigation request, she was told by father and daughter to wait while they compiled everything they already have in their hands.

Within some minutes, the book was ready. Faye threw it down in front of Rumi, and Rumi scoffed.

"I asked for investigation, not some project. Besides, why did your investigation take just some minutes as if you've been waiting for me?" 

"You don't expect us to tell you the story with our mouths, did you? And you're right, we've really been waiting for you to come." Astor replied, and that was when Rumi figured out how serious this is.

Still looking at them, she took the notebook and flipped it open, reading through as if fire lived in the pages.

It was the longest one hour of her life. She read attentively for one full hour, neither blinking nor showing any emotion throughout.

Astor and watched her silently till she dropped the book back on the table.

She stood and started walking out of the room, looking drained like she has just been rescued from drowning.

Her arms dropped by her sides, head down, eyes forward, and shoulders saggy. She looks destroyed.

"You're not doubting if what we gave you is true or not?" Faye asked.

"The ink in the notebook didn't look like it was written today, it looked at least a hundred years old, I'm smart enough to tell real from fake." Rumi replied without looking back.

She went downstairs, and as expected, the rest of the family were already waiting for her.

"You promised to tell us about the orb, remember?" Winona said.

Rumi cast the three a short look, going from her to Kasia to Blaise.

Then she went closer to Kasia, whispering things in her ear before leaving the palace.

"Mother, what did she say!" Blaise asked immediately.

"Mother? Spill it!" Winona said.

Kasia was still looking speechless when Faye and Astor came out.

They look like they're in a hurry.

"Astor, where to? Faye?" Kasia asked.

"We'll be right back!" Faye replied, rushing out with her father.

Winona grabbed her glasses, and before anyone could stop her, she was out of the palace too.

"What exactly did Xena tell you?" Blaise asked Kasia, but Kasia just went inside.

"Mother!"

[Rumi's House]

Rumi called Anatoli on her way home, so she expected to meet him here already, and he's indeed here...on the chair, eating from a bowl of lumi fries.

"See? I told you your twin was fine. She's getting married to Soren, and we just finished signing the deal. Your twin found her way into the Millions Family, isn't that great? But too bad it won't last, yunno what I mean..." He stood from the chair, bringing the bowl of fries to her where she stood.

"Here, where were you?" He asked with his usual deceptive smile.

"You intentionally slapped Azura's arse that night cos you wanted to use her to get into the Millions Family to get the sword! Mother would still be alive today if you didn't harbour this greed for power! It was all your doing..." She said calmly.

With a brow rumple, Anatoli took a step back and set the bowl of fries on the table.

"Rumi, what are you..."

"Mother's death was never what you made me believe it was! You planned everything! You're the one who told the motherless camp manager to come get me during mother's labour! You knew about the legend that says whoever kills the god with the sword and steals the orb gets half of the god's power! It has all been you!!!" Rumi slapped him in the face.

She's already crying.

"All my life, I spent hating my brother, thinking he was the cause of our misery, but it's actually you!!!"

Anatoli laughed at her face, suddenly grabbing her shoulder to hold.

"You should have kept shut when you found out, cos now you're going to regret it. I've always known you're a smart bîtch who's not so easy to play like her twin, but you shouldn't have gotten smart with me, cos now you pay for it." He threatened.

Rumi shook his hand off with a bite and tried to cast a spell on him to bleed, but nothing happened.

"Fûck you!!" She screamed, going for the door.

The door closed violently, obeying a spell from Anatoli.

She looked back, eyes too shocked. Too terrified.

Anatoli just cast a spell!

"I am a Siphon, I siphon abilities for a living. I have no power of my own, but I've gathered enough powers, enough for me to dominate, your spell casting ability and Xena's animal ability included," Anatoli smiled, and her chest squeezed.

She started trying to open the door, frantically and fruitlessly till Anatoli dragged her back inside.

No amount of her coke energy could help, she's too weak for him without her ability, so when he threw her in bed and kissed her roughly, she could only shed bitter tears.

"I am going to have you, then kill you," Anatoli said grossly, tearing her blouse away.

She was about to get rid of her underwear too when the door bursted open.

Astor entered with Faye who's in her snake form.

With her mouth, Faye pulled Rumi out from under Abatoli. She threw the half-naked girl on her back and crawled out of the room through the weak wall.

The wall broke and crumbled on top of them as she walked into it, and Rumi had to slip to the side of the snake to avoid getting crushed.

She quickly held tight to the snake's body, and Faye leapt to the sky, levitating away with her.

Now it's just Anatoli and Astor inside the room, but obviously, Anatoli isn't ready for this.

He stepped back.

"I'll see you when I see you, Astor Viper," he smiled, summoning butterflies to cover Astor's view.

By the time the butterflies cleared off, he was already gone.

Astor cursed under his heavy breaths, then he felt someone coming in behind him.

Even without turning, he knew who it was, so he smiled before backing the wall, finally facing her.

Winona.

"Astor Viper?" Winona removed her glasses, shocked to the bones.

"Too bad, I liked you as a stepdaughter, Winona. If you hadn't followed me, your days would have been longer by some days." Astor sighed casually.

"You're from the Viper family," Winona muttered as the pangs of betrayal settled deep, burning.

She crushed her glasses in her palm.

"Why?" She crushed the rims too. "Why!!!"

"Cos your family shouldn't even exist! Y'all are bunch of cruel peeps who don't deserve air!!! Your bloodline should be exterminated, and that is going to start from you. Blame yourself for making it this fast!" Astor shouted, taking one of Rumi's heels from the shoe rack.

He tossed it into the air, targeting her throat for death.

Smartly, Winona tumbled.

She was able to avoid it, but she wasn't very fast.

The thin heel stabbed her in the throat, cutting her windpipe and letting out hot blood.

As she crumbled on her knees, holding the bleeding neck, she suddenly remembered she has lived once as Pearl's personal maid.

She remembered this is her second life, and now... she's...

Tears rode out of her two eyes before she fell on her side, bleeding away her remaining life as it fell out of her.

Astor knelt beside her and covered her eyes with the kids, smiling.

"Rest in peace, stepdaughter,"

He extracted her core and ate it right there. No remorse. Just satisfaction.

[Fire Palace]

After the happenings in Russia, Ryker didn't even wait for the next meeting, he returned straight to Luminaria.

He hasn't said a word to Shayla throughout the comeback journey, and Shayla herself just stayed between the knights, looking like she has been robbed of her vocal cords.

He looks angry, like he doesn't want to talk, like everything is making him mad. The air, the heat, the atmosphere, even the ground and himself. Everything.

It got worse when they arrived at the front gate and met Liza waiting.

Kade left already cos he was tired of waiting, but her adamant self was determined to see this to the end.

She stood from the rock she sat on, facing the coming people.

"You look good as usual, daughter." She smiled, giving the knights a disapproving look before settling his gaze on Ryker whose eyes are burning presently.

She said nothing to him—she wouldn't dare.

She faced Shayla again.

"Why are you here?" Shayla asked, the anger in her voice messing up her tone. She sounds shaky.

"To see you, what else?" Liza replied boldly like she rules this street.

In the next minute, she was in Ryker's choky grasp.

Ryker vanished with her into the palace, and Shayla followed.

The knights were last

On getting inside, Liza was already bleeding.

Ryker actually grinded her face on the stone pieces. Her brows peeled off, and the nose is in half.

Her blood stained his face in dot splashes, and he's about to throw her into the lava and volcano between the black rocks. 

"No!" Shayla cautioned.

"Then kill her yourself," Ryker looked up, his sour expression not giving space for turndown.

He looks ready to go to hell.

"She's the villain of my youngest days, and I hate her, but I've been hiding my hate so well cos the moment I let it out, I knew I'd destroy her. I'm not like her, and I don't want to kill her." Shayla said.

"You're still being this soft for a wench who did series of unforgettable things to you? Are you kidding me?" Ryker sneered.

"It's not being soft, I just know my limits. I don't want to kill her, just let her go." Shayla replied.

"And that's my daughter," Liza laughed victoriously from the ground, her blood sweet on her tongue as it entered her mouth.

"You want me to let her go because you actually still care for Kade, and you don't want him to become motherless, isn't it?" Ryker smirked, his anger mounting.

Shayla went silent. He got her.

"Same guy who hates you more than anyone in this city for sharing a mother with him? You can't remain this terribly soft when you live in a world where everyone wants your powers! Softness is being thoughtless! Kill! Shed blood! Have no mercy! Be unapologetically crazy! Do it and don't look back! Stay unremorseful! Do Your Worse!" Ryker yelled angrily.

Liza smiled disgustingly again, liking that this is fruiting the right way. 

It's causing a misunderstanding like she wanted, and soon, things would clear up in her favor.

"I'm not you, Ry. You're the bloodthirsty one, and I didn't for once try to change you. Don't try to change me now and just tell me what exactly is going on cos I know you're angry for another reason, you're just using Liza to vent it out!" Shayla replied, voice already shaking.

Somehow, she feels nauseous, and her eyes feels like they're turning, but she contained it before talking gently.

"And stop yelling at me, it hurts when you do that..." 

"Since you're not ready to do it, I'll help you out," Ryker lifted Liza

The brazen woman gasped when her body flew towards the volcanic rocks.

That was when she realized this is going beyond her control.

"Shayla do something! Shayla! Shayla you can't let him... Shayla!!!"

"Ry... don't." Shayla attacked, but her strike wasn't as strong cos of her lingering nauseousness.

Her eyes widened.

"Don't do it! Don't d—"

But Liza's body already landed in the volcano, melting out of existence.

Shayla held the rock beside her, gasping repeatedly as her eyes clashed with Ryker's darkly unapologetic ones.

[Morozov Household]

The house has been empty since Dominika's death years ago, so only the sounds of Anatoli's metal digger as he digged the bedroom ground filled the house in echoes.

Since Caspian mentioned the exhumation, he hasn't stopped thinking about it.

If he could really exhume Dominika's body and crush her skeletal bones with his tea, it's all he needs to hone his powers. 

At least before Soren's birthday.

After about fifty digging process, his digger met the coffin lid, and he smiled like he had just conquered the world before dropping the digger.

Sweat fell from his brows as he dug the remaining earth with his fingers, unrelenting till he got all sand away.

The coffin is destroyed as expected of a 350 year old stuff, but no matter how, there should still be some crumbs of her bones inside.

He lifted the tattered termite-eaten lid of the coffin, expecting to meet some bones, but he met nothing.

No bones. No crumbs.

It's not even smelling.

He raised the coffin completely, searching the base for any fallouts. 

There's still nothing.

His heart hammered in his chest.

His phone rang, and he jumped at the suddenness before pulling it out with his soiled hand.

It's 'Master' calling.

"Where you at?" The question came unexpectedly.

"At my dead wife's grave. I heard about exhumation and wanted to try it, but couldn't find her bones." Anatoli replied breathlessly.

"Maybe you just need to try harder? It's been 350 years. Try harder, Anatoli." Master replied, hanging up.

"Why would she call by this time, and she called me by my name? Weird," Anatoli sighed, returning to his search.

Outside the house, the figure in red cloak stepped out of the broken pavement, into the dark night.

She pushed a phone bank into her cloak pocket, each step heavier than the last one.

She lifted her cloak away from her head, suspending her red veil from her face too.

She looks old, but at the same time young, like she has been in a battle with age for years.

Dominika Morozov, the mother.

Her cloak was flowing with the night breeze as she levitated, traveling with the air.

She didn't take long, didn't branch anywhere till she reached the underground palace where she perched majestically on the roof.

She broke the rocky roof to create a passage, and she jumped inside the palace from there.

As expected, Pearl was waiting like a predator with her arrow.

She released four at the same time, but Dominika slammed herself on the wall, allowing the arrows to fly past with dangerous whistling sounds before going for Pearl again.

But Pearl wasn't done. 

She pulled four arrows again, releasing them faster than the first batch.

"Who the hell are you? Barging into my palace? Rude!" 

Dominika avoided the four arrows, skidding and bending like they're ragdoll toys before.

Heroically, she caught one of the arrows before landing in front of Pearl, her footwear crushing the ground stones.

"I see you've got nerves!" Pearl mocked, using her bow to beat Dominika's face.

Another whistling sound stirred the air, but the woman didn't even move, didn't shake.

She targeted Pearl's stomach and stabbed it with the arrow she caught.

Pearl's scream was painfully piercing as Dominika twisted the arrow, deeply inside till it reached her womb.

Pearl fell back, leaning on her throne. Her eyes reddened, shooting up to the strange woman in front of her.

"Who....are...you...aghhghhhhh!!!!!!!"

"Godslayers like Esme who only cultivated the ability won't be able to do you a thing without the sword, but a slayer like me who was born with the ability...I can make you sleep for years without the sword, so you better answer the questions truthfully!" Dominika threatened.

Pearl swallowed repeatedly, hot bitter saliva.

"You'll never know the pain of a mother who was created to kill her own son, but at the same time she's doing anything and everything to protect him! I'll do whatever it takes to move evils like you out of his way, so give me answers!" Dominika continued with unpracticed fierceness, picking another arrow.

She used the sharp tip to raise Pearl's chin up, slowly but with enough precision that could kill.

Pearl froze, womb and stomach bleeding to her legs.

For the first time since she lived, her body trembled without rhythm.

"First question; who owns your wretched pregnancy? Ryker?" Dominika asked dominantly, her blue eyes madly mean.

Pearl hesitated, and Dominika shifted the arrow to her throat...

"I'm not pregnant!!! It was all my plan!!!! I'm not carrying a baby!!! I'm not pregnant!!!" Pearl confessed.

[Cold-hearted Harem]

Rumi and Anatoli had their seats beside the pool, clinked their glasses and shared another smile before drinking, trying to pretend to focus on the party and at the same time waiting for when the two intruders would get closer.

The champagne in their glasses refused to shake as their bodies stilled at the same time, and that was enough to alert the knights.

They're aware of being seen.

They took two steps back, and Rumi instantly stood with Anatoli.

Rumi cast a spell to stop every of the other guests from moving, and they all went still like the champagne, so they now have time for the knights.

Cheyenne took off her hat, and Sage shifted his hat back to the middle of his head, revealing his face too.

"It'd always be you guys, Ryker's hunter and huntress," Anatoli sneered darkly.

"Sneaking in? Height of foolishness." Rumi smiled, wrapping a chiffon on her waist to cover up.

"Since when?" Cheyenne smiled back, but there's enough venom to lace a dozen bullet under that smile.

"The same father who's the Genesis of the problems?" Sage nodded. "I'm convinced that you and Xena share a brain, half and half."

"You have a pretty face, too bad your mouth rivals a sewage stink!" Rumi laughed.

"Listen, daftie! We came here to teach you both a lesson for what you did, but here's an advice for you—free of charge. Run from your animal of a father, or your untimely death will come directly from him!" Cheyenne slammed angrily.

"No, don't run," Sage chuckled. "I'd love to see how the corpse of a daughter killed by her father looks."

"Done with the lectures?" Anatoli spoke up.

"Shut up, old wuss!" Cheyenne bared her long nails.

Sage suspended his black rope from his wrist.

Together, they lunged father and daughter.

Rumi's spell failed. She suddenly can't cast another one on them cos the people she spelled to stay still are still there, so she hid behind her father.

Anatoli cracked the wall beside the knights, crashing a dust concrete down on them, but they skillfully rolled out of it, and Cheyenne's nails found his bare legs. 

She tore him in five lines, representing all of her cruel nails.

Anatoli couldn't do anything but scream, shaking the silent neighborhood.

Unable to watch her father getting overpowered, Rumi kicked Cheyenne in the face, and the latter rolled back, but Sage didn't let her go for that.

He wrapped his rope on her neck from behind, strangling her.

Rumi gasped for breaths, writhing and shrinking to her knees.

Anatoli picked one of the round fallen concretes, replicating it to five just like he did the hairpin.

The five went to Sage, but before it could clash with him in the face, Cheyenne was already there.

She punched three away with her fists, and Sage kicked two.

Together, they blew father and daughter twice in their faces.

Rumi fainted flat, but Anatoli is strong as fûck. He successfully gave Sage a gash on the shoulder.

"Let's go!" Cheyenne dragged Sage, both levitating to the wired fence.

Anatoli ran after them, but they vanished from there.

Taking a breath, Anatoli fell on the ground, blood pouring from his face from the punch he received.

His jaw got dislocated, so his mouth isn't straight. Also, his nose is bended.

His chin has a large wound, too, his torn leg is still bleeding.

He looked over at Rumi on the ground. She's the same.

Dislocated jaws and wounded cheeks, bleeding mouth. There's a round wound on her neck from Sage's rope.

Anatoli crawled there, and instead of finding a way to tend to both of them,  his eyes fell on her exposed bóobs top

He was already bringing his hand to it when he heard someone cough, and the hand stopped halfway.

His face went up, and Caspian entered with a delighted smile.

He squatted in front of him.

"Yunno, I'm a man-slut or the debauchery pig like many would say, but lusting on my own daughter? That's a little too low, don't you think?" He whispered.

"It's not... what...you...think" Anatoli struggled to talk with his bended mouth as he sat on the ground.

"But..." Caspian leaned to him, eyes a refuge of evilness. "You're like my business partner, so your secret is safe." 

Anatoli looked away.

"But your face is a total mess, you got beaten by the knights?" He laughed.

"You knew the knights entered?" Anatoli squinted.

"The invited guests list shot up by two. Who else would be bold enough to sneak into my harem if not his knights? I deliberately let it slide cos I wanted to see what you and Rumi would do, but I'm disappointed." Caspian's smile disappeared, replaced with a hard acidic glare.

"You came here to snatch his wealth with your mediocre powers? You couldn't even defeat his knights, but you're targeting him? Are you shitting me? You've been planning this for how many years? And yet, you're still as useless as ashtray in a damn car!"

"Rumi believes I'm not very strong, I have to keep it that way." Anatoli replied, standing up.

He spat blood on the fallen bricks.

"You're only pretending to be a weak-ass dude cos of your daughter?" Caspian touched his shoulder.

"Good! Very good!" 

"You have a runny mouth, don't go and mistakenly say it. Rumi is smart and suspicious." Anatoli whispered.

"Rest assured," Caspian closed his eyes and opened it... "I'm no idiot."

Rumi coughed back to life on the ground, and the two men shifted their eyes to her.

She groaned, punching herself in the mouth to reset her jaw.

"The beauty is finally awake, can you at least remove the spell you put on the guests?" Caspian spoke.

"What about the broken walls? Won't they get suspicious?" Anatoli asked.

"Suspicious in my house?" Caspian scoffed. "It's part of the fun. Rumi, Remove the spell."

Rumi removed the stilling spell, and the guests began moving, resuming what they were doing.

They didn't even notice the broken walls in the hall.

"I'll have the home manager get someone to plaster the walls back," Caspian said, pulling a cigar to put light on.

"I'll be in the room. We got beaten so bad that I don't even wanna look you in the face right now, Pa." Rumi held her side rib as she walked away.

"It's worth the shame, girl. I understand you!" Caspian shouted.

"Stop it," Anatoli muttered, and Caspian laughed again.

He guided Anatoli to his home office since the old man can't walk by himself.

Cheyenne's nails marks on his legs is trying to really cripple him.

Caspian set him on the soft couch in the spacious tobacco-reeking office.

He got them a bottle of spirit which he popped with dramatic loudness.

"My doctor should be here anytime soon, you'll get treated." 

"You know human doctors can't do anything about wounds caused by Luminarians. I can heal myself,but Rumi isn't aware I have healing ability, so...I have to keep it bleeding till we get back to Luminaria. I'll pay a healer alchemist to heal us." Anatoli explained.

"Oh..." Caspian pushed one of the spirit bottle to him, grinning as he drank from his own.

"I get that, but tell me...when did all of your plans start? A storytime?" He shrugged, casually strolling around the old man's chair while swirling the cup of spirit he's holding.

"You're not normal," Anatoli looked up swiftly.

"I'm far from normal, dawg." Caspian stated frankly.

"It started when Ryker's pregnancy turned seven months," Anatoli said.

                   [350 Yrs Ago]

Dominika is now seven months gone, meaning in two to three months, she'd be giving birth to the bloodsucker child, but the surprising thing is that immediately the pregnancy clocked six months yesterday, she stopped trying to trying to kill herself.

She's calmer than usual, and she has been trying her best to not drink directly from the blood bags. 

She has been on blood transfusion without saying a word to her husband.

At first, Anatoli wondered why, but he later came to a conclusion; she got tired and decided to accept her fate.

That must be it.

Dominika just finished taking her second blood bag for today, and she had fallen asleep, so Anatoli took the twins to the top pavement to play with them and spend time.

Xena got herself busy with the popcorn bag, but Rumi didn't seem interested. She just sat beside her father, placing an head on his lap.

"Why does mother dislike the child in her belly?" She suddenly asked, her voice too rigid for a three year old.

"Because..." Xena drawled from the other side with her mouthful of popcorn.

"Because?" Anatoli faced her. Rumi lifted her head and faced her, too.

"Because... He's...bad. A bloodsucker." Xena replied, her voice nothing close to sharp. She's stuttering a little.

"But... teacher at daycare said children are blessings, just like us." Rumi said.

"Children are blessings, but that one in your mother's womb is a curse, a bloodthirsty one who makes your blood run dry." Anatoli said.

His phone buzzed.

Seeing it's a new line, he left the kids at the concrete, going to the staircase to pick up.

"Yes?"

"Your wife is carrying a demigod, and you're probably planning to kill your wife after she gives birth, cos you want to steal the child's powers in the future?" An unknown voice dropped.

Anatoli went lower on the staircase, his eyes dilating.

"What do you mean? Who are you? How did you —"

"I'm an helper, I wouldn't watch you kill yourself." The voice interrupted.

"Tell me who you are!" Anatoli demanded with desperacy.

"You won't be able to do anything without the godslayer sword, and to get it, you'll have to find your way into the Millions Family." The voice said, hanging up.

"Godsla—hello? Hello?" Anatoli repeated restlessly.

                   [Present Time]

"That was the first time we communicated, and since then, she has been my Master in the shadows, always helping." Anatoli said.

"And you haven't seen her?" Caspian demanded suspiciously, tapping his index on his glass rim.

"We'll only meet when I get the sword, we had a deal. She's a godslayer, and only a godslayer can wield the sword, so I'd be needing her help by then." Anatoli replied.

"A godslayer? You mean Esme? I know that whore." Caspian inquired.

"No. There are two. Esme is one, my hidden Master is the other." Anatoli said confidently.

"Cool. So...you found the sword yet?" Caspian sat in front of him.

"I found out the location already, but a little more time before it'd be eligible for me to get." Anatoli smiled.

"Isn't there a legend that whoever slays the god with the godslaying sword gets half of the god's powers? And if you're lucky to get the FrostOrb too, you get fully empowered as a demon," Caspian pulled his frame closer to whisper...

"The Bloody Demon," 

"Why else do you think I'm struggling so hard? Of course I want the power," Anatoli replied, staring down at his bleeding leg.

Caspian smiled, taking a sip.

That's never happening.

Not when he's here as the original demigod.

Anatoli has no idea about that fact, so he's just gonna use this old man.

"Ever heard of exhumation ritual?" He said, capturing his attention.

"Exhumation?" Anatoli blinked.

"Your wife is the only dead member of your family, was she burnt or buried?" Caspian asked.

"I always told people she was burnt, but she was actually buried in our former household, under the bed she birthed Ryker on." Anatoli replied.

"Good. When you get back to your city, go exhume her skeletal bones, grind them to dust and take it with your tea. She's the demigod's mother, her bones still carries powers you could use against him." Caspian said.

"Seriously?" Anatoli gasped.

[Rumi's Room]

Realizing that the wounds on her body would not heal unless they get back to Luminaria, Rumi just let them bleed away.

She's sitting at the feet of her bed, her mind bouncing back and forth with what she heard from Anatoli earlier...

"Rumi believes I'm not very strong, I have to keep it that way."

She was actually already awake when Anatoli said that, she only just lay there to hear more.

Now why would her father pretend to be weak for her?

Why??

She walked to the window, leaning there for a while.

Sensing she needed ginger, she got herself a small coke wrap and put some powder on her palm, sniffling it all at a go.

It hit the spot, right there.

Her head rang, loud and crazy. Shinny tears ran down her eyes, then she remembered Sage's words too...

"The same father who's the Genesis of the problems?" 

How?

She slammed her own head on the window, wounding herself severely more to calm down.

Also, why is her spell casting ability shaking? Earlier, she couldn't cast more than one spell. 

What's going on?

[Glass House]

Faye brought Rumi here, and Astor joined them after a while.

The three have been here, silently.

Rumi just sat on the bare ground of the room they're in, and as she stared into space with traumatized eyes, Faye and Astor studied her.

"It's worth getting traumatized, I understand." Astor nodded.

"You killed Winona?" Faye muttered.

"Yeah. Faster than I thought, but it only hastened things up, and I like it." Astor shrugged.

"Now you only have Kasia to take care of, and I'll take care of Blaise." Faye replied.

"Are you sure you can really take care of Blaise?" Astor muttered.

"What do you mean?" Faye faced him with a scowl.

"I see the way you've been relaxing around that redhead." Astor replied.

"You know nothing, father." Faye said, avoiding his gaze.

"Why did you two save me?" The expected question finally came from Rumi, and they both faced the girl on the ground.

"Once, I was a poor farmer, and I had a wife who was pregnant with Faye. One night, my wife and I were on our way back from our apricot plantation  when i fainted cos of my chronic chest pain. My wife was running to the city to get help when her water broke. She gave birth on the way, and unluckily, she was on the Spectres farmland. They got angry and killed her, also proceeded to kill every member of our household, all because my wife birthed on their land." Astor narrated.

"That didn't answer my question," Rumi stood from the ground.

"It turned out that my wife didn't give birth alone that day. She had help from a woman called Dominika. The woman was her way into the woods when she saw my wife labouring, and she helped, but when the Spectres were approaching, my wife begged the woman to take the baby far away, and she did that."

"When I woke up from my deep fainting, my peaceful household was gone, and I wanted to die, but my hope was rekindled when Dominika found me and handed baby Faye to me. Since then, I have pledged the life of I and my daughter to her." Astor said, finishing the narration.

"Dominika? There's only one Dominika in Luminaria. My mother, and she's dead! She was killed by Azura and Zeke after she gave birth to Ryker, and father buried her in our household by himself." Rumi argued.

"Born godslayers never die unless they kill the gods they were destined to kill. Your mother's body died that day, but her soul simply entered another body. She's alive." Faye said.

"And that's right," Dominika replied from the door, entering.

With quickness, Rumi turned, and her eyes widened so much when she saw the tall giant woman, clad in red.

Her legs soundlessly took her to the woman. She staggered several times before she arrived.

She's already shedding tears, again.

"You're...you're mother?"

"I came from an ordinary family, but I was shocked when I found out I was actually a godslayer. I mean...slayers are hella powerful, the only creatures with the ability to kill gods." Dominika smiled, starting her story.

"But then I got pregnant with my third child. It was simple like a normal pregnancy during the first month, but at the second month, things changed. I guess that was when the mantle entered the foetus's body. I started craving blood, and I started growing the urge to kill my own baby. I tried suicide multiple times, but it was futile, but when the pregnancy clocked seven months, I learnt that I was carrying a god in my tummy. I'm a godslayer, carrying a god? Meaning I was destined to kill my own baby? What could be crueller than that?"

"That realization made me calm. I started thinking of ways to help myself out. Instead of thinking of suicide, I bit myself like a beast, cos I didn't want to hurt my only male child. I fought my slaying urges boldly, then I got an additional ability—the ability to hear thoughts and read minds. I could hear thoughts of people, and that included your father. I read in his mind that after I gave birth, he was going to kill me and steal my baby's powers, so I used that greed against him. I became his Master on call everyday."

"I formed a fake belief that whoever kills the god with the godslaying sword and takes his FrostOrb would have the gods powers and become a Bloody Demon. With that believe, I sent him to the Millions Family cos I knew the sword was there, and it's the only thing that'd kill my baby. My plan is that once he gets the sword and hands it over, I'll destroy it and die with it to protect my baby Ryker, but I never expected things to go so bad to the point of ruining our family." 

"So...the theory about the FrostOrb empowering people is also fake?" Rumi asked speechlessly.

"I formed every fake theory. The FrostOrb or FireStone belong to the gods alone, and it won't empower anyone who's not a god. The only beneficial thing about them is their blood. Unless you can catch a god and drain their blood to drink, you can't have any share from their powers." Dominika smiled.

"Damn, I love you!" Rumi ran into her arms, hugging her very tight.

"I love you so much, mother."

"I was always watching you from the shadows when you were still at the motherless camp. I made you realize your spell casting ability that day when the bullies wanted to take your bread pieces." Dominika said.

"It was you..." Rumi broke the hug to look at her face.

Dominika nodded.

"And when Anatoli came to get you, I didn't fight it cos I wanted you to see for yourself who exactly he was, which has already happened now." 

"But Xena..." Rumi said.

"I'll get Xena back in my own way," Dominika assured.

"And Ryker. Will he ever forgive me for hating him for years? Will you ever forgive me for being a druggie? I did coke, molly, ecstasy and everything hard." Rumi sniffled, and Dominika smiled, holding her face.

"It's all my fault, and about Ryker...I'm going to get the sword first before we talk about him. He's the legend we have to settle last." 

"I understand," Rumi nodded, hugging her again.

"Father siphoned my ability, and I think he took Xena's ability too."

"Your abilities will come back to you both when Anatoli dies, I promise." Dominika assured.

"That was a long reunion," Faye commented, and Faye chuckled.

Rumi hugged Dominika tighter.

--

[Clouds Castle]

Ryker barged in, and Eternus stood from the high chair he was sitting on in his vine room.

"Finally remembered your old acquaintance?" He grinned, but the grin was short cos Ryker pulled his hair, and dragged him to the wall.

"Ryker you can't always solve everything with violence and your hotheaded stubbornness! Your anger issue would solve nothing! You can't always win so let me go! You killed my original body, you can't kill this one too!" Eternus spat.

Ryker still jammed his head to the wall, and the seer bled.

"Ryker! I'm telling you, you can't—"

"Why didn't you tell me about this shitty occurrence? You're a damn seer you should have seen it since months ago!" Ryker demanded loudly, the blue in his eyeballs so deep that it scared Eternus when their eyes clashed.

Also, in the middle of Ryker's forehead, there's a small freezing ice mark that's glittering too.

"Oh no you're loosing it! Ryker! Ryker calm down! Ryker!!" Eternus shouted, but Ryker only jammed his head again, harder.

"Give me an answer, why didn't you tell me!"

"Cos I didn't know!!" Eternus yelled. "I'm a seer but you both are legendary gods. I've been unable to see the future of you and Shayla. It has always been darkness whenever I tried, and that's why six months ago when you bought her as a slave and started feeling her warmth you came here to ask if she was your partner but I lied that she wasn't cos I didn't want the dark future to manifest! I thought if you didn't grow attachment, things would change." 

Ryker let go of him, but Eternus grabbed Ryker's robe fast.

"You need to calm down, like calm to the base. This anger would ruin you!" 

"Calm and start thinking of her with the real owner of the mantle? Everytime I think about her with him, I run mad! I've ran mad close to a thousand times since I got the realization! I've suffered the ice freezing tribulation for years till she came six months ago to calm my suffering, and now I have to let her and the mantle go for him? Even if I don't do it, I still die!" Ryker laughed, wanting to grab Eternus again.

The seer quickly shifted.

"They think there's no other way?" Ryker smirked, that mark in the middle of his forehead turning brighter. "Well they're in for a pleasant surprise."

Eternus's eyes widened, realizing what he means by that.

Ryker dashed into the room where the FrostOrb is and shattered the glass prism with his hands.

He took the orb.

"I had to take this out of my body 300 years ago cos it was worsening my stupid cold, but now? I'm going to fuckin die with it."

He returned it into himself through the tummy, then he went for the door

"Ryker, I know what you're thinking, but no. You can't..."

He waved Eternus away from the door and vanished out of the castle.

"Ryker!!!!"

--

[Human Realm, Cold-hearted Harem]

Expansion of the harem is going on, and Caspian himself is supervising with his arms behind himself and a big smile on his face.

"Her favorite color is pink, paint everywhere pink, even my own bedroom!" He instructed.

The workers, who have been wondering the kind of visitor he's expecting, exchanged glances.

But they ordered for the bowls of pink paint anyways.

"Flowers too, she loves them. Get from the florist!" Caspian ordered

Another batch of workers started working on creating a flower garden at the backspace of the large mansion.

Caspian sighed when he felt the presence of the head of his spies behind him, and he turned.

It's Rivera.

She went on a knee, bowing after folding her wings.

"I befriended Sadie, and though she's hard to tolerate, but I've been there. Soren's birthday is actually tomorrow, but everyone believes it's still some days away. Only the family are aware of the real date, and I'm aware cos of my bond with Sadie. I've proved my loyalty to the family many times, too. At 12am tomorrow, the sword appears." She reported.

"Interesting. Return, I'll be in Luminaria before the end of today, and tomorrow, I'll claim what's mine." Caspian replied.

Rivera flapped her wings, flying off to the skies.

"I better start prepping my bag of money to climb the ascension ladder." Caspian laughed, rushing down the hall.

--

[Luminaria, Fire Palace~Shayla's Bedchamber]

Shayla has stayed inside alone, nursing her nausea alone by stuffing her mouth with walnuts.

She's not even tasting it on her buds, she just chewed and spit it out to stop herself from throwing up.

She sat in front of her mirror, staring at her own pale face and eyes. 

What's happening to her?

This can't be because of her mother's death, Liza ain't that important.

Though, it's somehow painful that Kade is now motherless, but what can actually be done? She's gone.

She's still troubled about the real mantle owner tale, and darkness is fastly covering her bright ray of sunshine, but still...she trusts there'd still be a solution.

So why the change in her face?

Also, her laps are growing more, too. Though not noticeably.

Even her breàsts are getting slightly larger, and her nîpples have become so painfully erect.

Cheyenne uses Google a lot, so she picked up her phone and searched for her symptoms on Google.

The first thing she saw—pregnancy.

She spranged up from her chair, her eyes so wide.

"Am I really..." She touched her belly, her heart slamming.

If partner gods don't grow a love thread, they can't impregnate each other, so does this mean their love is greater than the love thread?

She's really carrying his baby?

But why was she still menstruating? Could it be part of the goddess hormonal difference stuff?

Maybe that's why the period was so painful. Maybe.

She covered her mouth as tears of joy spilled out of her eyes.

Finally, there's a way out.

She quickly rubbed a scented powder, covering her pale face with the makeup.

She rushed out of the room to break the news, but surprisingly, she found Ryker by the door. He sat there with a porcelain bottle of liquor.

Though he's drinking, he looks sane and fine, and he immediately stood from the ground to hug her.

"Sorry for killing Liza instead of you, I was just too pissed. Couldn't let her live one more day." He muttered.

"Why would I be angry for that? I only felt bad for—"

"Kade?" He broke the hug to place a finger on her lips. "Don't mention his name here."

"I won't, but tell me everything about what's going on, hmm?" She touched his face affectionately.

"Pink Island?" He smiled.

They both vanished.

Cheyenne and Sage appeared at the fireside pavilion, both sitting face-to-face.

"Things are getting messy," Cheyenne muttered.

"I'm scared, for real." Sage said.

"Wait till for 12am for the sword's appearance." Cheyenne replied.

[Pink Island]

Shayla sat on Ryker's laps, a table full of fruits spread in front of them. A blue moon sat on the illuminated sky, obeying Ryker's ice color.

It's deep blue, and it's beautiful, casting a mystical glow on the pink Island and making it prettier.

Shayla's head is on his chest, and he's caressing her thighs and scalp with gentle strokes, his head filled with what he's going to do already.

He already narrated everything about what Pearl told him when he was stuck in the life tree, but he avoided the parts about how he'd die no matter the choice he makes.

"I heard there are several Vales for gods. Forsaken Vale, Resting Vale, Judging Vale, Receiving Vale, then on top of them all is the Nine Heavens." She said, playing with the bracelet on her wrist.

"You heard right," he replied, rubbing his nose in her hair, sniffling the flowery hair oil.

The flowers around them bloomed, and the blue moon rotated in the sky

"Why haven't i gained access to any of the Vales before?" She asked.

"Cos you're too soft, Firefly. You have no desire for powers. Your wish is still to live as a human in the human realm, isn't it?" He replied.

Shayla looked up, nodding.

"But maybe if you teach me how to turn cold-hearted like you, I can learn." She muttered.

"Finally decided?" He smiled.

"Cos I want to be with you, and you want that. Even the fire inside me wants that, so I have no choice." She replied, rubbing his ear.

"Then after getting the sword at 12, after my revenge, we get to practice." He replied, and she nodded, giving him a light kiss.

"Promise me that love thread or not, nine heavens or not, you'll stay with me forever." She said.

"That shouldn't be a question," he replied frankly.

"Obeshchay mne, Ray (Promise me, Ry)" She insisted in Russian, her eyes resembling a doe's.

"Obeshchayu, vashe vysochestvo (I promise, your highness.)" He replied resolutely in Russian.

"After we get the sword, I have something to tell you," she smiled.

"Mmm, first time you prepared a surprise, now I can't wait." He smiled

Their lips sealed again, deep and obsessive. 

They kissed till they bled, but they ate through the blood.

Ryker's dark thought kept dominating his head. It never changed.

The blue moon poured down from the sky, filling the island with rare divine sparkles.

[West Palace, 11PM]

It's one hour before 12, and everyone is already prepared to leave for the East Palace.

That's where the battle for the sword is happening at.

But as everyone appeared down the staircase, Winona was nowhere.

It's just Kasia and Astor, Faye and Blaise.

"Now that I think about it, I haven't seen Winona for a lot of hours." Kasia said worriedly.

"Actually, since she left in the morning. After Astor and Faye left, she followed them and that was the last I saw her." Blaise said.

"Darn, where did she go? This ain't the time to be lost." Kasia picked her phone to dial her.

Blaise picked the Channeler Shell to connect with her too.

"I'm guessing what Rumi told you before was that you should go to the East Palace at 12, get the sword and kill the god to get his orb?" Astor suddenly said.

"It was Xena, not Rumi." Blaise rolled eyes.

"It was Rumi, disguised as Xena." Faye said.

"What!" Blaise dropped the shell.

"And how did you even know what she told me when you weren't here during the whispering?" Kasia looked up from her phone.

"Cos... today is the day of reckoning?" Astor replied with a corny smile.

"Meaning?" Kasia asked.

Faye vanished, and one minute later, she reappeared with Winona's body which she threw on the floor.

Kasia and Blaise screamed.

--

[East Palace, 11:50]

Xena has no idea why the wedding had to be tonight or why it has to be some minutes before 12, but that's the least of her problems right now.

Soren had thrashed her earlier today, and she had no idea how this wedding was planned.

All she knows is that when she regained consciousness, she was healed and she said everything she knew about Ryker. She signed the contract and she was tucked into a wedding robe which she's putting on right now as she walked down the staircase with Soren.

Her legs trembled inside her shoes cos suddenly, she could feel his aura. It's terrible, like death.

It's choking her, and she's scared as fûck, but there's no one to tell.

Not even her father who's among the guests below the staircase.

Anatoli is happy, jamming his palms together with the rest of the guests which only includes family.

Mercury, who's giving her a death stare like a hateful stepsister.

She's almost crushing the glass of wine she's holding.

Sadie, who's also giving her another death stare, and that one already crushed the wine glass she's holding.

Rivera, a neutral guest who's just blank, watching everything and waiting for the right time to snatch the sword for her master.

Esme, Granny Tara and Zeke who look the happiest tonight with their wine cups high.

They're aware that lots of enemies are surrounding this palace now, waiting for the sword to appear so  they'd grab, but they have a backup plan for the enemies.

Anatoli can't hide his smile. Tonight, he's taking the sword and nothing is stopping that.

He's going to kill Ryker and Xena and Rumi, waste everyone in this city and then become the greatest richest demon alive. 

The plan is grand and unstoppable.

The closer they got to the end of the staircase, the more Xena trembled, and Soren could feel her fear which only made him happy.

"From lifetime to lifetime, I'll haunt you. In the next life, and the next and next." He whispered in her ear just before they descended the last step.

Xena started crying, but no one even saw her tears cos at exactly 12am, everyone raised their glasses.

"Happy married life!" They chorused.

"And happy 341st birthday, Soren. Enjoy the rest of your life in hell." Zeke muttered, pulling out a knife—same one they sliced Jude with.

One second, the knife was with Zeke, and in the next, he threw it.

Soren didn't see it coming.

The knife met his throat, and his blood splashed on Xena's face.

"No!!!!!!!!!" She cried.

"Ren!!!!!!!!!" Sadie screamed.

Soren was already on his knees. 

His rabbit Sensei ran down the staircase, but before it could land, Soren died completely.

His body crashed below the rails, blood soaked his red wedding robe, but then he transformed totally.

The image was blurry at first cos of how his body and soul shredded right in front of them, but then...

Slowly, it became clear.

He transformed into the GODSLAYING SWORD.

He's the sword.

[Vipers Household, Minutes B4 12pm]

The blue moon changed to red, painting the whole city crimson as if a bloody war had just happened.

Dominika stood by the door of the main entrance, watching the change and with prepared eyes.

Tonight is gonna be a night of history, not easily forgotten.

If her plans goes as planned, then she destroys the sword and she'd die with it, together with her fake theories.

Ryker and his partner are going to be together for six more months, and during those six months, maybe they could find solution for the original owner issue.

She believes it could be done.

She has been in the shadows, watching their attachment from afar for six months, and she's aware of Ryker's deep-rooted obsession for his partner. When love runs too deep that you can't imagine the partner with anyone but yourself without running mad, then it's obsession.

Ryker is madly obsessed, and he'd do anything, so part of her prayers tonight is that he shouldn't try anything risky, or it'd ruin things.

Ryker might be the powerful, icy heartless one, but he's got no patience, and he possesses mad anger which could ruin if not well managed, but he has Shayla beside him, so she greatly hopes her presence does something tonight.

She'd try every possible best to make the plan work, cos if it doesn't, then things would go totally haywire.

"Are you sure Anatoli is gonna bring you the sword?" Rumi asked, coming to stand behind her by the door.

Dominika smiled, sending an arm around her daughter's shoulder.

"He will. Zeke is strong and strategic, and of course he has Esme and Tara with him, but Anatoli is ten times stronger than everyone of them, they don't just know. He'll bring me the sword no matter what measure he has to take," she said.

"That's some pretty sure devotion," Rumi smirked. "But Ryker, I'm sure he's aware of what's going to happen, he won't just sit still. What if he tells his partner to burn the city so they'd take the sword?"

"Ryker could go close to the sword when it was still Soren, but when it becomes a sword, they won't be able to go close, it'd deplete their power levels. The sword was made for them, so it sucks their powers even without stabbing them." Dominika explained.

"The heavens are crazy, why even make that shit?" Rumi sighed, looking ahead.

"And then there's Pearl, that psychopath has always irked me, I just hope she wasn't telling lies when she was narrating for you" 

"Not a single lie was told. I verified that by asking a trick question about the sword, and she told the truth that she never used it in her last life and she has no idea about the location." Dominika replied.

"Why are you so overly smart?" Rumi said, and the woman smiled again.

Her phone buzzed on the table inside the archive, and she went for it.

A message sat on the screen from Anatoli.

• Come at exactly 12.

"It's almost 12, I better go now," she told Rumi.

"I'm following you," Rumi replied.

"No. You do not even have your ability with you, it's better you stay back and I'll bring Xena back to you," Dominika assured.

"No," Rumi declined. "I might have no ability now, but I trust you to protect me and i—"

"Rumi, stay back. I'll be right back. I promise." Dominika smiled.

Rumi couldn't talk anymore after that.

Dominika left the household, going through the backdoor.

Now it's just Rumi inside.

For the first two minutes, she sat still, getting herself busy with bubble gum, but it wasn't enough to take her mind away from the upcoming.

She stood and left the house, too.

--

On the Lumi city road, is Granny Jojo's car which she has been driving for some hours now.

The drive to the temple was smooth, but the drive back was terrible.

Their car had problems twice which they had to stop to repair.

At the temple earlier, they found out that the sword is a person.

Not just any person—it's her grandson, Soren.

And he must be killed immediately he clocks 341 cos if they don't, then they have to wait for another birthday, but knowing Zeke's personality and his plan to take over every non-human city beyond Luminaria, he won't let it go past this year—tonight.

Tonight is the twins birthday so she has to be fast, or Soren would pass away before she gets there.

"I still don't understand though, why give birth to kids when you don't even love them." Lilith said.

Jojo had no time to even reply her. She's more focused on the road as she drove through a pothole.

"My human father's surname wasn't even known before he was execute with mother, and I wanted a surname for myself, that's why I insisted on getting married to take Ryker's surname." Lilith continued, looking at the ring on her finger.

"Why are you suddenly telling me?" Jojo replied, still not giving her a glance.

"Cos I feel like coming clean. Yes I'm a cruel bîtch, but I spent exactly a month with you at your Lannister household, and I learnt things. You protected me, fed and clothed me, so I won't let that mercy slide. I'll repay you tonight. I'll protect you." Lilith replied.

Jojo finally looked at her.

"Don't say rubbish," she said.

"But I'm deadass," Lilith shrugged.

Jojo ignored her once again and drove into the East street.

--

[Liza Kings Mansion]

Liza's life lantern went off in her study since some hours ago, so Kade is aware that she's dead.

He expected this outcome after he abandoned her at the Fire Palace gate, but the fact that Shayla actually carried it out is more surprising.

Seems his twin sister is more merciless than he actually thought or expected of her.

But now... Liza is dead, and every secret is dead with her.

The dead can't talk anyways. They only sleep in eternal strife and rest in restlessness till forever.

He smiled as he stared at Liza's portraits on the wall, then he began turning everything to the ground one by one till he got to the door and turned the last one.

He took the last burning candle by the door and threw it on the ground, letting the fire catch the rug.

Within a minute, fire spread throughout the house.

Kade walked out, hands pocketed, and the house blew up behind him.

No more Liza's memories, he's going to move to the second mansion and start afresh.

"No more house?" A husky voice filled the air behind him, and he halted, feeling coldness on his skin suddenly when his eyes met the evil diabolical eyes of Caspian.

He's smiling, but the wise would know the smile means calamity.

He hasn't seen this man before, but his appearance is enough to tell him everything is wrong.

He wore a coat which is getting carried by the air as he walked towards him.

Kade stepped back.

"Who are you?"

"I'm not introducing myself, it's your fault for not knowing my identity before now, and it's your bad luck that even after today, you won't be among the people who'd know my real identity cos you'd be dead." Caspian replied arrogantly, pulling a shotgun out from his coat pocket.

Kade took two more steps back, eyes suddenly horrific.

This ain't the time to get shot. Liza is gone so who'd heal him? The alchemist shop is far from here.

"I heard you treated her bad, and you were so mad at her for sharing a mother with you," Caspian said. 

Kade's brows arched. "Shayla? You're here cos of that bîtch?" 

"Bîtch?" Caspian smiled, giving his gun a stare as if examining the last weapon before destruction.

"Mother did worse to her, and now she's dead, so if you're on the lookout for somehow to rant on, then go find her in hell!" Kade snapped, momentarily forgetting about the gun

"Rant? Do I look like I'm here to rant? Does this weapon smell like rant?" Caspian looked up, cocking the gun.

Kade froze again, but not for long, cos he moved back twice before turning around.

The blaze from the burning house was lightening his steps as he ran away from the devil.

Caspian stood there, watched him till he took the tenth step, then he shot.

He fired three bullets at Kade, all on the head.

Kade crashed into the dust, blood gushing out of his skull.

His eyes was closing slowly as his blood wet the dust, turning it to clay

So...in this lifetime, he died because of Shayla, just like his mother died because of her.

This isn't the end anyways. It can never be the end.

The dues shall be payed, soon.

Caspian dragged his body  back to the burning house, throwing it inside to glow it up.

He stepped back afterwards, grinning.

"Aren't Luminarians so funny? They can live for as long as two, three thousand years without getting old or sick, they get healed whenever they're heavily injured, but just shoot them on the head or chest, or nail their throat, they're gone."

He looked at the house again, sighing before biting his lip.

"Too bad I didn't get to kill Liza with my own hands, but my woman killed her, so it counts." He smiled, swinging the gun in his index finger as he walked away from the house, making his way to the East Palace.
