[West Palace]

Blaise and Kasia are still crying, shaking Winona's body on the ground as if to wake her which is impossible since her core is gone.

Even without the core, she was attacked on the throat, and her pipe is cut, so it's a lost cause.

Kasia's snot mixed with her tears below her nose, and she could taste it in her mouth as she hugged Winona's inert body to herself.

She's sitting helplessly on the ground, looking pitiful, but there's no pity in the eyes of Faye and Astor as they stood over them.

"I should have noticed, I should have known!! You're the one who led us to the vipers household after Winona found out about the snake woman in the journal! Were we blind? Winona said the snake woman was a cobra but we were actually searching the vipers household for clues! Did you both blindfold us?" Blaise stood.

"We didn't need to blindfold y'all, your midnight curse was enough to veil your eyes. There were so many clues, but none of them was seen by anyone of y'all. Only Winona was smart, but too bad she didn't last. Her core was sweet, by the way." Astor smiled, and Kasia spranged up.

"Our ancestors sinned, they're the ones who killed your wife and household, not us! I haven't even been born back then, but you thought you could take it out on me and my children? I guess you're the one who killed my husband too, just to gain an opening to enter me!" 

"Yes! I killed your husband, Forrest. I killed him just so I could enter you by marrying you!" Astor yelled.

More tears rushed out of Kasia's eyes, and she staggered.

"No....no ... How could you!!!!"

"You killed father two years ago?" Blaise's lips were shivering as he talked.

"That was me.... actually. I killed your father, and I ate his core," Faye spoke up, and Blaise's heart shattered more.

He fell on the floor, right beside Winona's body.

"I knew something was up when I didn't like you both at first! I knew something was wrong when I just couldn't see you as my new father or Faye as my stepsister! I knew something was absolutely wrong!!!" He screamed, punching the ground.

"I am going to kill you, Astor! I'll kill you and your daughter!!!" Kasia removed the long pin in her hair, coming for him.

"It's 12am already—midnight. Think twice, cos right now you're powerless!" Astor replied.

"I do not care!!!" Kasia resumed her race till she arrived at him and drove the pin into his chest.

Blood dropped, but it didn't look like Astor even got stabbed cos he was grinning like a villain.

Suddenly, he shoved his hand into Kasia's tummy, getting a hold of her core from the inside.

The woman shed blood from her eyes, her mouth opened painfully, but she didn't let go of the pin at all.

"Mother!!!!" Blaise yelled, wanting to run over to them.

"Go, Blaise! Go away! Run!!!" Kasia yelled, driving the pin into Astor's chest harder.

Blaise stopped, tears falling from his eyes like a kid. 

Kasia's core is already in Astor's hand, so his efforts would be useless. There's no saving Kasia anymore.

"Go!!!" Kasia screamed again, and that was when he moved.

He ran out of the palace, and Faye followed him immediately.

"Think about this as an help, cos if you had gone to the East Palace, you'd have come back with nothing or end up dying in Zeke or Anatoli's hands. The FrostOrb and FireStone empowering tale? It's all fake!" Astor spat at her face.

"My ancestors sinned, and you thought taking it out on I and my kids is the right thing to do?" Kasia smiled painfully, crying hurtfully.

"Remember, that dues would be paid when it's time. It's not over yet." She muttered.

Astor pulled his hand out of her, bringing out her small core which he ate right in front of her.

Kasia's body crumbled on the ground, dying in top of Winona's.

--

Blaise barely got to the outside gate when Faye caught up with him.

She intentionally followed slowly, allowing him to run out of the gate.

He's running, she's walking, but he's still not out of her grasp. 

Cruel eyes and precise steps, she followed him closely till Blaise finally stopped some feet away from the gate

He faced her, presenting himself on a platter and awaiting his own death.

"Winona is gone, mother is gone. Why am I even running away from you? Kill me, do it." He sniffled, closing his eyes.

Faye stood there, unable to do a thing till Astor appeared behind her.

He raised her hand, whispering in her ear like a persuader...

"His ancestors killed your mother when she was still wet with the blood of your birth, they killed our entire household, mercy for none. Kill him and stop wasting time, daughter. Kill him!" He instructed, pushing her hand forward 

Faye's hand entered Blaise's tummy, and his eyes flew open, wide and pained.

He fell on his eyes in front of her as her hand came out of him, holding his bloody core.

He crumbled like the rest of his family, and Astor laughed out loudly, the sound evil than evil.

Faye wrapped her palm on Blaise's core, a little tear leaving her eyes.

--

[East Palace,12am]

Slowly, it became clear.

He transformed into the GODSLAYING SWORD.

He's the sword.

"It's true! It's very fuckin true!" Zeke laughed through Xena and Sadie's cries as he walked up to where the sword lay.

He squatted, staring at it.

It looks crafted from rare silver and blue gold, carries the sharpest big blade which looks like a saber but it's not. The handle has a dragon statue hanging on it, and on the body, 341 was crafted.

"Beautiful, I've never seen something as beautiful." Zeke grinned again.

"Bastard!!!!" Sadie removed her heels, throwing the two at Zeke's head before biting him on the neck, not letting go till she pulled off his flesh.

No one could get her off till Tara and Esme joined arms to hold her.

"I should have known something was wrong the moment you agreed to his wedding to this slût without batting a lid! It's our birthday but you made it the bitterest day for me, and I'll never forgive you! In the next lifetime, I'll be your worse nightmare!!!" She screamed, throwing her legs forward.

She broke free from the two women and went for Zeke again, but Zeke was prepared this time.

He picked the knife he stabbed Soren with, pointing it forward so Sadie just ran into it.

She got stabbed without stressing.

Sadie's body turned cold as she felt the metal plowing her belly, and the last tear rolled out of her eyes.

Her eyes met with Zeke's, and Zeke smiled at her.

"Listen, I hate kids, but I only married Azura to make kids because I heard I was destined to give birth to the sword. You have no use for me, and you can go join your brothers." 

With brutality, he removed the knife from her belly, and Sadie fell.

Xena cried louder, carrying Sensei in her arms.

She started rushing to her father's side, but that was when Jojo and Lilith entered the house.

Jojo started crying her eyes out immediately she saw the sword on the ground, and Sadie's body.

"We're late, shit." Lilith muttered.

"Zeke, you should have been created as a dog. No, you shouldn't even be a dog, it's an insult to that breed. You should be a sewage rat, what is wrong with your head and your mother's head!!!" Jojo yelled.

"Power drives people crazy, you weren't told?" Zeke chuckled, walking to the sword already.

"Watch me, then..." Jojo smiled, raising her hand.

The night air went hot, and the blood-red moon in the sky changed colour again, turning white.

Thunderstorm started, heavier than anyone that has ever happened in the city. It deafened lots or people, and it left houses crumbling, including the palace. Rainstorm joined the exercise, and it got a lot worse.

"She got the thunder ability back?" Granny Tara muttered.

"Her daughter is dead, of course she'd get it back," Zeke replied. "Esmeralda, get the sword!"

Esme ran to where the sword lay and picked it up, but that instant, Lilith let out a sonorous scream.

The East Palace started crumbling again for the second time.

"Through the tunnel!" Zeke pointed, opening a tunnel on the wall.

He ran inside with Esme and Tara, but Anatoli and Rivera followed them

"Father!" Xena tried to enter too, but Anatoli pushed her back before closing the tunnel door.

Xena curled herself under one of the falling bricks, crying as she held desperately to Sensei.

The rabbit is crying and squirming in her arms too, and a large brick fell on them together.

"Mother!!!!" Mercury cried. She's squatting somewhere around the ruined room.

Inside the water tunnel, Zeke and his women thought they're the only ones present, but when they peered back and saw Anatoli and Rivera, they had to stop in surprise.

"Who exactly are you? Your friend Sadie died and you did nothing?" Anatoli muttered beside Rivera.

"Caspian sent me here in case you need an extra hand to get the sword,". Rivera replied.

"That bastard Caspian must think I'm a weakling, but I don't need his help. You might have to stand down and let me handle my shit." Anatoli replied.

"Anatoli? Rivera? Why do we have you both in here?" Zeke questioned from the front.

"I'm not the stupid Old Chef whom you once knew, think you're the only one in the whole of Luminaria who's in need of the sword?" Anatoli replied, going closer.

"One more enemy to deal with?" Zeke smiled, signalling at Esme and Tara.

"Go with the sword!"

The women started running away, and Rivera followed them.

Anatoli and Zeke charged towards each other, both meeting at a dangerous point to throw hits.

They both tumbled, landing brutally on the muddy waters, but Zeke's landing was more brutal. He broke a arm bone, and he gasped.

He stood anyways, no time to wonder why Anatoli is so strong. 

The two old men went for each other, throwing strikes again. Dark energy collided with dark energy, and they both leapt, jabbing each other in their throats and spitting blood.

Their blood painted the tunnel walls as their bodies came back down, and their punches collided violently.

Zeke's energy was only enough to bend a bone on Anatoli's arm, but Anatoli's?

It crushed Zeke's arm completely, making the bones pierce the flesh to stab the skin.

"Arhhghh!!!!!!" Zeke cried, staggering to the tunnel body and punching a hole through it in the process.

Water rushed in, filling the tunnel more, reaching their calves.

In shock, Zeke looked up, unable to even stand from where he sat.

Anatoli siphoned his powers already.

"Who exactly are you?" He demanded.

"I'm what you can never understand, now bye!" Anatoli smiled, vanishing.

The moment he vanished off, Zeke started trying to stand again, but he couldn't.

It was harder than any task.

He got his channeler Shell out to contact Esme, but the shell broke before he could even talk into it.

Shayla and Ryker appeared in the tunnel, side by side with matching mocking smirks.

"I knew I shouldn't underestimate my errand boy," Ryker commented.

"You have no idea how pitiful you look right now," Shayla added, looking disgustingly at Zeke.

"You both... winning in every situation isn't by luck. You won't win this one." Zeke replied, bringing a vial of holy water out of his pocket.

He threw the opened vial at the two of them, and Ryker quickly covered Shayla, so the water sprinkle only met him.

Some parts of his robe burned into his skin, but that's just it. 

Nothing else.

"You ok?" Shayla asked, checking the skin out quickly.

"I'm fine," she replied before facing Zeke who's still handicapped on the ground.

Zeke groaned, looking at the water level with his shocked eyes.

"I'm guessing Xena told you my weakness is holy water from a church alter in the human realm?" He smirked darkly.

"At this point, i dunno who's more stupid between the two of you. I guess she saw it written close to the pavilion when I was still at the Heartless Tower, but the thing is...holy water only works on me if I am still at the first stage, but I attained stage 2 already. Next time, don't be so heartbreakingly dumb,"

"But there won't be next time," Shayla replied, getting out her fire sword.

Ryker pulled his ice sword, and together, they stabbed the madman in the chest, pulling his heart out to the the tunnel water.

Shayla set his body on fire, and they both vanished off.

--

Rivera was already rolling in a hot fight with Esme and Tara when Anatoli caught up with them in the middle of the tunnel.

The water level rose again, reaching the middle of their forelegs already.

Now it's two against two, nowhere to run to, so they faced it.

Tara and Rivera, Esme and Anatoli.

Rivera slapped the old granny with her wings many times, attempting to make her eyeballs fall out of her sockets, but Tara ain't so soft.

She fought back stubbornly till the last drop of her sweat.

Thanks to the sword Esme is holding, she got empowered, and she fought on par with Anatoli, but despite that, she couldn't match up.

After all, she cultivated her godslaying ability, she wasn't born with it.

She ran away when it got to a point, and Anatoli followed her.

Rivera slapped Tara with her wings again, and the old woman fell, so Rivera flew away, following Anatoli.

Tara didn't get the chance to stand when Shayla and Ryker appeared again, majestically.

Ryker dumped a snake on Tara's body, and the woman gasped, looking up as the shockwaves gripped her.

It's her snake, same one she had sent into his Heartless Tower to plant venom in his body.

"Your son probably inherited his foolishness from you, but it's entertaining." Ryker crouched in front of the old woman who nearly entered the leaking tunnel wall.

"Your snake came to me in my Phoria that cold morning to plant the venom you sent it to plant as your plan B, but too bad, you know what I did? I took it in as a pet, and since then, I've been feeding it with the toxins from my mind's eye. A bite, and you'd die with it." He whispered.

Tara quickly shook the snake away from herself, and the serpent dropped dead on the water surface.

"Oh...seems you got bitten," Shayla muttered from the back.

Tara gasped again, trying to stand, but she dropped back on the tunnel wall, her eyes losing all colour.

The snakebite on her firearm reddened as her life slipped out of her. She went still, and the water covered her head as she fell flat.

Ryker froze her, shattering her figure with the water.

--

Esme wasn't lucky.

She was about to leave the leaking tunnel when Anatoli caught up with her, and she faced him, knowing it's either fight or die.

Her mistake was using the sword to fight him cos the sword only works on the gods, so all her weldings amounted to nothing.

She missed everytime, till Anatoli grabbed her arm, dragging her closer

"You cultivated the ability for how many months? Maybe five? And you think you're good to go? Real cultivators spend thousands of years in the run before becoming professions, so to me, you're just an idiot who should have known her damn place!" He said, slamming his forehead on hers.

He cast an obeisance spell, and Esme let go of the sword willingly.

Esme fell in the water weakly, and she git submerged.

Anatoli bursted out of the tunnel, meeting himself in front of the dismantled main castle where a crowd is waiting at.

Caspian who's now standing with Rivera beside him.

Granny Jojo and Lilith.

Pearl who's holding her stomach and bleeding.

Mercury who's looking for her mother and crying.

And a lot of City people. 

Hundreds of them.

His Master should be here right now—the red cloak, so he started searching with his eyes.

"Anatoli! What are you waiting for? Come here!" Caspian waved, but he never answered.

His search for Master continued till she saw her standing with a foreboding stance in the middle of the crowd, her cloak swirling.

He smiled widely, holding the sword tighter as he ran to her.

People made ways till he arrived, his smile widened more.

"I told you, I was bound to win. Didn't I? We can take the world together with this. Let's go!" He said, pointing the sword forward.

He can't even lift it too much so ce it's too heavy. Only godslayers lift and wield it with ease.

"Should I go beat him up and take the sword?" Rivera asked beside Caspian.

"Shh, don't." Caspian muttered.

Dominika took it from Anatoli slowly, and her second hand went to her head to lower the cloak, revealing her face.

Her former body died 350yrs ago, and her soul has entered another body, so she didn't expect him to recognize her very fast, but her scent.

The sandalwood scent from her cloak gave her away.

She didn't have to rub sandalwood oil. Dominika always had that unique body scent since the start, and it's just her. Just like how she's the only one bearing Dominika in the city.

Anatoli's eyes widened, and he quickly retracted a step. He felt his bones getting fried, and his flesh getting thawed.

"Domini..."

"Mother!" Rumi's voice interrupted as she ran out of the crushed castle with Xena who's bleeding so much between life and death.

When did Rumi even get here???

"What!" Dominika gasped, touching Anatoli's shoulder. She turned him into a sewage rat for now, and she dumped him in her cloak pocket.

And that was when she noticed the sword is gone from her.

Completely.

Someone must have snatched it when she was distracted by Xena and Rumi one minute ago, but there's only one person who would have done that.

When she looked ahead, it's exactly who she expected.

Ryker stood there, imposing and threatening as ever, holding the sword in his hand with murderous eyes and reeking killer intents.

Blood dropped in his brain more times than he could count, and his jaws are ticking faster than a clock.

Cheyenne and Sage emerged from the crowd, both panting when they sensed the heaviness in the air.

Eternus emerged too, his eyes very madly wide.

"I told you, Ryker. You can't do it! Don't do it!!" He shouted, all sweaty.

Even Shayla is confused beside Ryker at this point.

They won, didn't they? They killed all enemies except Anatoli who'd be easy to kill later cos his death his special.

They got the sword, and though the sword aura is sucking their powers right now and weakening them, they can go destroy it in the volcano in her Fire Palace, and she'd finally tell him about her pregnancy.

So why? Why is everything looking like it's far from ending?

Why is it looking like it's just the beginning?

Dominika's eyes widened beyond her kids where she stood, and her feet started running towards Ryker cos she read his mind already, and she knows what he's thinking.

"No. My baby! Don't!!!"

"Motherfucin Ryker!!!" Caspian started running forward too, but their run came to an abrupt end when Ryker did his worse 

He suddenly hugged Shayla, and Shayla gasped at the suddenness, but then the absolute worse happened.

Lifting the sword with his remaining fluctuating energy, Ryker stabbed Shayla from behind.

Since they're hugging, the sword pierced her, came out of her stomach to enter his own stomach, coming out at his back.

Their blood mixed, pains at the same level, and their agonizing screams left their lips at the same time as blood poured out of their mouths, staining their faces.

"No!!!!!!!!!!!!!!" Dominika and Caspian co-yelled.

"Your lordship!!!!" Sage howled.

"Your ladyship!!!!!" Cheyenne rang.

Eternus staggered.

"Oh no..." Jojo started crying.

Lilith, too. She covered her mouth.

Pearl was speechless.

Rumi is still kneeling beside Xena's bedding body, and her teary eyes are wide.

"Brother..." She muttered.

"Ryker!!!!!!"

Mercury who's still searching for her mother stopped in shock, too.

Faye and Astor entered the crowd with a run, and their eyes nearly flew off their sockets.

Shayla was shocked, but at the same time, the feeling of betrayal bit her deeply in her mind, heart and soul as her life slipped, together with Ryker's who's stabbed with her.

They're still conjoined, still dying.

"I'm... sorry for being a villain at the end, but if I can't have you, then no other man will, especially not Caspian." Ryker whispered, crying.

"Did you doubt my love for you so much that you couldn't even... couldn't even...tell me...that...you were gonna do this?" Shayla smiled sadly, her tears mixing with her blood and snot.

Ryker said nothing.

At that same moment of death, memories of their first life came flooding into Shayla's head, and she smiled dryly, shutting her eyes as the bitterness settled deeper, the betrayal and disappointment.

They showed in her eyes when she opened them again. She has stopped shedding tears now, and it's like the world stilled around them.

Like they're the only ones left in this cold, cruel, wretched world.

"I'll never forgive you for killing our baby. This blood debt, you'll pay it till you cry blood, Ryker." She called his full name, and Ryker's eyes widened.

"What did you just say?"

"I was pregnant before you stabbed me, and that was enough to change everything. That was enough to convince the heavens, but you had to ruin things with your impatience and lack of trust in me." Shayla replied.

"No... No... No it's...." Ryker choked on his own tears.

"Impossible?" Shayla smiled bitterly, bringing an hand to his face.

Their black robes are terribly wet with their blood, and the sword is slowly diminishing their existence.

"I love you, and I don't care if it tastes bitter and sour and bland in your mouth. I love you, remember it, even in your next life. Remember you killed our hope." She muttered, and her eyes closed for the last time.

Breaths stopped, pulse stopped, neck cracked, body failed.

Ryker held her bloody face, hot tears pilfering out of his regretful eyes as he kissed her forehead.

The sword between them shone, and the shine swallowed the two of them, sucking them into oblivion.

A bright blinding light erupted from their disappearance, and a lot of people had to cover their eyes to kill the effects of the light, but Pearl saw it as an opportunity.

She had promised to die with him anyways, so it's just perfect.

She jumped into the light, disappearing too.

Sage and Cheyenne jumped into the light next, both determined to go extinct with their lord.

Mercury jumped in too, completely forgetting about her mother.

Lilith pulled Granny Jojo, but the old woman resisted.

"You're almost two thousand years old, Granny. Even as an Eternal, your life is short already. We can't miss this opportunity, I want to be reborn too and I can't leave you here!" Lilith scolded, pulling her inside just before the light went off.

"That motherfucker, that fuckin skunk!! Fûck!!!" Caspian yelled, crashing out beside Rivera.

Faye and Astor faced each other, looking neutral.

Rumi is still kneeling where she is, and she's still holding Xena's body.

Xena might be soaked in blood, but her eyes are slightly open, and she's crying too.

Dominika walked up to them, kneeling in front of her daughters.

"M...Ma?" Xena spoke through the blood in her mouth.

Dominika nodded with a smile despite the tears in her eyes.

"I'm sorry I came late,"

Xena closed her eyes completely, more tears sliding out.

Dominika placed her palms above her body, healing her wounds.

Eternus marched to them, squatting with the three.

"I told him. I warned him, but he was too stubborn. But on the other hand, I think it was meant to end like this. I mean...this lifetime was meant to end this way." He said.

"What do you mean?" Rumi asked.

"The next lifetime might be in ten or thousand years, but it'd happen, just not here cos Luminaria is in chaos already. It's desolate, and people are leaving the city, travelling to the human realm." Eternus said.

"Next lifetime? ten thousand years?" Dominika started crying again.

"Mightn't be up to that, just guesses." Eternus smiled bitterly, joining Dominika to heal Xena's wounds.

By now, Caspian and Rivera are gone.

Faye and Astor are gone, too.

The rest of the crowd are starting to depart from the ruins.

Luminaria is presently decaying and crumbling. Every building. Every form. Everything.

It's like the existence of the gods is the foundation of the city, so everything fell down.

"We have to leave," Dominika said.

Rumi helped Xena up, and thankfully, she can walk now.

Teleporting, they went to the Crossover Command Building where they met lots of people who're struggling to get down.

It's crowded, like the airport of a plaqued city in the human world.

Everyone's trying to escape.

They waited patiently for their turn, and in fifteen more minutes, they're part of the  last set of people to get on the ascension ladder.

As the door closed behind them, Luminaria got destroyed completely as if it was never there.

    [A Thousand Yrs Later, Russia]

[Morozov Residence]

It's not a very big house, not even a mansion but a small flat.

It's at least comfortable for Dominika and her daughters.

Since joining the human realm, they've all been working hard to put food on their table. Everyone including Dominika herself.

Rumi works at a mental hospital for relapsing drug addicts. She's quite good at taking care of them.

Xena is a ballerina, an important part of a ballet group called "Foxx"

Dominika herself is a florist. She has a pretty big flower shop, selling flowers and blue roses.

Though, aging took it's toll on the woman, and she's starting to grow strands of grey hair, but she's still strikingly beautiful.

Human world has become inhabited by Luminarians, and they seem to be multiplying everyday especially the spell casters like Rumi.

They use their spells in banks mostly to rob, and the robbery cases never ends. It's an everyday occurrence.

It's a really cold morning in the house, and Dominika was wearing clips in her hair as she emerged from her room, ready to go to work.

"You didn't wear mittens!" Rumi scolded from the living room where she stood with her arms akimbo.

"Darn! I forgot!" Dominika gasped.

"And later today, you'll start complaining about how you caught the flu! It's November and outside is practically freezing! You have sensitive hands!" Rumi continued scolding.

"I'll go wear them, stop nagging...urg!" Dominika waved a dismissive hand, going back into her room to wear her mittens.

By the time she came back, Rumi was already holding a bowl of cereal.

"You and your love for cereal. Anyways, see you when I get back!" Dominika kissed her cheek.

"I'll be back late. I have too many special patients so don't wait for me at dinner" Rumi replied.

Dominika went to the dinning where Xena has been sitting, busy on her laptop screen.

She kissed her cheek too.

"Alright, bye mum. Mhua!" Xena kissed back without looking at her.

Dominika got her car keys and left the house.

"The hell are you looking at on your laptop screen! You've been staring since like 5am!" Rumi went behind her to peek at her laptop screen.

"Katherine Pierce?" She read the name on the screen. "You're still obsessed with this lady?"

"Who wouldn't?" Xena looked up. "She's the most popular, the baddest ballerina in this city. She has been dancing ballet, going on exclusive concerts since she was ten, and yet, no one has seen her face! She performs with a mask on, always!"

Xena stood at this point, and Rumi shifted back.

"Now, she turned 26 today, and she's finally having an exclusive concert tonight where she'd be dancing without a mask on. My role model will be dancing without a mask on and you're telling me I didn't get a ticket to the concert? Meaning I won't be seeing my baby's face? Gawd!! Just kill me!" Xena sat sprawled on the ground dramatically.

"And you know the worse part? No one is allowed to snap pictures at the concert so only the people who'll go will get the chance to see her! Her team said she's so fuckin beautiful I can't miss it! I'm gonna cry!!"

"What's this unhealthy obsession with Katherine Pierce? The only good thing about her is her insane ballet dance talent, but even her team are saying she's rude and very wicked, and she hates kids." 

"I won't tolerate you talking absolute shit about my idol like that! So what if she's rude or hates kids? We Katherine addicts don't care!" Xena attacked, and Rumi shook her head, already looking tired of her twin.

Xena said nothing anymore, she just continued sitting there, sulking.

"Anyways, I'm gonna borrow your short burgundy wig, I hate the longness of my hair and I'm planning to trim it when I get back from work today, but for now, I need that short wig!" Rumi said, rushing inside.

Xena got up too, but she didn't go inside.

She picked up a rat food nylon from the center table, and she went out of that angle, opening the second door beside the study.

Inside the door, there are two more doors which she walked through before jumping down a window which led to a backyard.

There, there's a smaller room where a rough iron sheet cage is.

She opened the cage and was about to pour the rat food inside, but she had a change of mind.

She poured the food into a plate and lowered her pants, peeing on the food. She mixed it with her pee very well before dropping the plate inside the cage.

The rate ate like it has been starving for months which is true. It hasn't been fed for two months now.

Anatoli.

Xena was smiling as she watched him eat the pee-soaked food, and when he finished eating, she injected him on the belly and went ahead to place him on a gummy surface so he couldn't move when the pains from the injection shot up his head.

The stomach swelled till he started vomiting the food, but as he vomited, he ate again, not wanting to lose any of it cos only gawd knows when next he's gonna be eating again.

Xena grinned, satisfactorily.

"You've suffered in our hands for how many years now? A Thousand? But it's nothing compared to what Ryker would do to you if he luckily reincarnates." She muttered.

"Do you think there's really a chance for him and Shayla to reincarnate?" Rumi entered the room too, wearing the burgundy wig already. "It's been a thousand years, and I've not seen a single dead person from Luminaria get reborn."

The rat was squirming and crying in the cage, so Rumi slapped it, and the poor thing went flying to the cage body. The iron sheet cut it's limbs, and he cried harder, bleeding to the smelly stinking sawdust inside.

"Let's leave this room, or I might just kill this mad man." Rumi sighed, walking out of the room.

She's dressed corporately for work already, so her heels are clinking the marbles sassily.

"I don't believe they're gone just like that. Mom is still alive, that's enough reason to tell you that Ryker will be back. I don't want his forgiveness, I don't deserve it, but I just want to see him torture Anatoli." Xena replied, following her out of the room.

There was quite as the two girls walked back to the living room, but Rumi is aware of what's on Xena's mind, so she talked...

"I know what you're thinking, don't say it out."

"Katherine Pierce's concert!!" Xena screamed, hanging her arm on Rumi's neck tightly in a neck lock.

"Stop!" Rumi struggled.

"Put your obeisance spell on Katherine's company for me let them just give me one ticket!" Xena cajoled.

"In your dreams! Mom said I can never use my ability for evil, and I'm respecting her!" Rumi pushed her away, but Xena ran to her again.

Rumi ran away.

"You're lucky you got your ability back even when Anatoli is still alive, but me...my ability deals with animals and he's currently an animal so I can't get it back unless he dies totally! If I had my ability, I'd have sent Sensei to steal a ticket for me so now you're my only hope! Steal a ticket for me before tonight!" Xena insisted, running after her.

She's in front of the couch, and Rumi is at the back of it, both breathing heavily like they've been marathoning.

"I'm very late for work, you can't be doing this." Rumi panted.

"Give me what I want then," Xena spread her palms.

"I'm not stealing for you! I only did that in Luminaria and old things have passed away. You're a bad influence!" Rumi scoffed.

"Rumi please!" Xena jumped the couch, but Rumi backed up.

[7:15, Rado Cuisine]

"Calm down, sir," the young man replied calmly. 

He's the event planner.

He grabbed the second one and poured it too, from his hair to his suit and dress shirt.

"You don't do that to my boss! Fine, he made a big mistake with the birthday cake but we've been begging and begging! What else do you want!" She shouted protectively.

"You poor penniless thing! I should have known he's a hungry mongrel judging by his faded suit! He only wears one suit around and never changes tie! How could I make the mistake of giving him the job!" The old man continued ranting, raging.

"Do you want repayment?" the planner said calmly again. Even calmer than before.

"Is he insane? Why the hell is he calm after ruining the birthday! You're the worse planner ever! And I'll make sure you never get recommendations again you pauper! Get out of my damn way!" The old man pushed him out of the way with his walking stick.

He left the restaurant angrily.

"Are you ok, sir? Gosh, you're wet with the wine. Even your he hair. Why the hell would you even ask if he wanted a repay? Did you forget we payed half of your debt with his payment already?" She said.

The planner took his seat, sighing as he pushed his wet hair away from his face, revealing himself.

Ryker.

He was reincarnated thirty years ago, currently thirty years old.

Same face, same expression, but a poor man.

He has done series of jobs, but he always ended up in debt.

He was once a hotel manager 

Once a concierge.

Once a gas station attendant.

Once a model

Once a fisherman in the suburbs.

But they all ended in ruins.

Now he's an event planner, looks like this isn't his way either.

To him, he hasn't even started suffering. He wants more. He craves more suffering.

He reincarnated as a human, so it's hard, but he wants it even harder.

He thought killing himself with Shayla would make both of them go extinct for life, but seems he was wrong, and he's not smarter than the heavens after all.

Here he is, living again.

Despite dying by the godslaying sword, he reincarnated again.

Caspian Yusluv is now the richest man alive while he's a poor faded event planner who receives insults and vile words everyday.

Even his face card can't save him. It's like everyone hates him.

And he's sure Shayla is alive somewhere.

He's sure...

He looked up at her.

Lilith.

She reincarnated as his assistant, and though she has no memories of Luminaria, but at least she's good, and she's tall now.

Today's Friday, and feel free to treat requirements bad again, it only means I'm gonna be having another sweet break tomorrow and we won't be meeting till Monday. Your choice.

"Sh...ay...la?"

His bones froze, more than it does whenever he was frozen back then. His tongue seemed to form a immovable carpet under his mouth.

After he stuttered that name, he couldn't say anything else.

His blood surely froze with his bones, cos he wasn't feeling the circulation anymore. His feet vibrated inside his shoes, and his eyes dwindled.

Caspian's palm rubbed Shayla's hip, up to the waist as they walked side-by-side.

She's not smiling, but it's very obvious she likes it, and she's madly comfortable.

She's beautiful—lethally beautiful with the dark shade of her eyeshadow, and the tiny diamond ring on top of her nose.

They surrounded the restaurant, and Rivera particularly stood behind their designated table.

Shock.

After Ryker killed himself with Shayla a thousand years ago, Caspian had left Luminaria with dead hopes, thinking it was really the end.

She's from the rich Pierce family in this lifetime, and her parents are strict business people who invested in his company.

She's hotheaded, full of herself and wicked. Heavy on that wicked part.

He's too lost.

It has been snowing since Saturday night,, but going home is still not part of his to-do list at all. He stayed in his car, in the cold, waiting for when Cheyenne's gonna show up.

He has made inquiries from the bar owner, and after he described her features especially her mean unsmiling face, they confirmed it was her, but the only thing they know is that she's Cheyenne, nothing more.

She just comes to drum.

With this limited info, he could only wait, and he has been doing just that.

Even his guards waited with him when they couldn't get him to leave.

Thanks to the fact that his parents are on a business trip, he's somehow free.

At 7am, Soren's car crossed his car in the front, and he came out of the car to meet his brother.

"You've been here since Saturday night, and this is Monday, bro. What exactly are you after? Soren queried.

"Is this your way of scolding your elder brother?" Sage replied.

"Do you see me smiling? The weather is too cold. You haven't had good food since Saturday, haven't had your bath too, please go home." Soren said frankly.

"I'm not going back home till I see her. She'll come soon." Sage replied.

"Who are you talking about?" Soren rolled eyes.

"She's the drummer, and —"

"Yunno what, bro? Keep waiting. I'm going to the Foxx, and I won't be late cos of you. I'll take pity on you and send food over. Congratulations on your new vigilante job." Soren interrupted, getting back in his car.

"He's somehow heartless in this lifetime too," Sage smiled after Soren drove off.

--

[Foxx Ballet Group Center]

There's only a little difference between the center and college.

It's as large as one, and there are various departments of ballet, but Dark ballet is the most populated with the most talented members.

That's Xena and Faye's department.

They came out of the locker room after changing to their first outfit, both spaced out till they settled on a metal bench in the long crowded hall.

"I'm still in awe," Faye muttered.

"I haven't been sleeping since Saturday night," Xena muttered.

"How bad were you?" Faye asked.

"I called her a hunt dog, and I did worse to Ryker," Xena covered her eyes with her palms. "I'm such an animal!!"

"You're really an animal," Faye nodded.

"You both went to the exclusive concert on Saturday, why not tell us what it looked like?" A girl suddenly appeared with her groupie.

"Is Katherine really as beautiful as they say?"

"Can you both duet her dance moves?"

"Is her hair really the longest and healthiest in this city?"

The questions kept coming, but none of 'em are ready to chat, obviously.

"I also went, to the concert!" A arrogant voice said from the downside of the hall, and everyone left Faye and Xena.

Sadie walked into the hall, shoulders high and lips tight till she arrived in the middle.

Faye and Xena stood, but they don't look shocked cos Rumi already told them she saw Sadie at the hospital.

Obviously, she has no memory of Luminaria.

"Hey, girlies." She waved a finger, stealing all attention for herself.

"I went to the concert, too." Another golden voice muttered from the second downside.

Soren walked in, coincidentally wearing the same colour as Sadie.

Girls went gaga immediately.

"He's hot!"

"Raww!"

"Never seen him before!

"Never seen the girl before too, and what's this air of arrogance between the two of them!"

"They must be new!"

"You really came," Sadie smiled.

"I never go back on a deal," Soren replied simply, pulling her to his side.

"Soren Harland here" He waved.

"Sadie Robertson" Sadie added.

"We're both new, and we're ready to make friends" Sadie announced.

"Don't crush on be though, I don't date. Also, I and Sadie are besties from now on." Soren added.

Sadie chuckled, and she ballerinas shrouded them immediately, asking questions about the concert which they started answering.

They stole the spotlight totally.

Xena fell back weakly on her bench. She hasn't been breathing since Soren entered this hall.

So she saw right last night during the concert. 

She really saw him at the arena.

"Are you ok? Your crush from your last lifetime just appeared with his evil twin, and they're automatically besties in this life!" Faye asked.

"Don't talk to me for now. Just let me think." Xena said, sweating so much.

She got her small electric fan from her shoulder bag and turned it on to cool down, standing awkwardly.

Coincidentally, that was when Soren and Sadie were also leaving the hall.

Xena bumped into Soren, and her small purse fell from her bag.

"I'm...I'm... sorry.." she quickly shifted back, losing her breaths.

"I hate when girls throw themselves at guys in form of bumping. I don't like you." Soren replied, and Xena felt like entering the ground immediately.

"She doesn't look bad, actually." Sadie said beside him.

"I don't like her," Soren repeated glaringly, picking Xena's fallen purse from the ground.

There's a keychain inside which has Rumi's picture.

Soren stared at it for a while before throwing it at Xena with the purse.

"If the blonde in the keychain is your twin, tell her someone's crushing on her. I like her." He smirked, leaving with Sadie.

Xena was already crying as she ran out of the hallway.

"Xena, wait up!" Faye followed.

--

[Russia Origin Museum]

The museum is pulsing with life from young school kids who came to have a feel of history, and home kids who came with their parents.

Shayla sat somewhere in the middle of the museum, watching them with a ice cream cone to her lips which she's  eating like a model.

She loves sweet things, but at the same time she hates her love for it.

How could a grown woman like her not be able to do without sweets and candies and ice creams.

She threw the ice cream away, but she opened her bag and took a candy, unwrapped it into her mouth.

Yeah, that's how bad the obsession runs, and it's crazy at this point.

She likes coming here to watch kids from afar too, but she hates getting too close to them.

Whenever she gets too close, their presence always seemed to be choking her, and she hates that feel.

Her phone rang in her bag, but she didn't bother to pick.

It's definitely Caspian, but who's gonna pick his call after he left her hanging last night?

She left the harem without telling him or taking any of his guards with her, so he'd worry till he starts crying 

Her second phone rang, and she picked that one. It's her manager.

She has a separate phone for her agency actually.

"Yeah?" She stood.

She used her black shades, taking her bag and walking down the lane on her heels as she talked on phone.

It's about her dinner with her agency tonight. She almost forgot.

"Katherine!" A kid called behind her, and she stopped, amazed.

Why would anyone recognize her???

She kept walking.

"Katherine! I know it's you!"

The kid ran after her, and she quickened her pace, but the kid was in front of her in a whim.

"Get away, I'm not Katherine!" She pushed her crankily.

The kid fell, hurting her knees badly and started crying.

Shayla sighed "Kids will always be annoying,"

She turned back, seeing the kid's bleeding knees.

"Is the ground that rough? Fûck, you shouldn't be persistent like that, kiddo. Now you're hurt." She said.

"I went to your concert with my mum, and I drew your face, so don't try to deny. I know it's you." The kid said, bringing out the drawing.

"Your name is Jenna Wyoming?" Shayla scoffed, seeing her cute little signature on top of the drawing.

"Yes. I have a senior sister Cheyenne, and she wants to badly see you." Jenna smiled.

"Don't care about your sis, but who came to this museum with you?" Shayla asked.

"My mum, she went to the washroom when I saw you," Jenna explained, holding her hands.

"I love you so much, and I want to be a ballerina like you when I grow up." 

"No you can't. I'm a killer, I've killed too many. Just be yourself, don't be me." Shayla quickly glared.

"But you're beautiful, and you have long hair." Jenna said.

"That's all?" Shayla replied, glaring harder.

Jenna swallowed, suddenly realizing how scary she looks with the glare.

Shayla chuckled eventually.

"You're cute, let's get you to the museum clinic, your knees needs treatment." She said.

But before she could get up with Jenna, a blindfold covered her eyes from behind.

"Katherine!" Jenna shouted when she saw hoodlums, but she was blindfolded too.

Shayla sent her punch up, twice and hard and brutal as her adrenaline rushed to her arms.

Those two punches sent two hoodlums to the ground, but it wasn't enough, cos the third hoodlum sent Shayla's head to the metal railings.

Shayla dropped unconscious.

Blackout.

--

[Jazz Bar, 7PM]

Cheyenne terminated her promise of not coming here to drum again.

Her mum couldn't set up a meeting for her with Katherine.

Katherine's agency declined, so she's left helpless.

Tonight, she's going to drink and drum till the jazz tears, that's the goal to ease this hotness in her head.

She stepped into the bar, scrolling through her phone.

Caspian's company released their latest anklet jewelry today, and it sold out within eight minutes in all branches, topping the chart of treasure keepers.

"He should enjoy it while it lasts," she muttered, hissing out.

She was about to pocket the phone when she got a call from her mum, and she swiped green so fast.

"Is it set? Did you later arrange the meeting with Kath—"

"I can't find Jenna! I can't find her anywhere in the museum! I've been looking for her since 6!" Her mum's voice came.

"What!!" Cheyenne's body went cold.

"Stay where you are! Wait for me, and please tell me you already called the cops, I'll be right there!" 

She turned around to get out, but she was slammed against a familiar body.

Her phone fell from her to the neon ground, and her gasp was very loud when she staggered.

An arm went curved around her waist, pulling her back to safety. 

Her heartbeats picked up race.

[Ryker's One-room Apartment]

"Last week Saturday, we were both in the harem, getting beaten, but suddenly you stood and took me out with a run. I passed out, and I woke up to meet myself here! How?" Lilith complained, pacing around in front of Ryker who looks focused on what he's doing on his laptop.

Lilith hasn't been able to stop talking about that Saturday, and he's not ready to tell her he actually teleported.

She'd probably faint, judging by how dramatic she is, or she might think he's running insane.

The two options are off.

"Wait...you didn't teleport, did you?" Lilith suddenly asked, and his fingers stopped on top of the laptop keys.

"Are you a superhuman?" Lilith said again, and Ryker looked up.

"Shut up if you've ran out of words," 

"But I'm curious! How did I get home that Saturday? Did you carry me in your arms?" Lilith sat beside him.

"Wait...boss, you don't have a crush on me, right? I have a spec, for your information. You're madly handsome, but you're broke, so stop crushing on me if that's the case. You're not my type." She said.

Ryker pushed her away from that couch, and she fell on the ground.

"Stop using your mouth inappropriately, it's boring," he muttered, returning to his laptop.

But the door bursted open, and Caspian bursted inside.

"Mr. Caspian Yusluv? Oh my gawd!!" Lilith quickly hid behind Ryker.

"It was so easy to find you, Ryker. Your apartment is one of the top 3 smallest cages in this country, so it was a piece of peanut." Caspian smiled, widening his arms.

"You must have heard about my feat today. I became richer with my new jewelry, sold out across all branches. For your information, the first pieces of the anklet are on Katherine's gorgeous ankles. I should be happy now, and tonight should be a night of good fûck for me and my woman, but you had to go ruin it with your stupid hex!" Caspian yelled, picking the laptop from the table.

He threw it at the wall, breaking it and sending keys everywhere.

Lilith held her breaths, and Ryker remained silent, but his eyes are surveying the scattered keys on the ground.

"Today, I'm here to kill you." Caspian drew a gun suddenly like a prize, and Lilith fainted.

"You habitual bastard!!" Pearl yelled, coming inside with a barrel gun too.

Caspian faced the door, shooting at the same time with Pearl.

Their bullets went into their shoulders, and blood splashed.

They groaned agonizingly, their painful whimpers darkening the night as they staggered, feeling the weight of the metal in their bodies and the blood draining.

Ryker still stood there, unmoved.

Today isn't for Caspian. Caspian's day isn't even now at all.

Rivera rushed into the house.

Seeing what's going on, she only gasped for a second before rushing to her master to report.

"My lady left the harem without guards when you weren't around, and we got a report that she got kidnapped with a kid at the origin museum! It think it was done by your ops." She said breathlessly.

"What the fûck! My Katherine??" Caspian's eyes widened, his bleeding shoulder meaning nothing anymore.

"I wasn't around when she left, I was looking for a witch to lift her body hex like you instructed." Rivera said.

"Stop talking, and take me to her location with your wings! Use the tracker I planted in her anklet" Caspian ordered, and Rivera quickly flapped her wings, going down.

Caspian was about to climb her back for the flight when Pearl stood, holding her bleeding shoulder with a shrill laughter.

"Someone is faster than you," she said, looking at Ryker's spot.

Caspian's eyes went there, and truly, Ryker is gone.

Caspian's eyes widened again.

Ryker actually... teleported?

Meaning...he has the mantle?

Dafuq!

He literally jumped on Rivera's back, the the fae girl flapped her wings out of the house.

Lilith is still unconscious on couch when Pearl stood, smirking...

"Seriously, Ryker? You're going to give her your all in this lifetime too? While I'm here?"

--

[Wide Express]

Immediately Shayla woke up without the blindfold, she knew she was in a car. A madly speeding car reeking of smokes and drugs.

But the part that hit harder is that Jenna is also in the car with her.

She's unconscious though.

Seems the smokes induces weakness, cos right now she's at her weakest.

Moving a finger feels like a task, talking feels harder.

She was able to tilt up a bit though, and she had a glance at the driver in front. Leader of the hoodlums.

"You're obviously after me,, so let her go. She's just a kid." She said, almost not hearing her own voice.

"Her mistake was being there with you, so she's bound to perish with you, sister. You have no idea how long I've been planning this day." A husky voice came from the hoodlum.

He glanced at Shayla, grinning and flashing his set of rotten teeth.

It's Kade.

"Last lifetime, Caspian killed me cos of you, and I promised to make you pay the dues. This lifetime, I reincarnated as an orphan and found myself grown in an orphanage with my memories intact. But you, you dared to reincarnate as a rich woman? A popular ballerina? You deserve every bad thing I have in mind!" He barked.

"I dunno what you're talking about..." Shayla groaned, and Jenna stirred.

"Of course you don't, your stupid self didn't reincarnate with your memories, but it's ok... Your life's about to be over." Kade laughed, stepping on the gas when they got to the main highway.

The car sped up again, buildings whipping past them like ants and the breeze so terribly cold.

Jenna woke up.

She started crying when she saw what's going on.

"Are we gonna die? Are we?"

"No. Never" Shayla replied, pushing herself up forcefully without minding her breathing limbs.

She locked Kade's neck from behind, choking him with the leather backrest of the driver's seat.

"Then... let's go to hell together, you motherfucker! How dare you try to kill me with a kid!!" She screamed, squeezing his throat.

Blood stopped rushing in his body, and he nearly went rigid, but his hands didn't leave the steering.

He kept driving on the bridge.

"You fuckin...bitc...

"Katherine! Bridge! The bridge!!" Jenna screamed, and Shayla's head went up, fast.

Kade actually drove to the bridge edge as it's an uncompleted bridge still under construction.

The car crashed into the bridge rails, breaking it completely.

The water below is dark, wide and vast. It's the coldest in this continent, and surviving inside is impossible.

Shayla's eyes widened as her life flashed before her.

"Let's go to hell together like you want!" Kade remarked, letting go of the steering as the car fell.

Shayla let go of his throat, holding Jenna in her arms.

The little girl closed her eyes, and Shayla did the same.

But a minute before the car lands in the dark ocean, the ocean changed form, going from liquid to blue ice.

The car landed on the frozen ice, and the hard impact made the girls lose their consciousness.

Their heads slammed on the windows, and the glasses broke, tearing their facial flesh.

A glass pierced Shayla's chest, her blood soaked Jenna's body.

The car fuel pipe bursted, and the fuel poured out to the car body.

Ryker landed on the ice breathlessly, tearing the car door open.

He got Shayla out first, laying her on the ice very far from the car, then he went back to get Jenna.

Immediately he took the little girl out of the car, the car blew up, burning even on his hard ice.

He set Jenna down and went back to Shayla who's not even breathing anymore.

Seems the glass pierce on her chest reached her heart and grazed it, cos the bleeding is much.

Carefully, he removed the glass and tried healing her, but her body didn't even respond to his healing.

It refused to heal.

She refused to breath, and that scared him so much.

Is it because of her resentment? The resentment she has for him in the last lifetime must still be so strong that her body rejects his healing.

She just lay there, like a corpse.

He pressed two hands down on her bleeding chest, sniffling.

"Please... please, Shayla. Please, can you just let me heal you? Can you breath back to life? Please..." 

He tried again with blood soaked hands, but it's still the same.

"You haven't hated me enough. You need to come back and hate me more. You still have more slaps to give me, and more wicked things to do to me. Please... just..."

He paused, remembering something.

This is the human realm, and she's currently human without her powers, so she should respond to humanly resuscitation ways.

He tilted her up from the ground, and at the same time, Caspian and Rivera landed on the ice.

Caspian jumped down from Rivera's wings, and he started running forward.

"Katherine!" He shouted fearfully, seeing all the blood.

"No. It's always been Shayla, and will always be." Ryker replied, lowered his bent figure to Shayla's tilted body.

He opened her mouth with his tongue, then pressed his mouth down on hers, breathing inside.

"Shayla...."

Caspian watched the tongue going in, and his head exploded.

"Fucker! I'm going to kill you!!!" He growled, furiously snatching a gun from Rivera.

He shot once, twice, thrice. 

The icy ground shook with the deadly quake of the gunshots, and the three bullets entered Ryker's upper body, tearing into his shirt and penetrating into his skin, but he couldn't even feel the darn pain.

He kept his mouth sealed on Shayla's mouth, breathing inside again, whispering...

"Shayla......"

Caspian cocked the gun once more, ready to release bullets again, but he could only go one step close before the unexpected happened.

The fire tattoo on Shayla's back glittered dangerously, and the fire flash carried Caspian, throwing him back in a brutal backflip.

His head pounded the ice.

[Jazz Bar]

As Cheyenne's eyes met Sage's own, she wasn't aware a tear had dropped from her eyeballs. 

She was only alerted when it fell on her cleavage, and then the loud thudding of her heart as Sage's arm stayed belted on her waist.

He's crying too, but his are tears of ultimate joy.

He looks so worn-out, tired like he lost sleep for days which is true.

"Che," he whispered, the sound of the drinking people around them nearly drowning it as his voice was too emotional.

"Sage" she replied, not believing the amount of emotion stealing her own voice too.

Slowly, Sage let go of the waist, and her hands immediately reached for his face, touching...

"Sage," she repeated, smiling with the tears of joy.

"Che, it's me. Your crybaby co-knight whom you said you didn't love" He replied with a bigger smile.

"You fuckin jerk!" Cheyenne broke into real tears, hugging him like her air, her breaths.

Sage's arms went gilded around her, grip too tight that it's even painful on their skins.

"I had a hunch on Saturday and I've been waiting here since then. I knew it was you..." He sniffled.

"I've been searching senselessly too. I just knew you were somewhere, but I never expected it to be here. I missed you so much." Cheyenne hiccupped.

"I missed you more, growing up with my last life memories was torture," he replied, resting his chin on her shoulder properly.

"Me too, I was—"

Cheyenne stopped talking, gasping and breaking the hug suddenly.

"I nearly forgot! My sister! She went missing and I have to find her!"

"Your sister?" He blinked, and she nodded, pulling him out of the bar.

Her phone rang.

[Morregan Health Hospital]

"Make way, fuckin make way!!" Rivera shouted at the top of her voice as she ran with the paramedics into the hallway, pushing Caspian's wheeled stretcher inside.

Caspian's head is wrapped with a white shawl, but even the shawl is soaked with his blood, everywhere's red, and he's deeply unconscious.

After his head pounded the ice earlier at the frozen ocean, he got a terrible crack in his skull, and it has been going from bad to worse.

The patients on their wheelchair in the hall quick moved aside, and the doctors running on shifts gyrated when they saw the face of the person laying on the bloody stretcher.

"Caspian Yusluv?"

"It's him!"

"Geez! How did that happen?"

"Oh my gawd is he gonna survive?"

"Move!!!" Rivera pushed an intern out of the way, and they were directed to the emergency unit.

Arriving there, Doctor Blaise was already waiting.

As Caspian was moved from the stretcher to the bed, Rivera stepped aside and let Blaise do his job.

Blaise got to work on the cracked skull, trying to stop the bleeding first.

Rivera stood by the wall outside, panicking.

"You're a fae, not human. You should be able to heal him!" One of the harem guards said beside her, but she's not even in the mood to reply.

"Wait...do you only have wings to fly? Is that the only ability you posess?" The guard continued egging her.

Rivera gifted the guard a kick, and he fell to his knees forcefully.

"I'm a fae, of course my healing ability is limited. I can't heal such deep crack on his skull. I can only wait for the doctors to perform surgery and do my own part afterwards, so quit ranting on my face and let's just await the best!" Rivera spat.

The guard stood, rolling eyes and stepping back.

Another guard came in with a squad.

"Lady Katherine has been taken back to the harem. She's getting surgery at home since you've ordered us not to bring her to the hospital." He reported.

"We can't bring her to the hospital, the harem is safer now that the bastard got the ice mantle. Who knows when he might plan to kidnap her for himself?" Rivera replied.

"But according to you, the fire mark on her back glittered, doesn't that mean he got her powers back? And if she got her powers back, it'll include her memories, I mean memories of how the bastard killed her. She'd hate him more." The guard said.

"The mark glittered doesn't mean she has her memories back. I and boss made sure her memories and powers are sealed  The glitter was just a glitch cos the bastard kissed her." Rivera replied, sighing.

"How's her surgery going?"

"The doctor said the piece of glass really grazed her heart, resulting to  blood loss, but she'd be fine after replenishment." The guard reported.

"And the idiot Kade?" Rivera asked.

"He's in the crib at the harem, we're waiting for his fate." The guard further provided.

Rivera took a deep breath, and Doctor Blaise came out of the EU.

"We managed to stop the bleeding, but there are too many concussions, and the crack is in his occipital lobe. I and my team are currently preparing for surgery." He reported.

"Good," Rivera sighed.

[Ryker's One Room Apartment]

Standing in front of the long covered mirror, Ryker looks hopeless.

He's shirtless, black slacks hung low on his taut waist, and his palms are soaked with Shayla's blood.

His body still has the three bullets from Caspian, and he's groaning as he watched blood trickle out of them.

He unveiled the mirror, looking at his own reflection for a minute before sending his punch into it, shattering it to bits.

Earlier when Shayla's back tattoo glittered and carried Caspian to the ice, he had a little hope.

Very little, that she was back, but the hope crashed as fast as it came cos she never woke up.

Her mark only reacted that way cos Caspian was shooting him.

She hates him, but her mark doesn't. She hates him, but her soul and subconscious is still protecting him instead of taking his healing.

Does he even deserve that?

He had to let Rivera take her cos him doing it would have been greedy.

Also, he let them take Kade cos only Shayla has the right to kill that dog.

He killed Liza in Luminaria, he won't make the mistake of doing the same to Kade.

Shayla deserves to exact her revenge by herself too.

His hand went up to his bulleted body, and he removed the bullets from his flesh one by one, dropping the three on the ground.

The wounds healed immediately, and arms wrapped around him from behind, causing him to look up.

"Don't you pity me?" Pearl's voice came, her fingertips entering his bellybutton space.

Judging by the cracks in her voice, she's definitely crying.

"Don't you? I've loved you for two lifetimes, and this is the third one, but still...you don't see me!" She complained, running her fingers up on his chest, caressing.

"Get off," Ryker said, silently but with enough toxicity.

"No, I refuse to." She tightened her arms around him.

Ryker went down, slipping easily out of her grip, then his hand went up to grip her by the hair, flinging her forward and thudding her body down on the shatter of mirrors.

"Fûck!! How longer will you hurt me? How longer!!!" She cried, bleeding from the arms, legs, back, face, tummy and every visible part of her skin. The mirror shards hooked clustered in her flesh.

"How did you enter?" He muttered, taking one step away.

"Cos this is an unguarded apartment for crying out loud! Anyone can enter! Your fake life apartment! You're not poor! You're only punishing yourself for what? That bîtch? What does she even know about you! Nothing! No one knows you better than me! No one deserves you better! Why not just look at me for once, please..." Pearl stood, looking pitiful in her bloody state.

"In this lifetime, I was born without a mantle at all. I'm a weak human who has to rely on guns and bullets to fight enemies, so I'm begging you, genuinely. Be mine, and I'll be yours for the rest of my life! Aren't I pretty? Aren't I? I'm prettier than her so..."

Pearl was already on the ground again before she could finish.

Ryker waved her off, and her body swept the shards of mirrors once again,  giving her more deep cuts.

Her blood painted the oakwood ground, and one of the shards even pierced her eye corner, nearly blinding her, but it wasn't over.

Ryker removed the metal mirror frame and folded it, bringing it down on her body—the middle of her tummy.

Her ribcage broke in one part, and the bone protruded out of her tummy as she screamed out again, crying in blood and pain, sweat and tears.

"Don't ever compare your pathetic self to her, and a bîtch? Look at yourself in the mirror, you'll find out who's really one. She doesn't have to beg me for attention like you do, but she still has my everything including my life and heart!" Ryker growled, each word meaningful and emotive.

Pearl's tears paused, and she pushed out strangled breaths as the last word echoed in her ears.

Heart? 

His heart?

Is he really in love with... Shayla?

"Get up, and get out. You'd be dead already if you did this in Luminaria, but you're lucky my bloodthirst is in half right now. Get lost!!" He yelled.

Pearl crawled out of the room, her blood forming a trail.

As she crawled, her tears mopped the ground, and her heart.

If she can't have him this lifetime as a powerless human, then living is meaningless.

She doesn't deserve to live.

After she left, Ryker threw on a cotton shirt and left the room too, returning to the living room.

Lilith is still unconscious on the couch, no idea why she has been out for so long.

Then... Jenna is sitting on another couch, looking calm.

She's the only one Ryker took from the frozen ocean.

He had branched at an hospital to have her wounds taken care of before taking her here.

Her wounds are very minor.

Also, she gave him her sister and mother's mobile line, so he has called them to come pick her up.

Ryker squatted in front of her, and she blinked cutely, reminding her of Shayla's cute blinking attempts.

"I'm sure the wound on your knees wasn't caused by the accident," he glanced at her knees.

"I met Katherine at the museum, and I was persistently calling her, so she.... pushed me mistakenly." She replied.

"Katherine?" He looked up.

Jenna nodded.

"On her behalf, I'm sorry." He apologized without hesitation.

"No no... it's not necessary. I still love her, and she's my role model cos she's pretty and has long hair like me." Jenna replied, and Ryker pressed his lips together in the corners.

"But...who are you? Why are you kind to me? You even helped me call my sister and mom" Jenna asked.

Before Ryker could answer that, Lilith was awake.

She jumped up from the couch behind.

"Oh my head!" She held her temples, seeing Jenna with Ryker.

"I passed out for how many minutes, and there's already a kid here? Also, what's with your bloody hands!" 

That was all she could say before the door bursted open.

Cheyenne and Sage rushed inside with Mrs. Wyoming.

"Cheyenne!" Jenna smiled.

"Oh my baby!" Cheyenne knelt completely in front of her, hugging the little girl and checking out her subtly wounded face.

"Gosh, thankfully we have you back now. We were running wild with thoughts,"

Mrs. Wyoming knelt too, taking Jenna from her.

"I already scattered the museum with cops,never knew you've gone as far as this place...I'm so sorry I should have been more careful." The woman said, rubbing Jenna's face.

"It's not your fault, mum, Cheyenne, I'm fine now." Jenna smiled.

All these while, Sage is still not far from the door where he stood.

His eyes are impossibly wide, filled with emotions all-over again as he stared at Ryker.

He's the first person he saw when he entered earlier, and Ryker is looking at him surprisingly too.

"Sage," he muttered.

"Your Lordship," Sage replied.

That single word made Cheyenne shoot up, her eyes popping wide.

Her shoulder bag fell, and she covered her mouth with her palm.

"You made the call, your lordship?" 

Ryker nodded, smiling lightly.

"My lordship!!" Sage bursted, breaking into a small run to him.

He hugged Ryker dearly.

Cheyenne joined the hug, triple hold.

"You dunno how hard I've been searching for you using my parents network! I searched like crazy!" Sage wailed.

"Me too! I waspulling all nighters. I didn't know today would be the day I meet you and sage!" Cheyenne added.

Ryker hugged them back with one arm each, another subtle smile escaping his lips.

"By the look of things, you both got reborn into affluent families, that's the best news I've heard in this lifetime." He said.

Mrs. Wyoming, Jenna and Lilith stayed quiet, wondering what's really happening.

"Am I the only one not understanding?" Lilith muttered.

"You just said my mind, young girl." Mrs. Wyoming sighed.

"Reborn? Think I heard that in church before." Jenna mumbled.

The knights didn't break the hug for so many minutes, and when they finally did, they sighted Lilith, and their protective instincts kicked in.

They both literally flew to her.

Cheyenne grabbed her neck, and Sage gripped her hair, the attack so cleanly and intentionally done.

"What are you doing here in this lifetime again? To cause trouble like you always did?" Cheyenne spat.

"Huh? Banshee?" Sage added.

"Let me....go!!" Lilith screamed, struggling.

"She wasn't reborn with memories, let her go." Ryker said behind.

They both quickly let go of her.

"What was that? I did nothing to deserve neck grabbing and hair pulling!!" She yelled at the two, sneering before going to Ryker.

"Look, boss. I dunno what this madness in your house today is about, and I don't care about how you're going to get cash, but you better send me money for beverages and tonic cos my blood is dry! Ugh!" She snarled at his face and left, slamming the door.

Sage and Cheyenne laughed out loud at the same time.

"She's very funny in this lifetime, how did she end up working with his lordship?" Sage said.

"Beats me, she's now cute." Cheyenne replied, sighing.

Mrs. Wyoming eventually left with Jenna, leaving the knights with their newfound lord.

"Please...tell me this poor house is a play, I can't imagine you with porness." Sage said.

Ryker settled on a couch, his mind wandering around Shayla, so damn worried about her health.

"It's self-punishment for killing her, and our baby." He said.

"Her ladyship was pregnant?" Cheyenne's eyes widened madly again, getting another shock.

Ryker shut his eyes.

"Oh...the resentment of a pregnant goddess is bad." Sage muttered.

"Why am I having this hunch that she's Katherine though? I saw a drawing of Katherine and she's exactly her ladyship." Cheyenne said.

"She is," Ryker opened his eyes, leaning forward.

"She is???" Cheyenne and Sage chorused.

Another shock.

"And she's getting married to Caspian, in three days." Ryker replied, the words bitter in his mouth.

"What!!!" The knights let out another loud chorus.

"I'm going to go stake out at the harem gate," Ryker stood.

"We'll follow you," Cheyenne said.

"No. You both go to the glass mansion, find the new key card in the bedroom drawer, south part." Ryker explained, getting out of the house.

[Cold-Hearted Harem]

The wound on Shayla's chest has stopped bleeding, thanks to the doctors professionalism.

But the blood transfusion is failing presently.

Her blood group is weird and isn't even anywhere in the blood banks, so they had to risk using the closest one to her blood colour.

But as the blood entered her body with the pipes, it left her body through her mouth and ears.

They had to discard the blood bags and get the pipes out of her body, then they started looking for alternatives, calling Rivera to inform her of the latest development.

They were still on the call when Shayla regained consciousness.

Pale lips, white eyeballs, overall pale face and fragile body. It's like she lost pounds of flesh within that small period, and her legs are shaking on the ground as she stood.

"Ma'am, it's only advisable to lay down right now. Your condition is too critical." The doctors adviced.

"Jenna, the kid. Where's she?" She asked weakly, holding the drip stand beside her bed for balance.

"She was taken by the other man," the doctors replied.

"And the hoodlum?" She asked, holding her chest.

"Kade? He has been locked in the crib," the doctors replied.

Shayla made her way to the crib, holding the wall for support as she walked the ground with turning eyes, painful chest and empty tummy.

She feels totally hollowed out.

No amount of plea from the doctors and guards could stop her till she arrived at the crib.

The door was opened for her, and she picked a rusty knife from the ground, staggering inside.

A guard quickly held her before she could fall, but she detached and went in fully, meeting Kade on his knees, tied to metal bars on the ground.

He must have been horribly beaten cos his clothes are torn with his flesh, few of his tooth are on the ground, and his eyes are nearly not in the sockets anymore.

Like a living dead.

Using the knife blade, Shayla raised his chin, and he smiled vengefully as their eyes met.

"Do you know...that in our last lifetime, I was your twin? We were both not only birthed by Liza, but our father was also the same! We both have same human father who was killed immediately you were given birth to. But me...I stayed in the womb for extra five years, cos of what? Cos you're the goddess who's not supposed to share a womb with anybody at the same time. Your powerful aura held me back in Liza's womb for five extra years!"

"I used to say I hated you because you shared a mother with me, but truth is I hated you cos you were birthed before me, and because of you I had to spend five extra years! Another truth is that...you gods will always be the cause of the misfortunes of humans. To exist, you'll trample on anyone! You all and the deities in your heavens are all hypocrites!"

"I didn't choose to be a goddess," Shayla's powerful voice dropped, and Kade shivered hard, the the blade on his chin burning hot.

"I didn't chose to share a womb with you and I didn't chose to hold you back for five years! Pushing faults around? Blame the heavens! Blame the gods hierarchy. I'd have spared you if you didn't involve a kid, but involving a kid was mindless, and you'll pay with your life!" 

She jammed the knife into his neck, slicing his throat easily.

Kade fell to his death on the ground, and she dumped the bloody knife on his body before stumbling out of the room with tears in her eyes.

She went on her knees, and the guards all knelt around her, waiting to pick her up but also scared to touch her since everyone knows her bad temper.

She's too weak. Losing blood is too dangerous for a goddess since every drop of her blood is priceless.

She needs to replenish her blood, or she falls into a deep sleep which she doesn't even want.

She crawled back to her room, ordering for a full plate of fruits salad which was immediately brought.

She ate it all without leaving crumbs, gathering little energy with that, then she sat balled on the foot of her bed.

She took the watermelon bracelet from where she dropped it last night.

Her tears dropped on the bracelet beads before she knew it, and she folded her palms tightly on it, shoving her red sickly face into her weak knees.

Her body temperature so fuckin hot.

[Morregan Health Hospital, Next Morning]

Immediately Caspian was wheeled back to the ward, Rivera did her little healing work since the damage is now minimal.

She exhausted a lot of power from her core though, but it was worth it.

Caspian's eyes opened, and he sat up in bed, catching his breaths as if his whole unconsciousness was a running marathon game.

"Katherine is home, and the doctors say she's in her room, better than before. She still needs blood, though. She reported.

How could the mantle be in him? He froze the ocean, meaning he's even more powerful than his last life, meaning the mantle is his originally! Meaning I won't ever get to have the mantle in me! My long life is a punishment my the heavens, and very soon, even my wealth will start going down! The moment she gets too close to Ryker, my life is over!" Caspian ranted, jumping off the bed.

"That can't happen...it can't.." he shook his head, obviously scared.

Rivera followed her out of the ward.

"Caspian Yusluv, where did he go?"

"He left with his female guard just now, and he looked fine." The nurses reported.

"He looked really, fine..." The nurses repeated like they're under a spell.

"Doctor Blaise?" 

The call of his name made him turn, sighing out loud when he saw Winona and Kasia behind.

"Next checkup is next month, my misses." He said jovially.

"Kasia teaches in a daycare, and she brought one of her pupils, so I tagged along. It's not about our ulcer this time around." Winona cleared.

"Oh? Bring me to the kid then," Blaise replied, and he was led away.

Astor appeared from the last corner of the hall with folded fists, but he didn't stay for long before leaving.

[Morozov Residence]

This pesticide is her last resort, and she's crying regretfully as she sprayed

She could still remember how he told her on their wedding day that he'd torment her in every lifetime, and he's really doing just that.

She sniffled, and her nose hurts from overdoing it.

She's carrying Sensei, and she dropped the rabbit on Xena's head.
