[Cold-Hearted Harem]

Rivera and Caspian picked up the witch from a tarot shop on their way home, all ready to lift the hex when they arrived, but on getting home, Shayla was nowhere to be found.

Not in her room, not in the hall. Just the guards, and Kade's body in the crib. Even the doctors have no idea.

"Where is she?" Caspian asked, very low but hard.

No one could provide an answer.

"Where is she!!!" He yelled impatiently, pulling out his gun and aiming at two, shooting them down to calm, but it did nothing.

"Where is my fiancee!!!!" He gnarled, shooting the third.

--

Meanwhile, Shayla is outside the harem gate.

She was already out before Caspian arrived earlier, she just waited for him to go in before she moved her car, slowly down the lane.

Eyes and face is still pale, and she looks terribly sick, her hands barely gripping the steering wheel well.

Tears dropped from her chin to her laps as she's wearing bum shorts.

No destination in mind, she's just driving, with wounded chest, blood-starved system, and sick essence. She can't even feel her own movements, but she's conscious enough to know a car has been following her, slowly like a trail.

The car has been following her since she drove out of the harem lane.

She overlooked and continued driving, neither stopping nor halting till a destination came to mind 

The bridge where she fell last night.

She drove there, and she wasn't surprised to see the ocean is still frozen, but there are red RISKY tapes surrounding it like snakes, to keep people from climbing the ice.

She got out of her car and climbed anyways, walking barefoot on the ice and wiping the tear on her chin.

The ice felt hot on her feet cos her warm aura is easily making it hot, reddening her toes, but it's not as cold as this feeling pulling at her chest and threatening to tear it apart.

The memories, they're too heavy to carry, and the remembrance isn't bliss at all.

She could still see the sword in her tummy, piercing her together with him, she could still hear the cries of their most baby, but why can't she hear her own heart shattering?

Why is her heart still beating as if everything's fine?

Why is it still functioning well?

Why?

Just why? 

She felt something pulling at her smallest finger, and her eyes went down to the finger.

She raised it, and at the base of that finger, a dull red thread curled, pulling occasionally.

The thread extended with a line from her finger, and she looked forward, tracing where the other end of the rope will be.

Her feet moved slowly from her position when she saw where the thread ended at.

It's on the smallest finger of another person who's also standing on the ice with her, behind her.

She'd recognize those fingers even if they turn to ashes.

Her eyes went up.

The moment she saw Ryker, she turned around, her steps crazily quick as she walked away.

With a desperate run, Ryker was with her, hugged her from behind like she's everything that matters.

Like she's every importance, every essence, every length and breath of existence. Like she's the reason.

His voice was heavy with both guilt and starving emotions as he talked..

"Sorry won't fix the mess I made, and I don't care if you're with your memory or not, but please...don't run away from me. I thought I'd be able to take it, but I'm weaker than weak when it's about you. Please look at me, for once... please...

Shayla tore his arms away from herself, facing him and throwing a hard slap at his face.

"Good. You're doing well." He nodded regretfully, which cheek burning.

Her eyes were already red and teary as she brought her hand down to the thread on her finger.

She cut the thread to pieces, throwing it at his face.

"Hate can't even explain what I feel for you... leave me alone!!! I'll just go to the Forsaken Vale and forsake my mantle like Caspian did. I'll become a long-lived human like him and be with him forever cos I love him now, not you...a baby killer!" She ranted, her voice painful, hurtful, too much.

She was breaking as she talked, and her voice might be steady, but she looks so much apart, and her eyes are so dark and cold.

"You got your memories back..." Ryker muttered.

"Don't come closer, and don't worry, I'll send you an invite to my wedding party in three days, and you dare not make that wedding plan you terrible baby-killer bastard!" She spat, returning to her fast walk.

Ryker followed again, grabbing her for the second time.

She looked back, gave him a slash on the chest with her hand.

Ryker fell back on the ice, bleeding from the mouth as his insides turned overly hot with that one strike.

Her eyes was gold for a second before she balanced back on her feet.

"Don't touch me, you're nothing to me anymore!" She said, wanting to continue her walk again, but...

She noticed something pulling her finger again, and she checked to see it's another thread.

A new thread has grown, connecting her to Ryker again.

She cut it like she did the first one, and another one grew almost immediately, frustrating the hell out of her life.

"You damn ugly thing! Why not leave my fingers! I do not want to love this scum! I don't want to!! Please just...get away!!" She yelled frustratingly.

In anger, she threw her burning Firestone of fire at Ryker who's still on the ground.

He didn't move, so the fire landed on him, burning through his clothes and extending to his skin.

Shayla's eyes widened, and she let out an unplanned gasp...

"Ry!"

Shayla's arm sent a quick wave forward, and she fire went off immediately, but smokes enveloped him as the blaze was too powerful.

Ryker continued sitting there, getting a hold of his chest as a small trail of hot blood crawled out of his mouth, down to his chin and chest.

Almost instantly, Shayla held her chest too, a thin line of black blood came out of her lips corner.

Her hand went up to wipe it, but the tiredness she felt afterwards was insane, she staggered.

She needs blood—his blood, for replenishment. Otherwise, she'd really fall into that deep sleep.

She's losing time, and it's unadvisable, but she has no intention of taking his blood.

Ryker stood, the smokes still oozing out of his half burnt clothes as he dipped his hand in his pocket.

He brought out a small vial containing his blood that he drained, and he stretched the vial to her.

Immediately sue saw it, her thirst for replenishment heightened, but she held back, staring with balled fists, the space between them seeming to be growing bigger and bigger.

She could smell his blood even when enclosed in that vial.

The sweet smell of petals, and the darkness of doom. 

She could smell him so much it made her fold her palms tighter, hurting herself with the nails.

"Since the accident happened last night, I knew you'd need my blood. I'm not asking you to take it and forgive me, but I don't want you to fall into the deep sleep for years. Here, take it." He sent it to her through the air.

The vial hovered in front of Shayla, but she didn't take it.

Another drop of black toxic blood fell from her nose, and she sniffled, her eyes not leaving Ryker's smoky body and his bleeding lips.

She wiped the tear on her chin again, grabbing the vial of blood.

She opened it and drank every drop, then she threw the vial on the ground, breaking it somewhere.

Ryker smiled through his bloody lips, nodding.

"You did well, thank you."

"I took it because I really didn't want to fall into a deep sleep when my wedding is only in a few days. Like I said, I'll send you the invite myself, and you must come." She said, turning swiftly before vanishing.

Ryker went on a knee on the ice, trying to retch instead of throwing up blood.

"You're really something, aren't you, Ryker?" A known voice dropped behind him, and he lifted himself, facing Eternus.

The one thousand year looks good on him, the long white hair never changed, even the bodily build and facial features stayed good.

"I'm anticipating to see where all these ends at, but looks like it won't end well after all. If she drops her mantle like Caspian did, then the heavens will dump it on someone else, and that someone else will be your new partner. Also, she talked about inviting you to her wedding, seems like you really have no hope anymore, and you're finally gonna cry," he fanned himself with a fancy foldable fan as he spoke.

His long coat is almost dragging the iced ground.

Ryker didn't reply. The urge to reply wasn't even building.

"I begged you that day, didn't I? Don't do what you had in mind, but you went ahead and pierced your own child in her belly," Eternus continued.

"I didn't know!!" Ryker finally growled painfully.

"You do not trust anyone, Ryker. I am a seer, and I might not be able to see your future back then, but I saw darkness which meant calamity, and I knew whatever you did would lead to gore. The heavens might be hypocrites, but trust me there are still deities over there who are on your side! Also, your partner isn't just any partner, she's Shayla, and she loves you so deep that it broke the heavens plans and she got pregnant without growing a thread for you. Do you know how meaningful that is? But you ruined it with your impatience, so don't expect her to come around!" Eternus chastised.

Ryker went mute, but he shut his eyes and tears rolled out.

"Now she reincarnated again, do you know that in this lifetime, her powers and memories were sealed by Caspian? She'd only get the two back if her hatred for you stops, but look...she already got them back, meaning she doesn't even hate you! You put an hex on her which wasn't supposed to reincarnate with her, but it did, cos she feels for you more than you do for her." Eternus continued.

"She's getting married to him," Ryker replied in a regretful mutter. "And I must do the wedding planning." 

Eternus stayed quiet this time, but he sighed so loud.

"Last lifetime, you couldn't do anything cos out future was dark. Is everywhere dark again in this lifetime?" Ryker asked, not bothering to wipe his tears.

"I can clearly see your future and hers, as we speak." Eternus sighed again, fanning faster even on the ice and the cold weather.

"What's it gonna be?" Ryker asked.

"What if I tell you to wait, don't do anything, go ahead with the wedding planning?" Eternus replied, finally folding the fan.

"Don't act rash like you did in Luminaria, just stay calm. For once, stay calm about her. Can you do that, Ryker?" He asked genuinely.

Ryker was silent, but his Adam's apple moved, swallowing lumps as he imagined her dumping the mantle and walking down the aisle with Caspian. He died multiple times on that spot.

"I'll take that as a yes," Eternus nodded, turning around.

He smiled before vanishing off.

[Cold-Hearted Harem]

Caspian released a deep breath, rubbing her shoulders and smiling back gladly.

She didn't ask questions and didn't act crazy about it? The former Katherine would have probably said she's not interested in witchy stuff, but she actually sat for it?

His Katherine.

"There was something in my body, that's why you've been rejecting intimacy? And you didn't tell me?" She queried.

[Foxx Ballet Center]

"Thank you," Blaise looked back.

"It's nothing" she smiled.

"He's handsome, have you ever thought of that?" Dominika whispered beside her.

"Stop, muum." She frowned.

Dominika chuckled.

Lilith came with Jojo her mum, both are at the upper part of the arena.

"I still can't believe my boss is a multi-billionare! Owner of FrostFire? RyLa sold out in two minutes after it came out last month! I'm in heaven! And Katherine is his heavenly partner? Ouu!" She held her chest.

"Being the secretary of the richest man in the world is a flex..." Jojo replied.

"Big flex! I have two cars now, and we have a new house, what more?" Lilith laughed, and Jojo stroked her cheek.

Cheyenne and Sage are standing by the upper railings too.

Mrs. Wyoming and Jenna aren't far from them, and the woman had to carry Jenna on her shoulders since she's not tall enough.

She's the loudest screamer here, and the ribbons in her hair were dangling as her voice rang.

Cheyenne is holding Sage's hand in an interlock, and when Sage's eyes met with Soren's at the base, Soren smiled, and Sage replied with a wink.

Even Eternus is at a corner.

Mercury is present, too.

Mr. And Mrs Pierce are at the close front where they sat caging Ryker in their middle.

The three are all very focused on the stage, none talked.

As Shayla danced her Ophelia ballet performance on stage, her eyes hooked with Ryker's throughout, and his smile didn't disappear for once.

It stayed glued as he watched the most beautiful thing ever.

She gave her best performance yet, his eyes empowered her. She fell into it and became one with it.

Her on stage is magical.

She's wearing a pale pink outfit, and her aesthetic changed a lot, back to her soft girl era, but soft girl with a touch of craziness, for him only.

Her Ophelia dance ended inside the spotlight with her ankles crossed, and the arena erupted with a loud scream, shaking the base.

Ryker took it as his cue to act. 

He carried the bouquet of blue roses and pink flowers he bought, and he climbed the stage.

The screams heightened when he handed her the bouquet, and she took it with a adorable smile.

"Thank you,"

"That was a phenomenal performance, my love. I love you" he replied.

The screams went up again, howls and yells and insane loudness.

The blush that crept to Shayla's cheeks were redder than crimson as she hugged him back.

"I love you, baby." she returned, pulling apart to give him a kiss, deep but short.

Ryker removed her pointe shoes and carried her in his arms immediately.

The screams of the fans followed them to the backstage.

She already signed autographs before the start of the concert, so he's free to have her all to himself.

Even her team wouldn't dare stop him, and as he stepped out of the backstage to the park, their lips connected again, and she pushed his head closer with the bouquet.

Their red threads pulled at their fingers. When they broke the kiss, they looked up at the same time, seeing the divine bright ray of light from the open sky, their smiles mirrored.

They started levitating, getting swallowed bit by bit, by the ray.

They were almost all the way into the brightness when Dominika and the rest rushed out to them.

"It's tonight?" Dominika gasped.

"I suspected it, I swear" Rumi said.

"Shay!" Xena waved.

"Have fun, you both," Mrs. Pierce said with a nod.

"No fights!" Mr. Pierce added.

"Real, no fights.." Cheyenne said.

"I'll miss you, boss, and goddess," Sage smiled.

"You both are their knights, you have the right to go visit them over there once a year," Eternus replied.

"I know, but who'll take care of them over there?" Cheyenne muttered.

"There'll be divine maids, don't be silly" Dominika said.

"Good luck with the ascension, you both" Blaise said.

Till the ray swallowed them completely, Shayla was waving, and Ryker was smiling.

A jewelry fell at Dominika's feet, and she picked it up surprisingly.

It's Ryker's toe ring. The red ruby ring she put on him after birthing him.

She folded her palms on it, smiling as she looked up again.

So far the godslaying sword remains hidden, her duty as the godslayer is on hold. The sword doesn't even seem like it's inexistence anymore.

She feels too fulfilled.

"Cheers to a happy night of fulfillment, drinks everyone?" Rumi stood in front of them all.

"In!!" They chorused, and everyone laughed heartily.

--

[The Conjugation Vale]

The gods travelled past all the Vale of gods till they reached the conjugal point. The bliss deities were already waiting.

Their outfits changed to red dragging robes, long hairs including Ryker's.

Shayla got a new makeover too, and the two got their signs in the middle of their foreheads.

Crystal ice on Ryker's.

Burning fire on Shayla's.

They were glossing with joy as they kowtowed in front of the old deities, and the opening talisman card to the nine heavens was injected into their bodies at the same time.

"Be together, till the dust turns, and till the world ends. Save the helpless when the time is right, and help the needy constantly. The nine heavens door will open once every month, and you can visit your loved ones in the human realm," the deities instructed.

"Thank you, deities." The partners chorused.

That was all, before their bodies travelled again, out of the conjugation vale.

They ascended into the nine heavens.

Sparks followed their flight.

                              --

[Five Years Later, Nine Heavens]

Paradise would be an alternative name for the abode.

Flowers and blue roses surrounded it, pure fog water and tall flower trees with colourful birds singing melodiously in top of them.

There are too many buildings, all white and gold, some are black, though. The special buildings.

Ryker's Phoria, Bamboo Groove, Red pavilion. Everything's here too.

Even the Pink Island.

The fire and ice palace are beside each other, moved here already.

Servants in white are walking the grounds, hurrying to get their duties done when a pretty little girl ran out of the Fire Palace.

Her right eye is blue like Ryker's, and her left eye is gold like Shayla's. 

She's got insanely long hair like Shayla's, pale skin like Ryker's, cutely small hands and feet like Shayla's, and long lashes.

She's like an angel alive, and her robe and hair was flowing with the air as she ran with an embroidered handkerchief in hand and laughter on her lips.

Lyra, their only daughter.

"Ma! Pa!" She shouted, voice tiny.

The servants were quickly making way as she ran, the little scent purse on her waist dangling.

"Sweetheart!" Shayla's motherly voice made her stop, turning right.

In the Pink Island, Shayla sat on Ryker's laps, both stargazing.

"Ma, look! I found an embroidered handkerchief at the lawn!" She pointed it forward.

Ryker took it from her, then he gave Shayla a knowing smiley glance.

It's the handkerchief he gave her to wipe her tears that year after Lilith tried to hurt her in the beast forest.

"Somehow, it found it's way back to me just like the bracelet did" Shayla smiled, taking it from him.

"Pa, you both know who owns it?" Lyra asked with a blink.

"It used to belong to me, then your Ma," Ryker replied, stroking Lyra's face lovingly.

She's their whole world, they love her so much than words could write.

The last five years was easier than they expected. Their monthly visit to the human realm helped things.

Whenever they're in the nine heavens like this, Sage and Lilith take care of FrostFire activities, Xena and Cheyenne take care of Shayla's ballet agency and her schedules.

And whenever they visit the human realm, everything plays out easily.

Ryker's showcases and Shayla's concerts. Their wealth is longer than can calculated right now.

Networth uncountable.

They're heavily blessed.

"It's Christmas tomorrow, didn't you say we were going to have our monthly visit to the human realm today? I badly want to see Granny Dominika and my Grandparents Pierce, and Jenna, and aunties Xena and Rumi, also the knights and Eternus" Lyra said.

She's a talkative, obviously like her mother.

"Are you ready?" Shayla carried her.

"Yes!!" She screamed.

Ryker took her from Shayla, swaying her so hard she laughed till she starting panting and her eyes glowed gorgeously, the different colour so beautiful to watch.

"You ready, darling?" Ryker asked.

"Yes!!" She screamed again.

The main gate opened, and the wind chime by Shayla's palace door rang like a bell, signifying it's time for their departure

As they stepped out of the gate to go celebrate Christmas in the human realm, the gate closed, softly, Lyra's laughter filled the air.

THE END.
